  mnp_recipient_operator_code varchar [note: 'For MNP_PORT_OUT orders: the network operator code of the recipient (gaining) operator to whom the mobile number is being ported. Used for inter-operator coordination and MNP regulatory reporting. Null for non-MNP order types.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions associated with the fulfillment order, entered by agents, customers, or automated systems. May include customer preferences, access instructions for field technicians, or escalation context. Not used for structured data.']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'The sales or service channel through which the fulfillment order was submitted. Used for channel performance analytics, cost-to-serve analysis, and omnichannel reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ONLINE|RETAIL_STORE|CALL_CENTER|DEALER|SELF_SERVICE|B2B_PORTAL|FIELD_SALES — promote to reference product]']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the fulfillment order. Communicated to customers, field technicians, and partner systems. Sourced from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) as the canonical order reference number.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the fulfillment order, influencing queue position in orchestration workflows and resource allocation. CRITICAL is reserved for regulatory-mandated orders (e.g., emergency services, MNP regulatory deadlines).']
  order_source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system or application from which the fulfillment order was submitted. Used for channel attribution, integration troubleshooting, and data lineage tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OSM|SALESFORCE_CRM|SELF_SERVICE_PORTAL|B2B_PORTAL|DEALER_PORTAL|IVR|RETAIL_POS — promote to reference product]']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fulfillment order within the OSS/BSS orchestration workflow. Drives jeopardy management, SLA monitoring, and customer communication triggers. Sourced from Oracle Communications OSM order state machine.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment order by the nature of the service change being requested. Drives orchestration workflow selection, SLA type assignment, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NEW_ACTIVATION|UPGRADE|DOWNGRADE|DISCONNECTION|MNP_PORT_IN|MNP_PORT_OUT|CHANGE_OF_SERVICE — promote to reference product]']
  porting_authorization_code varchar [note: 'The Porting Authorization Code provided by the donor operator to authorize the number port. Required for MNP_PORT_IN orders to validate the customer\'s right to port. Null for non-MNP order types.']
  porting_number varchar [note: 'The MSISDN (mobile phone number) being ported in MNP_PORT_IN or MNP_PORT_OUT orders. Classified as confidential PII as it directly identifies a subscriber. Null for non-MNP order types. Required for NRTRDE and TAP regulatory reporting.']
  promised_completion_date date [note: 'The date committed to the customer for order fulfillment completion. Used for SLA breach detection, customer communication, and regulatory compliance reporting. Distinct from SLA target hours — this is the customer-facing commitment date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  regulatory_reference_code varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory rule, mandate, or case reference code applicable to this fulfillment order when is_regulatory_order is True. Examples: FCC MNP case number, Ofcom compliance reference. Used for regulatory audit trails and compliance reporting.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The type of telecommunications service being provisioned or modified by this fulfillment order. Drives workflow template selection, resource inventory allocation, and regulatory reporting categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIBER|IPTV|VOIP|FIXED_LINE|IOT|ENTERPRISE_WAN — promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of hours elapsed from SLA measurement start at which the SLA is formally classified as BREACHED. Typically equals sla_target_hours but may differ for grace-period configurations. Triggers regulatory reporting obligations and penalty calculations.']
  sla_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fulfillment order SLA was formally breached. Null for orders that have not breached. Used for regulatory reporting, penalty calculation, and SLA performance analytics. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  sla_measurement_end_event varchar [note: 'The business event that stops the SLA measurement clock for this fulfillment order. Defines what constitutes successful completion for SLA purposes — e.g., SERVICE_ACTIVATED for mobile activations, CPE_INSTALLED for FTTH orders.']
  sla_measurement_start_event varchar [note: 'The business event that triggers the start of SLA measurement clock for this fulfillment order. Varies by order type and SLA type — regulatory MNP orders typically start at ORDER_ACCEPTED, while commercial orders may start at PAYMENT_CONFIRMED.']
  sla_measurement_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the SLA measurement clock started for this fulfillment order, corresponding to the sla_measurement_start_event. Used as the reference point for all SLA elapsed-time calculations and regulatory compliance measurement. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current SLA compliance status of the fulfillment order based on elapsed time versus SLA thresholds. ON_TRACK: within warning threshold. AT_RISK: between warning and breach thresholds. BREACHED: exceeded breach threshold. Primary field for jeopardy detection and regulatory compliance dashboards.']
  sla_target_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum number of hours within which the fulfillment order must be completed to meet the SLA commitment. Measured from the SLA measurement start event. Used for jeopardy detection and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA commitment governing this fulfillment order. REGULATORY: mandated by FCC/Ofcom/BEREC regulations (e.g., MNP porting windows). COMMERCIAL: standard commercial SLA per product tier. ENTERPRISE: premium SLA for enterprise/B2B customers.']
  sla_warning_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of hours elapsed from SLA measurement start at which an AT_RISK warning is triggered for proactive jeopardy management. Must be less than sla_target_hours. Drives automated alerting in NOC and order management dashboards.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fulfillment order was submitted by the customer or agent into the order management system. This is the principal business event timestamp and the anchor point for SLA measurement start calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  workflow_template_name varchar [note: 'The name of the OSM workflow template (order specification) used to orchestrate this fulfillment order. Determines the decomposition strategy, task sequence, and system interactions. Examples: \'MOBILE_NEW_ACTIVATION_V3\', \'FTTH_PROVISIONING_V2\'.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every end-to-end order in the telecommunications lifecycle — new activations, upgrades, downgrades, disconnections, MNP port-in/port-out, and change-of-service requests. Sourced from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM). Captures order type, order channel, priority, customer reference, account reference, submitted timestamp, promised completion date, current order state, fallout reason, orchestration workflow instance ID, workflow template, decomposition strategy, SLA type (regulatory, commercial, enterprise), SLA target completion hours, warning threshold hours, breach threshold hours, SLA measurement start/end events, current SLA status (ON_TRACK, AT_RISK, BREACHED), and breach timestamp. This is the SSOT for order identity, lifecycle state, orchestration context, and SLA commitment within the order domain. All SLA monitoring, jeopardy detection, and regulatory compliance measurement derives from this master record\'s SLA attributes.'
}

Table order.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order line item within the fulfillment order management system. Primary key for the order_line entity. Role: TRANSACTION_LINE.']
  addon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.addon. Business justification: Addon products ordered as separate lines need direct catalog linkage for provisioning orchestration, billing setup, and quota management. Critical for value-added services fulfillment.']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle orders require direct catalog reference for component validation, bundle pricing enforcement, discount calculation, and eligibility verification. Critical for multi-product offer fulfillment.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Line-level revenue must be attributed to legal entity for proper revenue recognition and GL posting. Required for multi-entity telcos with separate legal entities per market or service type.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Line-level corporate account tracking enables product-level revenue attribution per enterprise customer, supports multi-site service deployments, and facilitates usage analysis for account management ']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical service delivery address for fixed-line, broadband, or FTTH order lines. Used for field dispatch scheduling in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management and network infrastructure provisioning.']
  device_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.device_offering. Business justification: Device orders must reference the specific device offering for subsidy calculation, inventory allocation, installment plan setup, and trade-in eligibility. Essential for device sales operations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or agent who captured this order line. Used for commission calculation, performance reporting, and accountability tracking in Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA.']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Line-level contract linkage enables contract utilization tracking, commitment burn-down analysis, volume discount tier validation, and supports complex enterprise pricing schedules with product-specif']
  enterprise_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.site. Business justification: Line items provision services at specific enterprise sites (circuits, managed services, UC seats). Required for site-level service inventory management, capacity planning, and multi-site enterprise de']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent fulfillment order header. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category. Links this line item to its containing order transaction in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  inventory_cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Order lines provision CPE devices (modems, routers, ONTs) to customers. Real business need: installation tracking, warranty claims, device lifecycle management, asset return processing. Telecommunicat']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: Order lines must reference the specific price plan being ordered for accurate rating, billing, and revenue recognition. Critical for order-to-cash process and finance reconciliation in telecom operati']
  ran_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: Mobile service order lines must specify serving RAN cell for coverage validation and capacity planning. Essential for mobile activations, cell load balancing, and ensuring service address is within ce']
  rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing rate plan in Amdocs Revenue Management that governs the recurring and usage-based charges for the product on this order line. Established at order capture and used to configure the billing account upon fulfillment.']
  sim_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sim_stock. Business justification: Order lines activate specific SIM cards for mobile service. Real business need: SIM allocation tracking, activation audit trail, MNP porting coordination, inventory consumption. Existing iccid/imsi ar']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial product or service offering in the product catalog that this order line is acting upon (e.g., a specific mobile plan, broadband tier, or add-on). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE RESOURCE_REFERENCE category. Sourced from Netcracker OSS/BSS Product Catalog.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer subscription record that this order line creates, modifies, or terminates. Links the order line to the recurring billing subscription in Amdocs Revenue Management for revenue lifecycle tracking.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provisioned service instance created or modified as a result of fulfilling this order line. Populated post-fulfillment by Oracle Communications Order and Service Management upon successful service activation. Links the commercial order line to the operational service layer.']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a ServiceNow ITSM trouble ticket raised against this order line due to a fulfillment failure, fallout, or customer complaint. Used for fault management correlation and order-to-ticket traceability.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field work order created in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management for order lines requiring a field technician visit. Populated when requires_field_visit is TRUE and the work order has been dispatched.']
  action_code varchar [note: 'The fulfillment action to be performed for this line item. Drives order decomposition and workflow routing in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. ADD = new activation; REMOVE = disconnection; MODIFY = change of service; SUSPEND = temporary suspension; RESUME = reactivation; PORT_IN = Mobile Number Portability (MNP) inbound; PORT_OUT = MNP outbound. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ADD|REMOVE|MODIFY|SUSPEND|RESUME|PORT_IN|PORT_OUT — promote to reference product]']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date on which fulfillment of this order line was completed and the service was activated, modified, or disconnected. Compared against committed_delivery_date for SLA performance measurement and MTTR (Mean Time to Repair) analytics.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'The sales or service channel through which this order line was initiated (e.g., ONLINE web portal, RETAIL_STORE, CALL_CENTER, DEALER partner, SELF_SERVICE app, B2B_PORTAL). Used for channel performance analytics, commission calculation, and order routing.']
  committed_delivery_date date [note: 'The date committed to the customer for delivery or activation of the service on this line, as agreed at order capture. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance monitoring and breach detection in service fulfillment reporting.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'The minimum contract commitment period in months associated with the product on this order line (e.g., 0 for month-to-month, 12, 24, 36). Drives early termination fee (ETF) calculation and churn risk assessment in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  cpe_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the CPE (Customer Premises Equipment) device — such as a router, ONT (Optical Network Terminal), or set-top box — assigned or dispatched as part of this order line. Used for inventory tracking in Netcracker OSS/BSS and field installation management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this order line record was first created in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Used for order aging analysis, SLA measurement baseline, and audit trail compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the monetary values on this order line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency enterprise and wholesale order scenarios.']
  decomposition_state varchar [note: 'Indicates the order decomposition processing state for this line within Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Order decomposition translates a commercial product order line into technical service and resource fulfillment tasks across OSS systems. Critical for orchestration monitoring and fault management.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this order line at the time of order capture, reflecting promotional offers, loyalty discounts, or negotiated enterprise pricing. Used by Amdocs Revenue Management for billing and revenue assurance reconciliation.']
  early_termination_fee decimal(18,4) [note: 'The early termination fee amount applicable if the customer cancels this order line before the end of the contract_term_months. Calculated at order capture based on the rate plan and contract term. Used by Amdocs Revenue Management for billing upon disconnection.']
  fallout_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the order line fallout or failure, supplementing the fallout_reason_code. Captured by Oracle Communications Order and Service Management workflow engine for manual intervention and customer communication.']
  fallout_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for order line fallout or failure during fulfillment orchestration (e.g., \'CREDIT_FAIL\', \'INVENTORY_UNAVAILABLE\', \'ADDRESS_NOT_SERVICEABLE\', \'PORTING_REJECTED\'). Used for fallout management, root cause analysis, and process improvement in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  iccid varchar [note: 'The unique SIM card identifier (ICCID) assigned to this order line for mobile service activations. Used for SIM provisioning in HLR/HSS/UDM systems and inventory management in Netcracker OSS/BSS. Classified as confidential as it identifies a specific subscriber SIM.']
  imsi varchar [note: 'The International Mobile Subscriber Identity assigned to the SIM for this order line. Used for subscriber provisioning in HSS (Home Subscriber Server) and UDM (Unified Data Management) systems. Restricted PII as it uniquely identifies a mobile subscriber globally.']
  is_esim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order line involves an eSIM (Embedded SIM) provisioning rather than a physical SIM card. When TRUE, triggers eSIM profile download orchestration via GSMA SGP.22 RSP (Remote SIM Provisioning) instead of physical SIM dispatch.']
  is_mnp_line boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order line involves a Mobile Number Portability (MNP) port-in or port-out transaction. When TRUE, triggers MNP-specific workflow orchestration including donor operator notification and number portability clearinghouse coordination.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this order line record was last updated in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, audit trail, and concurrency control.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this line item within the parent order. Used for ordering, display, and referencing individual lines in multi-line orders (e.g., line 1 = mobile plan, line 2 = broadband, line 3 = MNP port-in). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment lifecycle state of this individual order line. Tracks granular progress independently from the parent order header status, enabling partial fulfillment scenarios where some lines complete while others are held. Sourced from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management workflow engine.']
  mnp_donor_operator varchar [note: 'For PORT_IN action lines, the name or code of the donor (losing) network operator from whom the mobile number is being ported. Required for MNP (Mobile Number Portability) process orchestration, GSMA NRTRDE compliance, and inter-operator coordination.']
  mnp_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the number portability clearinghouse or regulatory body for a PORT_IN or PORT_OUT order line. Used for inter-operator tracking, regulatory compliance reporting to FCC/Ofcom/BEREC, and dispute resolution.']
  msisdn varchar [note: 'The mobile phone number (MSISDN) associated with this order line for mobile service activations, modifications, or MNP port transactions. Follows E.164 international numbering format. Classified as confidential PII as it directly identifies a subscriber.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net commercial price for this order line after applying discounts (unit_price × quantity − discount_amount). Represents the agreed charge basis passed to Amdocs Revenue Management for billing. Distinct from tax-inclusive total.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Processing priority assigned to this order line for fulfillment queue management. HIGH and CRITICAL priorities are used for enterprise SLA orders, regulatory-mandated activations, and emergency service restorations. Drives scheduling in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the product or service being ordered on this line (e.g., \'5G Unlimited Plan\', \'FTTH 1Gbps Broadband\', \'VoLTE Add-On\'). Denormalized from the product catalog for operational reporting and customer-facing order confirmations.']
  product_offering_code varchar [note: 'External-facing alphanumeric code identifying the specific product offering or SKU in the product catalog (e.g., \'MOB-5G-UNL-001\', \'BB-FTTH-1G-002\'). Used for catalog lookup, billing rate plan association, and order decomposition in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the product or service being ordered on this line. Drives workflow routing, provisioning system selection, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIXED_LINE|IPTV|VOIP|IOT|ENTERPRISE|ADD_ON — promote to reference product]']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Promotional or campaign code applied to this order line at the time of order capture, qualifying the customer for a specific discount, bundle offer, or incentive. Used for marketing campaign effectiveness analysis and discount validation in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product or service being ordered on this line. Typically 1 for most telecom service lines but may be greater for bulk SIM orders, enterprise seat licenses, or CPE equipment orders. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.']
  requested_start_date date [note: 'The customer-requested date on which the product or service on this line should become active. Drives service provisioning scheduling in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management and SLA commitment tracking.']
  requires_field_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fulfillment of this order line requires a field technician visit (e.g., FTTH installation, CPE installation, ONT splicing). When TRUE, triggers work order creation and field dispatch scheduling in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Billing and operational service type for this order line, distinguishing postpaid subscription, prepaid (Comverse ONE), hybrid, MVNO (Mobile Virtual Network Operator) resale, or wholesale service arrangements. Determines downstream billing system routing.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this order line record in the originating operational system (Oracle Communications Order and Service Management). Enables lineage tracing, reconciliation, and idempotent upsert processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Network access technology associated with the product on this order line (e.g., LTE, 5G NR, FTTH/GPON, DSL). Used for network provisioning routing to Ericsson Network Manager or Nokia NetAct, capacity planning, and technology migration analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 2G|3G|4G_LTE|5G_NR|FTTH_GPON|FTTB|DSL|CABLE|FIXED_WIRELESS — promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Commercial price per unit for the product or service on this line at the time of order placement, before discounts and taxes. Represents the catalog or negotiated price used for billing rate plan association in Amdocs Revenue Management. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a fulfillment order representing a single product or service action (add, remove, modify, suspend, resume). Each line carries the commercial product reference, requested action code, quantity, requested service start date, line-level status, and decomposition state. Supports multi-line orders where a customer simultaneously activates a mobile plan, adds a broadband service, and ports a number in a single order transaction.'
}

Table order.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order status history record. Primary key of the order_status_history data product. Immutable once assigned.']
  actor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific actor (system process name, agent employee ID, or customer reference) that triggered the transition or amendment. Supports audit trail requirements for FCC and BEREC regulatory reporting and dispute resolution.']
  appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field service appointment associated with this order history record, particularly relevant for APPOINTMENT_CHANGE amendments or transitions triggered by appointment outcomes. Links to Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management scheduling records.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with the fulfillment order. Enables customer-level SLA tracking, dispute resolution, and churn analysis across the order lifecycle.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or system identifier of the agent or supervisor who approved or rejected the amendment. Null for state transition records and for system-auto-approved amendments. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order for which this status history record was created. Links every history entry back to its originating order in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  service_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the decomposed service order generated from the parent fulfillment order in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management or Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite. Supports traceability between commercial order history and service fulfillment execution.']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a ServiceNow ITSM trouble ticket or incident raised in association with this state transition or amendment (e.g., a FALLOUT event that triggered an incident). Null when no ticket is associated. Supports cross-system traceability for fault management.']
  workflow_task_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific workflow task or orchestration step within Oracle Communications Order and Service Management that generated this history record. Enables traceability from the history record back to the originating workflow execution for technical root-cause analysis.']
  actor_system varchar [note: 'Name of the OSS/BSS system that originated the transition or amendment event (e.g., Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite, Salesforce CRM, ServiceNow ITSM). Enables source-system traceability for fallout root-cause analysis.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the type of actor that triggered the state transition or amendment. SYSTEM indicates an automated OSS/BSS process; AGENT indicates a human CSR or field technician; CUSTOMER indicates a self-service action; SUPERVISOR indicates a management override; AUTOMATED_RULE indicates a policy engine or workflow rule.']
  amended_field_name varchar [note: 'For amendment records, the logical name of the order attribute that was changed (e.g., installation_address, appointment_date, selected_product_id, service_configuration_value). Provides context for interpreting original_value and new_value. Null for state transition records.']
  amendment_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the amendment was formally requested by the customer or agent. Distinct from transition_timestamp (which records when the event was processed). Used to measure amendment processing lead time and SLA compliance. Null for state transition records. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the amendment request. PENDING: awaiting review; APPROVED: authorized but not yet applied to the order; REJECTED: denied by agent or system rule; APPLIED: successfully incorporated into the active order. Null for state transition records.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'For amendment records, classifies the type of post-submission change requested. Null for state transition records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: APPOINTMENT_CHANGE|PRODUCT_SELECTION_CHANGE|ADDRESS_CHANGE|SERVICE_CONFIGURATION_CHANGE|CONTACT_CHANGE|PAYMENT_METHOD_CHANGE — promote to reference product]']
  cost_delta decimal(18,4) [note: 'For amendment records, the incremental cost impact (positive or negative, in the account currency) resulting from the amendment — e.g., a product selection change that increases or decreases the order value. Null for state transition records and zero-cost amendments. Used for revenue assurance and billing reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this history record was first written to the Silver Layer lakehouse. Distinct from transition_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data pipeline monitoring, late-arrival detection, and audit trail completeness verification. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the cost_delta amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null when cost_delta is null. Supports multi-currency operations across international markets.']
  duration_in_state_seconds varchar [note: 'Number of seconds the order spent in the previous_state before transitioning to new_state. Calculated at ingestion time as the difference between this record\'s transition_timestamp and the prior record\'s transition_timestamp for the same order. Used for SLA bottleneck analysis and process mining.']
  fallout_category varchar [note: 'When new_state is FALLOUT, classifies the root-cause category of the fallout event (e.g., PROVISIONING_FAILURE, CREDIT_REJECTION, ADDRESS_VALIDATION_FAILURE, MNP_REJECTION, NETWORK_CAPACITY, SYSTEM_ERROR). Null for non-FALLOUT transitions. Essential for fallout root-cause analysis and NOC/OSS remediation workflows.']
  fallout_resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating how a FALLOUT condition was resolved (e.g., AUTO_RETRY_SUCCESS, MANUAL_INTERVENTION, ORDER_CANCELLED, REROUTED_TO_ALTERNATE_RESOURCE). Populated when transitioning out of FALLOUT state. Null for non-FALLOUT records.']
  is_regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this history record is subject to regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., FCC order completion reporting, BEREC quality-of-service metrics, OFCOM service provisioning KPIs). True flags records that must be included in regulatory submissions.']
  mnp_reference_number varchar [note: 'For MNP port-in or port-out order types, the inter-operator MNP reference number assigned by the number portability clearinghouse. Null for non-MNP orders. Required for GSMA MNP compliance reporting and inter-carrier dispute resolution.']
  new_state varchar [note: 'The order lifecycle state entered as a result of this transition. For amendment records, reflects the order state at the time the amendment was recorded. Valid states include SUBMITTED, VALIDATED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_PROVISIONING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, FALLOUT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUBMITTED|VALIDATED|IN_PROGRESS|PENDING_PROVISIONING|COMPLETED|CANCELLED|FALLOUT — promote to reference product]']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'For amendment records, the proposed or applied new value for the amended field (serialized as string for flexibility across amendment types). Null for state transition records. Paired with original_value to provide a complete before/after audit record.']
  original_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'For amendment records, the value of the amended field before the change was applied (serialized as string for flexibility across amendment types). Null for state transition records. Supports dispute resolution and regulatory audit trails.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a standard business rule, credit policy, or SLA policy was overridden to process this transition or amendment. When True, override_justification must be populated. Supports compliance monitoring and exception reporting.']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Free-text justification provided by a supervisor or authorized agent when a standard business rule or SLA policy is overridden to approve an amendment or force a state transition. Required for compliance audit trails when override_flag is True.']
  previous_state varchar [note: 'The order lifecycle state immediately before this transition occurred. Null for the initial SUBMITTED state entry. Valid states include SUBMITTED, VALIDATED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_PROVISIONING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, FALLOUT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUBMITTED|VALIDATED|IN_PROGRESS|PENDING_PROVISIONING|COMPLETED|CANCELLED|FALLOUT — promote to reference product]']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for the state transition or amendment (e.g., CREDIT_CHECK_FAILED, PROVISIONING_TIMEOUT, CUSTOMER_REQUESTED_CANCEL, MNP_PORT_DELAY, NETWORK_CAPACITY_UNAVAILABLE). Used for fallout root-cause analysis and SLA reporting.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the state transition or amendment reason, supplementing the structured reason_code. May include agent notes, system error messages, or customer-provided explanations captured at the time of the event.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this history record captures a state transition (order lifecycle state change) or a post-submission amendment (customer or agent-initiated change to order attributes). Drives downstream processing logic and reporting segmentation.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing integer representing the chronological order of history records for a given order_id. Sequence 1 is the initial SUBMITTED state entry. Enables ordered reconstruction of the complete order lifecycle and detection of out-of-sequence processing anomalies.']
  sla_reset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this state transition or amendment triggered a reset of the order fulfillment SLA clock. True when the SLA measurement window is restarted (e.g., due to a customer-requested amendment or a FALLOUT recovery). Critical for accurate SLA compliance reporting to FCC and BEREC.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The committed SLA completion deadline applicable at the time of this history record. If sla_reset_flag is True, this reflects the recalculated SLA target post-reset. Enables point-in-time SLA compliance measurement and regulatory reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native primary key or event identifier of this record in the originating source system (e.g., the Oracle Communications Order and Service Management order history event ID). Enables reverse traceability from the Silver Layer back to the operational system for reconciliation and reprocessing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this history record was sourced (e.g., Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite, Salesforce CRM). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the state transition or amendment event occurred. This is the principal business event timestamp used for SLA measurement, fallout root-cause analysis, and regulatory audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this history record was last modified in the Silver Layer (e.g., due to a late-arriving amendment status update or correction). For a fully immutable audit trail, updates should be rare and logged. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of every state transition and post-submission amendment for a fulfillment order throughout its lifecycle — from SUBMITTED through VALIDATED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_PROVISIONING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, or FALLOUT. For state transitions: records previous state, new state, transition timestamp, triggering actor (system or user), and reason code. For amendments: records amendment type (appointment change, product selection change, address change, service configuration change), original value, new value, amendment request timestamp, amendment status (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, APPLIED), approving agent reference, impact assessment (SLA reset flag, cost delta), and override justification. This is the SSOT for all order change history — both system-driven state transitions and customer/agent-initiated amendments. Essential for SLA measurement, fallout root-cause analysis, dispute resolution, and regulatory audit trails required by FCC and BEREC.'
}

Table order.task {
  task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each decomposed order task record generated by the Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) orchestration engine. Primary key for the order_task data product.']
  assigned_technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field technician assigned to execute this task in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management. Populated for CPE dispatch and GPON ONT activation tasks requiring physical field intervention. Drives field dispatch scheduling and route optimisation.']
  change_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.change_record. Business justification: Provisioning tasks coordinated with planned network changes to avoid activation conflicts. Standard change management practice in telco operations for service turn-up.']
  decomposition_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OSM decomposition rule applied to generate this task from the parent order line. Enables audit of which orchestration logic produced the task and supports regression analysis when decomposition rules are updated.']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: Field-work tasks are dispatched from specific depots managing territory coverage, parts inventory, and technician availability. Critical for logistics planning, resource allocation, and technician dis']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commercial order header that contains the order line from which this task was decomposed. Enables direct navigation from task to top-level order for end-to-end fulfillment traceability.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commercial order line from which this task was decomposed. Establishes the traceability chain from commercial order line to atomic fulfillment task within the OSM orchestration engine.']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Provisioning tasks target specific managed service instances (SD-WAN, MPLS, managed security) for configuration changes, capacity modifications, or service updates. Required for service orchestration ']
  network_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Provisioning tasks execute configuration commands against specific network elements (configure subscriber profile on BRAS, provision VLAN on switch). Core to automated service fulfillment workflows an']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ServiceNow ITSM incident or change request raised in response to a task fallout or execution failure. Populated when fallout_indicator is True and an ITSM ticket has been created for manual resolution. Enables cross-system traceability between order fulfillment and incident management.']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Decomposed order tasks configure ONT devices in child systems (OSS/BSS/EMS). Real business need: task-level device tracking, fallout resolution, technician dispatch coordination, configuration rollbac']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.ott_platform. Business justification: Decomposed orchestration tasks include platform-specific provisioning steps ("Provision Netflix access", "Activate Disney+ SSO"). Task execution requires knowing which OTT platform API to invoke for a']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service instance in the Netcracker service inventory that this task provisions, modifies, or deactivates. Links the fulfillment task to the service layer for end-to-end service lifecycle traceability.']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network or physical resource (e.g., GPON ONT, CPE device, IP address, MSISDN, SIM) in the Netcracker resource inventory that this task acts upon. Enables resource-level fulfillment traceability and inventory reconciliation.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field work order in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management associated with this task. Populated for tasks requiring physical field dispatch (CPE installation, GPON ONT activation). Links fulfillment task to field operations scheduling.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time at which execution of this task commenced in the downstream target system. Compared against scheduled_start_time to measure scheduling adherence and identify orchestration delays.']
  automation_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this task was executed automatically by the OSM orchestration engine (True) or required manual intervention by an operations team member (False). Key metric for measuring fulfillment automation rates and identifying candidates for further automation.']
  child_order_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of lower-level order generated by the decomposition of the parent commercial order line. Distinguishes between service-layer orders (service_order), resource-layer orders (resource_order), and network-layer orders (network_order) per TM Forum SID decomposition hierarchy.']
  child_system_target varchar [note: 'Identifies the downstream OSS/BSS system to which this task is dispatched for execution (e.g., HLR, HSS, GPON-OLT, Amdocs, Netcracker, Ericsson Network Manager). Critical for cross-system orchestration traceability and fallout diagnosis.']
  child_system_task_ref varchar [note: 'The task or work order identifier assigned by the downstream target system (e.g., Ericsson Network Manager work order ID, Netcracker service order number, ServiceNow change request number). Enables cross-system correlation and reconciliation between OSM and downstream fulfillment systems.']
  completion_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this task reached a terminal state (completed, failed, or cancelled) in the OSM orchestration engine. Used to calculate task duration, SLA compliance, and MTTR for fulfillment operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this order task record was first created in the OSM orchestration engine following order decomposition. Serves as the audit creation timestamp and the baseline for SLA elapsed-time calculations.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Data governance classification level for this order task record per the enterprise data classification policy. Determines access controls, retention policies, and handling requirements in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  decomposition_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version of the decomposition rule applied at the time this task was generated. Supports impact analysis when orchestration rules are updated and enables reproducibility of order decomposition logic for audit purposes.']
  dependency_task_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of order_task_id values that must reach \'completed\' status before this task can begin execution. Captures the dependency chain within the OSM orchestration workflow to enforce correct fulfillment sequencing (e.g., HLR provisioning must complete before VoLTE IMS registration).']
  due_time timestamp [note: 'The SLA-committed deadline by which this task must reach \'completed\' status. Derived from the parent order\'s committed delivery date and the task\'s position in the fulfillment sequence. Breach of this timestamp triggers escalation workflows.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Machine-readable error code returned by the downstream target system upon task execution failure (e.g., HLR error codes, GPON provisioning error codes, HTTP status codes from REST APIs). Distinct from fallout_reason_code which is an OSM-normalised classification.']
  execution_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The elapsed time in seconds between actual_start_time and completion_time for this task. Populated upon task completion. Used for provisioning performance benchmarking, SLA analysis, and identification of slow downstream systems contributing to order fulfillment delays.']
  execution_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering position of this task within the overall order fulfillment workflow. Determines the sequence in which tasks are executed relative to sibling tasks on the same order line. Lower values execute first.']
  fallout_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the error or exception that caused this task to fall out, as returned by the downstream target system or the OSM orchestration engine. Provides diagnostic context for manual resolution by NOC or BSS operations teams.']
  fallout_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this task has entered fallout status requiring manual intervention or escalation. True when the task has exceeded retry limits or encountered an unrecoverable error. Drives fallout management queues in OSM and ServiceNow ITSM.']
  fallout_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code identifying the root cause of task fallout (e.g., DOWNSTREAM_TIMEOUT, INVALID_MSISDN, RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE, GPON_PORT_EXHAUSTED, HLR_REJECT). Populated when fallout_indicator is True. Used for fallout trend analysis and systemic issue identification.']
  imsi varchar [note: 'The International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) of the SIM card being provisioned by this task. Populated for HLR/HSS provisioning and SIM activation tasks. A 15-digit globally unique subscriber identifier per 3GPP standards. Classified as confidential as it uniquely identifies a subscriber on the network.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this order task record was most recently modified in the OSM orchestration engine. Tracks state transitions, retry attempts, and synchronisation updates for audit and change-management purposes.']
  max_retry_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of retry attempts permitted for this task before it is automatically escalated to fallout management. Configured per task type and downstream system target in the OSM orchestration engine.']
  mnp_port_reference varchar [note: 'The Mobile Number Portability (MNP) porting reference number assigned by the number portability clearinghouse or donor network. Populated only for tasks of type \'number_portability_submission\'. Required for GSMA NRTRDE compliance and FCC MNP regulatory reporting.']
  msisdn varchar [note: 'The Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN) — the subscriber\'s mobile phone number — associated with the provisioning action performed by this task. Populated for HLR/HSS provisioning, SIM activation, and MNP tasks. Classified as confidential as it is a customer-linked network identifier.']
  network_technology varchar [note: 'The network technology domain relevant to this provisioning task. Determines which network management system (Ericsson Network Manager for RAN, Nokia NetAct for core, OLT for GPON) handles execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 4G_LTE|5G_NR|FTTH_GPON|VoLTE|IMS|MPLS|SD_WAN|DSLAM|fixed_wireless|eSIM|NFV|SDN — promote to reference product]']
  ont_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the Optical Network Terminal (ONT) / Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) being activated or configured by this task. Populated for GPON ONT activation and CPE dispatch tasks. Used for FTTH inventory reconciliation and field technician dispatch coordination.']
  order_action_type varchar [note: 'The commercial order action that triggered the creation of this task. Inherited from the parent order line and used to determine the appropriate provisioning logic in the downstream target system. MNP (Mobile Number Portability) port-in/port-out tasks require coordination with GSMA NRTRDE and TAP processes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_activation|upgrade|downgrade|disconnection|mnp_port_in|mnp_port_out|change_of_service — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this task, inherited from the parent order or overridden by the OSM orchestration engine based on customer segment, SLA tier, or order type. Drives execution queue ordering and resource allocation in fulfillment operations.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times execution of this task has been retried following a failure or timeout. Used to enforce maximum retry thresholds, trigger fallout management escalation, and analyse systemic provisioning failure patterns across downstream systems.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which this task is scheduled to begin execution within the OSM orchestration engine. Used for SLA target calculation, capacity planning, and workforce scheduling in field dispatch scenarios.']
  sla_breach_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this task has breached its SLA due_time commitment. Set to True when completion_time exceeds due_time, or when the task remains in a non-terminal state past due_time. Drives SLA reporting, customer compensation workflows, and regulatory compliance reporting to FCC/Ofcom.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this order task record originates (e.g., OSM, Netcracker, Amdocs). Used for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and cross-system reconciliation in the Revenue Assurance domain.']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the synchronisation state of this task with the downstream target system. Tracks whether the task execution result has been confirmed and reconciled back to the OSM orchestration engine from the child system (e.g., HLR, GPON-OLT, Netcracker).']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this order task as assigned by the OSM orchestration engine (e.g., TSK-2024-00123456). Used in NOC dashboards, trouble tickets, and cross-system correlation.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order task within the OSM orchestration engine. Drives SLA monitoring, fallout management, and retry logic. Valid states: pending (awaiting execution), in_progress (actively executing), completed (successfully fulfilled), failed (execution error), cancelled (task voided), suspended (paused pending dependency resolution).']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the atomic fulfillment action this task represents within the OSM orchestration workflow. Drives routing to the appropriate downstream system and execution handler. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hlr_hss_provisioning|ip_address_assignment|cpe_dispatch|gpon_ont_activation|number_portability_submission|sim_activation|service_configuration|esim_provisioning|voip_registration|ims_registration|mpls_circuit_setup|ran_configuration — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Granular decomposed work task generated by OSM order orchestration engine from a specific order line during order decomposition. Represents both the decomposition mapping (tracing commercial order line to lower-level service/resource/network orders) and the atomic fulfillment execution step (HLR/HSS provisioning, IP address assignment, CPE dispatch, GPON ONT activation, number portability submission). Captures parent order line reference, decomposition rule applied, child system target, child order type (service_order, resource_order, network_order), task type, execution sequence, dependency chain, start time, completion time, retry count, task-level status, synchronisation status with downstream systems, creation timestamp, and fallout detail. Provides full decomposition traceability from commercial order line through orchestration logic to network provisioning action — serving as the SSOT for both order decomposition mapping and task execution within this domain.'
}

Table order.fallout {
  fallout_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order fallout record in the Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) system. Primary key for the order_fallout data product.']
  assigned_technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: Fallout resolution for field-related failures (site access, equipment issues, address problems) requires direct technician assignment for expedited recovery. Critical for operational escalation tracki']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: When compliance audits discover order processing deficiencies (e.g., inadequate credit checks, missing consent verification), fallout records are tagged to audit findings for remediation tracking. Rea']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise fallout tracking enables account-level quality metrics, TAM escalation workflows, customer experience dashboards for B2B customers, and proactive account management for at-risk enterprise r']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fallout resolution incurs operational costs (manual intervention, system fixes, customer care) that must be tracked by cost center for process improvement ROI analysis and operational expense budgetin']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer whose order has fallen out. Enables customer-level fallout analysis and SLA breach tracking in Salesforce CRM and BSS operations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual NOC or BSS operations agent currently assigned to resolve this fallout record. Enables agent-level workload and performance tracking.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order that entered fallout state. Links this fallout record to the originating order in the Order Management domain.']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding ServiceNow ITSM incident ticket raised for this fallout event. Enables cross-system traceability between order management and IT service management workflows.']
  problem_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.problem_record. Business justification: Recurring order fallouts linked to known network problems for pattern analysis and permanent fix tracking. Enables proactive resolution of systemic provisioning issues.']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product catalog entry for the product or service being ordered. Used to analyze fallout rates by product and identify catalog or provisioning configuration issues.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'The service instance identifier from the Netcracker Service Catalog or Oracle OSM inventory that is the subject of the failing order. Enables service-level impact analysis.']
  task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific orchestration task within the order workflow that triggered the fallout condition. Maps to the OSM task-level decomposition unit.']
  affected_msisdn varchar [note: 'The Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN) of the subscriber line affected by this fallout. Stored in E.164 format. Classified confidential as it is a customer-linked service identifier.']
  affected_service_type varchar [note: 'The type of telecommunications service affected by the fallout. Used to route fallout cases to the appropriate specialist team and apply service-specific resolution procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIBER|IPTV|VOIP|ENTERPRISE_WAN|WHOLESALE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Name of the NOC or BSS operations team assigned to resolve this fallout. Drives workqueue routing and team-level SLA accountability reporting.']
  auto_resolution_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fallout record is eligible for automated resolution without manual agent intervention, based on fallout category, retry history, and configured automation rules.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The sales or service channel through which the original order was submitted. Used to identify channel-specific fallout patterns and drive channel-level quality improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ONLINE|RETAIL_STORE|CALL_CENTER|DEALER|SELF_SERVICE|B2B_PORTAL|API — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fallout record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment of the account associated with the fallen-out order. Denormalized for workqueue prioritization and segment-level SLA application without requiring a join to the customer domain.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the order orchestration engine first detected and recorded the fallout condition. This is the principal business event timestamp for SLA measurement and aging calculations.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'System-generated error code returned by the failing downstream system or orchestration engine (e.g., OSM, HLR/HSS, provisioning platform). Used for automated pattern detection and root cause analysis.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Full descriptive error message returned by the failing system at the time of fallout detection. Provides technical context for resolution agents and is used in automated error classification models.']
  escalated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fallout record was most recently escalated to a higher resolution tier. Null if the record has never been escalated.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation tier for this fallout record (0 = not escalated, 1 = team lead, 2 = manager, 3 = vendor/third-party). Incremented automatically when SLA thresholds are breached.']
  failing_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the downstream OSS/BSS system that returned the error or failed to respond, causing the order to fall out. Examples: HLR, HSS, PROVISIONING_ENGINE, INVENTORY_MANAGER, CREDIT_SYSTEM, MNP_CLEARINGHOUSE.']
  failing_system_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The transaction or correlation identifier returned by the failing downstream system. Enables cross-system tracing and incident correlation in ServiceNow ITSM and NOC tooling.']
  fallout_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause that drove the order into fallout state. Drives workqueue routing and resolution team assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VALIDATION_FAILURE|PROVISIONING_TIMEOUT|INVENTORY_SHORTAGE|DOWNSTREAM_SYSTEM_ERROR|CREDIT_CHECK_FAILURE|DUPLICATE_ORDER|MNP_PORT_FAILURE|FRAUD_HOLD|CAPACITY_EXHAUSTION|CONFIGURATION_ERROR — promote to reference product]']
  fallout_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fallout record within the NOC/BSS operations workqueue. Drives SLA breach prevention workflows and management dashboards.']
  last_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent automated or manual re-submission attempt for this fallout order. Used to enforce retry interval policies and prevent retry storms.']
  max_retry_allowed varchar [note: 'Maximum number of automated re-submission attempts permitted for this order type before mandatory escalation to manual resolution. Configured per order type and fallout category.']
  mnp_port_reference varchar [note: 'Mobile Number Portability (MNP) porting reference number applicable when the fallout is associated with a port-in or port-out order. Null for non-MNP order types. Used for MNP clearinghouse reconciliation.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to this fallout record, determining workqueue position and escalation urgency. Derived from order type, customer segment, and SLA exposure at time of fallout detection.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this fallout event by the OSS/BSS operations center. Used by NOC and BSS workqueue agents to track and communicate about the fallout case.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the order was being fulfilled. Used for regional fallout rate analysis and to route cases to geographically appropriate field or provisioning teams.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective action taken by the resolution agent to address the fallout root cause. Captured at resolution time and used for knowledge base and automation training.']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the resolution action applied to close this fallout. Enables resolution pattern analysis and automation opportunity identification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AUTO_RETRY_SUCCESS|MANUAL_CORRECTION|ORDER_CANCELLED|ORDER_MODIFIED|INVENTORY_ALLOCATED|SYSTEM_BYPASS|ESCALATED_TO_VENDOR|CUSTOMER_CONTACTED — promote to reference product]']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fallout record was marked as resolved in the operations workqueue. Used to calculate mean time to resolve (MTTR) for fallout SLA reporting.']
  resubmission_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the order re-submission attempt after resolution action was applied. Indicates whether the order successfully completed fulfillment, failed again, is awaiting outcome, or was cancelled.']
  resubmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was resubmitted into the orchestration engine after resolution action was applied. Null if the order has not yet been resubmitted.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of automated or manual re-submission attempts made for this fallout order. Used to determine when to escalate from automated retry to manual intervention.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Structured root cause analysis narrative captured by the resolution agent or automated diagnostic engine. Documents the underlying cause beyond the error code for knowledge management and recurrence prevention.']
  sla_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this fallout record will breach its applicable SLA if not resolved. Calculated at fallout detection time based on order type SLA thresholds. Null if no SLA applies.']
  sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA for this fallout record has been breached at the time of last update. True when current timestamp exceeds sla_breach_timestamp without resolution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record that originated this fallout record. Enables data lineage tracking and source-system-specific reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the fallout category providing additional specificity for root cause analysis. Examples: ADDRESS_VALIDATION_FAILED under VALIDATION_FAILURE, HLR_TIMEOUT under PROVISIONING_TIMEOUT, SIM_STOCK_DEPLETED under INVENTORY_SHORTAGE.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fallout record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance.']
  workflow_step_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific orchestration workflow step or process node within Oracle OSM that was executing when the fallout occurred. Enables precise identification of the failure point in the order decomposition graph.']
  workflow_step_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence position of the failing workflow step within the overall order orchestration process. Used to understand how far into fulfillment the order progressed before failing.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing every order that has entered a FALLOUT or ERROR state during orchestration. Stores the fallout category (validation failure, provisioning timeout, inventory shortage, downstream system error), affected order and task references, fallout detection timestamp, assigned resolution team, resolution action taken, and re-submission outcome. Drives NOC and BSS operations center workqueues for manual intervention and SLA breach prevention.'
}

Table order.jeopardy {
  jeopardy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the order jeopardy alert record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  assigned_technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: At-risk orders requiring field intervention are assigned to specific technicians for expedited completion. Essential for jeopardy management, SLA breach prevention, and tracking technician-driven reco']
  blocking_task_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific order task or workflow step within Oracle Communications OSM that is currently blocked and causing the jeopardy condition. Used for targeted remediation.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise jeopardy management requires corporate account context for escalation routing to account teams, SLA penalty calculation per contract terms, and executive visibility for high-value customer ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Jeopardy management costs (escalation resources, expedited provisioning, premium logistics) must be allocated to cost centers for operational budgeting and to measure the financial impact of SLA risk ']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer whose order is in jeopardy. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Customer 360 and Oracle OSM order context.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual (order operations manager, customer care agent, or field technician) currently assigned as the responsible owner for resolving this jeopardy alert.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment order that is at risk. Links to the parent order record in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM).']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Jeopardy detection rules are defined as KPIs (SLA breach risk %, on-time delivery rate). Links jeopardy records to their governing KPI definitions for threshold management, reporting, and regulatory c']
  network_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the network element (e.g., Optical Line Terminal (OLT), Radio Access Network (RAN) node, Digital Subscriber Line Access Multiplexer (DSLAM)) involved in the jeopardy condition, where the root cause is network-related.']
  outage_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.outage_record. Business justification: Order jeopardies caused by network outages require outage tracking for SLA exclusion clauses and regulatory reporting. Prevents customer penalties during force majeure events.']
  product_offering_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product or service offering associated with the at-risk order. Links to the product catalog in Netcracker OSS/BSS Product Catalog.']
  regulatory_penalty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_penalty. Business justification: When order jeopardy risks regulatory penalty (missed MNP porting deadline, failed E911 activation), telcos link jeopardy records to penalty exposure for executive escalation and remediation prioritiza']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated ServiceNow ITSM incident or change ticket raised to track and resolve the underlying technical issue causing the jeopardy condition.']
  assigned_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual currently assigned as the responsible owner for resolving this jeopardy alert. Retained for operational display on dashboards without requiring a join.']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Name of the operational team (e.g., Order Operations, Field Engineering, MNP Coordination, CPE Logistics) currently responsible for resolving the jeopardy condition.']
  blocking_system varchar [note: 'The Operations Support System (OSS) or Business Support System (BSS) component where the blocking condition exists. Identifies which system team must act to resolve the jeopardy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: osm|crm|network_provisioning|mnp_gateway|cpe_logistics|billing|workforce_management|external_carrier — promote to reference product]']
  committed_completion_date date [note: 'The contractually or operationally committed date by which the fulfillment order must be completed and delivered to the customer. Serves as the SLA baseline against which jeopardy is measured.']
  compensation_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The monetary value of service credit or compensation to be applied to the customer account as a result of the SLA breach. Expressed in the local operating currency. Feeds billing adjustment workflows in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  compensation_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is eligible for service credit or compensation as a result of the SLA breach associated with this jeopardy event. Drives downstream billing adjustment workflows in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  cpe_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Logistics shipment tracking number for the Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) associated with the order, applicable when the jeopardy type is CPE shipment delay. Used to monitor delivery status.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this jeopardy alert record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the compensation amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Ensures multi-currency support for international operations.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer was notified about the jeopardy condition and potential order delay. Null if the customer has not yet been notified.']
  customer_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been proactively notified about the jeopardy condition and the potential delay to their order. True if notification has been sent; False if not yet notified.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'The source or mechanism by which the jeopardy condition was detected. Distinguishes between automated SLA monitoring engine alerts, manual review by operations staff, customer complaints, Network Operations Center (NOC) alerts, and field reports.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the jeopardy condition was first detected by the order management system or monitoring process. This is the principal business event timestamp for this entity.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the jeopardy alert was escalated to a higher tier, capturing the business context for the escalation decision.']
  escalation_tier varchar [note: 'Current escalation level of the jeopardy alert. Tier 1 is front-line order operations; Tier 2 is specialist or supervisor level; Tier 3 is senior management; Executive is C-suite or regulatory escalation.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the jeopardy alert was most recently escalated to a higher tier. Null if the alert has not been escalated.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or territory where the order fulfillment is taking place. Used for regional jeopardy trend analysis and resource allocation by Network Operations Center (NOC) and field operations teams.']
  hours_to_breach decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated number of hours remaining before the order is predicted to breach its SLA threshold at the time of jeopardy detection. Drives urgency scoring and escalation routing on operations dashboards.']
  jeopardy_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number for the jeopardy alert, used by order operations managers and customer care agents when communicating about the at-risk order. Format: JEP- followed by 10 digits.']
  jeopardy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the jeopardy alert. Tracks progression from initial detection through resolution or closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|acknowledged|in_progress|escalated|resolved|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  jeopardy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the root cause category driving the jeopardy condition. Examples include capacity constraint, field technician unavailability, Mobile Number Portability (MNP) donor delay, Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) shipment delay, network outage, or provisioning failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: capacity_constraint|field_technician_unavailable|mnp_donor_delay|cpe_shipment_delay|network_outage|provisioning_failure|system_integration_failure|regulatory_hold — promote to reference product]']
  mnp_reference_number varchar [note: 'The Mobile Number Portability (MNP) reference number assigned by the donor network or porting authority, applicable when the jeopardy type is MNP donor delay. Used to track and chase the porting request.']
  predicted_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The predicted date and time at which the fulfillment order is expected to breach its committed completion date or Service Level Agreement (SLA) threshold if no corrective action is taken. Used for prioritisation on real-time operations dashboards.']
  priority_score varchar [note: 'Numeric priority score assigned to the jeopardy alert based on factors such as customer segment, service type, SLA tier, and hours to breach. Used to rank and sequence remediation actions on operations dashboards.']
  reopen_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this jeopardy alert has been reopened after a previous resolution. A high reopen count indicates recurring or persistent fulfillment issues and is used for quality and process improvement analysis.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken or planned to resolve the jeopardy condition and restore the order to its committed completion timeline.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the jeopardy condition was resolved and the order was returned to a non-jeopardy state. Null if the jeopardy is still active.']
  revised_completion_date date [note: 'The updated expected completion date for the order after the jeopardy condition has been assessed and a revised delivery commitment has been established. May differ from the original committed completion date.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code identifying the confirmed or suspected root cause of the jeopardy condition, aligned with the OSS/BSS fault taxonomy. Used for trend analysis and process improvement reporting.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause of the jeopardy condition, providing operational context beyond the root cause code for use by order operations managers and customer care agents.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The type of telecommunications service associated with the order in jeopardy. Examples include mobile, broadband, Fiber to the Home (FTTH), fixed voice, Internet Protocol Television (IPTV), Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP), Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS), or Software-Defined Wide Area Network (SD-WAN). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile|broadband|fiber_ftth|fixed_voice|iptv|voip|enterprise_mpls|sd_wan — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the jeopardy alert indicating urgency and business impact. Critical indicates imminent SLA breach; High indicates breach likely within hours; Medium indicates breach risk within the day; Low indicates early warning with time to remediate.']
  sla_breach_occurred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Service Level Agreement (SLA) was actually breached for this order. True if the committed completion date was missed; False if the order was completed within SLA despite the jeopardy alert.']
  sla_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time at which the Service Level Agreement (SLA) was breached, i.e., when the committed completion date was passed without order fulfillment. Null if no breach occurred.']
  sla_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum number of hours allowed for order fulfillment as defined in the applicable Service Level Agreement (SLA). Jeopardy is triggered when the predicted completion time approaches or exceeds this threshold.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this jeopardy alert record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary source is Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this jeopardy alert record was most recently updated in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Audit trail field for data lineage and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Proactive jeopardy alert record raised when a fulfillment order is at risk of breaching its committed completion date or SLA threshold. Captures jeopardy type (capacity constraint, field technician unavailability, MNP donor delay, CPE shipment delay), severity level, detection timestamp, predicted breach time, assigned owner, escalation tier, and resolution status. Feeds real-time dashboards for order operations managers and customer care agents.'
}

Table order.order_interaction {
