  priority_level varchar [note: 'The operational priority assigned to this SLA record, used to drive escalation routing and resource allocation in ServiceNow ITSM and Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. CRITICAL typically applies to regulatory MNP or enterprise SLAs.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this SLA commitment record, used in regulatory submissions, customer communications, and cross-system reconciliation (e.g., SLA-2024-00123456). Sourced from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority whose rules govern this SLA commitment: FCC (Federal Communications Commission, USA), OFCOM (Office of Communications, UK), BEREC (Body of European Regulators for Electronic Communications), ITU (International Telecommunication Union), ETSI, NTIA, GSMA, or NONE for purely commercial SLAs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FCC|OFCOM|BEREC|ITU|ETSI|NTIA|GSMA|NONE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_instrument_reference varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory rule, order, or directive citation that mandates this SLA (e.g., \'FCC 47 CFR Part 52 Section 52.35\', \'Ofcom General Conditions C5.3\', \'BEREC Guidelines on Switching 2020\'). Enables precise regulatory compliance traceability.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The type of telecommunications service to which this SLA applies: MOBILE, BROADBAND, FIBER_FTTH (Fiber to the Home), VOIP (Voice over Internet Protocol), IPTV (Internet Protocol Television), ENTERPRISE_MPLS (Multiprotocol Label Switching), SD_WAN (Software-Defined Wide Area Network), or FIXED_LINE. Determines applicable SLA regime and regulatory framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIBER_FTTH|VOIP|IPTV|ENTERPRISE_MPLS|SD_WAN|FIXED_LINE — promote to reference product]']
  sla_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the SLA defining the business process it governs: PROVISIONING (new service activation), REPAIR (fault resolution), PORTABILITY (Mobile Number Portability / MNP), INSTALLATION (physical CPE or FTTH installation), ACTIVATION (service turn-up), or DISCONNECTION (service termination). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PROVISIONING|REPAIR|PORTABILITY|INSTALLATION|ACTIVATION|DISCONNECTION — promote to reference product if additional categories are required]']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current real-time status of the SLA commitment: ON_TRACK (within target), AT_RISK (warning threshold exceeded), BREACHED (breach threshold exceeded), COMPLETED (fulfilled within SLA), WAIVED (SLA obligation formally waived), or SUSPENDED (clock paused due to customer or third-party hold).']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA commitment: REGULATORY (mandated by FCC, Ofcom, BEREC, or ITU), COMMERCIAL (contractually agreed with the customer), ENTERPRISE (premium business customer SLA), or INTERNAL (operational target not contractually binding).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this SLA record originated: ORACLE_OSM (Oracle Communications Order and Service Management), NETCRACKER (Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite), SERVICENOW (ServiceNow ITSM), AMDOCS (Amdocs Revenue Management), or MANUAL (manually entered).']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'The reason the SLA measurement clock was most recently suspended. Used for regulatory exclusion reporting and dispute resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CUSTOMER_HOLD|THIRD_PARTY_DEPENDENCY|REGULATORY_EXCLUSION|FORCE_MAJEURE|AWAITING_PAYMENT|SITE_ACCESS_DENIED — promote to reference product]']
  target_completion_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The contracted or regulatory maximum number of hours within which the order must be fulfilled from the measurement start event. This is the primary SLA commitment value (e.g., 24.00 hours for MNP port-in per FCC rules, 48.00 hours for residential broadband activation).']
  target_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The calculated deadline timestamp by which the order must be fulfilled to meet the SLA commitment. Derived from measurement_start_timestamp plus target_completion_hours, adjusted for any calendar or business-hours rules. Stored for direct comparison in monitoring queries.']
  total_suspended_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative number of hours the SLA clock has been suspended for this record due to customer holds, third-party delays, or regulatory exclusions. Subtracted from gross elapsed time to compute net elapsed hours for compliance reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this SLA record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED timestamp for change tracking, incremental processing, and audit trail purposes.']
  version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the SLA template or regulatory instrument applied to this record (e.g., \'v2.1\', \'2024-Q1\'). Enables tracking of SLA policy changes over time and ensures correct terms are applied for historical reporting.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the SLA obligation was formally waived (e.g., customer-requested delay, force majeure, regulatory exclusion). Populated only when sla_status is WAIVED. Required for audit and regulatory justification.']
  warning_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of elapsed hours from the measurement start event at which the SLA status transitions to AT_RISK, triggering proactive escalation workflows in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management and ServiceNow ITSM. Typically set as a percentage of target_completion_hours (e.g., 75%).']
  warning_triggered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the SLA status transitioned to AT_RISK (warning threshold exceeded). Used to measure the effectiveness of proactive escalation workflows and to audit response times.']

  Note: 'SLA commitment record attached to a fulfillment order or order type, defining the contracted or regulatory completion timeframe. Captures SLA type (regulatory, commercial, enterprise), target completion hours, warning threshold hours, breach threshold hours, measurement start event, measurement end event, current SLA status (ON_TRACK, AT_RISK, BREACHED), breach timestamp if applicable, and applicable regulatory body (FCC, Ofcom, BEREC). Enables real-time SLA monitoring and regulatory compliance reporting.'
}

Table order.notification {
  notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each outbound order notification record dispatched to a customer or internal stakeholder. Primary key of the order_notification data product.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who is the primary recipient of this notification. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Customer 360 and used to resolve recipient contact details.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order that triggered this notification. Links the notification to the end-to-end order lifecycle managed in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM).']
  privacy_consent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_consent. Business justification: Notifications (SMS, email, push) require consent verification under privacy regulations (GDPR Article 6, FCC CPNI rules). Real-world telco notification systems check consent records before dispatching']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the notification template used to render the message content. Links to the notification template catalog managed in the BSS product/service catalog (Netcracker OSS/BSS).']
  appointment_date date [note: 'The scheduled field technician appointment date included in appointment reminder notifications. Populated only when notification_type is APPOINTMENT_REMINDER. Sourced from Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management scheduling.']
  appointment_time_slot varchar [note: 'The scheduled appointment time window communicated to the customer in appointment reminder notifications (e.g., AM, PM, or specific time band). Sourced from Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management.']
  body_hash varchar [note: 'SHA-256 hash of the rendered notification body content at the time of dispatch. Provides tamper-evident audit evidence of the exact message content sent without storing the full message body (which may contain PII).']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason code for order cancellation included in ORDER_CANCELLATION notifications (e.g., CUSTOMER_REQUEST, CREDIT_FAIL, DUPLICATE_ORDER, FRAUD_DETECTED). Populated only when notification_type is ORDER_CANCELLATION.']
  consent_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer\'s communication consent record that authorises dispatch of this notification via the selected channel. Required for GDPR Article 6 lawful basis and TCPA compliance for SMS/IVR channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this notification record was first created in the order notification system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when delivery confirmation was received from the messaging gateway or carrier, confirming the notification reached the recipient endpoint. Null if delivery has not yet been confirmed or failed.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to dispatch this notification to the recipient (e.g., SMS, EMAIL, PUSH notification, IVR automated call, WhatsApp, or POSTAL mail). Determines routing logic and delivery SLA expectations.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current delivery outcome status of the notification as reported by the downstream messaging gateway or carrier. SENT = dispatched to gateway; DELIVERED = confirmed receipt by endpoint; FAILED = gateway rejection; BOUNCED = undeliverable address; PENDING = queued for dispatch.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the notification was submitted to the messaging gateway or delivery platform. Principal business event timestamp for SLA measurement and regulatory audit.']
  failed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when a delivery failure or bounce was reported by the messaging gateway. Populated only when delivery_status is FAILED or BOUNCED. Used to trigger retry logic and escalation workflows.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'The error or failure reason code returned by the messaging gateway when delivery_status is FAILED or BOUNCED (e.g., INVALID_NUMBER, MAILBOX_FULL, UNSUBSCRIBED, NETWORK_ERROR). Used for root cause analysis and retry strategy.']
  failure_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the delivery failure reason as returned by the messaging gateway. Complements failure_reason_code with additional context for operations and customer service teams.']
  fallback_channel varchar [note: 'The alternative delivery channel to be used if the primary delivery_channel fails after exhausting retry attempts (e.g., fall back to SMS if EMAIL bounces). Supports multi-channel notification resilience.']
  fallback_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fallback delivery channel was activated for this notification because the primary channel failed. True = fallback was used; False = primary channel was used.']
  gateway_message_reference varchar [note: 'The unique message identifier assigned by the external messaging gateway or carrier (e.g., SMS aggregator message ID, email SMTP message ID, push notification token). Used for delivery receipt correlation and dispute resolution with gateway providers.']
  gateway_provider varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the external messaging gateway or aggregator used to deliver this notification (e.g., Twilio, Syniverse, AWS SNS). Supports vendor performance monitoring and SLA management.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'The IETF BCP 47 language tag identifying the language in which the notification was rendered (e.g., en-US, fr-FR, es-MX). Supports multilingual customer communication obligations and regulatory requirements.']
  max_retry_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of delivery retry attempts permitted for this notification before it is marked as permanently failed and escalated. Configured per notification_type and delivery_channel in the notification policy.']
  mnp_port_reference varchar [note: 'The Mobile Number Portability (MNP) porting reference number included in port-in or port-out notifications. Populated only when order_type is MNP_PORT_IN or MNP_PORT_OUT. Required for regulatory audit under FCC MNP rules and GSMA PRD IR.73.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this notification record (e.g., NTF-0012345678). Used in customer-facing correspondence, audit trails, and regulatory evidence for MNP and disconnection notifications.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the business event that triggered this notification. Drives template selection, regulatory logging, and SLA measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ORDER_CONFIRMATION|APPOINTMENT_REMINDER|PROVISIONING_COMPLETE|PORT_IN_EXECUTION|ORDER_CANCELLATION|DISCONNECTION_NOTICE — promote to reference product]']
  opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient had opted out of this notification type or delivery channel at the time of dispatch. True = opt-out was recorded (notification may have been suppressed or sent as regulatory override). Used for GDPR/TCPA compliance audit.']
  port_execution_date date [note: 'The date on which the MNP port-in or port-out was executed, as communicated in PORT_IN_EXECUTION notifications. Required for regulatory audit under FCC MNP Order 18-122 and GSMA PRD IR.73.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The dispatch priority assigned to this notification, governing queue position and SLA targets in the messaging gateway. CRITICAL = regulatory/MNP notifications requiring immediate dispatch; HIGH = time-sensitive operational; NORMAL = standard; LOW = informational.']
  provisioning_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which service provisioning was completed, as communicated in PROVISIONING_COMPLETE notifications. Sourced from Oracle OSM service fulfillment completion event.']
  recipient_address varchar [note: 'The actual delivery endpoint used for this notification — phone number (E.164 format for SMS/IVR), email address, push device token, or postal address depending on delivery_channel. Stored as dispatched for audit and re-delivery purposes.']
  recipient_type varchar [note: 'Classifies whether the notification was dispatched to an external customer, an internal business stakeholder, a customer service agent, or a field technician. Governs data handling and privacy obligations.']
  regulatory_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this notification fulfils a mandatory regulatory obligation (e.g., MNP port-in confirmation, service disconnection advance notice). True = regulatory notification; False = operational/informational notification. Used for compliance reporting to FCC, Ofcom, BEREC.']
  regulatory_obligation_reference varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory rule, order, or article reference that mandates this notification (e.g., FCC MNP Order 18-122, Ofcom GC C7.9, BEREC Guidelines 2021). Populated only when regulatory_notification_flag is True.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'The number of delivery retry attempts made for this notification following an initial FAILED or BOUNCED status. Used to enforce maximum retry policies and trigger escalation to an alternative channel.']
  scheduled_dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which the notification was scheduled to be dispatched. Compared against dispatch_timestamp to measure scheduling accuracy and identify delayed notifications.']
  service_number varchar [note: 'The MSISDN or service telephone number (E.164 format) associated with the order being notified about. Included in the notification for customer identification and MNP regulatory audit purposes.']
  source_event_reference varchar [note: 'The unique event or transaction identifier from the originating source system (OSM, Salesforce, Amdocs) that triggered this notification. Enables end-to-end traceability from the source business event to the dispatched notification.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system that triggered this notification event (e.g., OSM = Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, SALESFORCE_CRM, AMDOCS, NETCRACKER, COMVERSE). Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation.']
  subject varchar [note: 'The subject line or title of the notification as rendered and dispatched (e.g., email subject line, SMS header, push notification title). Captured for audit, content review, and regulatory evidence.']
  template_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the notification template used at the time of dispatch (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Enables audit of which template content was sent, supporting regulatory compliance and A/B testing analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this notification record was last modified (e.g., delivery status updated from SENT to DELIVERED). Audit trail field for change tracking and data lineage.']

  Note: 'Outbound notification record dispatched to customers or internal stakeholders at key order lifecycle milestones — order confirmation, appointment reminder, provisioning completion, port-in execution, and order cancellation. Captures notification type, delivery channel (SMS, email, push, IVR), recipient reference, dispatch timestamp, delivery status (SENT, DELIVERED, FAILED, BOUNCED), template version, and language code. Supports regulatory notification obligations for MNP and service disconnection.'
}

Table order.amendment {
  amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order amendment record in the Telecommunication order management system. Primary key for the order_amendment data product.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who requested or is impacted by this order amendment. Supports customer-level audit trails and dispute resolution.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent or system identity that approved or rejected this amendment. Used for accountability, quality assurance, and agent performance reporting in Salesforce CRM.']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Order amendments for enterprise customers require contract linkage to validate change authorization per contract terms, assess pricing impacts, and trigger contract modification workflows for material']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order that this amendment modifies. Links the amendment to its originating order in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item affected by this amendment, when the modification is scoped to a single line rather than the entire order header.']
  parent_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to a prior amendment that this record supersedes or is derived from. Supports amendment chains where a rejected amendment is resubmitted with corrections.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field work order created or modified as a result of this amendment, when requires_field_visit is True. Links to the workforce management dispatch record in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management.']
  amended_field_name varchar [note: 'The logical name of the order attribute that was changed by this amendment (e.g., appointment_date, delivery_address_id, product_offering_code, service_configuration_value). Enables fine-grained change history and audit trail per field.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this amendment, used in customer communications, agent desktops, and dispute resolution workflows. Format: AMD- followed by 10 digits.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the amendment request: PENDING (submitted, awaiting review), APPROVED (authorised by agent or system), REJECTED (denied), APPLIED (successfully propagated to the fulfillment order), WITHDRAWN (retracted by the customer before decision).']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Categorises the nature of the post-submission modification: APPOINTMENT_RESCHEDULE (change of installation/delivery date), PRODUCT_CHANGE (swap of selected product or offering), ADDRESS_CHANGE (new delivery or service address), SERVICE_CONFIG_CHANGE (alteration to service parameters), CONTACT_UPDATE (change of contact details), CANCELLATION_REQUEST (request to cancel the order before completion). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: APPOINTMENT_RESCHEDULE|PRODUCT_CHANGE|ADDRESS_CHANGE|SERVICE_CONFIG_CHANGE|CONTACT_UPDATE|CANCELLATION_REQUEST — promote to reference product]']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the approved amendment was successfully propagated and applied to the underlying fulfillment order in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Null until status reaches APPLIED.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the amendment was approved by an authorised agent or automated workflow. Null if the amendment has not yet been approved or was rejected.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this amendment record to the immutable audit log entry in the compliance audit system (e.g., ServiceNow CMDB audit record or enterprise audit repository). Supports regulatory audit and dispute resolution evidence chains.']
  billing_adjustment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment triggers a billing adjustment in Amdocs Revenue Management (e.g., pro-rated credit, revised charge). When True, the billing team must process a corresponding adjustment record.']
  cost_delta decimal(18,4) [note: 'The incremental cost impact of this amendment on the order, expressed as a signed decimal. Positive values indicate an increase in order cost; negative values indicate a reduction. Used for revenue assurance and billing adjustment workflows in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  cost_delta_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost_delta amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Ensures multi-currency support for international operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this amendment record was first persisted in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Distinct from requested_timestamp which captures the business event time.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the customer was notified of the amendment outcome. Null if customer_notified_flag is False. Used for regulatory compliance reporting on customer communication timeliness.']
  customer_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been formally notified of the amendment outcome (approval, rejection, or application). Supports compliance with customer communication obligations and SLA notification requirements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date by which a PENDING amendment must be approved or it will automatically expire and be treated as WITHDRAWN. Enforces amendment processing SLAs and prevents stale pending requests from blocking order fulfillment.']
  impact_assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing the agent\'s or system\'s assessment of the downstream impact of this amendment on network provisioning, billing, scheduling, or third-party dependencies. Used during the approval workflow.']
  is_customer_initiated boolean [note: 'Distinguishes amendments initiated by the customer from those initiated internally by agents or automated systems (e.g., error corrections, product availability changes). Supports root-cause analysis of amendment volumes and customer experience reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent update to this amendment record in the Silver Layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change detection.']
  mnp_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment affects a Mobile Number Portability (MNP) port-in or port-out request associated with the parent order. When True, the MNP coordinator must be notified to update the porting window with the donor or recipient operator.']
  new_committed_delivery_date date [note: 'The revised committed delivery or installation date resulting from this amendment, applicable when the amendment type is APPOINTMENT_RESCHEDULE or when sla_reset_flag is True. Null if the amendment does not affect the delivery date.']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The replacement value for the amended field as requested by the customer or agent. Stored as a string to accommodate heterogeneous field types. Becomes the effective value once the amendment status reaches APPLIED.']
  original_committed_delivery_date date [note: 'The committed delivery or installation date that was in effect on the parent order before this amendment was applied. Preserved for SLA impact analysis and dispute resolution.']
  original_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the amended field as it existed on the order before this amendment was applied. Stored as a string to accommodate heterogeneous field types (dates, codes, identifiers, free text). Essential for dispute resolution and rollback.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this amendment for processing queue management: CRITICAL (immediate action required, e.g., regulatory or safety), HIGH (same-day processing), MEDIUM (standard SLA), LOW (best-effort). Drives agent workqueue ordering.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardised reason code explaining why the amendment was raised: CUSTOMER_REQUEST (voluntary change), AGENT_ERROR_CORRECTION (fix for data entry error), SYSTEM_ERROR (automated correction), PRODUCT_UNAVAILABLE (original product no longer available), ADDRESS_VALIDATION_FAIL (address rejected by validation), SCHEDULING_CONFLICT (appointment slot no longer available). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CUSTOMER_REQUEST|AGENT_ERROR_CORRECTION|SYSTEM_ERROR|PRODUCT_UNAVAILABLE|ADDRESS_VALIDATION_FAIL|SCHEDULING_CONFLICT — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the amendment reason beyond the structured reason code. Captured from agent notes or customer-provided comments during the amendment request.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment is subject to a regulatory hold (e.g., MNP porting window freeze, FCC-mandated processing pause, Ofcom dispute freeze). When True, the amendment cannot be processed until the hold is lifted.']
  rejected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the amendment was rejected. Null if the amendment was not rejected.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code indicating why the amendment was rejected: ORDER_ALREADY_FULFILLED, AMENDMENT_WINDOW_EXPIRED, PRODUCT_NOT_ELIGIBLE, CREDIT_CHECK_FAILED, DUPLICATE_REQUEST, POLICY_VIOLATION. Null when amendment is not in REJECTED status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ORDER_ALREADY_FULFILLED|AMENDMENT_WINDOW_EXPIRED|PRODUCT_NOT_ELIGIBLE|CREDIT_CHECK_FAILED|DUPLICATE_REQUEST|POLICY_VIOLATION — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the amendment was rejected, provided by the approving agent or automated decision system. Null when amendment is not in REJECTED status.']
  requested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time at which the customer or agent submitted the amendment request. This is the principal business event timestamp for the amendment lifecycle and is used for SLA measurement and audit.']
  requesting_channel varchar [note: 'The customer interaction channel through which the amendment request was submitted: IVR (Interactive Voice Response), AGENT_ASSISTED (call centre), SELF_SERVICE_WEB, SELF_SERVICE_APP, RETAIL_STORE, CHAT, EMAIL. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IVR|AGENT_ASSISTED|SELF_SERVICE_WEB|SELF_SERVICE_APP|RETAIL_STORE|CHAT|EMAIL — promote to reference product]']
  requires_field_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment necessitates a new or rescheduled field technician visit (e.g., address change for FTTH installation). Triggers workforce management dispatch in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management when True.']
  servicenow_change_request_reference varchar [note: 'The ServiceNow ITSM Change Request identifier raised to govern the technical configuration changes required to fulfil this amendment. Format: CHG followed by 7 digits. Null if no ITSM change request was required.']
  sla_reset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether applying this amendment resets the SLA clock on the parent order. True when the amendment materially changes the fulfillment scope (e.g., address change, product swap) and the committed delivery date must be recalculated. Critical for SLA compliance reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system that submitted this amendment: OMS (Oracle Communications Order and Service Management), CRM (Salesforce CRM), SELF_SERVICE (customer portal or app), IVR, RETAIL_POS, BATCH (automated batch correction). Used for lineage and root-cause analysis.']
  source_system_amendment_ref varchar [note: 'The native amendment or change-request identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Oracle OMS internal amendment ID, Salesforce case number). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  version varchar [note: 'Sequential version counter for amendments on the same order, starting at 1. Enables ordering of the complete change history and identification of the latest amendment state for a given order.']

  Note: 'Record of every post-submission modification to a fulfillment order — customer-requested changes to appointment date, product selection, delivery address, or service configuration before order completion. Captures amendment type, amendment request timestamp, original value, new value, amendment status (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, APPLIED), approving agent reference, and impact assessment (SLA reset flag, cost delta). Maintains a complete change history for dispute resolution and audit.'
}

Table order.order_charge {
  order_charge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order charge record. Primary key.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account that will be invoiced for this charge. Links the charge to the customer financial account for payment collection.']
  billing_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer dispute case if this charge is under investigation. Null if charge is not disputed. Links to dispute management system for resolution tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Charges allocated to cost centers for internal management reporting, especially for B2B/enterprise orders where costs must be tracked by sales channel, region, or product line for profitability analys']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location where the service is delivered. Used for installation charges, equipment delivery fees, and location-based taxation. Null for non-location-specific charges.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or agent identifier for the sales representative who originated the order. Used for commission calculation and sales performance tracking. Null for self-service channels.']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Order charges for enterprise customers must link to contracts for pricing validation, discount application per contract terms, revenue recognition rules, and contract-specific billing arrangements.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order that generated this charge. Links the charge to the originating service order.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that includes this charge. Null if charge has not yet been invoiced. Enables reconciliation between order charges and billing invoices.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Order charges generate journal entries for GL posting. Direct traceability from charge to journal entry is required for revenue assurance audits, SOX compliance, and financial reconciliation processes']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item that triggered this charge. Enables traceability to the product or service being charged.']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_package. Business justification: Billing line items for IPTV/OTT subscriptions reference the content package tier for pricing, revenue recognition, and invoice detail. Real process: monthly recurring charge calculation and billing st']
  price_alteration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_alteration. Business justification: Discounts and promotional adjustments applied to charges must trace to catalog price alterations for audit trail, revenue assurance, and campaign ROI analysis. Required for finance reconciliation.']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: Charges originate from catalog price plans; linkage required for revenue assurance audits, charge validation, and GL account mapping. Essential for financial controls and regulatory reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue attribution to profit centers is standard practice for segment P&L reporting in telcos with multiple business units (mobile, fixed, enterprise). Required for management accounting and performa']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.promo_offer. Business justification: Promotional charges must link to catalog promo_offer for campaign ROI tracking, budget consumption monitoring, redemption limit enforcement, and finance reconciliation. Required for marketing analytic']
  revenue_leakage_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.revenue_leakage_case. Business justification: Order charges disputed or unbilled triggering revenue leakage investigations. Links order-to-cash discrepancies to revenue assurance cases for recovery actions.']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product catalog entry associated with this charge. Identifies the specific service or equipment being charged.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the charge before taxes, discounts, or adjustments. Represents the base price for the service or product being charged.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the charge was approved for billing. Null if charge is still pending approval or was waived/cancelled before approval.']
  billed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the charge was successfully posted to a customer invoice. Null if charge has not yet been billed or was waived/cancelled.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the order was placed. RETAIL_STORE for physical locations, ONLINE_WEB for website orders, MOBILE_APP for mobile application, CALL_CENTER for phone orders, DEALER for authorized resellers, PARTNER for co-branded channels, DIRECT_SALES for field sales teams, TELESALES for outbound sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RETAIL_STORE|ONLINE_WEB|MOBILE_APP|CALL_CENTER|DEALER|PARTNER|DIRECT_SALES|TELESALES — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'Detailed classification of the charge purpose. ACTIVATION for new service setup, INSTALLATION for field technician deployment, CPE_PURCHASE for customer premises equipment sale, CPE_RENTAL for equipment lease, MNP_FEE for mobile number portability administrative charge, EARLY_TERMINATION for contract breach penalty, SERVICE_CHANGE for mid-contract modifications, UPGRADE/DOWNGRADE for plan changes, DISCONNECTION for service termination, ADMINISTRATIVE for processing fees. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACTIVATION|INSTALLATION|CPE_PURCHASE|CPE_RENTAL|MNP_FEE|EARLY_TERMINATION|SERVICE_CHANGE|UPGRADE|DOWNGRADE|DISCONNECTION|ADMINISTRATIVE — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the charge. PENDING awaiting approval or billing system processing, APPROVED validated and ready for invoicing, BILLED successfully included in customer invoice, WAIVED forgiven per policy or customer service decision, REVERSED cancelled post-billing with credit issued, DISPUTED under customer challenge investigation, CANCELLED voided before billing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PENDING|APPROVED|BILLED|WAIVED|REVERSED|DISPUTED|CANCELLED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the charge based on billing frequency and nature. ONE_TIME for activation/installation fees, RECURRING for subscription charges, USAGE for consumption-based charges, PENALTY for early termination or late payment fees, ADJUSTMENT for manual corrections, CREDIT for refunds or promotional credits.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the service contract commitment in months. Used to calculate early termination fees and contract expiration dates. Null for month-to-month services.']
  cpe_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the customer premises equipment associated with this charge. Applicable for CPE purchase, rental, or installation charges. Null for non-equipment charges.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this charge record was first created in the order management system. Used for audit trail and charge lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this charge record. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the charge. Includes promotional discounts, loyalty credits, bundle savings, and negotiated contract reductions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the charge becomes applicable for billing purposes. Determines which billing cycle will include this charge.']
  external_charge_reference varchar [note: 'Charge identifier from the source operational system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking. Format varies by source system.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting code for revenue recognition. Maps the charge to the appropriate general ledger account for financial reporting and compliance.']
  is_prorated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the charge amount was prorated based on partial billing period. True for mid-cycle activations or changes, False for full-period charges.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that performed the most recent update to this charge record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this charge record. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data synchronization.']
  net_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final billable amount after applying discounts and adding taxes. Calculated as charge_amount minus discount_amount plus tax_amount. This is the amount that will appear on the customer invoice.']
  product_offering_code varchar [note: 'Business code identifying the commercial product offering. Used for product catalog lookup, pricing rule application, and revenue reporting.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Marketing campaign or promotional offer code that influenced the charge amount. Used to track discount attribution and campaign effectiveness. Null if no promotion was applied.']
  proration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days used to calculate prorated charge amount. Null if charge is not prorated.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the charge. Used for customer communication, dispute resolution, and cross-system reconciliation.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when revenue from this charge should be recognized in financial statements. May differ from charge_effective_date based on accounting policies and revenue recognition standards.']
  reversal_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context for the charge reversal. Includes investigation findings, customer communication notes, or corrective action details. Null if charge was not reversed.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why a previously billed charge was reversed. BILLING_ERROR for incorrect invoicing, DUPLICATE_CHARGE for double-billing, SERVICE_NOT_DELIVERED for unfulfilled orders, CUSTOMER_DISPUTE for contested charges, SYSTEM_ERROR for technical failures, FRAUD for unauthorized charges, REGULATORY_ADJUSTMENT for compliance corrections. Null if charge was not reversed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BILLING_ERROR|DUPLICATE_CHARGE|SERVICE_NOT_DELIVERED|CUSTOMER_DISPUTE|SYSTEM_ERROR|FRAUD|REGULATORY_ADJUSTMENT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the charge reversal was processed. Null if charge was not reversed. Used for financial reconciliation and audit trail.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that originated this charge record. OSM for Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, NETCRACKER for Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite, AMDOCS for Amdocs Revenue Management, COMVERSE for Comverse ONE, SALESFORCE for Salesforce CRM. Used for data lineage and system integration troubleshooting.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax liability for this charge. Includes sales tax, value-added tax (VAT), goods and services tax (GST), telecommunications-specific regulatory fees, and universal service fund contributions.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Tax treatment classification for this charge. STANDARD for normal sales tax rate, REDUCED for discounted rate items, EXEMPT for non-taxable services, ZERO_RATED for taxable but 0% rate, TELECOM_SPECIFIC for industry regulatory fees, USF for Universal Service Fund contributions.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'Business event that caused this charge to be generated. ORDER_COMPLETION for order fulfillment charges, SERVICE_ACTIVATION for go-live fees, INSTALLATION_COMPLETE for field work charges, CONTRACT_SIGNATURE for commitment fees, MNP_PORT_IN for number portability administrative charges, SERVICE_DISCONNECTION for termination fees, CONTRACT_TERMINATION for early exit penalties, PLAN_CHANGE for modification fees, EQUIPMENT_DELIVERY for CPE charges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ORDER_COMPLETION|SERVICE_ACTIVATION|INSTALLATION_COMPLETE|CONTRACT_SIGNATURE|MNP_PORT_IN|SERVICE_DISCONNECTION|CONTRACT_TERMINATION|PLAN_CHANGE|EQUIPMENT_DELIVERY — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the employee who authorized the charge waiver. Used for audit trail and policy compliance verification. Null if charge was not waived.']
  waiver_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context for the charge waiver. Includes customer service notes, manager approval justification, or policy reference. Null if charge was not waived.']
  waiver_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why a charge was waived. CUSTOMER_RETENTION for churn prevention, SERVICE_FAILURE for quality issues, BILLING_ERROR for system mistakes, PROMOTIONAL for marketing campaigns, GOODWILL for customer satisfaction, EXECUTIVE_ESCALATION for senior management decisions, REGULATORY_COMPLIANCE for legal requirements, CONTRACT_NEGOTIATION for commercial agreements. Null if charge was not waived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CUSTOMER_RETENTION|SERVICE_FAILURE|BILLING_ERROR|PROMOTIONAL|GOODWILL|EXECUTIVE_ESCALATION|REGULATORY_COMPLIANCE|CONTRACT_NEGOTIATION — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'One-time or recurring charge record generated at order completion and passed to the billing domain for invoicing — activation fees, installation charges, CPE purchase or rental fees, early termination fees, and MNP administrative charges. Captures charge type, charge amount, currency, tax category, charge trigger event, billing account reference, charge status (PENDING, BILLED, WAIVED, REVERSED), and waiver reason if applicable. Represents the commercial financial output of the order fulfillment process.'
}

Table order.bulk_order {
  bulk_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bulk order submission. Primary key for the bulk order entity.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Enterprise bulk orders (corporate contracts, wholesale agreements) must be assigned to legal entity for contract accounting, revenue recognition, and statutory reporting. Essential for multi-entity te']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Bulk orders are predominantly enterprise use cases (new site rollouts, mass upgrades, fleet provisioning). Corporate account linkage essential for contract validation, consolidated billing, and bulk o']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enterprise customer, MVNO partner, wholesale carrier, or government entity that submitted the bulk order request.']
  dealer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.dealer. Business justification: Dealer networks submit bulk activation orders for multiple customers (e.g., corporate sales, promotional campaigns) - requires dealer attribution for bulk activation quota tracking and tiered commissi']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enterprise account manager or partner relationship manager responsible for this bulk order customer relationship.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Corporate/wholesale bulk orders often target specific catalog offerings for contract compliance validation, volume pricing enforcement, and enterprise sales tracking. Required for B2B order management']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Bulk orders often submitted by enterprise partners or wholesale customers require partner attribution for contract compliance tracking, volume commitment verification, and partner-specific pricing/set']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the orchestration workflow instance managing the bulk order decomposition and fulfillment coordination.']
  reconciliation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.reconciliation_run. Business justification: Bulk order billing reconciliation detecting revenue leakage from provisioning vs. billing mismatches. Standard revenue assurance process for enterprise/wholesale orders.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the bulk order processing was completed (all individual orders reached terminal state).']
  bulk_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the bulk order submission, used for customer communication and tracking. Format: BLK followed by 10 digits.']
  bulk_order_type varchar [note: 'Category of bulk order operation being requested across all individual orders within the batch. Determines decomposition strategy and fulfillment orchestration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_activation|upgrade|downgrade|disconnection|mnp_port_in|change_of_service|site_rollout|batch_provisioning — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for bulk order cancellation (e.g., customer request, contract termination, validation failure).']
  cancellation_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context for the bulk order cancellation.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bulk order was cancelled by the customer or system, halting further processing of pending individual orders.']
  committed_completion_date date [note: 'Service provider committed delivery date for bulk order completion, based on capacity planning and SLA agreements.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the master service agreement, enterprise contract, or wholesale agreement governing the terms of this bulk order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bulk order record was first created in the order management system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values associated with this bulk order.']
  decomposition_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all individual fulfillment orders were successfully created and queued from the bulk order batch.']
  decomposition_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bulk order began decomposition into individual fulfillment orders for orchestration.']
  decomposition_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used to decompose the bulk order into individual fulfillment orders (sequential processing, parallel processing, or hybrid approach).']
  error_summary_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed error report listing all validation failures and processing errors for failed order lines.']
  failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual order lines that have failed validation, decomposition, or fulfillment within this bulk order batch. Requires remediation or manual intervention.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bulk order record was last updated, reflecting the most recent status or attribute change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing special instructions, deployment notes, or additional context provided by the submitter for this bulk order.']
  pending_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual order lines still in progress or awaiting fulfillment within this bulk order batch.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the bulk order, determining SLA targets, resource allocation, and escalation thresholds.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bulk order as it progresses through intake, validation, decomposition, and fulfillment orchestration stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|validating|validated|decomposing|in_progress|completed|partially_completed|failed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requested_completion_date date [note: 'Customer-requested target date by which all individual orders within the bulk batch should be fulfilled and activated.']
  sales_channel_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sales channel through which the bulk order was originated (e.g., enterprise direct, partner portal, wholesale desk).']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the bulk order has breached its committed SLA target completion time.']
  sla_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SLA breach was detected for this bulk order, triggering escalation and notification workflows.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target number of hours for bulk order completion as defined by the applicable SLA tier and contract terms.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA tier applicable to this bulk order based on customer contract and submitting entity type. Determines target completion timeframes.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the upstream system or portal from which the bulk order originated (e.g., partner portal, enterprise self-service, wholesale API).']
  submission_file_format varchar [note: 'Data format of the bulk order submission file. Determines parsing logic and validation schema applied during intake processing.']
  submission_file_name varchar [note: 'Original file name of the uploaded bulk order file, if submission method was file-based. Used for audit trail and troubleshooting.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Technical channel through which the bulk order was submitted to the OSS/BSS platform. Impacts validation rules and processing workflows.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bulk order was initially submitted to the order management system. Marks the start of SLA measurement.']
  submitter_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the individual who submitted the bulk order, used for status notifications and error report delivery.']
  submitter_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who submitted the bulk order on behalf of the enterprise or partner organization.']
  submitter_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the individual who submitted the bulk order, used for urgent escalations and clarifications.']
  submitting_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organization type submitting the bulk order request. Determines routing, approval workflows, and SLA tiers.']
  success_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual order lines that have been successfully fulfilled and completed within this bulk order batch.']
  total_line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual order lines or subscriber records included in the bulk order submission. Used for progress tracking and capacity planning.']
  total_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all individual orders within the bulk order batch, representing total revenue opportunity.']
  validation_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when intake validation and business rule checks were completed for the bulk order submission.']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual order lines that failed business rule validation during intake processing. Typically requires correction and resubmission.']
  workflow_template_name varchar [note: 'Name of the workflow template used for orchestrating this bulk order type (e.g., Enterprise_Site_Rollout, MVNO_Batch_Activation).']

  Note: 'Master record for enterprise and wholesale bulk order submissions where a single commercial request encompasses multiple individual fulfillment orders — B2B enterprise site rollouts, MVNO subscriber batch activations, government contract deployments, and SD-WAN/MPLS multi-site provisioning. Captures bulk order reference, submitting entity type (enterprise, MVNO, wholesale partner), total line count, submission method (file upload, API batch), processing status, success count, failure count, and account manager reference.'
}

Table order.inventory_reservation {
  inventory_reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory reservation record. Primary key.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer for whom this resource is reserved. Enables customer-level reservation tracking and dispute resolution.']
  device_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.device_model. Business justification: Device inventory reservations must specify the exact catalog model being reserved for warehouse picking, stock allocation, and supply chain planning. Essential for device logistics operations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or service account that created the reservation. Supports audit and troubleshooting.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment order that triggered this reservation. Links the reservation to the consuming order workflow.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item that requires this reserved resource. Enables line-level resource tracking.']
  location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or logical warehouse location holding the reserved resource. Relevant for CPE, ONT, and physical SIM inventory.']
  reconciliation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.reconciliation_run. Business justification: Inventory reservation vs. consumption reconciliation for resource leakage detection (SIM cards, CPE). Standard inventory assurance control in telco operations.']
  resource_pool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory pool from which this resource was reserved. Enables pool-level capacity management and replenishment.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target service instance that will consume this resource upon fulfillment. Null until service is provisioned.']
  allocation_rule_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the business rule that governed this reservation. Enables audit of allocation logic and policy compliance.']
  consumed_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of reserved units that have been consumed by fulfillment. Tracks partial consumption for bulk reservations.']
  consumed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reserved resource was consumed by fulfillment and allocated to the service. Null if not yet consumed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reservation record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation will automatically expire if not consumed. Prevents indefinite resource locking.']
  is_auto_released boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reservation was automatically released by system expiry logic (true) or manually released by an operator (false).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reservation record was last updated. Tracks all state transitions and attribute changes.']
  release_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for reservation release. Examples: EXPIRY, ORDER_CANCELLED, MANUAL_OVERRIDE, INVENTORY_REBALANCE, FULFILLMENT_FAILURE.']
  release_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the reservation was released. Supports operational analysis and process improvement.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation was manually released back to inventory. Null if not released.']
  reservation_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Configured hold period in minutes before automatic expiry. Derived from order type and resource type policies.']
  reservation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes about the reservation. Captures special handling instructions, constraints, or exceptions.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the reservation. Used for operational tracking and customer service reference.']
  reservation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for this reservation. Influences allocation sequence when inventory is constrained. Inherited from order priority.']
  reservation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for the reservation. Aligns with order action type and supports reservation analytics.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reservation. ACTIVE=held and available, CONSUMED=used by fulfillment, EXPIRED=time limit exceeded, RELEASED=manually freed, CANCELLED=order cancelled.']
  reservation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resource was reserved from inventory. Marks the start of the reservation hold period.']
  reserved_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that initiated the reservation. Typically Oracle Communications Order and Service Management or equivalent OSS.']
  reserved_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of resource units reserved. Typically 1 for discrete items like SIM cards, may be greater for bulk resources like IP address blocks.']
  resource_identifier varchar [note: 'The actual identifier of the reserved resource instance. For MSISDN this is the phone number, for SIM the ICCID, for IP the address block, for ONT the serial number.']
  resource_specification_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the resource catalog specification defining the characteristics and constraints of the reserved resource type.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Category of resource being reserved. Determines inventory pool and allocation rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SIM|ESIM|MSISDN|ICCID|IMSI|IP_ADDRESS|ONT|CPE|FIBER_PORT|GPON_PORT|VLAN|MAC_ADDRESS|EQUIPMENT_SERIAL — promote to reference product]']
  service_region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for which this resource is reserved. Ensures regional inventory alignment for MSISDN ranges and local resources.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Network technology for which the resource is reserved. Ensures technology-compatible resource allocation.']

  Note: 'Inventory reservation record created at order submission to hold physical or logical resources required for fulfillment — SIM/eSIM stock, ONT/CPE hardware, IP address blocks, MSISDN ranges, and fiber port capacity. Captures resource type, resource identifier, reserved quantity, reservation timestamp, expiry timestamp, reservation status (ACTIVE, CONSUMED, EXPIRED, RELEASED), and consuming order reference. Prevents double-allocation of scarce resources across concurrent orders.'
}

Table order.validation_result {
  validation_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order validation result record. Primary key.']
  compatibility_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.compatibility_rule. Business justification: Compatibility validation outcomes must reference the catalog rule evaluated for troubleshooting order fallouts, rule tuning, and product configuration quality improvement. Critical for order success r']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise order validation (credit checks, service eligibility) requires corporate account context for account-level credit limits, contract-based eligibility rules, and enterprise-specific validatio']
  eligibility_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.eligibility_rule. Business justification: Validation results must trace to the specific eligibility rule evaluated for audit trail, rule effectiveness analysis, override justification, and regulatory compliance reporting. Essential for order ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or agent identifier who authorized the validation override. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting. Nullable if no override was applied.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order that was validated. Links this validation result to the parent order being processed.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item that was validated. Nullable if validation applies to the entire order rather than a specific line.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Validation rules often enforce regulatory requirements (credit checks under consumer protection law, MNP cooling period compliance, E911 capability verification). Telcos must link validation rules to ']
  address_validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of address validation check. VALID indicates address is verified, CORRECTED indicates address was standardized, INVALID indicates address cannot be verified. Applicable only for ADDRESS validation category.']
  calea_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the order complies with Communications Assistance for Law Enforcement Act (CALEA) requirements for lawful intercept capability. Applicable for regulated telecommunications services.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this validation result record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_limit_approved decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit limit approved for the customer based on credit check. Applicable only for CREDIT validation category. Nullable for non-credit validations.']
  credit_score varchar [note: 'Numeric credit score returned by credit check validation. Applicable only for CREDIT validation category. Nullable for non-credit validations.']
  deposit_required_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Security deposit amount required based on credit check outcome. Applicable only for CREDIT validation category. Nullable if no deposit required or for non-credit validations.']
  inventory_availability_status varchar [note: 'Availability status of required inventory items (Customer Premises Equipment (CPE), Subscriber Identity Module (SIM) cards, etc.) for order fulfillment. Applicable for orders requiring physical inventory.']
  is_overridden boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a failed validation was manually overridden by an authorized agent. True if override was applied, False otherwise.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this validation failure can be manually overridden by an authorized agent. True if override is permitted, False if validation failure is absolute.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this validation result record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  mnp_cooling_period_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Mobile Number Portability (MNP) request complies with regulatory cooling-off period requirements. Applicable only for MNP port-in orders. Nullable for non-MNP orders.']
  mnp_cooling_period_end_date date [note: 'Date when the Mobile Number Portability (MNP) regulatory cooling-off period expires and porting can proceed. Applicable only for MNP port-in orders. Nullable for non-MNP orders.']
  msisdn_availability_status varchar [note: 'Availability status of the requested Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN). Applicable for new activation and Mobile Number Portability (MNP) orders. Nullable for non-mobile service orders.']
  network_capacity_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sufficient network capacity exists to fulfill the service request at the specified location. Applicable for bandwidth-intensive services (Fiber to the Home (FTTH), 5G New Radio (5G NR), etc.).']
  override_agent_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee or agent who authorized the validation override. Used for audit reporting. Nullable if no override was applied.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for validation override (e.g., CUST_REQUEST, EXEC_APPROVAL, SYSTEM_ERROR, BUSINESS_EXCEPTION). Nullable if no override was applied.']
  override_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description explaining why the validation failure was overridden. Required for compliance and audit purposes when override is applied.']
  override_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the validation failure was overridden. Nullable if no override was applied.']
  product_compatibility_status varchar [note: 'Result of product compatibility validation checking whether requested products can coexist or if existing services require modification. Applicable for change-of-service and upgrade orders.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this validation check was retried due to transient errors or timeouts. Used for reliability monitoring and troubleshooting.']
  service_eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of service eligibility check indicating whether requested service can be provisioned at the specified location. Applicable only for ELIGIBILITY validation category.']
  validation_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the validation check performed. Groups validation rules into functional domains for reporting and orchestration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CREDIT|ELIGIBILITY|ADDRESS|INVENTORY|REGULATORY|COMPATIBILITY|TECHNICAL|BUSINESS_RULE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  validation_duration_ms varchar [note: 'Time taken to execute the validation check in milliseconds. Used for performance monitoring and optimization of validation processes.']
  validation_error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code for failed validations. Used for automated error handling, routing, and reporting. Nullable if validation passed.']
  validation_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this validation result expires and must be re-executed. Some validations (credit checks, inventory reservations) have time-limited validity.']
  validation_message varchar [note: 'Detailed message describing the validation result. For failures, contains specific reason and remediation guidance. For warnings, contains advisory information.']
  validation_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the validation check execution. PASS indicates validation succeeded, FAIL indicates validation failed and order cannot proceed, WARNING indicates potential issue but order may proceed, SKIPPED indicates validation was not executed, ERROR indicates validation could not be completed due to technical issue.']
  validation_response_payload varchar [note: 'Raw response payload from the validation system. Stored for audit and detailed troubleshooting. May contain JSON or XML formatted data.']
  validation_rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the validation rule that was executed (e.g., CREDIT_CHECK, ADDRESS_VAL, MSISDN_AVAIL, MNP_COOLING, CALEA_COMP, PROD_COMPAT, SVC_ELIGIBILITY). Used to categorize and track validation types.']
  validation_rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the validation rule executed (e.g., Credit Check, Service Eligibility Verification, Mobile Number Portability (MNP) Cooling-Off Period Check).']
  validation_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order in which this validation was executed within the overall validation workflow. Used to track validation orchestration and dependencies.']
  validation_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the validation result. CRITICAL failures block order progression, HIGH failures require manual review, MEDIUM and LOW may allow conditional progression.']
  validation_system_source varchar [note: 'Name of the system or service that performed the validation check (e.g., Amdocs Credit Engine, Salesforce Address Validator, Netcracker Inventory, Internal MNP Gateway). Used for troubleshooting and system dependency tracking.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the validation check was executed. Critical for audit trail and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']

  Note: 'Record of all validation checks executed against a submitted order before orchestration begins — credit check outcome, service eligibility confirmation, address validation, MSISDN availability, regulatory compliance check (MNP cooling-off period, CALEA compliance), and product compatibility validation. Captures validation rule code, validation outcome (PASS, FAIL, WARNING), failure message, validation timestamp, and override flag with authorising agent reference for manual overrides.'
}

Table order.decomposition {
  decomposition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order decomposition mapping record. Primary key.']
  child_order_fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lower-level order generated during decomposition. May point to service order, resource order, or network order depending on child_order_type.']
  decomposition_fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order. Provides context for the order line being decomposed.']
  decomposition_prerequisite_order_fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to another child order that must complete before this child order can begin execution. Null if no prerequisite exists.']
  parent_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the high-level fulfillment order line that was decomposed. Links to the commercial order line from which child orders were generated.']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Native order identifier assigned by the target fulfillment system. Used for cross-system correlation and troubleshooting.']
  rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the orchestration rule or template applied by Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) to generate the child order. Enables traceability to the decomposition logic.']
  child_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for the child order. Used for operational tracking and cross-system correlation.']
  child_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the child order generated during decomposition. Indicates the layer of the fulfillment stack this order targets (service, resource, or network).']
  child_system_target varchar [note: 'Target system or domain responsible for fulfilling the child order. Examples: Netcracker Service Inventory, Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, CPE Provisioning System.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the child order was marked as completed in the target system and synchronized back to the orchestration layer. Null if not yet completed.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Source system that created this decomposition mapping record. Typically Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM).']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decomposition mapping record was created and the child order was generated by the orchestration engine.']
  decomposition_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical depth of this decomposition step. Level 1 indicates first-level decomposition from commercial order line; higher levels indicate nested decomposition.']
  decomposition_status varchar [note: 'Current state of this decomposition mapping. Tracks whether the child order has been successfully created, dispatched, and synchronized with the target system.']
  dependency_type varchar [note: 'Execution dependency relationship between this child order and other child orders. Sequential requires completion before next; parallel allows concurrent execution; conditional depends on outcome; blocking prevents downstream execution until resolved.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the child order was dispatched to the target system for fulfillment. Null if not yet dispatched.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Detailed human-readable description of the failure reason. Provides context for troubleshooting and root cause analysis.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for child order failure. Examples: RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE, VALIDATION_ERROR, SYSTEM_TIMEOUT, NETWORK_UNREACHABLE.']
  failure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the child order failed in the target system. Null if no failure occurred.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this decomposition mapping record. Tracks changes to status, synchronization state, or retry attempts.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent synchronization event between the orchestration layer and the target system for this child order.']
  max_retry_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of retry attempts allowed for this child order before escalation or manual intervention is required.']
  next_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the next retry attempt. Null if no retry is scheduled or max retries exceeded.']
  orchestration_context varchar [note: 'Additional context or metadata passed from the orchestration engine to the target system. May include workflow variables, customer preferences, or technical parameters.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Execution priority of this child order. Inherited from parent order or adjusted based on decomposition rules and SLA requirements.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the orchestration engine has attempted to dispatch or synchronize this child order after initial failure. Used for retry policy enforcement.']
  rollback_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this child order requires rollback due to parent order cancellation or downstream failure. True triggers compensating transaction.']
  rollback_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the rollback process for this child order. Tracks whether compensating actions have been successfully executed.']
  rollback_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rollback action was initiated for this child order. Null if no rollback occurred.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordering of this child order within the set of child orders generated from the same parent order line. Used to enforce execution dependencies.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this child order has breached its SLA target. True triggers escalation and reporting workflows.']
  sla_target_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target timestamp by which this child order must be completed to meet the parent order SLA. Used for proactive breach detection.']
  synchronization_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the child order state in the target system is synchronized with the orchestration layer. Critical for maintaining end-to-end order visibility.']
  target_system_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the child order as reported by the target system. May differ from decomposition_status during synchronization lag.']

  Note: 'Decomposition mapping record that links a high-level fulfillment order line to the set of lower-level service orders and resource orders generated by OSM during orchestration. Captures parent order line reference, child order type (service_order, resource_order, network_order), child system target, decomposition rule applied, creation timestamp, and synchronisation status. Provides full traceability from commercial order to network provisioning action.'
}

Table order.decomposition_rule {
  decomposition_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for decomposition_rule']
  parent_decomposition_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on decomposition_rule (parent_decomposition_rule_id)']
  action_expression varchar [note: 'Expression defining the transformation or action performed when the condition is met.']
  applicable_order_category varchar [note: 'Order category to which the rule applies.']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Logical expression that must be true for the rule to fire.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires; null indicates open‑ended.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the rule.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer priority determining rule evaluation order; lower numbers evaluate first.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule logic and purpose.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name that identifies the rule.']
  rule_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or team responsible for the rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of rule behavior (e.g., split, merge, transform).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version counter incremented on each change to the rule definition.']
