  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the facility location.']
  storage_capacity_cubic_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total volumetric storage capacity available for inventory assets measured in cubic meters.']
  supports_hazmat_storage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified and equipped to store hazardous materials such as batteries and chemicals.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the facility location used for scheduling and operational coordination.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical locations where inventory assets are housed, stored, or staged — including Points of Presence (POP), central offices (CO), data centers, headends, street cabinets, cell tower compounds, central warehouses, regional depots, field technician vans, and retail stores. Facility attributes: name, type, address, GPS coordinates, floor space, power/cooling capacity, security tier, lease/ownership status, equipment rack count. Stocking attributes: storage capacity, responsible manager, operating hours, climate control specifications, min/max stock levels per part category, cycle count schedule. SSOT for all facility and stocking location inventory supporting equipment hosting, spare parts positioning, field dispatch logistics, and inventory management.'
}

Table inventory.fiber_strand_assignment {
  fiber_strand_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fiber strand assignment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  a_end_rack_slot_port_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.rack_slot_port. Business justification: Fiber strand assignments terminate at specific physical ports in racks. Currently a_end_port_number is a STRING; replacing with FK to rack_slot_port provides full port hierarchy (facility → equipment ']
  backup_strand_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the backup fiber strand assignment providing redundancy for this primary strand. Null for unprotected strands or backup strands themselves.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Unique circuit identifier for the service or wholesale transport circuit riding on this fiber strand. Used for operational tracking and trouble ticketing.']
  fiber_cable_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fiber cable segment containing this strand.']
  lawful_intercept_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.lawful_intercept_order. Business justification: Lawful intercept orders for wireline/fiber services require provisioning intercept taps on specific fiber strands carrying target traffic. LEA compliance requires documenting which fiber strands were ']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the wholesale dark fiber lease agreement or Indefeasible Right of Use (IRU) contract governing this strand assignment. Null for owned infrastructure.']
  primary_fiber_lessor_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner organization providing the leased fiber strand. Null for owned infrastructure.']
  primary_fiber_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating termination point facility or Point of Presence (POP) for this fiber strand.']
  primary_fiber_network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network equipment (Optical Line Terminal, router, switch, or DWDM multiplexer) at the A-end termination point.']
  spliced_from_fiber_strand_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fiber_strand_assignment (spliced_from_fiber_strand_assignment_id)']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service instance utilizing this fiber strand. Null for dark/spare/unassigned strands.']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the technician who performed the most recent installation, testing, or maintenance work on this fiber strand.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when this fiber strand was lit and placed into active service. Null for dark/spare strands.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when this fiber strand was assigned to a service, circuit, or lease arrangement. Null for dark/spare strands.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the fiber strand. Dark indicates unlit/unassigned fiber available for lease or future use; lit indicates active service; spare indicates reserved capacity; maintenance indicates temporarily out of service; reserved indicates allocated but not yet activated; quarantined indicates isolated due to quality issues.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fiber strand assignment record was first created in the inventory system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (lease cost and revenue).']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when this fiber strand was deactivated and returned to dark/spare status. Null for currently active strands.']
  fiber_mode varchar [note: 'ISO/IEC 11801 fiber mode classification. OS1/OS2 for single-mode; OM1-OM5 for multi-mode with varying bandwidth capabilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OS1|OS2|OM1|OM2|OM3|OM4|OM5 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fiber_type varchar [note: 'Physical type of optical fiber. Single-mode for long-haul transmission; multi-mode for short-distance high-bandwidth; bend-insensitive for tight installation spaces.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this fiber strand assignment record, used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent optical performance test (OTDR or power meter test) conducted on this fiber strand.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the lease agreement. Null for IRU agreements (typically 20+ years) or owned infrastructure.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the lease or IRU agreement for this fiber strand. Null for owned infrastructure used internally.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Ownership or lease arrangement for this fiber strand. Owned indicates company-owned infrastructure; leased indicates rental from third party; IRU (Indefeasible Right of Use) indicates long-term lease with ownership-like rights; swap indicates fiber exchange agreement; joint_build indicates shared construction arrangement.']
  measured_loss_db decimal(6,3) [note: 'Actual measured optical loss in decibels from the most recent Optical Time Domain Reflectometer (OTDR) test. Used to verify strand quality and detect degradation.']
  monthly_lease_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monthly recurring cost for leasing this fiber strand from a third party. Null for owned infrastructure. Used for cost allocation and profitability analysis.']
  monthly_lease_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monthly recurring revenue generated from leasing this fiber strand to wholesale customers. Null for internal use. Used for revenue assurance and wholesale billing.']
  next_test_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory optical performance test based on maintenance policy or regulatory requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, known issues, or historical context about this fiber strand assignment.']
  optical_loss_budget_db decimal(6,3) [note: 'Total acceptable optical power loss in decibels (dB) for this fiber strand from A-end to Z-end, including fiber attenuation, splice losses, and connector losses. Critical for service quality assurance.']
  protection_scheme varchar [note: 'Redundancy and protection mechanism for this fiber strand. Unprotected indicates no backup; 1+1 indicates dedicated backup strand; 1:1 indicates shared backup with automatic switchover; 1:N indicates one backup for multiple working strands; ring indicates self-healing ring topology.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that is the authoritative source for this fiber strand assignment record (Oracle OSM, Netcracker, or network management system).']
  reflectance_db decimal(6,3) [note: 'Optical return loss or reflectance measurement in decibels, indicating the amount of light reflected back toward the source. Lower values indicate better quality connections.']
  service_impact_level varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification indicating the impact of an outage on this fiber strand. Critical for backbone/core transport; high for enterprise services; medium for business services; low for residential; none for dark/spare strands.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement tier governing restoration time objectives and performance guarantees for this fiber strand. Higher tiers require faster Mean Time to Repair (MTTR).']
  strand_color_code varchar [note: 'Industry-standard color coding of the fiber strand for physical identification during splicing and maintenance operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blue|orange|green|brown|slate|white|red|black|yellow|violet|rose|aqua — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  strand_number varchar [note: 'Physical strand number within the fiber cable, typically numbered sequentially from 1 to the total fiber count in the cable.']
  test_wavelength_nm varchar [note: 'Wavelength in nanometers used during the most recent OTDR test, typically 1310nm or 1550nm for single-mode fiber.']
  wavelength_nm varchar [note: 'Specific wavelength in nanometers assigned to this strand for Dense Wavelength Division Multiplexing (DWDM) systems. Null for non-DWDM dark fiber.']
  z_end_port_number varchar [note: 'Physical port identifier on the Z-end equipment where this fiber strand terminates.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual fiber strand or lambda allocation within a fiber cable segment. Tracks strand number, wavelength (if DWDM), assignment status (dark/lit/spare/maintenance), assigned service or circuit reference, A-end and Z-end termination points, optical loss budget, and lease/IRU reference for wholesale dark fiber services. Essential for fiber capacity management and dark fiber leasing operations.'
}

Table inventory.rack_slot_port {
  rack_slot_port_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rack slot port record. Primary key for the physical mounting hierarchy within Point of Presence (POP) or Central Office (CO) facilities.']
  connected_cable_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the physical cable connected to this port. Used for cable management and tracing physical connectivity.']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent network equipment (chassis, switch, router, or line card) that contains this rack slot port.']
  parent_rack_slot_port_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rack_slot_port (parent_rack_slot_port_id)']
  pop_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the POP or Central Office facility where this rack slot port is physically located.']
  remote_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network equipment at the far end of the cable connection. Enables end-to-end circuit tracing.']
  administrative_status varchar [note: 'Administrative configuration state of the port as set by network operations. Disabled or shutdown ports are intentionally taken out of service.']
  bandwidth_allocation_mbps varchar [note: 'Allocated bandwidth for the service or circuit on this port. May be less than the physical port speed due to Quality of Service (QoS) policies.']
  circuit_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the logical circuit or service provisioned on this port. Links physical infrastructure to service layer.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the port was commissioned and made available for operational use after installation and testing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rack slot port record was first created in the inventory system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the port was taken out of service and decommissioned. Null for active ports.']
  fiber_type varchar [note: 'Type of fiber optic cable connected to this port. Singlemode for long-distance, multimode (OM1-OM5) for shorter distances with different bandwidth capabilities.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the rack slot port equipment was physically installed and mounted in the facility.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance activity performed on this port or its parent equipment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rack slot port record was most recently updated in the inventory system.']
  max_cable_distance_meters varchar [note: 'Maximum supported cable length in meters for the port based on its speed, transceiver type, and fiber/copper specifications.']
  monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this port is actively monitored by the Network Management System (NMS) for performance metrics, alarms, and fault detection.']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next planned maintenance activity based on maintenance intervals and equipment lifecycle policies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special configurations, known issues, or other relevant information about this rack slot port.']
  occupancy_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the port is currently connected to a cable or device (occupied), available for use (vacant), reserved for planned deployment, or quarantined due to issues.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the rack slot port indicating whether it is in service, out of service, under maintenance, or reserved for future use.']
  poe_power_budget_watts decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum power in watts that can be delivered through this port via PoE. Common values are 15.4W (802.3af), 30W (802.3at), or 60-90W (802.3bt).']
  port_duplex_mode varchar [note: 'Duplex configuration of the port indicating whether it can transmit and receive simultaneously (full duplex) or must alternate (half duplex).']
  port_function varchar [note: 'Functional role of the port in the network architecture (e.g., access port for end devices, trunk port for inter-switch links, uplink to core network). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: access|trunk|uplink|downlink|management|monitoring|backup|interconnect — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  port_identifier varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique identifier for the port, typically following vendor naming conventions (e.g., Gi0/0/1, Ethernet1/1/1, Port-1).']
  port_label varchar [note: 'Physical label or tag affixed to the port for visual identification during field operations and cable management activities.']
  port_number varchar [note: 'Sequential port number on the line card or equipment module. Used in combination with slot number to uniquely identify the physical port.']
  port_speed_mbps varchar [note: 'Maximum transmission speed of the port in megabits per second. Common values include 100, 1000, 10000, 25000, 40000, 100000.']
  port_type varchar [note: 'Physical interface type of the port, indicating the connector and transmission medium (electrical copper, optical fiber, or pluggable transceiver type). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: electrical|optical|sfp|sfp_plus|qsfp|qsfp28|qsfp_dd|cfp|xfp|rj45|coaxial|fiber_lc|fiber_sc|fiber_mpo — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  power_over_ethernet_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port supports Power over Ethernet (PoE) to supply electrical power to connected devices over the data cable.']
  protection_scheme varchar [note: 'Type of redundancy or protection mechanism configured for this port (e.g., 1+1 protection, 1:1 protection, 1:N protection, ring protection).']
  quality_of_service_class varchar [note: 'QoS classification applied to traffic on this port for prioritization and Service Level Agreement (SLA) enforcement (e.g., gold, silver, bronze, best-effort).']
  rack_position varchar [note: 'Physical rack location identifier within the facility, typically expressed as row-column-rack format (e.g., A-03-12, R1-C5-RK8).']
  rack_unit_height varchar [note: 'Height of the equipment in rack units. Standard equipment heights are 1U, 2U, 4U, etc.']
  rack_unit_start varchar [note: 'Starting rack unit position from the bottom of the rack where the equipment is mounted. Rack units are standardized 1.75-inch (44.45mm) increments.']
  redundancy_group varchar [note: 'Identifier for the redundancy or protection group this port belongs to. Used for high-availability configurations and failover planning.']
  remote_port_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the port on the remote equipment that this port connects to. Used for documenting point-to-point connections.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of telecommunications service carried by this port (data, voice, video, network management, signaling, or mixed services). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data|voice|video|management|signaling|synchronization|mixed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  slot_number varchar [note: 'Slot number within the chassis or equipment frame where the line card or module is installed. Null if the equipment does not have modular slots.']
  snmp_index varchar [note: 'SNMP ifIndex value for this port as reported by the network element. Used for correlation with SNMP monitoring data.']
  transceiver_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the installed optical transceiver module. Used for inventory tracking and warranty management.']
  transceiver_type varchar [note: 'Type and model of the pluggable optical transceiver installed in the port (e.g., SFP-10G-SR, QSFP28-100G-LR4). Null for fixed electrical ports.']
  vlan_number varchar [note: 'VLAN identifier assigned to this port for network segmentation. Valid range is 1-4094 per IEEE 802.1Q standard.']
  wavelength_nm varchar [note: 'Operating wavelength of the optical transceiver in nanometers. Common values are 850nm (multimode), 1310nm, and 1550nm (singlemode).']

  Note: 'Master record for the physical mounting hierarchy within POP/CO facilities — racks, slots within chassis, and ports on line cards. Tracks rack position (row/column/RU), slot number, port type (electrical/optical/SFP), port speed, occupied/vacant status, connected cable reference, and parent equipment reference. Enables physical capacity planning and cable management within facilities.'
}

Table inventory.fiber_lease {
  fiber_lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each fiber lease agreement record. Primary key for the association.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the carrier partner leasing the fiber infrastructure. References the carrier registry in the interconnect domain.']
  fiber_cable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fiber cable segment being leased. References the physical infrastructure asset in the inventory domain.']
  circuit_capacity_gbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Allocated transmission capacity in gigabits per second for this lease agreement. Represents the bandwidth or fiber count allocated to the carrier. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'External contract or agreement reference number linking this lease to the legal commercial agreement between the parties.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this lease agreement record was created in the system.']
  fiber_count_allocated varchar [note: 'Number of individual fiber strands allocated to the carrier under this lease agreement. Relevant for dark fiber and IRU agreements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this lease agreement record was last updated.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Date when the fiber lease agreement expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null indicates perpetual IRU agreement. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Date when the fiber lease agreement becomes effective and the carrier gains access to the leased fiber capacity. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lease agreement. Active indicates live service, pending indicates contract signed but not yet provisioned.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease arrangement: IRU (Indefeasible Right of Use - long-term capital lease), dark fiber (unlit fiber lease), lit service (managed wavelength service), or wavelength (specific lambda lease).']
  maintenance_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for physical maintenance, repairs, and monitoring of the leased fiber segment. Critical for operational coordination and cost allocation. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  monthly_lease_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Recurring monthly cost for the fiber lease in the settlement currency. Used for financial settlement and revenue recognition. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for lease payments. May differ from the carrier\'s default settlement currency for roaming.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA classification for this lease agreement defining restoration time objectives, availability guarantees, and support response times. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the commercial lease contract between fiber_cable infrastructure assets and interconnect_carrier partners. It captures IRU (Indefeasible Right of Use) agreements, dark fiber leases, and capacity allocation arrangements where telecommunications operators lease fiber infrastructure to/from carriers. Each record links one fiber_cable segment to one interconnect_carrier with lease terms, pricing, capacity allocation, SLA commitments, and maintenance responsibility assignments that exist only in the context of this commercial relationship.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications wholesale operations, fiber infrastructure lease agreements represent true many-to-many commercial relationships. A single fiber cable route can have segments leased to multiple carriers simultaneously (e.g., Carrier A leases fibers 1-12, Carrier B leases fibers 13-24 on the same cable), and each carrier leases multiple cable segments across different geographic routes to build their network footprint. These are actively managed commercial contracts with lease terms, pricing, capacity allocation, SLA commitments, and maintenance responsibilities that belong to the relationship, not to either the cable asset or the carrier entity alone.'
}

Table inventory.service_equipment_assignment {
  service_equipment_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this service equipment assignment record. Primary key.']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the managed service instance being delivered']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the network equipment asset assigned to deliver this managed service']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when this equipment was activated and began delivering traffic for this managed service instance']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this equipment assignment: ACTIVE (currently in service), INACTIVE (temporarily disabled), PENDING (provisioned but not yet activated), DECOMMISSIONED (permanently removed)']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service equipment assignment record was created in the system']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when this equipment was deactivated and stopped delivering traffic for this managed service. Null for currently active assignments.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this assignment record']
  primary_backup_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator: TRUE if this equipment is designated as primary for the service, FALSE if backup or secondary']
  redundancy_role varchar [note: 'Redundancy configuration role for high availability: ACTIVE (currently carrying traffic), PASSIVE (standby ready for failover), ACTIVE_ACTIVE (both carrying traffic simultaneously), NONE (no redundancy)']
  role_in_service varchar [note: 'Functional role this equipment plays in delivering the managed service: PRIMARY (main active equipment), BACKUP (hot standby for failover), LOAD_BALANCED (active-active traffic sharing), STANDBY (cold spare), FAILOVER (automatic switchover target)']
  traffic_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of service traffic routed through this equipment in load-balanced configurations. Range 0.00-100.00. Sum across all active equipment for a service should equal 100.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment relationship between network equipment and managed enterprise services. It captures which equipment instances (routers, switches, SD-WAN appliances) are allocated to deliver specific managed service instances, including redundancy roles, traffic distribution, and lifecycle dates. Each record links one network equipment asset to one managed service with attributes that exist only in the context of this service delivery assignment.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications service delivery, managed enterprise services (SD-WAN, MPLS VPN, UCaaS) routinely require multiple network equipment instances for redundancy and load balancing—primary and backup routers, active-active PE router pairs, or SD-WAN appliance clusters. Conversely, shared network infrastructure such as MPLS PE routers and metro Ethernet switches simultaneously serve multiple enterprise service instances. The business actively manages these assignments, tracking which equipment delivers which service, in what role (primary/backup), with what traffic split, and for what time period.'
}

Table inventory.colocation {
  colocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this colocation agreement record. Primary key.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the partner organization whose equipment is housed in the facility']
  pop_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical facility where partner equipment is colocated']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Physical access rights granted to the partner for this facility. Determines whether partner technicians can enter unescorted, require escort, or must use remote hands service.']
  circuit_count varchar [note: 'Number of network circuits or cross-connects provisioned for the partner at this facility. Used for capacity planning and billing.']
  colocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the colocation arrangement. Active indicates equipment is installed and operational.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled expiration date for the colocation agreement at this facility. Null for evergreen agreements or month-to-month arrangements.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when the partner began colocating equipment at this facility. Marks the beginning of billing and SLA obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this colocation agreement record was created.']
  monthly_colocation_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Recurring monthly fee charged to the partner for colocation services at this facility. Typically based on rack space, power allocation, and service tier.']
  power_allocation_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Electrical power capacity allocated to the partner for their equipment in this facility, measured in kilowatts. Part of the colocation SLA and billing calculation.']
  rack_space_allocated_units decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of rack units (RU) allocated to the partner within this facility. Typically measured in standard 19-inch rack units where 1U = 1.75 inches.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this colocation agreement record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the colocation contract between pop_facility and partner. It captures the physical hosting arrangement where partner equipment is housed within a telecommunications facility. Each record links one pop_facility to one partner with attributes that exist only in the context of this colocation relationship: rack space allocation, power allocation, access rights, and financial terms.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications operations, POP facilities routinely host equipment from multiple partners (tower companies, content providers, MVNO partners, interconnect carriers) through colocation agreements, and each partner typically maintains equipment presence across multiple facilities for geographic redundancy and network reach. Colocation is a recognized operational business process managed by facility operations teams who allocate rack space, power budgets, and access rights on a per-partner-per-facility basis, with distinct financial terms and SLAs for each arrangement.'
}

  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty for the splice closure expires.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for splice_closure. Referenced by splice_closure_id.'
}

  redundancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the circuit is part of a redundant design.']
  redundancy_type varchar [note: 'Type of redundancy implemented for the circuit.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service level agreement tier associated with the circuit.']
  sla_availability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target availability percentage defined in the SLA.']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed response time for service incidents, in milliseconds.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the circuit.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the circuit contract ends.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the circuit record.']
  vlan_number varchar [note: 'Virtual LAN identifier associated with the circuit.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for circuit. Referenced by circuit_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for maintenance_contract. Referenced by maintenance_contract_id.'
}

Table interconnect.carrier_agreement {
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for carrier_agreement']
  administrative_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.administrative_region. Business justification: Roaming agreements are governed by jurisdiction-specific regulations and dispute resolution mechanisms. Has governing_law_country_code but regulatory compliance, tax treatment, and legal enforcement r']
  commercial_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee responsible for managing and renewing this carrier agreement. Typically the Wholesale or Interconnect Account Manager. Used for escalation and renewal workflow routing.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Roaming agreements are signed by specific legal entities. Financial consolidation, regulatory filings, and intercompany settlement reporting require tracking which company code owns each agreement. Cr']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Wholesale and MVNO agreements are commercial contracts with enterprise customers. Business need: tracking which enterprise account holds the roaming/interconnect agreement for billing, SLA management,']
  counterparty_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the domestic or international operator that is the counterparty to this agreement. Links to the carrier/partner master record in the interconnect domain.']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rate table applicable to this agreement. The IOT table defines per-unit wholesale rates for voice (per minute), SMS (per message), and data (per MB/GB) exchanged between operators.']
  network_qos_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.qos_profile. Business justification: Roaming and interconnect agreements specify QoS requirements (latency, jitter, packet loss) that must be enforced by network QoS profiles. Required for SLA compliance verification and service assuranc']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Roaming agreements with MVNO/reseller partners (not traditional carriers) require partner_id link. Business process: MVNO roaming agreement lifecycle management, partner contract tracking, consolidate']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Roaming/interconnect agreements must comply with specific regulatory obligations (FCC Part 64, international settlement policies, GDPR). Tracks which obligation each agreement satisfies for audit trai']
  sla_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Service Level Agreement (SLA) record governing quality of service commitments under this carrier agreement, including network availability, latency, and fault resolution targets.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive commercial name of the carrier agreement (e.g., \'Roaming IOT Agreement – Carrier X 2024\'). Used for display in BSS portals and settlement reports.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the bilateral or multilateral carrier agreement. Used in correspondence, TAP file headers, and inter-operator settlement documents. Maps to the contract reference in Amdocs Revenue Management and Netcracker OSS/BSS.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the carrier agreement. DRAFT = under negotiation; ACTIVE = commercially live and binding; SUSPENDED = temporarily halted by mutual or unilateral action; TERMINATED = permanently ended; EXPIRED = reached end date without renewal; PENDING_APPROVAL = awaiting regulatory or internal sign-off.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier agreement by commercial relationship type. IOT = Inter-Operator Tariff roaming; CAMEL = Customised Applications for Mobile networks Enhanced Logic; MVNO_HOSTING = Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) hosting arrangement; NATIONAL_ROAMING = domestic network sharing; WHOLESALE_BROADBAND = wholesale broadband resale; CAPACITY_RESALE = bandwidth/capacity resale; TRANSIT = transit traffic routing; PEERING = direct traffic exchange. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IOT|CAMEL|MVNO_HOSTING|NATIONAL_ROAMING|WHOLESALE_BROADBAND|CAPACITY_RESALE|TRANSIT|PEERING — promote to reference product]']
  agreement_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the agreement document (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\'). Incremented when amendments are executed. Enables tracking of agreement evolution and ensures the correct rate tables are applied for each version period.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal amendments executed against this agreement since its original signing. Provides a quick indicator of agreement complexity and negotiation history.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews upon reaching its effective_until_date without requiring a new signed instrument. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action.']
  covered_services varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of telecommunication services covered under this agreement (e.g., VOICE, SMS, DATA, VOLTE, VONR, MMS, CAMEL_GPRS). Governs which service types are eligible for IOT settlement and TAP file exchange. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VOICE|SMS|DATA|VOLTE|VONR|MMS|CAMEL_GPRS|USSD — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier agreement record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  data_protection_addendum_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a data protection addendum (DPA) is attached to this agreement, governing the processing of subscriber personal data exchanged between operators in compliance with GDPR or equivalent regulations.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to standard IOT rates when discount_type is PERCENTAGE or VOLUME_TIERED. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Null when discount_type is FLAT_RATE or NONE.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Type of discount structure applied to IOT rates under this agreement. FLAT_RATE = fixed per-unit reduction; VOLUME_TIERED = discount scales with traffic volume; PERCENTAGE = percentage reduction off standard IOT; NEGOTIATED_RATE = bespoke bilaterally agreed rate; NONE = standard IOT applies.']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving financial or operational disputes arising under this agreement. GSMA_MEDIATION refers to the GSMA Billing and Accounting Dispute Resolution process.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the carrier agreement becomes commercially binding and operationally active. Governs the start of IOT tariff applicability, TAP file exchange obligations, and NRTRDE reporting requirements.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the carrier agreement ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended agreements. When populated, triggers renewal workflow and settlement true-up processing in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  fraud_prevention_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement includes specific fraud prevention obligations such as NRTRDE, SIM box detection cooperation, or GSMA Fraud Information Gathering System (FIGS) participation.']
  governing_law_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern this agreement (e.g., GBR for English law, USA for US law). Relevant for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.']
  interconnect_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'External reference number or document identifier for the physical signed agreement document, as stored in the document management system or SAP S/4HANA contract repository. Enables traceability to the legal instrument.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was last formally reviewed by the commercial team for rate competitiveness, volume performance, and compliance. Supports the agreement lifecycle management process.']
  min_volume_commitment_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum traffic volume committed by one or both parties under this agreement, expressed in the unit defined by min_volume_unit (e.g., minutes, MB, messages). Triggers true-up billing if actual volume falls below this threshold.']
  min_volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum volume commitment (min_volume_commitment_units). MINUTES = voice traffic; MEGABYTES/GIGABYTES = data traffic; MESSAGES = SMS/MMS; ERLANGS = traffic intensity for capacity agreements.']
  network_generation varchar [note: 'Radio access network generation(s) covered by this agreement. 2G = GSM/GPRS/EDGE; 3G = UMTS/HSPA; 4G_LTE = Long-Term Evolution (LTE); 5G_NR = 5G New Radio (NR); ALL = all generations. Determines applicable IOT tariff tables and NRTRDE reporting scope.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this agreement. Triggers workflow notifications to the commercial owner and finance team in advance of the review.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate the agreement. Governs the termination workflow in contract lifecycle management.']
  nrtrde_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement requires Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) reporting per GSMA PRD FS.11. NRTRDE enables fraud detection by providing near-real-time CDR visibility to the home operator.']
  nrtrde_transmission_window_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which NRTRDE files must be transmitted to the home operator after the roaming CDR is generated. Standard GSMA requirement is 4 hours; some bilateral agreements specify tighter windows.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each automatic renewal cycle when auto_renewal_flag is True. Null when auto-renewal is not applicable.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross revenue shared with the counterparty operator under MVNO hosting, content, or wholesale broadband agreements. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 20.00 = 20%). Null for pure IOT/TAP agreements.']
  roaming_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of roaming traffic covered by this agreement. INBOUND = foreign subscribers roaming on Telecommunication\'s network; OUTBOUND = Telecommunication\'s subscribers roaming on the counterparty\'s network; BILATERAL = both directions.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial settlement under this agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Governs currency conversion in Amdocs Revenue Management inter-operator billing.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which financial settlement is performed between the parties under this agreement. Governs the billing cycle in Amdocs Revenue Management inter-operator invoicing.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which all parties executed the agreement. May precede the effective_from_date during a transition or notice period. Used for legal and audit trail purposes.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this agreement record was sourced. AMDOCS = Amdocs Revenue Management; NETCRACKER = Netcracker OSS/BSS; SALESFORCE = Salesforce CRM; SAP = SAP S/4HANA; MANUAL = manually entered.']
  tap_file_exchange_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement mandates Transferred Account Procedure (TAP) file exchange for roaming CDR settlement. True = TAP files must be exchanged per GSMA PRD TD.57 specifications.']
  tap_version varchar [note: 'Version of the GSMA Transferred Account Procedure (TAP) standard used for CDR file exchange under this agreement. Governs file format and field mapping in the TAP processing pipeline.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date on which the agreement was terminated, if applicable. Distinct from effective_until_date which is the planned end; this captures early or unilateral termination events.']
  true_up_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which minimum volume commitment true-up calculations are performed. If actual traffic falls below min_volume_commitment_units within the period, a shortfall charge is raised in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier agreement record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for change tracking and incremental ETL processing.']

  Note: 'Master record of bilateral or multilateral commercial agreements between Telecommunication and domestic/international operators covering roaming, wholesale, and MVNO relationships. Captures agreement type (IOT, CAMEL, LTE, 5G NR, MVNO hosting, national roaming, wholesale broadband, capacity resale), counterparty carrier reference, effective dates, covered services (voice, SMS, data, VoLTE, VoNR), discount structures, TAP file exchange obligations, NRTRDE requirements, minimum volume commitments and true-up terms, revenue share terms, SLA references, and agreement status. SSOT for all inter-operator commercial relationship governance.'
}

Table interconnect.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each carrier record in the interconnect carrier master registry. Primary key for all interconnect domain relationships.']
  administrative_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.administrative_region. Business justification: Carriers operate under specific regulatory jurisdictions. Regulatory compliance reporting, spectrum licensing, and dispute resolution require tracking the administrative region beyond country_code. Es']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Regional carriers and MVNOs are both interconnect partners AND enterprise customers buying wholesale services. Business need: unified customer view for cross-selling, consolidated billing, and relatio']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Many interconnect carriers are also business partners (MVNOs, wholesale customers, resellers). Business process: Unified partner relationship management, consolidated billing across interconnect and p']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or licence identifier assigned to the carrier by its national telecommunications regulator (e.g., FCC Registration Number, Ofcom licence number, NTIA identifier). Used for regulatory reporting and compliance verification.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or trading name of the carrier, transit operator, MVNO, or wholesale partner as registered with the relevant regulatory authority. Used as the primary human-readable identifier across all interconnect domain products.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier relationship. Active indicates live traffic exchange; suspended indicates temporary halt pending dispute or non-payment; terminated indicates the relationship has ended; pending indicates onboarding in progress; inactive indicates dormant with no current traffic.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier by network ownership and service role: MNO (Mobile Network Operator), MVNO (Mobile Virtual Network Operator), transit (transit/wholesale carrier), IXC (Interexchange Carrier), LEC (Local Exchange Carrier), ISP (Internet Service Provider). Drives applicable tariff tables, settlement rules, and regulatory reporting.']
  clearinghouse_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Carrier identifier assigned by the clearinghouse (e.g., Syniverse or BICS carrier code) used for TAP file routing, NRTRDE submission, and financial settlement matching within the clearinghouse platform.']
  clearinghouse_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial and data clearinghouse through which TAP file exchange and inter-operator financial settlement is processed for this carrier (e.g., HSCSD, Syniverse, BICS). Required for TAP file routing and settlement reconciliation.']
  commercial_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the carrier\'s commercial or wholesale account management team. Used for agreement negotiations, invoice disputes, and settlement correspondence.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the carrier\'s primary country of registration and network operation. Used for geographic segmentation, IOT tariff lookup, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier record was first created in the interconnect carrier master registry. Audit trail field conforming to ISO 8601 extended format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding settlement balance (in settlement currency) permitted for this carrier before traffic suspension or escalation is triggered. Used for wholesale credit risk management and revenue assurance controls.']
  data_roaming_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GPRS/LTE/5G NR data roaming is active with this carrier. When true, data CDRs from this carrier\'s subscribers are processed through the TAP/NRTRDE pipeline and rated against the applicable IOT data tariff.']
  fcc_registration_number varchar [note: 'Ten-digit FCC Registration Number (FRN) assigned to US-based carriers by the Federal Communications Commission. Required for FCC regulatory filings, Form 499 reporting, and USAC fund contributions tracking.']
  five_g_roaming_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 5G NR (New Radio) roaming is active with this carrier. Requires 5G roaming agreement, SEPP (Security Edge Protection Proxy) interconnect, and N32 interface configuration per 3GPP TS 33.501.']
  fraud_risk_level varchar [note: 'Fraud risk classification for this carrier based on historical NRTRDE anomalies, grey route incidents, and GSMA FASG (Fraud and Security Group) advisories. Drives real-time fraud monitoring thresholds and NRTRDE file frequency.']
  interconnect_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Type of interconnect arrangement with this carrier: direct (direct circuit interconnect), transit (traffic routed via a third-party transit carrier), peering (IP or voice peering arrangement), wholesale (wholesale traffic purchase/sale), none (no active interconnect).']
  iot_tariff_group varchar [note: 'IOT tariff group classification assigned to this carrier, used to look up applicable Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rates for roaming settlement. Aligns with GSMA IOT rate tables and bilateral agreement terms. Drives rating of inbound and outbound roaming CDRs.']
  ir21_last_updated_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier\'s GSMA IR.21 roaming information profile was last updated in the GSMA RAEX (Roaming Agreement Exchange) system. Stale IR.21 data can cause roaming activation failures.']
  ir21_profile_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the carrier\'s GSMA IR.21 roaming information document on file. IR.21 contains network configuration data required for roaming service activation including IMSI ranges, supported services, and signalling parameters.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this carrier record in the interconnect carrier master registry. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  last_tap_file_received_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently received TAP (Transferred Account Procedure) file from this carrier. Used to monitor TAP file exchange health and detect stale or missing files that may indicate a processing issue or carrier-side problem.']
  last_tap_file_sent_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently sent TAP file to this carrier. Used to monitor outbound TAP file exchange health and ensure timely delivery within GSMA-mandated submission windows.']
  lte_roaming_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether LTE (4G) data roaming is active with this carrier. Distinct from VoLTE; covers data-only LTE roaming sessions rated and settled via TAP3 or DIAMETER-based online charging.']
  mcc varchar [note: 'Three-digit ITU-assigned Mobile Country Code (MCC) identifying the country of the carrier\'s home network. Combined with MNC to form the full PLMN identifier used in roaming and signalling.']
  mnc varchar [note: 'Two- or three-digit ITU-assigned Mobile Network Code (MNC) identifying the carrier\'s specific network within its country. Combined with MCC to form the PLMN identifier used in HLR/HSS, roaming agreements, and CDR processing.']
  noc_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the carrier\'s Network Operations Center (NOC) used for fault escalation, outage notifications, and interconnect incident management. Operational contact for network-layer issues.']
  noc_phone varchar [note: '24/7 phone number for the carrier\'s Network Operations Center (NOC) in E.164 international format. Used for real-time fault escalation, SLA breach notification, and interconnect outage coordination.']
  nrtrde_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier participates in NRTRDE (Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange) for near-real-time roaming fraud detection and usage monitoring. When true, NRTRDE files must be exchanged within the GSMA-mandated 4-hour window.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier was formally onboarded into Telecommunication\'s interconnect systems including OSS/BSS provisioning, TAP file pipeline configuration, and clearinghouse registration. Distinct from relationship_start_date which may precede system activation.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment must be received from or made to this carrier. Standard values are 30, 45, or 60 days. Used by Amdocs Revenue Management for collections and dunning scheduling.']
  preferred_interconnect_route varchar [note: 'Preferred routing method for interconnect traffic to/from this carrier: direct (direct circuit), via_transit (through a transit carrier), via_iplc (International Private Leased Circuit), via_mpls (MPLS backbone), via_sdwan (SD-WAN overlay). Used by NOC for traffic engineering.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region grouping for the carrier (e.g., APAC, EMEA, AMER, LATAM). Used for regional traffic analysis, IOT tariff banding, and wholesale reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: APAC|EMEA|AMER|LATAM|MEA|CEUR — promote to reference product]']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the commercial and operational relationship with this carrier was or is scheduled to be terminated. Null for active ongoing relationships. Used for agreement expiry tracking and traffic wind-down planning.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the commercial and operational relationship with this carrier was formally established. Marks the beginning of the carrier\'s lifecycle in the interconnect registry and the effective date of the first agreement.']
  roaming_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Type of roaming agreement in place with this carrier: bilateral (direct two-way roaming), multilateral (via GSMA or regional body), MVNO_host (carrier hosts an MVNO), transit_only (transit traffic only, no retail roaming), none (no roaming, interconnect only).']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial settlement with this carrier (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the currency in which TAP invoices, IOT charges, and interconnect invoices are denominated and paid.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of financial settlement with this carrier for interconnect and roaming charges. Determines the billing and reconciliation schedule in Amdocs Revenue Management and the clearinghouse.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used trading name of the carrier, used in operational reports, dashboards, and TAP file processing where space is constrained.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA tier assigned to this carrier relationship, determining priority of fault resolution, MTTR targets, and escalation paths. Platinum and gold tiers receive 24/7 NOC support with sub-4-hour MTTR commitments.']
  sms_interconnect_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SMS (Short Message Service) interconnect is active with this carrier. Drives SMS CDR routing, A2P/P2P SMS settlement, and grey route detection.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this carrier record was sourced: NETCRACKER (Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite), AMDOCS (Amdocs Revenue Management), ORACLE_OSM (Oracle Communications OSM), MANUAL (manually entered). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  spid varchar [note: 'Four-digit Service Provider Identifier (SPID) assigned by the North American Numbering Plan Administrator (NANPA) or local number portability administrator. Used for MNP (Mobile Number Portability) routing and LNP database queries.']
  tadig_code varchar [note: 'Five-character GSMA-assigned TADIG code uniquely identifying the carrier for TAP file exchange and inter-operator roaming settlement. Mandatory for all MNO and MVNO roaming partners. Used as the primary key in TAP file headers and NRTRDE records.']
  tap_version varchar [note: 'TAP (Transferred Account Procedure) file format version supported by this carrier for roaming CDR exchange. Determines the file structure and encoding used when generating or receiving TAP files. Critical for TAP file processing pipeline configuration.']
  voice_interconnect_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether voice traffic (PSTN/VoIP/VoLTE) interconnect is active with this carrier. Drives CDR routing, rating, and settlement processing for voice traffic.']
  volte_roaming_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether VoLTE (Voice over LTE) roaming via IMS (IP Multimedia Subsystem) is active with this carrier. Requires bilateral IMS interconnect configuration and specific IR.92/IR.94 profile exchange.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all domestic and international carriers, transit operators, MVNOs, and wholesale partners with whom Telecommunication has interconnect, roaming, or settlement relationships. Captures carrier code (TADIG, MCCMNC, carrier ID), carrier name, country, carrier type (MNO, MVNO, transit, IXC, LEC, ISP), regulatory identifiers, NOC contact details, clearinghouse affiliations, and operational status. Central reference entity for all interconnect domain relationships — every agreement, invoice, TAP file, and traffic record references this registry.'
}

Table interconnect.iot_tariff {
  iot_tariff_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for the IOT tariff record in the interconnect domain. Serves as the SSOT identifier for wholesale rate lookup during TAP file rating and settlement invoice generation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business authority (person, team, or system) that approved this IOT tariff record for production use. Required for internal governance and audit compliance in the interconnect settlement process.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this IOT tariff record was formally approved for production use. Part of the tariff lifecycle audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  charging_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'The billing increment in seconds used for voice service IOT charge calculation. For example, a value of 60 means charges are calculated per full minute; a value of 1 means per-second billing. Applicable only for voice service types.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this IOT tariff record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the IOT rate is denominated (e.g., EUR, USD, SDR). GSMA IOT rates are typically denominated in EUR or IMF Special Drawing Rights (SDR). Used for settlement invoice currency conversion and financial reconciliation.']
  destination_zone_code varchar [note: 'The GSMA-defined or operator-defined destination zone code to which this IOT rate applies. Zones group countries or network destinations for wholesale tariff banding. Used in TAP file rating to match usage records to the correct IOT rate.']
  destination_zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the destination zone (e.g., \'Zone 1 - EU\', \'Zone 2 - Rest of World\'). Used in settlement reports, finance dashboards, and operator billing correspondence.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Negotiated discount percentage applied to the standard IOT rate under a bilateral roaming agreement. Expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.1500 = 15% discount). Null if no discount applies. Used in settlement invoice calculation to derive the effective net rate.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this IOT tariff version becomes binding and applicable for wholesale roaming charge rating. Aligns with the GSMA IOT effective date published in the inter-operator tariff notification.']
  effective_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The net IOT rate after applying any negotiated discount_percentage to the standard iot_rate. Stored as a pre-computed field to support high-volume TAP file rating performance. Expressed in the tariff currency per rate_unit.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this IOT tariff version ceases to be applicable. Null indicates an open-ended tariff with no defined expiry. Used to enforce correct rate selection during TAP file rating for a given usage date.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to convert the IOT rate from currency_code to settlement_currency_code for settlement invoice generation. Sourced from the agreed exchange rate mechanism defined in the bilateral roaming agreement (e.g., ECB rate, fixed contractual rate).']
  gsma_rate_group varchar [note: 'The GSMA-defined rate group classification for this IOT tariff entry. Rate groups are published in the GSMA IOT rate tables and used to categorise wholesale rates for settlement and benchmarking purposes. Examples: RG1, RG2, RG3 for voice; DG1, DG2 for data.']
  home_network_plmn varchar [note: 'The GSMA-assigned PLMN code (MCC+MNC) of the home operator network for which this IOT tariff is applicable. Enables multi-PLMN operators to maintain separate IOT rate tables per home network entity.']
  initial_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The initial billing period in seconds for voice calls, which may be charged at a different rate or as a flat fee before the standard per-interval rate applies. Common in IOT tariffs where the first 30 or 60 seconds are charged as a connection fee.']
  initial_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The IOT rate applied during the initial_duration_seconds period of a voice call, if different from the standard iot_rate. Null if no differentiated initial rate applies. Expressed in the tariff currency.']
  inter_operator_tariff_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IOT tariff by commercial arrangement type. \'standard\' for GSMA-published rates, \'preferential\' for bilaterally negotiated lower rates, \'reciprocal\' for symmetric bilateral agreements, \'benchmark\' for reference rates used in dispute resolution.']
  iot_publication_date date [note: 'The date on which this IOT tariff was officially published by the GSMA or notified by the visited operator. Used for audit trail and to verify compliance with GSMA notification lead-time requirements prior to the effective_from_date.']
  iot_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The wholesale IOT rate charged by the visited operator to the home operator for the specified service type and destination zone. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. For voice: rate per minute; for SMS: rate per message; for data: rate per kilobyte or megabyte as defined by rate_unit. This is the principal quantitative fact of the IOT tariff record.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,8) [note: 'The minimum wholesale charge applicable per usage event under this IOT tariff, expressed in the tariff currency. Ensures a floor charge is applied even for very short calls or minimal data usage. Used in TAP file rating to enforce minimum billing thresholds.']
  notification_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number or identifier of the official GSMA or bilateral operator notification document that introduced or amended this IOT tariff. Provides an audit trail linking the tariff record to the formal inter-operator communication.']
  nrtrde_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether usage records rated under this IOT tariff are subject to NRTRDE near-real-time roaming data exchange reporting to the home operator. True for operators with NRTRDE obligations under GSMA PRD TD.57. Used for fraud detection and real-time roaming cost monitoring.']
  peak_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this IOT tariff applies to peak, off-peak, or flat (time-independent) usage periods. Some bilateral roaming agreements differentiate IOT rates by time-of-day. \'flat\' indicates no time-of-day differentiation.']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the IOT rate. Defines the denominator for the iot_rate value. \'per_minute\' for voice, \'per_message\' for SMS/MMS, \'per_kb\' or \'per_mb\' for data, \'per_event\' for supplementary services. Required for correct TAP file charge calculation.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, or exceptions associated with this IOT tariff record. Used by the interconnect team to document bilateral negotiation context, GSMA waiver references, or rate dispute notes.']
  roaming_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inter-operator roaming agreement under which this IOT tariff applies. \'bilateral\' for direct operator-to-operator agreements, \'multilateral\' for GSMA-governed multi-party agreements, \'hub\' for roaming hub-mediated arrangements, \'mvno\' for MVNO wholesale agreements, \'transit\' for transit traffic arrangements.']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'The rounding method applied to calculated IOT charges during TAP file rating. Governs how fractional charge amounts are rounded to the precision defined by tap_decimal_places. Ensures consistent charge calculation between home and visited operators.']
  service_subtype varchar [note: 'Granular classification of the roaming service within the service_type. Examples: MOC (Mobile Originated Call), MTC (Mobile Terminated Call), MO-SMS, MT-SMS, GPRS, VoLTE, 5G NR data. Used for detailed IOT rate differentiation and TAP file reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: moc|mtc|mo_sms|mt_sms|gprs|volte|5g_nr|mms_mo|mms_mt|supplementary — promote to reference product]']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The category of roaming service to which this IOT rate applies. Drives the rating engine selection logic in TAP file processing. Values align with GSMA TAP3 service type codes: voice (MOC/MTC), SMS (MO/MT), data (GPRS/LTE/5G), MMS, and supplementary services.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for the actual financial settlement between operators, which may differ from the IOT rate currency_code. For example, IOT rates may be denominated in EUR but settled in USD per bilateral agreement.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this IOT tariff in the originating operational system (e.g., Amdocs Revenue Management tariff ID, Netcracker OSS catalogue ID). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the system of record.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this IOT tariff record was sourced or ingested (e.g., \'AMDOCS_RM\', \'NETCRACKER_OSS\', \'GSMA_PORTAL\'). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  tap_decimal_places varchar [note: 'The number of decimal places used to express the IOT rate in TAP file records, as defined by the GSMA TAP3 specification. Governs the precision of charge amounts encoded in TAP files exchanged between operators. Typically 5 for most currencies.']
  tap_file_type varchar [note: 'The GSMA file exchange format type for which this IOT tariff is applicable. TAP3 for standard roaming settlement files, NRTRDE for Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange files used for fraud detection, RAP for Returned Account Procedure files handling billing disputes.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this IOT tariff entry within the GSMA/operator tariff catalogue. Used as the business key in TAP file references and inter-operator correspondence.']
  tariff_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the IOT tariff entry, used in settlement reports, operator portals, and finance reconciliation dashboards.']
  tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the IOT tariff record. Controls whether the tariff is eligible for use in TAP file rating and settlement invoice generation. \'superseded\' indicates a newer version has replaced this record.']
  tariff_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the IOT tariff record, enabling tracking of rate changes over time. Incremented each time the tariff is revised. Used to ensure TAP file rating applies the correct rate version.']
  tax_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IOT rate is inclusive of applicable taxes (True) or exclusive of taxes (False). Relevant for operators in jurisdictions where VAT or GST applies to wholesale roaming charges. Impacts settlement invoice tax treatment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this IOT tariff record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  visited_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the visited network where the roaming subscriber consumed the service. Used to map usage to the correct IOT tariff zone and for regulatory reporting of international roaming charges.']
  visited_network_plmn varchar [note: 'The GSMA-assigned Public Land Mobile Network (PLMN) code (MCC+MNC) of the visited operator network. Used to uniquely identify the roaming partner network for IOT rate lookup and TAP file routing.']

  Note: 'Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rate table defining the wholesale rates charged between operators for roaming voice, SMS, and data services. Captures tariff version, effective period, service type, destination zone, IOT rate, currency, TAP decimal places, and GSMA rate group. Used for TAP file rating and settlement invoice generation. SSOT for IOT pricing.'
}

Table interconnect.tap_file {
  tap_file_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the TAP file record in the interconnect settlement system. Primary key for the tap_file data product.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.carrier_agreement. Business justification: TAP file currently references roaming agreement via string roaming_agreement_reference. Normalizing to carrier_agreement enables proper settlement tracking, tariff validation, and dispute resolution. ']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: MVNO and wholesale scenarios where TAP files contain usage by enterprise customer\'s subscribers. Business need: attributing roaming usage to enterprise accounts for billing reconciliation, revenue ass']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: TAP file processing is performed by specific operational teams (roaming operations, clearing house teams). Operational cost allocation and efficiency tracking require knowing which cost center process']
  sender_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: TAP file sender is currently denormalized via sender_tadig_code. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier provides authoritative carrier master reference and eliminates redundant country_code. The FK will ']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the recipient operator or clearinghouse issued a formal acknowledgement (RAP or positive ACK) for this TAP file. Marks the completion of the file exchange lifecycle for settlement purposes.']
  clearinghouse_name varchar [note: 'The name of the inter-operator clearinghouse or data exchange hub through which this TAP file was routed (e.g., Syniverse, BICS, GSMA Clearing House). Identifies the intermediary responsible for file routing, validation, and settlement statement generation.']
  clearinghouse_reference varchar [note: 'The unique reference number assigned by the inter-operator clearinghouse (e.g., GSMA Clearing House, Syniverse, BICS) upon receipt of this TAP file. Used for end-to-end traceability, dispute resolution, and reconciliation against clearinghouse settlement statements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this TAP file record was first captured in the interconnect settlement data platform (Silver layer). Audit trail field for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  data_period_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the latest CDR event contained within this TAP file, representing the end of the usage data period covered by the batch. Used alongside data_period_start_timestamp for gap detection and completeness validation.']
  data_period_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the earliest CDR event contained within this TAP file, representing the start of the usage data period covered by the batch. Used for period-based reconciliation, billing cycle alignment, and regulatory reporting.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of any commercial dispute raised against this TAP file by the recipient operator or clearinghouse. Populated when file_status is \'disputed\'. Links to the dispute management workflow for IOT charge challenges and resolution tracking.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the TAP file settlement currency to the home operator\'s local accounting currency at the time of settlement. Used for financial reporting, revenue assurance, and EBITDA calculations in multi-currency environments.']
  file_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the TAP file was generated by the sending operator\'s mediation or billing system (Amdocs Revenue Management). Represents the principal business event time for the file batch. Used for timeliness SLA measurement per GSMA NRTRDE and TAP submission deadlines.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'The physical size of the TAP file in bytes as received from the sender or clearinghouse. Used for transmission integrity checks, storage capacity planning, and anomaly detection (unusually large or small files may indicate encoding errors).']
  file_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the TAP file within the inter-operator settlement workflow. Transitions: created → sent → acknowledged (clean acceptance) or rejected (validation failure) → disputed (commercial challenge) or resubmitted (corrected re-send). Drives settlement reconciliation and SLA monitoring.']
  file_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of inter-operator data exchange file: TAP (standard Transferred Account Procedure batch), NRTRDE (Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange for fraud detection), RAP (Returned Account Procedure for rejected records), or STOP (Stop file halting processing). Determines processing pipeline and settlement treatment.']
  iot_tariff_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) schedule applied to rate the CDRs in this TAP file. Ensures the correct tariff table is used for settlement validation and dispute resolution when tariff changes occur mid-period.']
  is_test_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TAP file is a test/simulation file (True) or a live production file (False) carrying real roaming charges. Test files must be excluded from financial settlement calculations and revenue reporting.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'The predominant radio access network technology type of the CDRs contained in this TAP file (e.g., LTE, 5G NR, VoLTE). Used for technology-segmented roaming analytics, IOT tariff differentiation, and network capacity planning.']
  nrtrde_reference varchar [note: 'The reference identifier linking this TAP file to its corresponding NRTRDE near-real-time roaming data exchange record. NRTRDE files are transmitted within 4 hours of call completion for fraud detection; this reference enables correlation between the NRTRDE early notification and the final TAP settlement file.']
  original_sequence_number varchar [note: 'For resubmitted TAP files, the sequence number of the original file that this resubmission replaces. Enables lineage tracing from corrected files back to the original rejected batch. Null for first-time submissions.']
  processing_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome of the TAP file processing pipeline within the operator\'s BSS/OSS systems (Amdocs Revenue Management, Netcracker OSS/BSS). Indicates whether all CDRs were successfully rated and posted to settlement accounts, partially processed, or failed. Drives exception management workflows.']
  rap_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the RAP (Returned Account Procedure) file generated in response to validation failures in this TAP file. Links the TAP file to its corresponding rejection notification for dispute tracking and resubmission management.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the TAP file was received and acknowledged at the clearinghouse or by the recipient operator. Used to calculate transit latency and confirm delivery within bilateral SLA windows.']
  recipient_country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the recipient operator\'s home country. Used for bilateral traffic analysis, regulatory reporting, and determining applicable IOT tariff zones and settlement currency conventions.']
  recipient_tadig_code varchar [note: 'The five-character TADIG code identifying the receiving (home) network operator to whom this TAP file is addressed. Determines routing through the clearinghouse and the settlement counterparty for IOT charges.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The reconciliation state of this TAP file against the clearinghouse settlement statement. Tracks whether the file\'s declared charges match the clearinghouse-confirmed amounts. Discrepancies trigger revenue assurance investigations. Used for MRR and ARPU reporting accuracy.']
  resubmission_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this TAP file has been resubmitted after rejection or dispute. A value greater than zero indicates the file has been corrected and resent. Used for operator performance monitoring and SLA compliance tracking.']
  roaming_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this TAP file represents inbound roaming (foreign subscribers using our network — we are the visited operator and sender) or outbound roaming (our subscribers using a foreign network — we are the home operator and recipient). Critical for revenue assurance and IOT settlement accounting.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'The sequential file number assigned by the sending operator to uniquely identify this TAP file within the bilateral exchange. Follows GSMA TAP3 five-digit sequence numbering convention (00001–99999, wrapping). Used for duplicate detection and gap analysis in file receipt tracking.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the TAP file charges are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Governs the currency for clearinghouse settlement and financial reconciliation between operators.']
  settlement_statement_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the clearinghouse settlement statement that includes charges from this TAP file. Links the TAP file batch to the financial settlement document used for inter-operator payment and accounting entries in SAP S/4HANA.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system of record that generated or ingested this TAP file record (e.g., Amdocs Revenue Management, Comverse ONE, Netcracker OSS/BSS). Used for data lineage tracking and issue triage in the Silver layer.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the TAP file was submitted to the clearinghouse or directly to the recipient operator. Used to measure compliance with GSMA TAP submission timeliness requirements (typically within 36 hours of the last CDR in the file).']
  tap_version varchar [note: 'The TAP3 protocol release version used to encode this file (e.g., TAP3R10, TAP3R12). Governs the ASN.1 schema and field set applicable to the file. Critical for parser selection during file ingestion and validation.']
