  tax_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'The total tax component included in the TAP file charge amount, as declared in the file header. Applicable in jurisdictions where VAT or withholding tax applies to inter-operator roaming settlements. Used for financial reporting and tax compliance.']
  total_cdr_count varchar [note: 'The total number of Call Detail Records (CDRs) contained within this TAP file as declared in the file header. Used for completeness validation against the actual parsed record count and for settlement volume reporting.']
  total_charge_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'The gross total Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) charge amount declared in the TAP file header, representing the sum of all roaming charges for CDRs in this batch. Expressed in the settlement currency. Used as the primary financial control total for clearinghouse reconciliation and operator settlement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this TAP file record was most recently modified in the interconnect settlement data platform. Tracks status transitions, validation updates, and reconciliation changes.']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'The total number of validation errors identified during TAP file parsing and structural/business rule validation. A non-zero value triggers RAP (Returned Account Procedure) generation. Used for quality monitoring and operator performance tracking.']
  validation_error_summary varchar [note: 'A structured summary of the validation errors encountered during TAP file processing, including error codes and descriptions as defined in the GSMA TAP3 specification. Supports root cause analysis, operator dispute resolution, and resubmission guidance.']

  Note: 'Transferred Account Procedure (TAP) file header and control record representing a batch of roaming CDRs exchanged between operators via the TAP3 protocol. Captures TAP file sequence number, sender/recipient TADIG codes, file creation timestamp, TAP version, total records, total charge amount, currency, file status (created, sent, acknowledged, rejected, disputed, resubmitted), clearinghouse reference, submission/receipt timestamps, validation error summary, processing outcome, and reconciliation status against clearinghouse statements. SSOT for TAP batch lifecycle management including clearinghouse exchange tracking and error handling.'
}

Table interconnect.tap_record {
  tap_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual TAP roaming usage record (CDR) within the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the tap_record data product.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bilateral roaming agreement governing this inter-operator traffic exchange, linking to the applicable IOT tariff and settlement terms.']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the radio cell or base station where the roaming subscriber was served during this event, as reported by the visited network. Used for geographic traffic analysis and network capacity planning.']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.iot_tariff. Business justification: TAP record has iot_rate_applied and iot_rate_unit but no FK to the tariff definition. Normalizing to iot_tariff enables proper rate validation and audit trail. Note: iot_rate_applied and iot_rate_unit']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Fixed-wireless access (FWA) and fiber-to-home roaming scenarios (emerging in 5G) require tracking which ONT device generated roaming traffic for revenue assurance, fraud detection, and device-level us']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: TAP record processing is an ETL pipeline operation. Linking each record to its processing pipeline run enables data lineage tracking, reprocessing workflows, and root cause analysis when TAP file reco']
  privacy_consent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_consent. Business justification: TAP records contain subscriber usage data (IMSI, MSISDN) subject to privacy consent under GDPR/CPNI. Links usage record to consent for data processing verification and privacy audit trails.']
  rap_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.rap_file. Business justification: TAP record currently references RAP file via string rap_file_reference. Normalizing to rap_file enables proper rejection tracking and resubmission workflow. The FK will be populated when TAP record is']
  tap_file_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent TAP3 file batch from which this individual roaming CDR was extracted. Supports file-level reconciliation and settlement tracing.']
  visited_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: TAP record visited carrier is denormalized via visited_network_tadig and visited_country_code. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier eliminates redundancy and provides authoritative carrier master refer']
  call_event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billable roaming event captured in this TAP record. MOC=Mobile Originated Call, MTC=Mobile Terminated Call, GPRS=data session, SMS_MO/MT=Short Message Service originated/terminated, MMS_MO/MT=Multimedia Message Service. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOC|MTC|GPRS|SMS_MO|SMS_MT|MMS_MO|MMS_MT — promote to reference product]']
  called_number varchar [note: 'E.164 telephone number of the called party (B-number) for MOC voice events. Used for traffic analysis, fraud detection (e.g., IRSF detection), and regulatory reporting. Null for MTC, GPRS, and messaging events.']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the charge category applied to this roaming event (e.g., AIRTIME for standard voice/data, ROAMING_SURCHARGE for additional roaming fees, INTERCONNECT for transit charges). Used for revenue assurance and settlement reconciliation.']
  charged_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Gross monetary amount charged to the home network operator for this individual roaming event, expressed in the settlement currency. Calculated by the visited network by applying the applicable IOT rate to the consumed duration or volume.']
  data_volume_downlink_kb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of data transmitted from the network to the subscriber\'s device (downlink) during a GPRS/data session, measured in kilobytes. Null for non-data call event types.']
  data_volume_uplink_kb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of data transmitted from the subscriber\'s device to the network (uplink) during a GPRS/data session, measured in kilobytes. Null for non-data call event types.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for the called party in MOC voice events. Used for international traffic analysis and IOT tariff zone classification.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Discount applied to the gross charged amount for this roaming event as per the bilateral roaming agreement or special IOT discount arrangement. Reduces the net settlement obligation.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the voice call or data session in seconds as recorded by the visited network. Zero for instantaneous events (SMS/MMS). Used for IOT charge calculation and traffic analytics.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert the settlement currency amount to the home network\'s functional accounting currency at the time of TAP file processing. Used for financial reporting and revenue assurance reconciliation.']
  fraud_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag set to True when this TAP record has been identified as potentially fraudulent by the fraud management system (e.g., IRSF, SIM cloning, abnormal roaming patterns). Triggers investigation workflow in the Revenue Assurance domain.']
  home_currency_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Charged amount converted to the home network operator\'s functional accounting currency using the exchange_rate. Used for financial reporting, EBITDA calculation, and MRR/revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA.']
  home_network_tadig varchar [note: '5-character GSMA TADIG code identifying the home network operator (HPMN) to which the roaming subscriber belongs and to whom the TAP file is addressed for settlement.']
  imei varchar [note: '15-digit unique identifier of the mobile device used by the roaming subscriber during this call event. Used for fraud detection, device analytics, and GSMA equipment identity register checks.']
  imsi varchar [note: '15-digit globally unique identifier assigned to the visiting subscriber\'s SIM card, comprising MCC+MNC+MSIN. Used to identify the roaming subscriber for settlement and fraud detection. Classified as restricted PII as it uniquely identifies an individual subscriber.']
  iot_rate_applied decimal(18,8) [note: 'The specific IOT unit rate applied by the visited network to calculate the charged amount for this roaming event (e.g., rate per minute for voice, rate per KB for data). Sourced from the applicable IOT tariff table in effect at the time of service.']
  iot_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the IOT rate applied to this roaming event (e.g., PER_MINUTE for voice calls, PER_KB or PER_MB for data sessions, PER_MESSAGE for SMS/MMS).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp of the most recent modification to this TAP record in the Silver Layer (e.g., status change from PENDING to ACCEPTED, fraud flag update, RAP reference population). Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  location_area_code varchar [note: 'Location Area Code (LAC) or Tracking Area Code (TAC) identifying the geographic area within the visited network where the roaming subscriber was registered during this event.']
  msisdn varchar [note: 'The E.164 telephone number of the visiting roaming subscriber. Used for call routing, subscriber identification, and customer-facing settlement records. Classified as restricted PII.']
  network_access_technology varchar [note: 'Radio access technology used by the visiting subscriber during this roaming event (e.g., 2G/GSM, 3G/UMTS, 4G LTE, 5G NR). Relevant for IOT tariff differentiation and network analytics.']
  nrtrde_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this roaming CDR was also transmitted via the NRTRDE near-real-time roaming data exchange mechanism (within 4 hours of the call event) in addition to the standard TAP3 file. Used for fraud detection and real-time roaming cost monitoring.']
  nrtrde_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this CDR was transmitted via the NRTRDE mechanism to the home network. Null if nrtrde_flag is False. Used to verify compliance with the GSMA 4-hour NRTRDE transmission SLA.']
  partial_record_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TAP record represents a partial CDR (e.g., a long-duration data session split across multiple records by the visited network). True=partial record requiring reassembly; False=complete single record.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing and settlement lifecycle status of this TAP roaming record. ACCEPTED=validated and accepted for settlement; REJECTED=failed TAP validation; DISPUTED=under inter-operator dispute; PENDING=awaiting validation; SETTLED=financially settled.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'GSMA-standard error/rejection code assigned when a TAP record fails validation (record_status = REJECTED). Codes are defined in GSMA TD.57 Annex and indicate the specific validation rule that was violated (e.g., missing mandatory field, invalid IMSI format, timestamp out of range).']
  roaming_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the direction of roaming from the perspective of the home network operator. INBOUND=a subscriber from another network is roaming on our network (we are the VPMN); OUTBOUND=our subscriber is roaming on another network (we are the HPMN).']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this CDR within its parent TAP3 file, as assigned by the visited network operator. Used for ordering, gap detection, and duplicate identification during TAP file processing.']
  service_code varchar [note: '3GPP teleservice or bearer service code identifying the specific service used during this roaming event (e.g., 11=telephony, 22=SMS, 41=data circuit async). Maps to the TAP3 BasicServiceUsed element.']
  service_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp marking the exact moment the billable roaming event ended (call release for voice, session close for GPRS). Null for instantaneous events such as SMS/MMS.']
  service_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp marking the exact moment the billable roaming event began (call answer time for voice, session start for GPRS, message submission for SMS/MMS). Principal business event time per TAP3 specification.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inter-operator settlement charge for this TAP record is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determined by the bilateral roaming agreement.']
  settlement_period varchar [note: 'Calendar month (YYYY-MM format) to which this TAP record is assigned for inter-operator financial settlement. Determines the billing cycle for IOT invoice generation between the visited and home network operators.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this TAP record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer (e.g., Amdocs Revenue Management TAP processing module, Comverse ONE, NRTRDE hub, or industry clearinghouse). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tap_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this TAP record was first created and written to the Databricks Silver Layer during TAP file ingestion and parsing. Audit trail for data lineage and SLA monitoring.']
  tap_file_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential file number assigned by the visited network operator to the TAP3 file containing this record. Together with visited_network_tadig and home_network_tadig, uniquely identifies the source TAP file for audit and reconciliation.']
  tap_spec_version varchar [note: 'Version of the GSMA TAP3 specification used to encode this roaming record (e.g., TAP3R12). Required for correct parsing, validation, and backward-compatibility handling during TAP file processing.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Tax or levy amount applicable to this roaming event as specified in the TAP record, if any. Included per TAP3 TaxInformation element where the visited network applies local taxation to wholesale charges.']

  Note: 'Individual roaming usage record (CDR) within a TAP3 file, representing a single billable roaming event (MOC, MTC, GPRS, SMS-MO, SMS-MT) for a visiting subscriber. Captures TAP record sequence, call type, IMSI, MSISDN, visited network TADIG, home network TADIG, service start timestamp, duration/volume, charged amount, IOT rate applied, and record status. Line-item detail supporting TAP file settlement.'
}

Table interconnect.nrtrde_record {
  nrtrde_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each NRTRDE event record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bilateral roaming agreement governing this traffic exchange between the visited and home operators. Determines applicable Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rates and settlement terms.']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the radio cell in the visited network where the roaming subscriber was served. Used for network-level fraud detection (e.g., impossible travel analysis) and geographic usage pattern analysis.']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.iot_tariff. Business justification: NRTRDE record currently references IOT tariff via string iot_tariff_code. Normalizing to iot_tariff enables proper rate validation and settlement tracking. The FK will be populated during NRTRDE proce']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Near-real-time roaming data exchange for FWA services must link to specific ONT assets for immediate fraud alerts and high-usage threshold monitoring. Critical for real-time revenue assurance on fixed']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: NRTRDE records are ingested via ETL pipelines. This link supports data quality monitoring, SLA breach analysis (transmission latency), and reprocessing workflows when NRTRDE validation fails or reconc']
  privacy_consent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_consent. Business justification: NRTRDE records contain near-real-time subscriber data requiring consent for processing and sharing with roaming partners. Essential for consent validation in real-time data exchange and GDPR complianc']
  tap_file_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TAP file that this NRTRDE record will ultimately reconcile against during post-event settlement. Links pre-TAP roaming visibility to the formal TAP billing cycle.']
  visited_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: NRTRDE record visited carrier is denormalized via visited_tadig, visited_plmn_id, visited_country_code. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier eliminates redundancy and provides authoritative carrier mas']
  apn varchar [note: 'Access Point Name used by the roaming subscriber for the data session, identifying the packet data network gateway and service type (e.g., internet, IMS, enterprise VPN). Applicable for DATA record types. Used for data service classification and IOT tariff selection.']
  batch_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the NRTRDE transmission batch (file) from the visited operator, used to detect missing batches and ensure complete data receipt. Increments with each new file transmission per GSMA BA.40 batch numbering rules.']
  call_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the voice call or supplementary service event in seconds, as reported by the visited network. Applicable for VOICE and SUPPLEMENTARY record types. Used for IOT charge calculation and revenue assurance validation.']
  charge_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the estimated IOT charge is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency settlement reconciliation between international operators.']
  charge_variance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary difference between the estimated IOT charge in this NRTRDE record and the final charge confirmed in the reconciled TAP file. Populated after TAP reconciliation. Positive value indicates NRTRDE overestimated; negative indicates underestimation. Used for revenue assurance leakage analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when this NRTRDE record was first ingested and created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Audit trail field for data lineage and processing SLA measurement.']
  data_volume_downlink_kb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of data transmitted from the network to the roaming subscriber\'s device (downlink) in kilobytes during a GPRS/data session. Applicable for DATA record types. Used for IOT data charge calculation.']
  data_volume_uplink_kb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of data transmitted from the roaming subscriber\'s device to the network (uplink) in kilobytes during a GPRS/data session. Applicable for DATA record types. Used for IOT data charge calculation.']
  dialed_number varchar [note: 'The destination number dialed by the roaming subscriber for voice calls (B-party number in E.164 format). Applicable for MOC record types. Used for fraud detection (e.g., premium rate number abuse, international revenue share fraud) and traffic analysis.']
  estimated_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated wholesale charge for this roaming event calculated by the visited network using the applicable IOT tariff at time of NRTRDE transmission. This is a pre-settlement estimate; the final charge is confirmed in the TAP file. Used for early revenue assurance and cash flow forecasting.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date (UTC) on which the roaming service event occurred, derived from service_start_timestamp. Used as a partition key in the Silver layer lakehouse for efficient time-range queries and daily revenue assurance reporting.']
  fraud_alert_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating that this NRTRDE record has triggered a fraud alert to the Revenue Assurance domain based on anomalous usage patterns (e.g., impossible travel, velocity breach, SIM cloning signatures). True = fraud alert raised; False = no alert.']
  high_usage_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag set by the visited network indicating that this subscriber\'s cumulative roaming usage has exceeded a predefined high-usage threshold, triggering potential fraud alert or credit limit notification to the home operator per GSMA BA.40 high-usage detection rules.']
  home_tadig varchar [note: '5-character GSMA TADIG code identifying the subscriber\'s home network operator. Used to route NRTRDE records to the correct home PLMN for revenue assurance and fraud detection.']
  imei varchar [note: '15-digit unique identifier of the mobile device used during the roaming event. Used for device-level fraud detection (e.g., blacklisted IMEI, IMEI/IMSI mismatch indicating SIM cloning) and equipment analytics.']
  imsi varchar [note: '15-digit globally unique identifier assigned to the subscriber\'s SIM card, used to identify the roaming subscriber across visited and home networks. Classified as restricted PII as it directly identifies an individual subscriber.']
  location_area_code varchar [note: 'Location Area Code identifying the geographic area within the visited network where the roaming event occurred. Used alongside Cell ID for subscriber location tracking and fraud detection in the interconnect domain.']
  msisdn varchar [note: 'The subscriber\'s mobile phone number in E.164 format, representing the dialable number associated with the roaming event. Classified as restricted PII as it directly identifies an individual.']
  network_technology varchar [note: 'Radio access technology generation used to deliver the roaming service. Values: 2G (GSM/GPRS/EDGE), 3G (UMTS/HSPA), 4G_LTE (Long-Term Evolution), 5G_NR (5G New Radio), WIFI (Wi-Fi calling/VoWiFi). Impacts IOT tariff applicability and fraud pattern analysis.']
  nrtrde_version varchar [note: 'Version of the GSMA BA.40 NRTRDE standard used to generate this record (e.g., 2.0, 3.0). Required for backward-compatible processing as operators may transmit records under different standard versions during migration periods.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the NRTRDE record within the interconnect operations processing pipeline. RECEIVED=ingested from visited network; VALIDATED=passed format/content checks; ENRICHED=IOT tariff and agreement data applied; RECONCILED=matched to TAP file; REJECTED=failed validation; DISPUTED=charge under dispute.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Specific CDR record type classifying the direction and nature of the roaming event. MOC=Mobile Originated Call, MTC=Mobile Terminated Call, GPRS=data session, SMS_MO=outbound SMS, SMS_MT=inbound SMS. Aligns with TAP3 record type taxonomy.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason a NRTRDE record was rejected during validation processing (e.g., invalid IMSI format, unknown TADIG, missing mandatory field, duplicate sequence number). Populated only when processing_status = REJECTED. Aligns with GSMA TAP error code taxonomy.']
  roaming_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this roaming event is inbound (foreign subscriber roaming on our network — we are the visited operator) or outbound (our subscriber roaming on a foreign network — we are the home operator). Determines revenue assurance treatment and settlement flow.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to the NRTRDE record within a transmission batch, used to detect gaps, duplicates, and ordering issues during inter-operator exchange per GSMA BA.40.']
  service_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (UTC) when the roaming service event concluded. For voice calls this is call release time; for data sessions this is PDP context deactivation. Used to derive call/session duration and validate against usage volume.']
  service_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (UTC) when the roaming service event began — the principal real-world event time. For voice calls this is call answer time; for data sessions this is PDP context activation; for SMS this is submission time. Core field for NRTRDE 4-hour transmission SLA compliance measurement per GSMA BA.40.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of telecommunications service consumed during the roaming event. Drives IOT tariff selection, usage volume interpretation, and revenue assurance thresholds. Values: VOICE (voice call), SMS (short message), DATA (packet data), MMS (multimedia message), SUPPLEMENTARY (supplementary services).']
  sla_breach_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this NRTRDE record was transmitted outside the GSMA BA.40 4-hour SLA window (transmission_latency_minutes > 240). True = SLA breached; False = within SLA. Used for inter-operator SLA performance reporting and penalty calculation.']
  source_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the NRTRDE transmission file from which this record was extracted, following GSMA BA.40 file naming conventions. Used for batch traceability, reprocessing, and audit trail in the interconnect operations pipeline.']
  tap_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of reconciliation between this NRTRDE pre-TAP record and the corresponding TAP3 billing record. PENDING=TAP file not yet received; MATCHED=successfully reconciled; UNMATCHED=no corresponding TAP record found; PARTIAL=partial match with charge discrepancy. Critical for revenue assurance.']
  transmission_latency_minutes varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in minutes between service_start_timestamp and transmission_timestamp. Measures compliance with the GSMA BA.40 4-hour (240-minute) near-real-time transmission SLA. Records exceeding 240 minutes are flagged for SLA breach reporting.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the NRTRDE record was transmitted by the visited network to the home network. Used to calculate transmission latency against the GSMA BA.40 4-hour SLA requirement from service delivery.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when this NRTRDE record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse, such as after TAP reconciliation, status update, or fraud flag enrichment. Audit trail field for data lineage.']

  Note: 'Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) event record providing early visibility into roaming usage within 4 hours of service delivery per GSMA BA.40 standard. Captures NRTRDE sequence, IMSI, MSISDN, visited TADIG, home TADIG, service type (voice, SMS, data), usage timestamp, volume/duration, estimated charge, high-usage indicator, and transmission latency. Used by interconnect operations for early revenue assurance on inbound/outbound roaming traffic and for triggering fraud alerts to the assurance domain when thresholds are breached. SSOT for pre-TAP roaming visibility.'
}

Table interconnect.transit_traffic_record {
  transit_traffic_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each transit traffic record in the Telecommunications wholesale interconnect system. Primary key for the transit_traffic_record data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bilateral or multilateral roaming or transit agreement under which this traffic was carried. Links to the governing commercial agreement for tariff and settlement terms.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Transit traffic generates revenue/expense postings to specific GL accounts. Supports accrual accounting for non-invoiced transit services and revenue assurance reconciliation. Critical for financial c']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the wholesale settlement invoice on which this transit traffic record was billed. Links the traffic record to the financial invoice for reconciliation and dispute management.']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.iot_tariff. Business justification: Transit traffic record currently references IOT tariff via string iot_rate_code. Normalizing to iot_tariff enables proper rate validation and settlement tracking. The FK will be populated during trans']
  original_record_transit_traffic_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original transit_traffic_record_id when this record is a correction, reversal, or reprocessed version of a prior record. Null for original records. Supports audit trail and settlement reconciliation.']
  originating_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier entity that originated the transit traffic and handed it off to Telecommunications network for transit routing. Used for wholesale billing and IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) application.']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: Transit traffic CDRs are processed via rating pipelines. Linking to pipeline_run enables late CDR tracking, reprocessing of disputed records, and data lineage for revenue assurance audits when settlem']
  primary_transit_pop_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the POP (Point of Presence) or network node where the transit traffic entered the Telecommunications network from the originating carrier. Critical for capacity planning and interconnect cost allocation.']
  primary_transit_terminating_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier entity that receives the transit traffic from Telecommunications network for final delivery to the end destination. Used for wholesale settlement and capacity planning.']
  tap_file_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GSMA TAP (Transferred Account Procedure) file in which this transit traffic record was included for inter-operator exchange. Used for TAP file reconciliation, NRTRDE (Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange) matching, and dispute resolution.']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Transit traffic records must track which transmission links carried the traffic for network utilization analysis, capacity planning, and cost allocation. Operations teams use this for link dimensionin']
  billing_period varchar [note: 'The calendar month billing period (YYYY-MM format) to which this transit traffic record is assigned for wholesale settlement and invoice generation. Used for period-based aggregation in Amdocs Revenue Management and MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) reporting.']
  call_completion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transit call or session was successfully completed (True) or failed/unanswered (False). Used for answer seizure ratio (ASR) KPI calculation, QoS (Quality of Service) monitoring, and wholesale SLA compliance reporting.']
  call_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the transit traffic session or call was terminated. Used in conjunction with call_start_timestamp to derive duration for voice and signaling traffic billing.']
  call_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the transit traffic session or call began, as recorded at the ingress POP. This is the principal real-world event timestamp used for CDR (Call Detail Record) rating, IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) application, and TAP (Transferred Account Procedure) file generation.']
  cdr_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this CDR (Call Detail Record) or transit traffic record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements across OSS/BSS systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: amdocs|netcracker|comverse|ericsson|nokia|mediation|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this transit traffic record was first created or ingested into the Telecommunications data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance monitoring for CDR (Call Detail Record) processing timeliness.']
  data_volume_kb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of data traffic transited through the Telecommunications network in kilobytes. Used for per-KB or per-MB IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) rate application for data and MMS transit traffic. Null for voice and SMS traffic types.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Wholesale discount or credit applied to the gross billed amount for this transit record, per the terms of the interconnect or transit agreement. May reflect volume discounts, promotional rates, or contractual adjustments.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any active or historical inter-carrier dispute associated with this transit traffic record. Populated when settlement_status is \'disputed\'. Used for dispute tracking and resolution in wholesale operations.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the transit voice or signaling session in seconds. Used for per-second or per-minute IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) rate application and wholesale billing calculations. Null for non-voice traffic types.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transit traffic record has been identified as potentially fraudulent (True) by fraud management systems, including IRSF (International Revenue Share Fraud) detection. Triggers investigation and potential blocking in the SOC (Security Operations Center).']
  gross_billed_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The gross wholesale amount billed for this transit traffic record before any adjustments, discounts, or credits. Computed as transit_rate_applied multiplied by the applicable volume or duration. Used in Amdocs Revenue Management for invoice generation and settlement.']
  late_cdr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CDR (Call Detail Record) was received after the standard TAP (Transferred Account Procedure) file submission deadline (True) or within the normal processing window (False). Late CDRs may require special handling in settlement and may be subject to GSMA late submission penalties.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The final net amount payable for this transit traffic record after applying all discounts and adjustments. Equals gross_billed_amount minus discount_amount. Used as the authoritative financial figure for inter-carrier settlement and revenue assurance reconciliation.']
  nrtrde_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequence number assigned to this record within the NRTRDE (Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange) data stream. Enables near-real-time fraud detection and roaming usage monitoring per GSMA PRD IR.71 requirements.']
  originating_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country from which the transit traffic originated. Used for international settlement rate determination, IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) zone classification, and regulatory reporting to FCC and ITU.']
  originating_number varchar [note: 'The calling line identity (CLI) or originating MSISDN/number from which the transit traffic was generated. Stored for CDR (Call Detail Record) reconciliation, fraud detection, and regulatory lawful intercept compliance. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4 and FCC CPNI regulations.']
  protocol_type varchar [note: 'The signaling protocol used to establish and manage the transit traffic session. Relevant for network operations, fault management, and interconnect compatibility assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ss7|sigtran|sip|isup|h323|diameter — promote to reference product]']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure to which the transit_rate_applied corresponds. Defines how the rate is applied to the traffic volume or duration to compute the billed amount (e.g., per_minute for voice, per_kb for data, per_message for SMS).']
  rated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this transit traffic record was rated by the billing/rating engine (Amdocs Revenue Management or Comverse ONE). Distinct from the call start timestamp; used for rating latency monitoring and billing cycle management.']
  record_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this transit traffic record, used in inter-carrier settlement communications, dispute resolution, and wholesale billing reconciliation. Corresponds to the CDR (Call Detail Record) sequence number or transit record identifier exchanged between carriers.']
  release_cause_code varchar [note: 'The ITU-T Q.850 or SIP response code indicating the reason for call or session termination. Used for quality analysis, failed call identification, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance monitoring in wholesale transit services.']
  reprocessed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transit traffic record has been reprocessed or re-rated (True) due to a correction, dispute resolution, or system error. Used for audit trail and to prevent double-billing in wholesale settlement.']
  revenue_assurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transit traffic record has been flagged by the revenue assurance process for investigation (True) or has passed revenue assurance controls (False). Supports leakage detection and financial integrity in wholesale billing.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'The network routing number or trunk group identifier used to route this transit traffic through the Telecommunications network. Used for capacity planning, route optimization, and interconnect cost allocation.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transit traffic settlement amounts (gross, discount, net) are denominated. Supports multi-currency wholesale settlement with international carriers.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transit traffic record within the wholesale financial settlement workflow. Drives downstream billing, dispute management, and revenue assurance processes in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  sms_count varchar [note: 'Number of SMS messages included in this transit traffic record. Applicable when traffic_type is \'sms\'. Used for per-message IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) rate application in wholesale SMS hubbing arrangements.']
  terminating_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country to which the transit traffic was destined. Used for international settlement rate determination, IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) zone classification, and regulatory reporting.']
  terminating_number varchar [note: 'The called party number or terminating MSISDN/E.164 number to which the transit traffic was routed. Used for CDR (Call Detail Record) reconciliation, number portability (MNP) validation, and regulatory compliance. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4 and FCC CPNI regulations.']
  traffic_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the directional flow of the transit traffic relative to the Telecommunications network: inbound (entering for termination), outbound (exiting for origination), or transit (passing through for hubbing).']
  traffic_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transit traffic by service type. Determines applicable IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) rate table and settlement methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voice|sms|data|mms|signaling|roaming_voice|roaming_data — promote to reference product]']
  transit_rate_applied decimal(18,6) [note: 'The per-unit wholesale transit rate applied to this record at the time of rating, expressed in the settlement currency. Represents the IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) unit price used to compute the billed amount. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity of carrier pricing.']
  trunk_group_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical or logical trunk group over which this transit traffic was carried between the ingress and egress POPs. Used for network capacity planning, congestion analysis, and interconnect infrastructure management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this transit traffic record was last modified, such as when settlement_status changed or a dispute was raised. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and downstream incremental processing in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Record of transit voice and data traffic routed through Telecommunication\'s network on behalf of third-party carriers (transit/hubbing). Captures transit record ID, originating carrier, terminating carrier, ingress POP, egress POP, traffic type (voice, SMS, data), start timestamp, duration/volume, transit rate applied, billed amount, and settlement status. Supports wholesale transit billing and capacity planning.'
}

Table interconnect.peering_arrangement {
  peering_arrangement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the peering arrangement record in the interconnect domain. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT.']
  location_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.location_site. Business justification: Peering arrangements occur at physical exchange points (IXPs, data centers). Site link required for facility access coordination, port provisioning, cross-connect management, and physical capacity pla']
  peer_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: Peering arrangement peer is currently denormalized via peer_carrier_name. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier provides authoritative carrier master reference with full carrier attributes (TADIG, MCC/M']
  technical_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to people.employee. Business justification: Peering arrangements require technical ownership by network engineers responsible for BGP configuration, capacity planning, and SLA monitoring—distinct from commercial ownership. This supports NOC esc']
  arrangement_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the peering arrangement (e.g., PEER-2024-NYC-001). Used in operational communications, NOC dashboards, and inter-carrier correspondence.']
  arrangement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name for the peering arrangement (e.g., \'Equinix NY4 Private Peering with AS64512\'). Used in reporting and NOC tooling.']
  arrangement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes capturing special conditions, negotiation history, technical exceptions, or contextual information about this peering arrangement not captured in structured fields.']
  arrangement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the peering arrangement. draft = under negotiation; pending_approval = awaiting internal or regulatory sign-off; active = live and carrying traffic; suspended = temporarily halted; terminated = permanently ended; expired = passed effective end date without renewal.']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this peering arrangement automatically renews upon expiry without requiring explicit renegotiation. True = auto-renews; False = requires manual renewal action before expiry_date.']
  availability_target_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Agreed minimum uptime percentage for the peering session as specified in the SLA (e.g., 99.9900 = 99.99%). Used for SLA compliance reporting and penalty calculation.']
  bgp_session_type varchar [note: 'Type of BGP session established for this peering arrangement. ebgp = External BGP (standard inter-AS peering); ibgp = Internal BGP (used in route-reflector scenarios); ebgp_multihop = eBGP session across multiple IP hops.']
  committed_bandwidth_mbps decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed bandwidth in megabits per second (Mbps) committed under this peering arrangement. Used for capacity planning, SLA enforcement, and billing in paid peering scenarios.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this peering arrangement record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field supporting data governance and lineage requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the peering arrangement becomes operationally binding and traffic exchange may commence. Aligns with BGP session activation date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the peering arrangement is scheduled to expire. Null for open-ended arrangements. Used for renewal alerting and SLA compliance tracking.']
  ip_version varchar [note: 'IP protocol version(s) supported by this peering session. ipv4 = IPv4 only; ipv6 = IPv6 only; dual_stack = both IPv4 and IPv6 sessions active. Drives network engineering and capacity planning.']
  ixp_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Internet Exchange Point (IXP) where public peering occurs (e.g., Equinix IX, DE-CIX, AMS-IX, LINX). Null for private PNI arrangements. Used for peering point inventory and cost allocation.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which this peering arrangement was last formally reviewed by network operations or commercial teams for traffic balance, SLA compliance, and commercial terms adequacy.']
  latency_target_ms decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum agreed one-way network latency in milliseconds (ms) for traffic traversing this peering arrangement as defined in the SLA. Used for QoS monitoring and SLA breach detection.']
  max_bandwidth_mbps decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable bandwidth in megabits per second (Mbps) for this peering arrangement before traffic shaping or penalties apply. Defines the upper capacity envelope for the interconnect port.']
  md5_auth_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether MD5 TCP authentication is enabled on the BGP session for this peering arrangement to prevent session hijacking. True = MD5 authentication active; False = no MD5 authentication.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this peering arrangement. Used to drive review workflow scheduling in NOC and commercial operations.']
  noc_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the peer carrier\'s Network Operations Center (NOC) for operational escalations, fault notifications, and maintenance coordination. Classified confidential as organizational contact data.']
  noc_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the peer carrier\'s Network Operations Center (NOC) for 24/7 operational escalations and incident coordination. Classified confidential as organizational contact data.']
  our_asn varchar [note: 'BGP Autonomous System Number (ASN) of this carrier\'s routing domain used in this peering session. Required when the carrier operates multiple ASNs across different network segments.']
  our_bgp_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of this carrier\'s BGP router used to establish the peering session with the counterparty. May be IPv4 or IPv6. Classified confidential as it reveals internal network topology.']
  packet_loss_target_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum agreed packet loss percentage for traffic on this peering arrangement as defined in the SLA (e.g., 0.0100 = 0.01%). Used for QoS (Quality of Service) performance monitoring.']
  peer_asn varchar [note: 'BGP Autonomous System Number (ASN) of the peering counterparty as assigned by a Regional Internet Registry (ARIN, RIPE, APNIC). Uniquely identifies the peer\'s routing domain. Range: 1–4294967295 (32-bit ASN per RFC 6793).']
  peer_bgp_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the counterparty\'s BGP router used to establish the peering session. May be IPv4 or IPv6. Classified confidential as it reveals network topology details.']
  peering_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the peering arrangement by commercial and technical model. public_ixp = peering via a public Internet Exchange Point; private_pni = Private Network Interconnect (dedicated cross-connect); settlement_free = no financial settlement between parties; paid = one or both parties pay for traffic exchange; partial = selective prefix exchange rather than full table.']
  port_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical or logical interconnect port used for this peering arrangement (e.g., switch/port designation from the IXP or PNI cross-connect). Links to network inventory management.']
  port_speed_gbps decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical port speed in gigabits per second (Gbps) of the interconnect port used for this peering arrangement (e.g., 1.000, 10.000, 100.000). Used for capacity planning and utilization reporting.']
  prefix_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of BGP route prefixes this carrier will accept from the peer. Exceeding this limit triggers a BGP session reset or route filtering. Critical for route table stability and security.']
  ratio_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowable percentage deviation from the agreed traffic ratio before the arrangement is considered out of balance and subject to review or financial settlement. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 20.00 = ±20%).']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before expiry_date that a renewal notice must be issued to the peer carrier. Used to trigger automated renewal workflow alerts in the NOC and commercial teams.']
  rpki_validation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI) route origin validation is enforced on BGP announcements received from this peer. True = RPKI validation active; False = not enforced. Supports FCC routing security mandates.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial settlement in paid peering arrangements (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for settlement-free arrangements.']
  settlement_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Agreed per-unit financial rate applied to traffic exchange in paid peering arrangements (e.g., rate per Mbps or per GB). Expressed in the settlement_currency. Business-confidential commercial term.']
  settlement_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the settlement rate (e.g., per_mbps = per megabit per second of committed bandwidth; per_gb = per gigabyte of traffic; flat_monthly = fixed monthly fee regardless of volume).']
  sla_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this peering arrangement to the applicable Service Level Agreement (SLA) document governing latency, packet loss, availability, and jitter commitments. Used for SLA compliance reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating operational system record for this peering arrangement (e.g., Netcracker OSS/BSS resource inventory record ID or Nokia NetAct configuration management object ID). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of calendar days\' written notice required to terminate this peering arrangement. Contractual obligation that governs decommissioning timelines.']
  traffic_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether traffic flows in both directions (bilateral), only from this carrier to the peer (unilateral), or whether the peer provides transit routing (transit). Drives settlement and capacity planning.']
  traffic_ratio_inbound decimal(10,4) [note: 'Agreed inbound traffic ratio component used to calculate the traffic balance ratio (e.g., 1.0 in a 1:1 ratio). Settlement-free peering typically requires ratios within a defined tolerance band (e.g., 1:2 or 2:1).']
  traffic_ratio_outbound decimal(10,4) [note: 'Agreed outbound traffic ratio component used to calculate the traffic balance ratio (e.g., 2.0 in a 1:2 ratio). Imbalance beyond the agreed threshold may trigger renegotiation or conversion to paid peering.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this peering arrangement record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record of IP peering and voice interconnect peering arrangements with other carriers, ISPs, and content delivery networks. Captures peering type (public IXP, private PNI, settlement-free, paid, partial), peering point location (IXP name, POP, data center), agreed traffic ratios and balance thresholds, bandwidth commitments, BGP session parameters, effective/expiry dates, SLA references, and arrangement status. Distinct from carrier_agreement — peering arrangements are technical/operational agreements governing traffic exchange mechanics, often managed by network operations separately from commercial roaming/wholesale contracts.'
}

Table interconnect.settlement_invoice {
  settlement_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the inter-carrier settlement invoice record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the settlement_invoice data product.']
  administrative_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.administrative_region. Business justification: Settlement invoices require jurisdiction-specific tax treatment, regulatory reporting, and revenue recognition rules. Has carrier_country_code but tax compliance, statutory reporting, and audit trails']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bilateral roaming or interconnect agreement under which this settlement invoice is raised. Governs applicable Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rates and settlement terms.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the interconnect carrier (domestic or international) that is the counterparty to this settlement invoice. Links to the carrier/partner master record.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Wholesale billing where settlement invoices are issued to enterprise customers operating as MVNOs or resellers. Business need: linking interconnect settlement to enterprise billing accounts for paymen']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Settlement invoices post to specific GL accounts (interconnect revenue/expense accounts). Monthly financial close, P&L reporting, and audit trails require this link. Already has gl_account_code which ']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Interconnect settlement invoices may be issued to/from partners (MVNOs, wholesale customers) in addition to carriers. Business process: Partner billing reconciliation, revenue share settlement, consol']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: Settlement invoices are generated by batch billing pipelines. This link provides audit trail for invoice generation, supports dispute resolution by identifying which pipeline run produced disputed inv']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to people.employee. Business justification: Settlement invoices in carrier billing operations require preparation by finance analysts who validate TAP/NRTRDE records, calculate amounts, and reconcile with agreements. This tracks accountability ']
  tap_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.tap_file. Business justification: Settlement invoice currently references TAP file via string tap_file_reference. Normalizing to tap_file enables proper traceability from invoice back to source CDR batch, supporting dispute resolution']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the person who formally approved this settlement invoice for payment or issuance. Required for financial controls, audit trail, and segregation of duties compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the settlement invoice was formally approved. Provides an audit trail for the approval workflow and supports financial controls compliance.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the billing period covered by this settlement invoice. Defines the upper boundary of traffic and charges included. Must be greater than or equal to billing_period_start_date.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the billing period covered by this settlement invoice. Together with billing_period_end_date, defines the window of traffic and charges included. Aligns with the TAP file billing cycle or contractual settlement period.']
  carrier_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the counterparty carrier\'s home country. Used for geographic segmentation of inter-carrier settlement volumes, regulatory reporting, and IOT tariff zone determination.']
  carrier_reference_number varchar [note: 'The invoice or settlement reference number assigned by the counterparty carrier. Used for cross-referencing and reconciliation between Telecommunication\'s internal invoice number and the carrier\'s own settlement document identifier.']
  carrier_tadig_code varchar [note: 'The GSMA TADIG (Transferred Account Data Interchange Group) five-character code uniquely identifying the counterparty carrier\'s network. Used in TAP file processing, NRTRDE exchange, and inter-operator settlement identification.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA cost center code to which the settlement invoice charges are allocated. Used for internal financial reporting, OPEX/CAPEX tracking, and interconnect cost management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this settlement invoice record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage, compliance, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this invoice are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determined by the bilateral roaming or interconnect agreement. Critical for multi-currency settlement reconciliation and FX conversion.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the reason for disputing this invoice. Common reasons include tariff misapplication, CDR count discrepancy, TAP file rejection, duplicate billing, or IOT rate disagreement. Populated when dispute_reference is set.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'The formal dispute case reference number raised against this invoice, if applicable. Populated when invoice_status is \'disputed\'. Links to the dispute management workflow in ServiceNow ITSM or the carrier dispute management system.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The portion of the gross amount that is currently under formal dispute by either party. Populated when invoice_status is \'disputed\'. Net payable is calculated excluding this amount until dispute resolution. Tracked for financial reporting and carrier dispute management.']
  due_date date [note: 'The contractual date by which payment must be received or remitted. Calculated from invoice_date plus the payment terms defined in the roaming or interconnect agreement. Used for overdue tracking and dunning escalation.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency to Telecommunication\'s functional/reporting currency (e.g., USD to local currency) at the time of invoice generation or settlement. Required for multi-currency financial reporting and revenue assurance reconciliation.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net_payable_amount converted to Telecommunication\'s functional currency using the exchange_rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting, EBITDA calculation, and revenue assurance analytics.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter code for Telecommunication\'s functional/reporting currency into which the invoice amounts are converted using exchange_rate. Enables consolidated financial reporting across multi-currency carrier settlements.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross amount of all charges included in this settlement invoice before any disputes, credits, or adjustments are applied. Expressed in the invoice currency. Represents the sum of all traffic charges, IOT-rated usage, and applicable surcharges.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the settlement invoice was formally issued and transmitted to the counterparty carrier. This is the principal business event date for the invoice lifecycle and triggers the payment due date calculation.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable invoice reference number assigned by Amdocs Revenue Management at invoice generation. Used in all carrier-to-carrier correspondence, dispute resolution, and financial reconciliation. Conforms to the operator\'s invoice numbering scheme.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the settlement invoice within the inter-carrier settlement workflow. Transitions from \'draft\' through \'issued\', \'acknowledged\', optionally \'disputed\', to \'settled\' or \'cancelled\'. \'overdue\' is set when payment due date has passed without settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|acknowledged|disputed|settled|cancelled|overdue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the direction and nature of the settlement invoice. \'payable\' indicates Telecommunication owes the carrier; \'receivable\' indicates the carrier owes Telecommunication; \'credit_note\' and \'debit_note\' represent adjustments to prior invoices; \'adjustment\' covers miscellaneous corrections.']
  iot_tariff_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) table applied when rating the traffic charges on this invoice. Ensures auditability of the tariff applied and supports dispute resolution when tariff versions change mid-period.']
  net_payable_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net amount due for payment after subtracting disputed_amount and applying any approved credits or adjustments from the gross_amount plus tax_amount. This is the actionable settlement figure used for payment processing and financial ledger posting.']
  netting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is subject to bilateral netting against a corresponding payable or receivable invoice with the same carrier for the same billing period. When True, the net_payable_amount reflects the netted position. Supports cash flow optimization in carrier settlement.']
  netting_invoice_reference varchar [note: 'The invoice_number of the offsetting invoice used in bilateral netting. Populated only when netting_flag is True. Enables traceability of the netting pair for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, comments, or annotations related to this settlement invoice. Used by the interconnect settlement team to record special handling instructions, dispute context, or carrier communication summaries.']
  nrtrde_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the NRTRDE data exchange associated with this invoice. NRTRDE provides near-real-time roaming CDR data used for fraud detection and early settlement validation prior to TAP file processing. Null for non-roaming invoices.']
  paid_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total amount actually received or remitted against this settlement invoice to date. Used to calculate outstanding balance and confirm full or partial settlement. Updated upon payment confirmation from the treasury or accounts payable/receivable system.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which full or final payment was received or remitted for this settlement invoice. Null until payment is confirmed. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation and settlement cycle analytics.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or mechanism used to settle this invoice. \'wire_transfer\' and \'swift\' are standard for international carrier settlements; \'netting\' applies when bilateral payable and receivable invoices are offset; \'cheque\' and \'direct_debit\' for domestic arrangements.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the settlement invoice. \'unpaid\' indicates no payment received; \'partial\' indicates partial payment; \'paid\' indicates full settlement; \'overpaid\' indicates excess payment requiring refund; \'written_off\' indicates the amount has been written off after failed collection.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this settlement invoice record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking. Primary source is Amdocs Revenue Management; SAP S/4HANA for financial postings.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax charges applicable to this settlement invoice, including withholding tax, VAT, or other applicable levies as per the bilateral agreement and jurisdictional requirements. May be zero for tax-exempt inter-operator settlements.']
  total_cdr_count varchar [note: 'The total number of Call Detail Records (CDRs) included in this settlement invoice. Used for volume reconciliation against TAP files and NRTRDE data. Discrepancies between CDR counts and billed amounts are a primary trigger for disputes.']
  total_data_volume_mb decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total data volume in megabytes (MB) covered by this settlement invoice. Used for data roaming and interconnect data charge reconciliation. Applicable to roaming TAP, interconnect_data, and wholesale invoice types.']
  total_sms_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SMS messages included in this settlement invoice. Used for SMS interconnect charge reconciliation and IOT tariff validation. Applicable to roaming TAP and SMS termination/origination invoice types.']
  total_usage_minutes decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregate voice usage duration in minutes covered by this settlement invoice. Used for traffic volume analysis, IOT tariff validation, and inter-carrier settlement reconciliation. Applicable primarily to voice termination, origination, and roaming invoices.']
  traffic_type varchar [note: 'Categorises the type of inter-carrier traffic covered by this invoice. \'roaming_tap\' covers outbound/inbound roaming settled via Transferred Account Procedure (TAP); \'transit\' covers traffic transiting through Telecommunication\'s network; \'termination\' and \'origination\' cover voice/SMS termination and origination charges; \'interconnect_data\' covers data interconnect; \'wholesale\' covers wholesale capacity arrangements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this settlement invoice record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Financial settlement invoice raised between Telecommunication and an interconnect carrier for a billing period, covering roaming (TAP), transit, termination, and origination charges. Captures invoice number, billing period, carrier reference, invoice type (payable/receivable), gross amount, disputed amount, net payable, currency, due date, and payment status. SSOT for inter-carrier financial settlement documents.'
}

Table interconnect.settlement_invoice_line {
  settlement_invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each settlement invoice line item within the interconnect domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bilateral or multilateral roaming agreement under which this settlement line item is charged. Links to the governing inter-operator agreement.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Individual invoice lines post to different GL accounts based on service type and traffic direction. Revenue recognition by service category and financial reporting require this link. Replaces denormal']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rate table entry applied to this settlement line item for pricing voice, SMS, or data roaming traffic.']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: Invoice line items are created by rating/billing pipelines. This link enables detailed lineage for revenue assurance, supports root cause analysis when line-level disputes occur, and tracks which pipe']
  settlement_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent settlement invoice header to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header/detail relationship for inter-carrier settlement invoices.']
  tap_file_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TAP file from which this settlement line item was derived. Enables traceability from invoice line back to the originating TAP3 data exchange file.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary adjustment applied to this settlement line item following dispute resolution or TAP file error correction. Positive values increase the charge; negative values reduce it.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for any adjustment applied to this settlement line item (e.g., TAP file error correction, rate dispute resolution, volume reconciliation difference). Populated when line_status is \'adjusted\'.']
  call_event_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual Call Detail Records (CDRs) or usage events aggregated into this settlement line item. Used for reconciliation against TAP file record counts.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA cost center code to which this settlement line item is allocated for internal financial reporting and OPEX tracking of interconnect and wholesale costs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this settlement invoice line record was first created in the system (Amdocs Revenue Management). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for this settlement line item. Used for transit traffic routing analysis and regulatory reporting to FCC/Ofcom/BEREC.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this settlement line item per the terms of the roaming or wholesale agreement (e.g., volume discount, bilateral discount, promotional rate reduction).']
  discount_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage rate of the discount applied to the gross charge for this settlement line item, as stipulated in the inter-operator agreement or IOT tariff schedule.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any active or resolved dispute raised against this settlement line item. Populated when line_status is \'disputed\' or \'adjusted\'. Links to the dispute management process.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the rate_currency_code to the settlement_currency_code for this line item. Sourced from the agreed exchange rate mechanism in the roaming agreement.']
  gross_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total gross charge for this settlement line item before any discounts, adjustments, or taxes are applied. Calculated as traffic_volume multiplied by unit_rate.']
  home_network_plmn varchar [note: 'The PLMN code (MCC+MNC) of the home network operator for roaming traffic on this settlement line. Used to identify the subscriber\'s home network in TAP3 and NRTRDE processing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this settlement invoice line record. Tracks adjustments, dispute resolutions, and status changes for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of this settlement invoice line item, providing additional context about the service, traffic type, or special billing arrangement for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  line_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric reference code for this settlement invoice line, used in dispute correspondence and reconciliation with the counterpart carrier.']
  line_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this line item within the parent settlement invoice. Used for display ordering and reconciliation referencing.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this settlement invoice line item. Tracks progression from draft through issuance, acceptance, dispute resolution, adjustment, and final settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|accepted|disputed|adjusted|settled|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  net_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The final net charge for this settlement line item after applying discounts and taxes. Represents the actual amount payable/receivable for this line in the inter-carrier settlement.']
  nrtrde_file_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the NRTRDE file associated with this settlement line, enabling near-real-time roaming fraud detection cross-referencing and reconciliation against TAP-settled charges.']
  originating_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the originating country for this settlement line item. Used for inbound roaming analysis and bilateral traffic balance reporting.']
  partner_operator_code varchar [note: 'GSMA TADIG (Transferred Account Data Interchange Group) code identifying the partner/counterpart operator for this settlement line. Used for carrier identification in TAP file processing and IOT settlement.']
  partner_operator_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or trading name of the partner operator (carrier, MVNO, or transit provider) associated with this settlement line item.']
  rap_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Return Account Procedure (RAP) file if this settlement line was returned or rejected during TAP processing. Enables tracking of rejected CDRs and their financial impact.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit_rate. May differ from the settlement_currency_code when rate tables are maintained in SDR (Special Drawing Rights) or a reference currency.']
  revenue_assurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this settlement line item has been flagged by the revenue assurance process for further investigation (e.g., volume anomaly, rate mismatch, missing TAP records).']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the telecommunications service type for this settlement line item. Drives IOT tariff selection and financial reporting by service type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voice_termination|sms|data_roaming|transit|mms|supplementary_service|iptv|voip|content — promote to reference product]']
  service_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the billing period or traffic window covered by this settlement invoice line item. Together with service_period_start, defines the chargeable period.']
  service_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the billing period or traffic window covered by this settlement invoice line item. Defines the temporal scope for traffic volume and charge calculation.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the settlement amounts (gross, discount, tax, net) are expressed for this line item. Typically agreed in the bilateral roaming agreement.']
  tap_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The TAP file sequence number assigned by the sending operator, used to uniquely identify the TAP batch within the GSMA data exchange framework and to detect missing or duplicate TAP files.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to this settlement line item, including withholding tax, VAT, or other applicable levies as required by the jurisdictions of the settling parties.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code identifying the type of tax applied (e.g., VAT, withholding tax, GST) as configured in the billing system (Amdocs Revenue Management) for inter-carrier settlement.']
  tax_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The applicable tax rate (as a percentage) used to calculate the tax_amount for this settlement line item. Determined by the tax jurisdiction and service type.']
  traffic_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the traffic flow direction and type for this settlement line. Distinguishes inbound roaming (MVNO/visited network charges), outbound roaming, transit traffic, and wholesale termination for revenue assurance and regulatory reporting.']
  traffic_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of traffic units (calls, messages, data bytes, or events) included in this settlement line item. The unit of measure is defined by traffic_volume_unit.']
  traffic_volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the traffic_volume field. Varies by service category: seconds/minutes for voice, messages for SMS/MMS, kilobytes/megabytes/gigabytes for data roaming. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seconds|minutes|messages|kilobytes|megabytes|gigabytes|events — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The IOT or wholesale rate applied per unit of traffic volume for this settlement line item. Expressed in the settlement currency. Commercially sensitive inter-carrier pricing.']
  visited_network_plmn varchar [note: 'The PLMN code (MCC+MNC) of the visited network where the roaming service was consumed. Used in TAP3 reconciliation and IOT tariff application for inbound/outbound roaming settlement.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail of a settlement invoice, breaking down charges by service type, traffic category, or TAP file reference. Captures line sequence, service category (voice termination, SMS, data roaming, transit), traffic volume, unit rate, gross charge, discount applied, tax amount, and net charge. Enables granular reconciliation of inter-carrier settlement invoices.'
}

Table interconnect.settlement_dispute {
  settlement_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the settlement dispute record in the interconnect domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  assigned_analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to people.employee. Business justification: Interconnect disputes are manually investigated by revenue assurance analysts who validate TAP records, compare against IOT tariffs, and negotiate with carrier partners. Tracking the assigned analyst ']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bilateral roaming or interconnect agreement under which the disputed settlement invoice or TAP file was generated.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the domestic or international carrier (counterparty) against whom the dispute is raised. Aligns with the PARTY_REFERENCE canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Disputes are managed by specific operational teams (interconnect operations, revenue assurance). Cost allocation, resource planning, and operational efficiency tracking require knowing which cost cent']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IOT tariff table entry that Telecommunication believes should have been applied to the disputed charges. Central to rate_error and iot_mismatch dispute types.']
