  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Interconnect settlement disputes may involve partners (MVNOs, wholesale customers) not just carriers. Business process: Partner dispute resolution workflow, escalation management, consolidated partner']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: Dispute records are created/updated via dispute management pipelines. This link supports audit trail for dispute lifecycle tracking, enables data quality monitoring of dispute processing, and tracks w']
  rap_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.rap_file. Business justification: Settlement dispute may originate from RAP file rejections. Adding FK to rap_file enables proper traceability from dispute back to rejection event, supporting dispute resolution workflow. The FK will b']
  regulatory_penalty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_penalty. Business justification: Settlement disputes that escalate or involve regulatory violations may result in penalties (late settlement, fraudulent billing). Tracks penalty linkage for dispute escalation management and financial']
  settlement_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inter-operator settlement invoice that is the subject of this dispute. Null when the dispute is raised directly against a TAP file rather than a consolidated invoice.']
  tap_file_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific TAP file whose charges are disputed. Null when the dispute is raised at the consolidated invoice level rather than at the individual TAP file level.']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.trouble_ticket. Business justification: Settlement disputes requiring technical investigation (TAP file discrepancies, rating errors, network issues) generate trouble tickets. Critical for dispute resolution workflow linking commercial and ']
  accepted_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of the original invoice or TAP file charge that Telecommunication accepts as valid and agrees to pay. The difference between the invoiced amount and the disputed amount. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category (adjustment component).']
  carrier_response_date date [note: 'Date on which the counterparty carrier formally responded to the dispute notification. Used to measure SLA compliance and track negotiation progress.']
  carrier_response_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of the carrier\'s formal response to the dispute, including their position, counter-evidence references, and proposed resolution.']
  credit_note_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the credit note issued by the carrier as part of the dispute resolution. Populated when resolution_type is credit_note or partial_credit.']
  dispute_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the dispute: financial (monetary discrepancy), technical (CDR or TAP file format/content issue), or contractual (disagreement on agreement terms or IOT applicability).']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the nature of the dispute, the specific charges contested, and the business justification for the claim. Authored by the settlement analyst at the time of dispute creation.']
  dispute_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether Telecommunication is the disputing party (outbound — Telecommunication raises dispute against carrier) or the receiving party (inbound — carrier raises dispute against Telecommunication).']
  dispute_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to this dispute at the time of creation. Used in all carrier-to-carrier correspondence and regulatory filings. Format: DSP-YYYY-NNNNNN. Aligns with BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER canonical category.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the settlement dispute. Drives workflow routing in the BSS settlement module. Aligns with LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_review|escalated|resolved|withdrawn|closed — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the root cause of the dispute. Rate error indicates an incorrect Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) was applied; volume_discrepancy indicates a mismatch in CDR counts; duplicate_record indicates a charge appears more than once in the TAP file; missing_record indicates expected CDRs are absent; currency_conversion indicates an FX rate dispute; iot_mismatch indicates the applied IOT does not match the agreed tariff table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rate_error|volume_discrepancy|duplicate_record|missing_record|currency_conversion|iot_mismatch — promote to reference product]']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross monetary amount being disputed by Telecommunication, expressed in the settlement currency. Represents the full value of the contested charges before any agreed adjustments. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.']
  disputed_amount_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the disputed amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency inter-operator settlement environments. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.']
  disputed_cdr_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual Call Detail Records (CDRs) included in the dispute. Relevant for volume_discrepancy, duplicate_record, and missing_record dispute types.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the dispute was escalated to senior management or a formal inter-carrier arbitration body. Null if the dispute has not been escalated.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for escalation, such as failure to respond within SLA, disagreement on IOT applicability, or significant financial exposure.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Document management or object storage reference (e.g., S3 path, ServiceNow attachment ID) pointing to supporting evidence submitted with the dispute, such as CDR extracts, TAP file dumps, or IOT tariff documentation.']
  interconnect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interconnect arrangement under which the disputed charges arose: bilateral roaming, transit traffic, peering, wholesale voice, or wholesale data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bilateral_roaming|transit|peering|wholesale_voice|wholesale_data — promote to reference product]']
  invoiced_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount as stated on the original settlement invoice or TAP file that is the subject of this dispute. Provides the baseline for calculating the disputed and accepted portions.']
  is_sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier has failed to respond or resolve the dispute within the contractually agreed SLA timeframe. True when current date exceeds sla_resolution_due_date and dispute_status is not resolved or closed.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispute record, including status transitions, amount updates, or evidence additions. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category.']
  nrtrde_file_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the NRTRDE file associated with the disputed roaming charges. Enables near-real-time reconciliation of roaming CDRs against TAP file charges as per GSMA NRTRDE specification.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the dispute based on financial exposure and SLA urgency. Drives workflow queue ordering in the BSS settlement module.']
  raised_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the formal dispute was raised by Telecommunication against the carrier. Aligns with BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP canonical category (date-precision sufficient for settlement SLA tracking).']
  raised_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the BSS settlement system. Provides sub-day precision for SLA measurement and audit trail. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any regulatory filing or complaint submitted to a governing body (e.g., FCC, Ofcom, BEREC) in connection with this dispute. Null if no regulatory filing has been made.']
  resolution_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final agreed monetary amount to be credited, refunded, or adjusted as the outcome of the dispute resolution. Populated only when dispute_status is resolved or closed. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category (net total).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the dispute was formally resolved and agreed between Telecommunication and the carrier. Null while dispute_status is open, under_review, or escalated.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the agreed resolution outcome, including the rationale for the resolution amount and any corrective actions agreed between the carriers.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the dispute was resolved: credit_note (carrier issues a credit), partial_credit (partial adjustment agreed), full_acceptance (Telecommunication accepts the original charge), withdrawal (dispute withdrawn by Telecommunication), arbitration_award (resolved via formal arbitration).']
  settlement_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing or settlement period covered by the disputed invoice or TAP file.']
  settlement_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing or settlement period covered by the disputed invoice or TAP file. Used to correlate disputes with specific traffic periods and IOT tariff versions.']
  sla_resolution_due_date date [note: 'Contractual deadline by which the dispute must be fully resolved as defined in the inter-operator agreement SLA terms. Used for SLA breach monitoring and escalation triggers.']
  sla_response_due_date date [note: 'Contractual deadline by which the carrier must respond to the dispute as defined in the roaming or interconnect agreement SLA terms.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this dispute record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  source_system_dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this dispute record in the originating operational system (e.g., Amdocs case number, Netcracker dispute ID). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the system of record.']
  tap_sequence_number varchar [note: 'TAP file sequence number as defined in the GSMA TAP3 specification, identifying the specific TAP batch within the carrier exchange. Used to trace disputed charges back to the originating TAP file.']
  traffic_type varchar [note: 'Type of telecommunications traffic associated with the disputed charges. Determines which IOT tariff schedule and settlement rules apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voice|data|sms|mms|roaming|transit|wholesale — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Record of a formal dispute raised against a settlement invoice or TAP file charge between Telecommunication and a carrier. Captures dispute reference, disputed invoice or TAP file, dispute type (rate error, volume discrepancy, duplicate record, missing record), disputed amount, supporting evidence reference, dispute status (open, under review, resolved, escalated), resolution amount, and resolution date.'
}

Table interconnect.rate {
  rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each interconnect rate record in the wholesale rate schedule. Primary key for the interconnect_rate data product.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bilateral carrier agreement or roaming agreement under which this rate applies. Links the rate to its governing commercial contract.']
  network_qos_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.qos_profile. Business justification: Interconnect rates often vary by QoS class (e.g., premium voice vs. best-effort data). Tariff design requires linking rate structures to network QoS profiles for accurate billing and service different']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: Rate table updates are loaded via ETL pipelines. This link supports audit requirements (which pipeline run loaded each rate version), enables rollback workflows when incorrect rates are loaded, and tr']
  superseded_by_rate_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the newer rate record that replaced this one when rate_status is \'superseded\'. Enables forward navigation of the rate version chain for audit and dispute resolution purposes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the commercial or regulatory manager who approved this rate for activation. Required for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance in wholesale rate management.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this rate was formally approved for activation. Part of the rate lifecycle audit trail required for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.']
  basis varchar [note: 'Structural basis on which the rate is calculated: flat (single rate regardless of volume), tiered (rate changes at volume thresholds), volume_discount (discounted rate applied retroactively above threshold), or distance_based (rate varies by geographic distance between interconnect points). Determines the rating algorithm applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this rate record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the interconnect rates are denominated and settlement is conducted (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Must align with the settlement currency defined in the governing carrier agreement.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the destination country for international termination or transit rates (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used for international settlement and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  destination_prefix varchar [note: 'E.164 dialled number prefix or number range (e.g., \'+1202\', \'+44207\') to which this rate applies. Enables prefix-based rate lookup during CDR rating and settlement. May represent a country code, area code, or specific number block.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Calendar date from which this interconnect rate becomes effective and applicable for CDR rating and settlement calculations. Inclusive start boundary of the rate validity period.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this interconnect rate ceases to be effective (exclusive end boundary). Null indicates an open-ended rate with no scheduled expiry. Used to enforce rate versioning and prevent application of expired rates.']
  interconnect_point_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific Point of Presence (POP) or interconnect gateway at which traffic is exchanged under this rate. Rates may vary by interconnect point due to transport cost differences. Aligns with network inventory records.']
  iot_tariff_class varchar [note: 'IOT tariff classification code as defined by GSMA inter-operator tariff tables, applicable when this rate is used for roaming wholesale settlement. Maps to the GSMA IOT rate category (e.g., IOT-A, IOT-B, IOT-C) for NRTRDE and TAP file processing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this rate record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance.']
  mcc varchar [note: 'ITU-assigned three-digit Mobile Country Code (MCC) identifying the country of the counterparty mobile network. Used in roaming rate lookup and NRTRDE/TAP file processing to match rates to visited network operator records.']
  minimum_call_duration_sec varchar [note: 'Minimum billable call duration in seconds applied during rating. Calls shorter than this threshold are billed at this minimum (e.g., 30-second minimum billing). Common in wholesale voice interconnect agreements to prevent micro-call gaming.']
  mnc varchar [note: 'Two or three-digit Mobile Network Code (MNC) identifying the specific mobile network operator within the MCC country. Combined with MCC forms the PLMN identifier used in roaming rate resolution and TAP file matching.']
  network_generation varchar [note: 'Mobile or fixed network generation standard to which this rate applies (e.g., 2G, 3G, 4G LTE, 5G NR, VoLTE, VoIP). Rates may differ by network generation due to technology-specific interconnect costs and regulatory frameworks.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for commercial or operational annotations related to this rate (e.g., special negotiation conditions, dispute history notes, regulatory waiver references). Not used in automated rating; for human reference only.']
  nrtrde_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate is subject to NRTRDE (Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange) reporting obligations. When true, CDRs rated under this rate must be transmitted to the counterparty within the NRTRDE transmission window for fraud prevention purposes.']
  off_peak_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Per-unit wholesale charge applied during off-peak hours (typically evenings and nights on weekdays). Lower than peak rate to reflect reduced network demand. Expressed in settlement currency per billing unit.']
  originating_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the traffic originates. Required for international transit and termination rates to distinguish bilateral from multilateral routing scenarios and apply correct regulatory frameworks.']
  peak_end_time timestamp [note: 'Local time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which the peak rate period ends each weekday. Defines the upper boundary of the peak window for CDR rating tier selection.']
  peak_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Per-unit wholesale charge applied during peak hours (typically business hours on weekdays). Expressed in the settlement currency per billing unit (e.g., per minute for voice, per event for SMS, per MB for data). This is the principal quantitative rate value for the resource.']
  peak_start_time timestamp [note: 'Local time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which the peak rate period begins each weekday. Used by the rating engine to determine which rate tier applies to a given CDR based on call start time.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this rate entry within the wholesale rate schedule. Used in TAP file references and inter-operator billing reconciliation.']
  rate_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the rate entry (e.g., \'US Domestic Voice Termination Peak\', \'EU Roam-Like-At-Home SMS\'). Used in rate schedule documentation and commercial negotiations.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate record: active (currently in force for rating), pending (approved but not yet effective), superseded (replaced by a newer rate version), expired (past effective period), or withdrawn (cancelled before activation). Controls whether the rate is used in CDR rating and settlement.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate by traffic direction and role: termination (delivering traffic to destination network), origination (collecting traffic from originating network), transit (passing traffic through an intermediate network), transit_international (cross-border transit), or access (local loop access charge). Governs regulatory framework applicability.']
  regulatory_cap_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Maximum rate permitted by the applicable regulatory framework for this service type and destination (e.g., EU RLAH wholesale voice cap of €0.032/min, Ofcom FAMR charge control ceiling). The actual rate must not exceed this cap. Used for compliance validation and regulatory reporting.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or citation of the specific regulatory instrument governing this rate (e.g., \'FCC Part 51 Access Charges\', \'Ofcom FAMR 2021 Charge Control\', \'EU Regulation 531/2012 RLAH Cap\', \'BEREC Roaming Guidelines\'). Enables compliance traceability and regulatory reporting.']
  rounding_increment_sec varchar [note: 'Duration increment in seconds to which call durations are rounded up for billing purposes (e.g., 6-second or 60-second rounding). Directly impacts settlement amounts and must align with the counterparty carrier agreement terms.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of telecommunications service to which this rate applies: voice (per-minute voice call termination/origination), sms (short message service per-event), data (per-kilobyte or per-megabyte data transit), mms (multimedia messaging), or premium_sms (premium-rate short message service). Determines the billing unit and regulatory regime.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this rate was ingested (e.g., \'Amdocs Revenue Management\', \'Netcracker OSS/BSS\', \'Manual Upload\'). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between source systems and the lakehouse.']
  tap_rate_category varchar [note: 'TAP file rate category code as defined in the GSMA Transferred Account Procedure (TAP3) specification. Maps this rate to the correct TAP record type for inter-operator roaming settlement file generation and validation.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\') used to interpret peak/off-peak/weekend time boundaries for this rate. Ensures correct tier selection across geographic regions and daylight saving transitions.']
  traffic_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this rate applies to inbound traffic (received from the counterparty carrier), outbound traffic (sent to the counterparty carrier), or bilateral (symmetric rate applicable in both directions). Critical for settlement calculation and IOT tariff alignment.']
  unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure against which the rate is applied for billing purposes: per_minute or per_second for voice, per_event for SMS/MMS, per_mb/per_kb/per_gb for data. Determines how CDR volumes are multiplied by the rate to produce settlement charges.']
  version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this rate record, incremented each time the rate is amended. Enables rate history tracking and ensures the correct version is applied to CDRs within a given effective period. Version 1 is the initial rate.']
  volume_tier_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum traffic volume (in the rate_unit) at which a tiered or volume-discount rate becomes applicable. Null for flat-rate structures. Used by the rating engine to select the correct rate tier based on cumulative monthly traffic volumes.']
  weekend_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Per-unit wholesale charge applied during weekend periods (Saturday and Sunday). May differ from both peak and off-peak weekday rates. Expressed in settlement currency per billing unit.']

  Note: 'Wholesale interconnect termination and origination rate schedule defining per-minute or per-event charges for domestic and international voice, SMS, and data traffic exchanged at interconnect points. Captures rate type (termination, origination, transit), destination prefix/number range, peak/off-peak/weekend rate tiers, effective/expiry dates, currency, regulatory reference (FCC access charges, Ofcom FAMR, EU Roam-Like-At-Home caps), and rate status. Distinct from IOT tariffs which cover roaming wholesale rates — this product covers domestic interconnect and international termination rates governed by regulatory frameworks.'
}

Table interconnect.mnp_transaction {
  mnp_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Mobile Number Portability (MNP) transaction record in the interconnect domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inter-operator carrier agreement governing the MNP transaction between Telecommunication and the counterparty operator.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise customers porting number blocks during office relocations or provider changes. Business need: tracking which enterprise account initiated bulk porting requests for service continuity, billi']
  donor_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: MNP transaction donor carrier is heavily denormalized with donor_tadig_code, donor_carrier_name, donor_mcc, donor_mnc. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier eliminates all redundancy and provides author']
  hlr_network_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Mobile number portability requires HLR/HSS updates to redirect routing. Operations must track which HLR/HSS network element processed each port for audit, troubleshooting, and regulatory compliance. R']
  mnp_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.mnp_compliance. Business justification: Each porting transaction generates a compliance record tracking SLA adherence, authorization verification, regulatory notification. Essential for porting compliance monitoring, regulatory reporting, a']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: MNP transactions are processed via porting pipelines. This link enables SLA monitoring (porting duration tracking), supports reprocessing of failed ports, and provides audit trail for regulatory compl']
  recipient_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Number portability transactions often involve partner networks (MVNOs) as recipient operators. Business process: MNP coordination with partner operators, regulatory compliance reporting, partner opera']
  authorisation_code varchar [note: 'The Port Authorisation Code (PAC) or equivalent subscriber-provided authorisation token used to validate the subscriber\'s consent for the port-out. Mandatory in many regulatory frameworks (e.g., Ofcom PAC regime, FCC subscriber verification). Null for port-in transactions where PAC is consumed by the gaining operator.']
  batch_file_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the batch file or TAP/NRTRDE file submission in which this MNP transaction was included for inter-operator exchange or regulatory reporting. Supports reconciliation with interconnect file processing workflows.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the MNP request was cancelled. Populated only when port_status = \'cancelled\'. Common reasons include subscriber withdrawal, duplicate request cancellation, or operator-initiated cancellation.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the number portability transfer was successfully completed and the MSISDN became active on the recipient network. Null if the port has not yet completed.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the MNP transaction is executed. Determines the applicable regulatory framework (FCC, Ofcom, BEREC NRA, etc.) and porting timeline obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this MNP transaction record was first created in the system of record (Oracle Communications Order and Service Management / Netcracker OSS). Audit trail field for data lineage.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the fraud prevention check performed on the MNP request. \'not_checked\' — fraud screening not yet performed; \'passed\' — no fraud indicators detected; \'flagged\' — suspicious activity detected, under review; \'blocked\' — request blocked due to confirmed fraud. Supports GSMA fraud prevention guidelines.']
  hlr_hss_update_status varchar [note: 'Status of the HLR (2G/3G) or HSS/UDM (4G/5G) subscriber profile update triggered by the MNP transaction. \'pending\' — update not yet sent; \'completed\' — subscriber profile successfully updated; \'failed\' — update failed, requiring manual intervention.']
  iccid varchar [note: 'The Integrated Circuit Card Identifier of the SIM card associated with the MSISDN at the time of the porting request. Captured to support SIM swap validation and fraud prevention during the MNP process.']
  imsi varchar [note: 'The International Mobile Subscriber Identity associated with the subscriber\'s SIM at the time of the porting request. Used for HLR/HSS deprovisioning on port-out and provisioning on port-in. Constitutes PII as it uniquely identifies a subscriber.']
  inter_operator_charge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an inter-operator charge (IOT) is applicable for this MNP transaction under the governing carrier agreement. True = charge applies and will be raised in settlement; False = no charge applicable (e.g., regulatory zero-rating or waiver).']
  msisdn varchar [note: 'The full E.164-formatted mobile phone number being ported. This is the subscriber\'s publicly dialable number and constitutes PII as it directly identifies an individual subscriber.']
  network_generation varchar [note: 'The radio access network generation (2G/3G/4G/5G) on which the subscriber\'s service is provisioned at the time of the MNP transaction. Relevant for HLR vs HSS/UDM routing update determination and network-specific porting procedures.']
  npac_order_reference varchar [note: 'The order identifier assigned by the Number Portability Administration Centre (NPAC) or equivalent national clearinghouse system (e.g., Syniverse, CODA) for this porting transaction. Used for cross-system reconciliation and regulatory audit.']
  number_range_owner varchar [note: 'The operator or entity that holds the ITU/national regulatory authority allocation of the number range (NXX or equivalent) containing the ported MSISDN. May differ from the donor operator in MVNO or wholesale scenarios.']
  order_management_ref varchar [note: 'The order reference number from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) or Netcracker OSS/BSS that orchestrated the service fulfillment actions associated with this MNP transaction (e.g., HLR/HSS update, service deprovisioning/provisioning).']
  port_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this MNP porting request by the Number Portability Administration Centre (NPAC) or equivalent national clearinghouse. Used for cross-operator tracking and regulatory audit.']
  port_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MNP transaction. \'pending\' — request received and awaiting validation; \'validated\' — subscriber eligibility confirmed; \'completed\' — number successfully ported; \'rejected\' — request denied (see rejection_reason); \'cancelled\' — request withdrawn before completion.']
  port_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the direction of the number portability event from Telecommunication\'s perspective. \'port_in\' means the subscriber is moving their number to Telecommunication from another operator (donor). \'port_out\' means the subscriber is leaving Telecommunication to another operator (recipient).']
  porting_duration_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'The elapsed time in hours between request_timestamp and completion_timestamp. Used for SLA compliance monitoring against regulatory maximum porting time mandates (e.g., FCC 1-business-day rule, Ofcom 1-working-day rule). Null if port not yet completed.']
  porting_window_end timestamp [note: 'The scheduled end date and time of the porting window. If the port is not completed within this window, the transaction may expire or require rescheduling per regulatory requirements.']
  porting_window_start timestamp [note: 'The scheduled start date and time of the porting window during which the number transfer is authorised to execute. Regulatory frameworks (e.g., FCC, Ofcom) mandate specific porting windows.']
  recipient_carrier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable commercial name of the recipient (gaining) operator. Complements recipient_tadig_code for reporting and business user readability.']
  recipient_tadig_code varchar [note: 'The GSMA TADIG code identifying the recipient (gaining) operator network to which the MSISDN is being ported. Complements donor_tadig_code to fully describe the inter-operator porting pair.']
  regulatory_deadline timestamp [note: 'The latest permissible timestamp by which the MNP transaction must be completed under applicable regulatory rules (e.g., FCC 1-business-day, Ofcom 1-working-day). Used for SLA breach detection and regulatory compliance monitoring.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'The official reference number assigned by the national regulatory authority (e.g., FCC, Ofcom, BEREC member NRA) or Number Portability Administration Centre (NPAC) for this porting transaction. Required for regulatory audit and compliance reporting.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code indicating why the MNP request was rejected. Common codes include: MSISDN_NOT_FOUND, OUTSTANDING_BALANCE, ACTIVE_CONTRACT, INVALID_ACCOUNT_HOLDER, DUPLICATE_REQUEST, REGULATORY_HOLD. Populated only when port_status = \'rejected\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MSISDN_NOT_FOUND|OUTSTANDING_BALANCE|ACTIVE_CONTRACT|INVALID_ACCOUNT_HOLDER|DUPLICATE_REQUEST|REGULATORY_HOLD|FRAUD_SUSPECTED|TECHNICAL_ERROR — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the rejection reason, providing additional context beyond the rejection_reason_code. Populated only when port_status = \'rejected\'. Used for dispute resolution and regulatory reporting.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the MNP porting request was formally submitted by the subscriber or the gaining operator. This is the principal business event timestamp marking the start of the porting lifecycle.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this MNP transaction has been retried following a failure or rejection. Used for operational monitoring, SLA tracking, and identifying systemic porting issues with specific operators.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'The routing number (RN) assigned to the ported MSISDN in the Number Portability database, used by all operators to correctly route calls and SMS to the ported number on the recipient network. Aligned with ITU-T E.164 routing principles.']
  routing_number_update_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the routing number in the Number Portability database and HLR/HSS/UDM has been successfully updated following port completion. \'pending\' — update queued; \'updated\' — successfully propagated; \'failed\' — update error requiring intervention; \'not_required\' — no routing change needed.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subscriber service type associated with the porting MSISDN. Determines applicable porting rules, timelines, and regulatory obligations. \'mvno\' indicates the subscriber is on a Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) arrangement.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The monetary amount charged or credited between Telecommunication and the counterparty operator for this MNP transaction under the applicable inter-operator tariff (IOT). Expressed in the settlement currency defined in the carrier agreement. Null if inter_operator_charge_applicable = False.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which the inter-operator settlement amount for this MNP transaction is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Must align with the settlement currency in the governing carrier agreement.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MNP transaction exceeded the regulatory or contractual maximum porting time SLA. True = SLA breached; False = within SLA. Used for regulatory compliance reporting and inter-operator dispute management.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this MNP transaction record. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability across BSS/OSS platforms.']
  subscriber_account_number varchar [note: 'The account number of the subscriber in Telecommunication\'s BSS (Amdocs Revenue Management / Salesforce CRM) associated with the MSISDN being ported. Used to link the MNP event to the subscriber\'s billing and service records.']
  subscriber_consent_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber\'s explicit consent for the number port has been verified and recorded, as required by regulatory frameworks. True = consent confirmed; False = consent not yet verified or verification failed. Critical for regulatory compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this MNP transaction record was most recently modified in the system of record. Used for change tracking, SLA monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing.']

  Note: 'Mobile Number Portability (MNP) transaction record capturing each port-in or port-out event for a subscriber number between Telecommunication and another operator. Captures MSISDN, donor network TADIG/carrier reference, recipient network reference, port type (port-in/port-out), request timestamp, porting window, completion timestamp, MNP status (pending, validated, completed, rejected, cancelled), rejection reason, regulatory reference number, and routing number update status. SSOT for inter-operator number portability execution within the interconnect domain.'
}

Table interconnect.poi {
  poi_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Point of Interconnect (POI) master record. Primary key for the interconnect_poi entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capital_project. Business justification: New POIs or POI upgrades are delivered through capital projects. Project accounting, budget tracking, and capex settlement to assets require this link. Real business process: tracking which capex proj']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier interconnect or roaming agreement governing the commercial and technical terms for this POI. Links to the interconnect.carrier_agreement SSOT.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or operator whose network connects at this POI. Links to the carrier/partner master record for the interconnecting operator.']
  enterprise_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.site. Business justification: Physical interconnection points located at enterprise customer sites (on-net buildings, colocation facilities). Business need: tracking which enterprise site hosts interconnect infrastructure for capa']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Points of interconnect involve capitalized infrastructure (routers, switches, transmission equipment). Asset management, depreciation calculation, and capex tracking require linking POIs to their unde']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) table applicable for traffic exchanged at this POI. IOT rates govern the financial settlement between operators for roaming and interconnect traffic.']
  location_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.location_site. Business justification: Points of interconnection are physical facilities requiring site access management, maintenance coordination, and capacity planning. Currently references data_center_name and city as text; structured ']
  mpls_tunnel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.mpls_tunnel. Business justification: Carrier interconnect traffic is often routed via MPLS tunnels for QoS and traffic engineering. Network operations must track which MPLS tunnels serve each POI for SLA enforcement, traffic steering, an']
  nms_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding network element in the Network Management System (NMS) — Nokia NetAct or Ericsson Network Manager — that monitors this POI. Enables correlation between the business POI record and real-time network telemetry.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the network engineer or technical account manager who owns and is accountable for this POI configuration. Used for escalation routing and change approval workflows.']
  peering_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.peering_arrangement. Business justification: Point of Interconnect (POI) represents physical/logical handoff points that may be associated with specific peering arrangements. Adding FK to peering_arrangement links infrastructure to commercial ag']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: POI configuration data is loaded via network inventory pipelines. This link supports change tracking for capacity planning, enables audit of POI configuration changes, and tracks which pipeline run up']
  redundant_poi_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier of the backup or redundant POI that provides failover capacity for this POI. Null if no redundant POI is designated. Used in network resilience and failover planning.']
  responsible_technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: Telecommunications POIs require assigned technicians for maintenance, fault resolution, capacity upgrades, and emergency response. Business process: POI maintenance scheduling, SLA compliance tracking']
  sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Service Level Agreement (SLA) defining performance commitments (availability, latency, packet loss) for this POI. Used for SLA monitoring and breach reporting.']
  spectrum_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.spectrum_license. Business justification: Points of interconnection operate on licensed spectrum; POI must reference the license authorizing its frequency use. Required for spectrum compliance verification, license utilization tracking, and i']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Interconnect points of interconnection physically terminate on transmission links. Operations teams need to track which transmission infrastructure serves each POI for capacity planning, fault isolati']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which this POI was first activated and began carrying live traffic. Used for asset age tracking, depreciation scheduling, and regulatory reporting of interconnect history.']
  bandwidth_mbps decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total provisioned bandwidth capacity at this POI in megabits per second (Mbps). Applicable primarily for IP/SIP, MPLS, and Ethernet interconnect types. Used for traffic engineering and QoS planning.']
  clli_code varchar [note: 'Telcordia/iconectiv Common Language Location Identifier (CLLI) code for the network element or building where this POI is located. Industry-standard 11-character code used in OSS provisioning and regulatory filings.']
  cmdb_ci_reference varchar [note: 'ServiceNow CMDB Configuration Item (CI) identifier for this POI. Enables linking the POI business record to ITSM incident, change, and problem management workflows.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country where the POI is physically located (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Determines applicable regulatory framework and IOT tariff jurisdiction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this POI record was first created in the system of record (Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  data_center_name varchar [note: 'Name of the data center or colocation facility hosting the POI for IP-based or MPLS interconnects (e.g., \'Equinix NY5\', \'CyrusOne Dallas\'). Applicable for IP/SIP and MPLS interconnect types.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which this POI was or is scheduled to be permanently decommissioned and removed from service. Null if the POI is still active. Used for lifecycle management and capacity reclamation planning.']
  exchange_name varchar [note: 'Name of the telephone exchange, central office, or network exchange facility where the POI is physically housed (e.g., \'Manhattan Central Office\', \'Chicago Exchange 5\'). Used in OSS topology mapping.']
  interconnect_type varchar [note: 'Technology type of the interconnect at this POI. TDM = Time Division Multiplexing (legacy PSTN); IP_SIP = IP-based Session Initiation Protocol; MPLS = Multiprotocol Label Switching; ETHERNET = Layer 2 Ethernet handoff; FIBER = direct fiber interconnect.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Traffic jurisdiction classification for this POI. INTERSTATE = traffic crossing state lines (FCC jurisdiction); INTRASTATE = within-state traffic (state PUC jurisdiction); INTERNATIONAL = cross-border traffic; LOCAL = local exchange traffic. Determines applicable tariff and regulatory regime.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical or logical audit of this POI, verifying that the as-built configuration matches the system of record. Required for regulatory compliance and CMDB accuracy.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Scheduled maintenance window for this POI expressed as a cron-style or free-text schedule (e.g., \'SUN 02:00-04:00 UTC\'). Used by NOC for planned outage coordination and change management.']
  network_generation varchar [note: 'Mobile or fixed network generation supported at this POI. Determines applicable technical standards, QoS parameters, and IOT tariff applicability. 5G NR = 5G New Radio; LTE = Long-Term Evolution.']
  noc_contact varchar [note: 'Contact details (name, phone, or email) for the Network Operations Center (NOC) team responsible for monitoring and managing this POI. Used for fault escalation and incident coordination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about this POI, including special configuration details, known limitations, or historical context not captured in structured fields.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the POI. ACTIVE = in-service and carrying traffic; PROVISIONING = being set up; SUSPENDED = temporarily halted; DECOMMISSIONED = permanently retired from service.']
  peering_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the peering arrangement at this POI. PUBLIC = via internet exchange point (IXP); PRIVATE = direct bilateral peering; BILATERAL = two-party agreement; MULTILATERAL = multi-party peering fabric. Applicable for IP peering POIs.']
  poi_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the POI by traffic scope. DOMESTIC = national carrier interconnect; INTERNATIONAL = cross-border carrier handoff; TRANSIT = pass-through traffic routing; PEERING = bilateral traffic exchange; WHOLESALE = MVNO or reseller interconnect.']
  poi_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the POI, used in regulatory filings, carrier agreements, and OSS/BSS systems. Unique across all interconnect points. Sourced from Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory.']
  poi_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the POI, typically referencing the exchange location or data center (e.g., \'NYC-Manhattan-Equinix-POI-01\'). Used in operational dashboards and carrier communications.']
  pop_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Point of Presence (POP) network node associated with this POI. A POP is the physical network access location; multiple POIs may share a POP. Used in network topology and routing tables.']
  protocol_standard varchar [note: 'Signaling or routing protocol standard used at this POI. SIP = Session Initiation Protocol; H.323 = ITU multimedia protocol; SS7 = Signaling System 7 (PSTN); ISUP = ISDN User Part; SIGTRAN = SS7 over IP; BGP = Border Gateway Protocol; LDP = Label Distribution Protocol (MPLS).']
  redundancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this POI has a redundant or backup interconnect path configured (True = redundant path exists; False = single path, no redundancy). Critical for SLA risk assessment and network resilience planning.']
  regulatory_designation varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the POI as determined by applicable telecommunications authority (e.g., FCC, Ofcom, BEREC). Determines whether interconnect rates are tariff-regulated or commercially negotiated. Critical for compliance reporting.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the telecommunications regulator (e.g., FCC, Ofcom) for this POI or its associated interconnect authorization. Used in regulatory filings and compliance audits.']
  total_capacity_circuits varchar [note: 'Total provisioned circuit capacity at this POI expressed as number of circuits (DS0 equivalents for TDM, or logical channels for IP). Principal quantitative measure of the POI resource. Used for capacity planning and SLA enforcement.']
  traffic_direction varchar [note: 'Permitted traffic flow direction at this POI. INBOUND = traffic received from the connected carrier; OUTBOUND = traffic sent to the connected carrier; BIDIRECTIONAL = traffic flows in both directions.']
  trunk_group_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the trunk group associated with this POI. A trunk group is a logical bundle of circuits or channels provisioned at this interconnect point for traffic routing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this POI record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and Silver Layer incremental load processing.']
  used_capacity_circuits varchar [note: 'Number of circuits currently in active use at this POI. Used to calculate utilization percentage for capacity management, congestion alerting, and expansion planning.']

  Note: 'Point of Interconnect (POI) master record defining the physical or logical handoff points between Telecommunication\'s network and a carrier\'s network. Captures POI ID, POI name, location (city, exchange, data center), interconnect type (TDM, IP/SIP, MPLS), connected carrier reference, trunk group reference, regulatory designation, capacity, and operational status. SSOT for interconnect topology.'
}

Table interconnect.rap_file {
  rap_file_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the RAP file record in the interconnect settlement system. Primary key for the rap_file data product.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bilateral roaming agreement under which the rejected TAP records were exchanged. Links the RAP file to the governing commercial and technical terms for the inter-operator relationship.']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IOT tariff table applicable to the rejected TAP records. Used to validate whether the rejection relates to tariff mismatches and to recalculate correct charges upon resubmission.']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: RAP file processing is an ETL workflow. This link enables monitoring of rejection file handling, supports resubmission workflows when TAP files are rejected, and provides audit trail for dispute resol']
  primary_rap_resubmission_tap_file_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrected TAP file resubmitted by the sender after processing this RAP rejection. Tracks the correction lifecycle and links the original rejection to its resolution.']
  sender_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: RAP file sender is denormalized via sender_tadig. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier provides authoritative carrier master reference. The FK will be populated at RAP file creation when sender is know']
  source_tap_file_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating TAP file whose records are being rejected in this RAP file. Links the RAP rejection back to the specific TAP file that contained the erroneous records.']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recipient operator formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of this RAP file. Required for GSMA bilateral exchange compliance and settlement audit.']
  clearing_house_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GSMA-accredited data clearing house (DCH) or billing clearing house (BCH) that processed or routed this RAP file between operators (e.g., SYNIVERSE, HROAMING). Required for multi-lateral settlement audit.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RAP file was generated by the sending operator\'s settlement system. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this RAP file record.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any formal inter-operator dispute raised in connection with this RAP file rejection. Links the RAP record to the dispute management workflow in the BSS settlement system.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the rejected charge amounts from the TAP file currency to the settlement currency at the time of RAP file generation. Required for multi-currency settlement reconciliation.']
  file_checksum varchar [note: 'Cryptographic checksum (e.g., MD5 or SHA-256 hash) of the RAP file used to verify file integrity during transmission and ingestion. Supports non-repudiation and data quality assurance.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Physical size of the RAP file in bytes as received from the clearing house or sender operator. Used for file integrity validation and transmission monitoring.']
  nrtrde_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rejected TAP records were originally subject to NRTRDE near-real-time roaming data exchange obligations. True if NRTRDE-governed records are included, enabling fraud and compliance monitoring.']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the settlement operations team regarding this RAP file, such as root-cause analysis findings, escalation context, or correction instructions communicated to the counterparty operator.']
  primary_rejection_code varchar [note: 'GSMA-standard primary rejection reason code identifying the main cause for TAP record rejection in this RAP file (e.g., \'12\' for missing mandatory field, \'15\' for invalid IMSI). Sourced from GSMA TD.32 rejection code table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product — GSMA TD.32 defines 50+ rejection codes]']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RAP file was fully processed and the rejected TAP records were actioned in the settlement system. Supports audit trail and SLA measurement.']
  rap_file_type varchar [note: 'GSMA-defined RAP file type code indicating the direction and nature of the rejection. HOMERJE = home operator rejecting visitor records; VISRJE = visited operator rejecting home records; ACK variants are acknowledgements; SYSERR = system error file. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HOMERJE|VISRJE|HOMERJE-ACK|VISRJE-ACK|SYSERR — promote to reference product]']
  rap_release_version varchar [note: 'GSMA RAP release version within the specification version, providing finer-grained versioning of the RAP file format used for this exchange.']
  rap_spec_version varchar [note: 'GSMA RAP specification version number used to encode this RAP file (e.g., \'04\' for RAP version 4). Governs the file structure and rejection code set applicable to this record.']
  rap_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RAP file within the inter-operator settlement workflow. Tracks progression from receipt through validation, processing, acknowledgement, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|validated|processed|acknowledged|disputed|closed — promote to reference product]']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RAP file was received and ingested by the recipient operator\'s settlement system. Used for SLA compliance tracking and NRTRDE window monitoring.']
  recipient_tadig varchar [note: 'GSMA TADIG code of the operator receiving this RAP file (the operator whose TAP records are being rejected). Identifies the counterparty in the bilateral exchange.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RAP file record was first inserted into the Silver layer data product. Audit trail field for data lineage and lakehouse ingestion tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RAP file record was last modified in the Silver layer data product. Supports change data capture and audit trail requirements.']
  rejected_record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of TAP CDR records rejected in this RAP file. Core settlement metric used to reconcile the volume of rejected usage records against the originating TAP file.']
  rejection_category varchar [note: 'GSMA-defined severity classification of the rejection: fatal (entire TAP file rejected), severe (significant errors requiring correction), or minor (non-critical errors). Determines the correction and resubmission workflow.']
  rejection_code_list varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of all GSMA rejection reason codes present across the rejected records in this RAP file. Provides a summary of all error types encountered, enabling root-cause analysis and TAP file correction prioritization.']
  resubmission_deadline_date date [note: 'Contractual or GSMA-mandated deadline by which the sender must resubmit corrected TAP records following this RAP rejection. Used for SLA monitoring and dispute escalation triggers.']
  resubmission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the TAP file resubmission process following this RAP rejection. Tracks whether the sender has corrected and resubmitted the rejected records, or if the rejection is under dispute or has been waived.']
  roaming_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rejected TAP records relate to inbound roamers (foreign subscribers using the home network) or outbound roamers (home subscribers using a foreign network). Determines the financial flow direction for settlement.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'GSMA-standard five-digit sequence number assigned to this RAP file by the sender, used to uniquely identify the RAP file within a bilateral exchange pair. Follows GSMA TD.32 sequencing rules.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of telecommunications service covered by the rejected TAP records (e.g., voice, data, SMS, MMS, supplementary services). Supports rejection analysis by service type for root-cause investigation.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the rejected charge amounts are denominated for inter-operator settlement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Must align with the bilateral roaming agreement currency.']
  settlement_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing/settlement period covered by the rejected TAP records in this RAP file. Used with settlement_period_start_date to bound the financial exposure of the rejection.']
  settlement_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing/settlement period covered by the rejected TAP records in this RAP file. Aligns the rejection to the correct inter-operator settlement cycle.']
  source_tap_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The GSMA sequence number of the originating TAP file being rejected, as carried within the RAP file header. Enables direct correlation to the TAP file without requiring a system join.']
  tap_release_version varchar [note: 'TAP release version of the originating TAP file being rejected, providing finer-grained versioning within the TAP specification version. Used for format validation during RAP processing.']
  tap_version varchar [note: 'TAP specification version of the originating TAP file being rejected (e.g., \'03\' for TAP3). Required to validate that the RAP file is processed against the correct TAP format rules.']
  total_rejected_charge decimal(18,6) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all TAP records rejected in this RAP file, expressed in the settlement currency. Represents the gross financial exposure of the rejection batch for inter-operator settlement reconciliation.']
  total_tax_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Aggregate tax component of the rejected TAP records included in this RAP file. Required for accurate financial settlement reconciliation and tax reporting between operators.']
  transfer_cutoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'GSMA-mandated cutoff timestamp by which the RAP file must be transmitted to the recipient operator. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and GSMA bilateral agreement enforcement.']

  Note: 'Return of Accounting Procedure (RAP) file record representing a formal rejection of TAP records returned by the recipient operator to the sender. Captures RAP file ID, originating TAP file reference, sender TADIG, recipient TADIG, RAP file type (GSMA standard), rejected record count, total rejected value, rejection reason codes, RAP status, and resubmission reference. Manages the TAP rejection and correction lifecycle.'
}

Table workforce.technician {
  technician_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the field or technical workforce personnel. Primary key for the technician master record.']
  analytical_model_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_model_registry. Business justification: Technician attrition prediction, skill gap forecasting, and productivity models are registered analytical assets. Telecommunications workforce planning uses ML models to predict technician churn risk ']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.contractor. Business justification: Technicians can be direct employees or contractor-provided workforce. The employment_type and workforce_classification fields indicate this distinction exists. Contractor-provided technicians need FK ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Technicians are assigned to cost centers for payroll allocation, overhead distribution, and workforce cost reporting - standard HR-finance integration for labor cost tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Human Resources system employee identifier. External business identifier used for payroll, benefits, and HR processes.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Technician performance metrics (first-time fix rate, MTTR, customer satisfaction score, safety compliance) are enterprise KPIs. Telecommunications workforce management requires linking individual tech']
  primary_corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Dedicated technician assignment model for strategic enterprise accounts (Fortune 500, government, wholesale partners). Telcos assign senior techs to major accounts for relationship continuity, account']
  segment_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.segment_definition. Business justification: Workforce segmentation (high performers, flight risk, skill-based cohorts, union vs non-union) drives retention programs and development initiatives. Telecommunications HR analytics segments technicia']
  supervisor_technician_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the direct supervisor or field manager responsible for this technician\'s performance management and work assignment.']
  attendance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of scheduled work days attended. Used for reliability assessment and workforce planning.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent background check or security clearance verification. Required for access to customer premises and sensitive network infrastructure.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate status of required industry certifications (e.g., fiber optic, electrical safety, tower climbing). Drives dispatch eligibility and compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the technician master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  current_performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent overall performance evaluation rating from the latest review cycle. Used for promotion decisions and workforce optimization.']
  customer_satisfaction_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average customer satisfaction rating (0-100 scale) from post-service surveys. Key indicator of service quality and technician soft skills.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that provided this technician data: Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management (WFM), SAP S/4HANA Human Capital Management (HCM), manual entry, or integration feed.']
  drug_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent drug screening test. Required for safety-sensitive positions and compliance with Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations for commercial drivers.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated emergency contact person for field safety incidents and urgent notifications.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the designated emergency contact for field safety incidents and urgent notifications.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment lifecycle status indicating availability for work assignment and dispatch.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship: full-time employee, part-time employee, contractor, Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) partner technician, or temporary worker.']
  first_time_fix_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of work orders resolved on the first visit without requiring return trips. Key performance indicator (KPI) for technician efficiency and customer satisfaction.']
  home_depot_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the technician\'s assigned home depot or service center for reporting, equipment storage, and route optimization.']
  improvement_plan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technician is currently on a formal performance improvement plan. Used for HR compliance and management escalation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in the technician master record. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review. Used to schedule upcoming reviews and ensure compliance with review frequency policies.']
  mean_time_to_repair decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average time in minutes to complete repair work orders. Critical KPI for workforce productivity and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal performance review. Drives manager notifications and review preparation workflows.']
  safety_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage score (0-100) representing adherence to safety protocols, personal protective equipment (PPE) usage, and incident-free work history. Critical for field workforce risk management.']
  service_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic service zone or territory assignment for dispatch optimization and workload balancing.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Overall technical proficiency level used for work order assignment complexity matching and career progression tracking.']
  sla_compliance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of work orders completed within committed SLA timeframes. Critical for customer satisfaction and penalty avoidance.']
  tools_kit_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the standard tools and equipment kit assigned to the technician. Used for inventory tracking and maintenance scheduling.']
  training_hours_ytd decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total training and professional development hours completed in the current calendar year. Used for compliance reporting and skill development tracking.']
  union_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technician is a member of a labor union. Affects scheduling rules, overtime policies, and grievance procedures.']
  vehicle_assigned varchar [note: 'Identifier of the company vehicle or fleet unit assigned to this technician for field operations and equipment transport.']
  work_orders_per_day decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of work orders completed per working day. Productivity metric used for capacity planning and workload balancing.']
  workforce_classification varchar [note: 'Technical specialization area: Radio Access Network (RAN), Fiber to the Home (FTTH), Gigabit Passive Optical Network (GPON), Customer Premises Equipment (CPE), fiber splicing, transport network, core network, or hybrid multi-skill. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ran|ftth|gpon|cpe|splicing|transport|core|hybrid — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for field and technical workforce personnel with periodic performance evaluation history. Identity and employment: technician ID, name, employment status, home depot/zone assignment, employment type (full-time, contractor, MVNO partner), contact details, hire/termination dates, workforce classification (RAN, FTTH, GPON, CPE, splicing). Performance history: quarterly/annual review records with KPIs (first-time fix rate, MTTR, work orders/day, SLA compliance, CSAT, safety compliance, attendance), review period, reviewer (supervisor), overall rating, individual KPI scores, development goals, acknowledgement status, and improvement plan references. SSOT for individual workforce identity and performance within the workforce domain. Sourced from Granite Telecommunications WFM and SAP S/4HANA HCM.'
}

Table workforce.skill {
  skill_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the technical skill in the workforce skill taxonomy. Primary key.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external certification authority or vendor (e.g., Ericsson, Nokia, Fiber Optic Association, OSHA) if certification_required_flag is true.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the required certification (e.g., Ericsson Certified RAN Expert, Nokia NetAct Administrator, FOA CFOT).']
  certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal external certification is required to hold this skill (e.g., vendor certification for Ericsson RAN, Nokia NetAct, safety certifications).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the workforce administrator who created this skill record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this skill record was first created in the Granite WFM skills module.']
  dispatch_priority_weight varchar [note: 'Numeric weight used in Granite WFM dispatch algorithm to prioritize technicians with this skill for matching work orders (higher weight = higher priority).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this skill was deprecated or made obsolete. Null if skill is still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this skill became active in the workforce skill taxonomy and available for assignment.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment or system this skill applies to (e.g., RAN Base Station, OLT, ONT, BNG, BRAS, IMS Core, HSS, CPE Router).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the workforce administrator who last modified this skill record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this skill record was last updated in the Granite WFM skills module.']
  prerequisite_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of skill codes that must be held before this skill can be acquired (e.g., basic safety certification required before advanced RAN work).']
  proficiency_level_competent_definition varchar [note: 'Definition of competent proficiency level for this skill: ability to perform tasks independently with standard supervision and quality expectations.']
  proficiency_level_expert_definition varchar [note: 'Definition of expert proficiency level for this skill: advanced capabilities, ability to handle complex scenarios, mentor others, and perform quality assurance.']
  proficiency_level_trainee_definition varchar [note: 'Definition of trainee proficiency level for this skill: expected capabilities, supervision requirements, and task limitations.']
  recertification_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required recertification events if recertification_required_flag is true.']
  recertification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether periodic recertification or reassessment is mandatory to maintain this skill.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this skill is subject to regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., FCC licensing, OSHA certification, state/local permits).']
  replacement_skill_code varchar [note: 'Skill code of the replacement skill if this skill has been deprecated or obsoleted (e.g., LTE skill replaced by 5G NR skill).']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this skill involves safety-critical work (e.g., tower climbing, high-voltage work, confined space entry) requiring additional safety protocols.']
  skill_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the skill domain: RAN (Radio Access Network), Transport (MPLS, SD-WAN), Access (FTTH, GPON, DSLAM), Core (IMS, HSS, UDM), CPE (Customer Premises Equipment), Safety, Compliance, or Other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RAN|Transport|Access|Core|CPE|Safety|Compliance|Other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  skill_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the skill, used in Granite WFM dispatch matching and work order assignment (e.g., FIBER-SPLICE, 5G-RAN-COMM, GPON-OLT-CFG).']
  skill_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the skill, including technical scope, typical tasks, and application context (e.g., \'Ability to perform single-mode and multi-mode fiber splicing, OTDR testing, and loss certification for trunk and distribution fiber networks\').']
  skill_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the technical skill (e.g., Fiber Splicing, 5G NR RAN Commissioning, GPON OLT Configuration, VoLTE IMS Troubleshooting).']
  skill_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the skill in the taxonomy: Active (in use), Deprecated (being phased out), Obsolete (no longer used), Planned (future skill).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where this skill record originated (e.g., Granite WFM, HR System, Training Management System).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this skill record in the source system (e.g., Granite WFM skill ID).']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification within the skill category (e.g., Fiber Optics, 5G NR, LTE, VoLTE, IPTV, Broadband Gateway).']
  technology_domain varchar [note: 'Specific technology or standard the skill applies to (e.g., 5G NR, LTE, VoLTE, GPON, FTTH, MPLS, SD-WAN, IMS, NFV, SDN).']
  training_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated number of training hours required to achieve competent proficiency level for this skill.']
  validity_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months this skill remains valid before recertification or reassessment is required. Null if skill does not expire.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the equipment or software vendor if vendor_specific_flag is true (e.g., Ericsson, Nokia, Huawei, Cisco, Juniper).']
  vendor_specific_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this skill is specific to a particular equipment vendor (e.g., Ericsson RAN, Nokia NetAct, Huawei Transport) versus vendor-agnostic.']
  work_order_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of work order types this skill is applicable to (e.g., Installation, Maintenance, Repair, Commissioning, Decommissioning, Troubleshooting, Upgrade).']

  Note: 'SSOT for the technical skill taxonomy and individual technician skill proficiency holdings. Catalog section: skill code, name, category (RAN, transport, access, CPE, safety), proficiency level definitions (trainee/competent/expert), prerequisite skills, and validity period — e.g., fiber splicing, 5G NR RAN commissioning, GPON/OLT configuration, VoLTE IMS troubleshooting. Technician-skill section: which skills each technician holds, current proficiency level, assessment date, assessor, expiry date, recertification requirements, and active status. Enables skill-based dispatch matching in Granite WFM — ensures only appropriately skilled technicians are assigned to specialized work orders (e.g., only fiber-splicing-expert for trunk splice jobs). Sourced from Granite WFM skills module.'
}

Table workforce.workforce_certification {
  workforce_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the workforce certification record. Primary key.']
  certification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certification type from the certification catalog. Links to the certification master record defining the qualification type.']
  learning_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the training activity or course completion that resulted in this certification. Links to the training activity record. Null if certification was obtained externally or through prior experience.']
  mandating_regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Specific workforce certifications are mandated by regulatory obligations (FCC tower climbing safety certification, state electrical licenses for fiber splicing, OSHA confined space entry certification']
  skill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.skill. Business justification: Certifications validate specific technical skills. Each certification holding (workforce_certification record) validates proficiency in a specific skill from the skill taxonomy. The technology_domain ']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the technician who holds this certification. Links to the workforce technician master record.']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the scanned certificate document, digital badge, or credential stored in the document management system. Used for audit and verification purposes.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number or credential identifier issued by the certifying authority. This is the externally-known identifier for this specific certification instance.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'Level or grade of the certification indicating proficiency or seniority. Examples include Basic, Intermediate, Advanced, Expert, Level 1, Level 2, Journeyman, Master, or vendor-specific tier designations.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification holding. Active indicates the certification is valid and in good standing; expired indicates the validity period has lapsed; suspended indicates temporary hold; pending_renewal indicates renewal process initiated; revoked indicates certification withdrawn by authority; in_progress indicates certification process underway but not yet completed.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the certification is currently in compliance with all regulatory and company requirements. True indicates compliant; False indicates non-compliant (expired, suspended, or missing required renewals).']
