  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user or automated process that last modified this distribution window record. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this distribution window record was last modified or updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Audit trail for record changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or business context related to this distribution window. Used for operational notes, exception handling, or studio-specific requirements.']
  offline_download_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscribers are permitted to download content for offline viewing during this window (True) or if streaming-only access is enforced (False). Reflects studio download rights.']
  offline_viewing_period_hours varchar [note: 'The number of hours a downloaded asset remains playable offline before requiring re-authentication or license renewal. Nullable if offline downloads are not allowed.']
  parental_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental control PIN or age verification is required (True) to access content during this window, or if unrestricted access is allowed (False). Enforces regulatory and studio content protection requirements.']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms or channels where this window applies (e.g., \'Mobile App\', \'Web Portal\', \'Set-Top Box\', \'Smart TV\', \'OTT Platform Name\'). Defines the technical distribution channels covered by this rights window.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'The pricing tier or monetization model applicable during this window: premium (highest price point), standard (regular subscription), basic (entry-level), free (ad-supported or promotional), or promotional (limited-time offer).']
  purchase_price_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The per-transaction purchase price for electronic sell-through (EST) or permanent ownership models. Nullable for rental or subscription windows. Represents the amount charged for perpetual access to the content.']
  quality_profiles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of video quality profiles or resolutions available during this window (e.g., \'SD,HD,4K,HDR\'). Defines the streaming quality tiers supported by the CDN and licensed by the studio.']
  rental_price_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The per-transaction rental price for TVOD or PVOD windows. Nullable for subscription-based or ad-supported windows. Represents the amount charged to the customer for a single viewing period.']
  subtitle_languages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-2 three-letter language codes for subtitle tracks available during this window (e.g., \'eng,spa,fra,deu\'). Defines localization support for accessibility and international markets.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes defining the geographic territories where this rights window is valid (e.g., \'USA,CAN,MEX\' for North America). Defines the geographic distribution rights.']
  window_end_date date [note: 'The date when this distribution window expires and the content is no longer available under these terms. Nullable for perpetual or open-ended windows. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  window_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this distribution window (e.g., \'Premium Early Access\', \'Standard Subscription Window\', \'Free Tier Window\').']
  window_start_date date [note: 'The date when this distribution window becomes active and the content becomes available for exploitation under the defined terms. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  window_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the distribution window: draft (being configured), scheduled (future activation), active (currently in effect), expired (past end date), suspended (temporarily disabled), or cancelled (terminated before expiration).']
  window_type varchar [note: 'Type of distribution window defining the exploitation period: theatrical (cinema release), PVOD (Premium Video on Demand), TVOD (Transactional Video on Demand), SVOD (Subscription Video on Demand), AVOD (Advertising-based Video on Demand), FAST (Free Ad-Supported Streaming TV), or linear (traditional broadcast TV). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theatrical|pvod|tvod|svod|avod|fast|linear — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Defines the distribution windows and exploitation periods for content assets across different platforms and distribution channels (e.g., theatrical → PVOD → SVOD → AVOD → linear TV). Captures content asset reference, window type (theatrical, PVOD, SVOD, AVOD, FAST, linear), platform/channel scope, window start and end dates, exclusivity flag, territory scope, and pricing tier applicable during the window. Enables automated content availability management aligned with studio distribution agreements.'
}

Table content.ad_insertion_policy {
  ad_insertion_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ad insertion policy. Primary key.']
  ab_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_test. Business justification: Ad policy effectiveness (revenue per impression, viewer drop-off rate, frequency cap impact) is tested via A/B experiments to optimize ad load and maximize advertising revenue.']
  fallback_ad_insertion_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ad_insertion_policy (fallback_ad_insertion_policy_id)']
  policy_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to people.employee. Business justification: Ad insertion policies require business owners (advertising operations managers) to define rules, approve changes, coordinate with sales/legal teams, and ensure compliance with advertiser contracts. Cr']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Ad insertion policies drive advertising revenue streams attributed to specific profit centers (e.g., advertising sales division). Required for revenue recognition, CPM performance tracking, and profit']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Ad policies must comply with advertising regulations: COPPA restrictions on children\'s content, FCC political ad disclosure rules, EU AVMSD ad frequency caps. Links policy to regulatory obligation it ']
  ad_decision_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of ad decisioning architecture: server-side (ad stitching at origin), client-side (ad selection at player), or hybrid (combination of both).']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'Format of the advertisement to be inserted: pre-roll (before content), mid-roll (during content), post-roll (after content), overlay (on-screen graphic), companion (adjacent banner), or bumper (short non-skippable).']
  ad_pod_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of a single ad pod (group of consecutive ads) in seconds. Defines the length of each advertising break.']
  ad_server_integration_endpoint varchar [note: 'URL or API endpoint of the ad server system that provides ad decisioning and creative delivery for this policy.']
  advertiser_category_exclusions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of advertiser categories or industries that are prohibited from this ad insertion policy (e.g., alcohol, gambling, political). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alcohol|gambling|tobacco|pharmaceuticals|political|adult|firearms|cryptocurrency — promote to reference product]']
  applicable_channel_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of channel codes or identifiers to which this ad insertion policy applies. Defines the content distribution channels covered.']
  applicable_content_package_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content package codes to which this ad insertion policy applies. Defines the content offerings covered.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or username of the person who approved this ad insertion policy for production use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad insertion policy was approved and authorized for operational deployment.']
  audit_logging_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether detailed audit logs of ad insertion events and decisions are captured for compliance and analytics.']
  competitive_separation_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether competitive separation rules are enforced to prevent ads from competing brands appearing in the same pod.']
  competitive_separation_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum time separation in minutes required between advertisements from competing brands or product categories.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry compliance frameworks that this ad insertion policy adheres to (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, COPPA, FCC regulations).']
  content_rating_restrictions varchar [note: 'Content rating levels to which this ad insertion policy applies (e.g., G, PG, PG-13, R). Ensures age-appropriate advertising.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or username of the person who created this ad insertion policy record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad insertion policy record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values associated with this ad insertion policy.']
  device_type_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of device types to which this ad insertion policy applies (e.g., mobile, tablet, smart TV, set-top box, web browser).']
  dynamic_ad_insertion_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether dynamic ad insertion technology is enabled, allowing personalized ad targeting and real-time ad stitching.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this ad insertion policy expires or is scheduled to be retired. Null indicates open-ended policy.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this ad insertion policy becomes active and begins governing ad insertion operations.']
  frequency_cap_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the same advertisement can be shown to a single viewer within the frequency cap window. Prevents ad fatigue.']
  frequency_cap_window_hours varchar [note: 'Time window in hours over which the frequency cap count is enforced. Defines the period for measuring ad repetition.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or territories where this ad insertion policy is applicable, specified as comma-separated ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region identifiers.']
  insertion_trigger_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism that triggers ad insertion: time-based (fixed intervals), scene-based (content analysis), chapter-based (content markers), cue-tone (audio signal), SCTE-35 (industry standard signaling), or manual override.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or username of the person who last modified this ad insertion policy record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad insertion policy record was last updated or modified.']
  max_ad_load_per_hour_minutes decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum total duration of advertising content permitted per hour of programming, measured in minutes. Ensures compliance with regulatory limits and viewer experience standards.']
  max_ad_pod_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable duration for an ad pod in seconds. Prevents excessively long ad breaks that degrade viewer experience.']
  min_ad_pod_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Minimum allowable duration for an ad pod in seconds. Ensures ad breaks meet minimum length requirements for monetization.']
  minimum_cpm_floor decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum cost per thousand impressions (CPM) floor price for ad inventory under this policy. Ensures minimum monetization threshold.']
  ott_platform_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of OTT platform codes where this ad insertion policy is enforced. Defines which streaming platforms use this policy.']
  personalization_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether ad personalization based on viewer demographics, behavior, and preferences is enabled for this policy.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the ad insertion policy, used for external reference and integration.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the ad insertion policy, including business rules and intended use cases.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the ad insertion policy for display and reporting purposes.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ad insertion policy indicating whether it is operational, under development, or retired.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Type of ad insertion policy: AVOD (Advertising Video on Demand), FAST (Free Ad-Supported Streaming TV), linear (live linear channels), VOD (Video on Demand), or live streaming.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of this policy when multiple policies could apply to the same content. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  programmatic_buying_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether programmatic ad buying through real-time bidding (RTB) exchanges is enabled for this policy.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of advertising revenue shared with content providers or partners under this policy. Business-confidential commercial term.']
  subscriber_tier_eligibility varchar [note: 'Subscriber tier levels to which this ad insertion policy applies: free (ad-supported free tier), basic (entry-level paid), premium (ad-free or reduced ads), or all tiers.']
  trigger_rule_definition varchar [note: 'Detailed specification of the trigger rules including timing intervals, scene detection parameters, or cue point configurations that determine when ads are inserted.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this ad insertion policy, incremented with each modification to track policy evolution and change history.']

  Note: 'Defines advertising insertion rules and policies for AVOD, FAST, and ad-supported linear channels. Captures policy name, ad format (pre-roll, mid-roll, post-roll, overlay), insertion trigger rules (time-based, scene-based), maximum ad load per hour, ad pod duration limits, frequency capping rules, advertiser category exclusions, and applicable content/channel scope. Critical for monetizing free-tier and ad-supported content offerings increasingly central to telco OTT strategy.'
}

Table content.ott_subscription {
  ott_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this OTT platform subscription record. Primary key.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the corporate account subscribing to the OTT platform']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created this OTT subscription record.']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the OTT streaming platform being subscribed to by the corporate account']
  billing_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OTT platform subscription charges for this corporate account are integrated into the telco\'s unified billing system or billed separately by the OTT provider.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration or termination date of the OTT platform subscription for this corporate account. Used for renewal tracking and access termination.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the OTT platform subscription contract for this specific corporate account. May differ from the master partnership contract_start_date in ott_platform.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OTT subscription record was created in the system.']
  deep_link_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deep linking to specific content within the OTT platform is enabled for this corporate account, allowing direct navigation to shows/movies from the telco\'s portal.']
  integration_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the OTT platform integration for this corporate account. Tracks whether the integration is live, being configured, temporarily suspended, or terminated.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this OTT subscription record.']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous streams allowed for this corporate account\'s subscription to the OTT platform. May be negotiated based on employee count or account tier.']
  monthly_subscription_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monthly recurring charge for this corporate account\'s subscription to the OTT platform. May be a negotiated enterprise rate different from consumer pricing.']
  provisioning_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which end-user accounts are provisioned on the OTT platform for employees of this corporate account. Determines whether provisioning is automated via API, requires manual setup, or allows self-service enrollment.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of subscription revenue allocated to the OTT platform provider for this specific corporate account. May differ from the master partnership revenue_share_percentage if account-specific commercial terms were negotiated.']
  sso_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Single Sign-On authentication is enabled for this corporate account\'s access to the OTT platform, allowing employees to authenticate using corporate credentials.']
  subscription_tier varchar [note: 'Service tier or plan level subscribed by this corporate account for the OTT platform, determining feature access, video quality, and concurrent stream limits.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between ott_platform and corporate_account. It captures the commercial and technical terms under which a corporate account subscribes to or integrates an OTT streaming platform as part of their enterprise service bundle. Each record links one ott_platform to one corporate_account with attributes that exist only in the context of this subscription relationship, including integration configuration, SSO settings, revenue sharing terms, and contract lifecycle dates.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications B2B operations, corporate accounts subscribe to multiple OTT streaming platforms as part of bundled enterprise service offerings (e.g., a hotel chain offering Netflix, Disney+, and HBO Max to guests; a corporate office providing streaming benefits to employees). Each OTT platform serves multiple corporate accounts with account-specific commercial terms, integration configurations, and contract dates. The business actively manages these subscriptions as operational entities with distinct SSO settings, provisioning modes, revenue share terms, and lifecycle states per account-platform pair.'
}

Table content.lineup_channel_membership {
  lineup_channel_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this channel membership record. Primary key.']
  content_channel_lineup_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the channel lineup package that contains this channel']
  iptv_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the IPTV channel included in this lineup']
  added_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel was added to this lineup in the system. Supports audit trail of lineup composition changes.']
  channel_position_number varchar [note: 'The logical channel number or position where this channel appears within this specific lineup. Same channel may have different positions in different lineups.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this channel is removed from this lineup. Null indicates ongoing membership. Supports historical tracking of lineup composition changes.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this channel becomes available within this lineup. Supports time-bound channel inclusion for licensing or promotional periods.']
  featured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel is featured or highlighted within this specific lineup for marketing or promotional purposes.']
  is_hd_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the HD variant of this channel is included in this lineup. Same channel may be SD in one lineup and HD in another.']
  is_premium_tier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel is included as part of a premium tier within this lineup, potentially requiring additional subscription fees.']
  licensing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding licensing terms, restrictions, or special conditions for this channel\'s inclusion in this specific lineup.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this channel membership: active (available to subscribers), pending (scheduled for activation), suspended (temporarily unavailable), removed (permanently removed from lineup).']
  removed_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel was removed from this lineup. Null for active memberships.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sort order for this channel within the lineup\'s EPG interface. Controls presentation sequence independent of channel number.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the membership relationship between channel lineups and IPTV channels. It captures the inclusion of specific channels within curated lineup packages, including positioning, timing, and presentation attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship. Each record links one channel lineup to one IPTV channel with attributes defining how and when that channel appears in that specific lineup.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications IPTV operations, channel lineups are curated packages (Basic, Premium, Sports) that bundle multiple channels, and individual channels appear across multiple lineup tiers. The business actively manages lineup composition as a core content packaging function, tracking which channels belong to which lineups with specific positioning, timing, and presentation attributes. This is an operational relationship that content managers create, modify, and track for licensing compliance, billing accuracy, and subscriber entitlement.'
}

Table content.package_platform_inclusion {
  package_platform_inclusion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this package-platform inclusion record. Primary key.']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the OTT streaming platform included in this content package']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the content package that includes this OTT platform']
  auto_provision_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this platform should be automatically provisioned when a subscriber activates the package (true) or requires manual activation (false). Supports opt-in vs. auto-include business rules.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this package-platform inclusion record was created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this OTT platform is removed from the content package. Null for currently active inclusions. Supports historical tracking for partner billing reconciliation.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this OTT platform becomes included in the content package and available for provisioning to subscribers. Supports time-based package composition changes.']
  inclusion_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this platform inclusion in the package. active=currently included and provisionable, pending=scheduled for future inclusion, suspended=temporarily unavailable, removed=historically included but no longer active.']
  is_primary_platform boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OTT platform is the primary or featured platform in the package for marketing and UI presentation purposes. Typically one primary platform per package.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this inclusion record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inclusion record was last modified.']
  marketing_label varchar [note: 'Package-specific marketing label or promotional text for this platform inclusion (e.g., \'Included at no extra cost\', \'Premium add-on\', \'3 months free\'). Used in package marketing materials.']
  ott_platforms_included varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of OTT streaming platform names or identifiers included in this package (e.g., \'Netflix, Disney+, HBO Max\'). Empty if no OTT platforms are bundled. [Moved from content_package: This comma-separated list attribute in content_package is a denormalized representation of the M:N relationship. The actual platform inclusions should be managed as individual records in the package_platform_inclusion association, which provides proper normalization, supports relationship-specific attributes (priority, dates, revenue share), and enables historical tracking. The ott_platforms_included attribute becomes a derived/cached field or should be removed entirely in favor of querying the association table.]']
  platform_display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric order for displaying this platform in the package description, marketing materials, and subscriber portal. Lower numbers display first.']
  provisioning_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority order for provisioning this platform when a subscriber activates the package. Lower numbers provision first. Used for dependency management (e.g., SSO platform must provision before dependent platforms).']
  revenue_share_override decimal(5,2) [note: 'Package-specific revenue share percentage override for this platform inclusion. Overrides the default revenue_share_percentage from the ott_platform master when this platform is sold as part of this specific package. Null means use default from platform record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this inclusion record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the commercial bundling relationship between content packages and OTT streaming platforms in telecommunications service offerings. It captures which OTT platforms are included in each content package, along with provisioning rules, display ordering, revenue share terms, and effective dates. Each record links one content package to one OTT platform with attributes that exist only in the context of this bundling relationship. This is a core operational entity in telecommunications OTT aggregation and resale business models.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications OTT aggregation business models, content packages routinely bundle multiple OTT streaming platforms (e.g., \'Entertainment Plus\' includes Netflix + Disney+ + HBO Max), and each OTT platform appears in multiple packages across different tiers and markets. The telco actively manages these bundling relationships with specific provisioning rules, display ordering, package-specific revenue share terms, and time-based inclusion windows for promotional campaigns and partnership lifecycle management.'
}

Table content.package_lineup_inclusion {
  package_lineup_inclusion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this package-lineup inclusion relationship. Primary key.']
  content_channel_lineup_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the channel lineup included in this content package']
  content_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the content package that includes this lineup']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the content package that includes this channel lineup']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inclusion relationship was created in the system']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this lineup is no longer included in this package. Null for current inclusions. Enables temporal tracking of package composition.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this lineup becomes included in this package. Supports historical tracking of package composition changes for billing accuracy and product lifecycle management.']
  inclusion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this lineup inclusion: active (currently included), pending (scheduled for future inclusion), suspended (temporarily removed), discontinued (permanently removed). Supports package composition lifecycle management.']
  is_primary_lineup boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary or featured channel lineup for this package (true) or a secondary/add-on lineup (false). Used for marketing presentation and default provisioning logic.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inclusion relationship was last modified']
  lineup_display_priority varchar [note: 'Marketing display priority for this lineup within the package composition. Used to determine prominence in package descriptions and subscriber-facing interfaces.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sequence order for this lineup within the package when presenting package contents to subscribers or in marketing materials. Lower numbers appear first.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inclusion relationship between content packages and channel lineups in the telecommunications content catalog. It captures which IPTV channel lineups are bundled into which commercial content packages, along with the business rules governing that inclusion. Each record links one content package to one channel lineup with attributes that define the inclusion terms, display priority, effective dates, and primary lineup designation for package composition and subscriber entitlement resolution.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications content packaging, content packages are commercial offerings that bundle multiple content types including VOD catalogs, OTT platforms, and channel lineups. A single channel lineup (e.g., Premium Sports) can be included in multiple commercial packages (e.g., Entertainment Plus Bundle, Ultimate Package, Sports Tier), and each package typically includes multiple lineups to create tiered offerings. The business actively manages these inclusion relationships with specific effective dates, primary lineup designations, and display priorities for marketing and provisioning purposes.'
}

Table content.package_territory_availability {
  package_territory_availability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this package-territory availability configuration. Primary key.']
  content_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to content.content_package. Identifies which content package this availability record applies to.']
  modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or system user who last modified this configuration record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the content package being offered in this territory']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to location.service_territory. Identifies the geographic territory where this package is available.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this package in this territory: available (actively sold), suspended (temporarily unavailable), discontinued (permanently removed), pending_launch (configured but not yet active).']
  content_licensing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding territory-specific content licensing restrictions, blackout periods, or special conditions that apply to this package in this territory. Used to document regulatory or contractual constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this package-territory availability configuration was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this content package is no longer available for new sales in this specific service territory. Null indicates ongoing availability. Used for market-specific discontinuation or licensing expiry.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this content package becomes available for sale in this specific service territory. Territory-specific launch dates may differ from the package\'s global effective_start_date to support phased rollouts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this package-territory availability configuration was last modified.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Territory-specific pricing tier code for this content package, overriding or refining the package\'s base pricing_tier_reference. Allows for regional pricing variations based on market conditions, competitive landscape, or purchasing power.']
  promotional_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the specific promotional campaign governing this package-territory offering. Null if promotional_flag is False. Links to marketing campaign management systems.']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this package-territory combination is currently part of a promotional campaign (True) or offered at standard terms (False). Used to identify market-specific promotional activity.']
  sales_channel_restrictions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sales channels through which this package may be sold in this territory (e.g., online, retail, telesales, partner). Null indicates no restrictions. Used for territory-specific distribution strategies.']
  territory_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the territory_monthly_charge. May differ from the package\'s base currency_code for international territories.']
  territory_monthly_charge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monthly recurring charge for this content package in this specific territory, potentially overriding the package\'s base monthly_recurring_charge to reflect regional pricing. Null indicates use of base package pricing.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the commercial availability configuration between content packages and service territories. It captures territory-specific launch dates, pricing tiers, promotional campaigns, and availability windows that exist only in the context of offering a specific content package within a specific geographic territory. Each record links one content_package to one service_territory with market-specific commercial parameters managed by product management teams.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications content distribution, content packages are commercially offered across multiple service territories with territory-specific launch dates, pricing tiers, and promotional campaigns. Product management teams actively configure and manage these package-territory availability records as operational business entities, tracking which packages are available in which markets with market-specific commercial parameters. This is a true operational M:N relationship where both directions are many (one package is offered in many territories, one territory offers many packages) and the relationship itself carries meaningful business data.'
}

Table content.license_territory {
  license_territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for license_territory']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.license. Business justification: A license can be valid for multiple geographic territories; each territory record must reference the license it belongs to. This creates a 1:N relationship (many territory rows per license).']
  parent_license_territory_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on license_territory (parent_license_territory_id)']
  area_sq_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Physical size of the territory in square kilometres.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the territory.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the territory becomes valid for licensing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the territory ceases to be valid for licensing; null if open‑ended.']
  licensing_rights_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the content licensing rights applicable to this territory.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority governing licensing in this territory.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory record.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the territory (e.g., ISO‑3166‑2 or internal market code).']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the geographic or market territory.']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory for licensing purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the territory record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for license_territory. Referenced by license_territory_id.'
}

Table content.programme {
  programme_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for programme']
  drm_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Digital Rights Management policy applied to the programme.']
  parent_programme_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on programme (parent_programme_id)']
  series_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the series to which this programme belongs, if applicable.']
  audio_languages varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of audio language tracks available.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability state of the programme for streaming.']
  content_format varchar [note: 'Delivery format of the programme.']
  content_provider varchar [note: 'Entity that supplies or owns the rights to the programme.']
  content_quality varchar [note: 'Technical quality level of the programme.']
  content_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Agency that assigned the parental rating.']
  content_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the programme metadata.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the programme as a movie, series, episode, etc.']
  content_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the content (e.g., director\'s cut).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the programme record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price.']
  description varchar [note: 'Full textual description or synopsis of the programme content.']
  director varchar [note: 'Name of the director of the programme.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total runtime of the programme in minutes.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the programme becomes active in the catalogue.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the programme is no longer offered (nullable).']
  episode_number varchar [note: 'Episode number within the season.']
  episode_title varchar [note: 'Title of the specific episode.']
  external_programme_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partners or content providers.']
  genre varchar [note: 'Primary genre classification of the programme.']
  hd_flag boolean [note: 'True if the programme is available in high definition.']
  is_ad_supported boolean [note: 'True if the programme contains ad‑supported content.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the programme is exclusive to a single platform.']
  is_original boolean [note: 'True if the programme is original content produced by the company.']
  is_premier boolean [note: 'True if the programme is a premiere release.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language of the programme audio track.']
  licensing_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) where the programme is licensed, using ISO‑3 country codes.']
  licensing_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the licensing agreement.']
  parental_rating varchar [note: 'Content rating indicating suitability for different age groups.']
  price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary price for pay‑per‑view or purchase of the programme.']
  production_company varchar [note: 'Company that produced the programme.']
  rating_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Aggregated audience rating score (e.g., IMDb).']
  release_date date [note: 'Date the programme was first made publicly available.']
  rights_end_date date [note: 'Date when the licensing rights for the programme expire.']
  rights_start_date date [note: 'Date when the licensing rights for the programme become effective.']
  season_number varchar [note: 'Season number within the series.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the programme record.']
  subtitle_languages varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of subtitle language tracks available.']
  synopsis varchar [note: 'Brief narrative summary of the programme.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the programme as displayed to customers.']
  ultra_hd_flag boolean [note: 'True if the programme is available in ultra‑high definition (4K/8K).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the programme record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for programme. Referenced by programme_id.'
}

Table content.series {
  series_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for series']
  parent_series_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on series (parent_series_id)']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current catalog availability of the series for subscribers.']
  average_episode_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Mean length of an episode in minutes, used for scheduling and bandwidth planning.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Technical classification of how the series is delivered to the subscriber.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the country where the series was produced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the series record was first created in the data lake.']
  description varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the series for marketing and discovery.']
  drm_policy varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Digital Rights Management policy applied to the series.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the series concluded or was removed from the catalog.']
  genre varchar [note: 'Broad category describing the thematic content of the series (e.g., drama, comedy, documentary).']
  is_adult_content boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the series contains adult‑only material.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the series is exclusive to the provider\'s platform.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language of the series audio track.']
  licensing_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current licensing agreement for the series expires.']
  licensing_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current licensing agreement for the series becomes effective.']
  production_company varchar [note: 'Legal entity that produced the series.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Standardized audience rating indicating suitability for different age groups.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the first episode of the series was first made available.']
  rights_owner varchar [note: 'Entity that holds the distribution and licensing rights for the series.']
  series_code varchar [note: 'External catalog or reference code used to uniquely identify the series across systems.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the series within the content catalog.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary audience segment for which the series is intended.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the series as presented to customers.']
  total_episodes varchar [note: 'Total number of episodes released for the series.']
  total_seasons varchar [note: 'Total number of seasons produced for the series.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the series record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for series. Referenced by series_id.'
}

Table assurance.alarm_event {
  alarm_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network alarm event record. Primary key for the alarm event entity.']
  affected_svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.svc_instance. Business justification: Network element alarms affect specific service instances. Required for proactive customer notification, service impact assessment, and correlating network events to customer experience degradation.']
  alarm_triggering_kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Alarms are triggered by KPI threshold breaches. NOC operations link alarm types to KPI definitions for root cause analysis, threshold tuning, and alarm correlation. Real-world assurance systems track ']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Alarms affecting specific services require product context for customer impact assessment, SLA breach determination, and targeted customer notification. Enables product-specific alarm correlation and ']
  e911_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.e911_compliance. Business justification: Alarms on E911 infrastructure (ALI/ANI database failures, PSAP connectivity loss, location server outages) trigger compliance reviews and regulatory reporting. Critical for public safety compliance an']
  iptv_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.iptv_channel. Business justification: Network monitoring alarms for multicast stream failures, CDN node errors, or encoder malfunctions must reference the affected channel to enable rapid identification and restoration of broadcast servic']
  kpi_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alarm suppression rule applied to this alarm event, if any. Used for managing alarm storms and maintenance windows.']
  managed_object_cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: When managed_object_class=\'CPE\', correlates customer equipment alarms (offline, low signal, reboot loops) with specific device serial numbers for remote troubleshooting, warranty claims, and proactive']
  managed_object_ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: When managed_object_class=\'ONT\', links fiber access alarms (LOS, dying gasp, high temperature) to specific ONT serial numbers for technician dispatch optimization and vendor quality analysis.']
  network_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network element (RAN, transport, core, GPON, FTTH node) that raised this alarm.']
  root_cause_alarm_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the root cause alarm event when this alarm is determined to be a secondary or correlated alarm. Null if this is the root cause alarm.']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ServiceNow ITSM trouble ticket created for this alarm event. Null if no ticket has been generated yet.']
  acknowledged_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the NOC or SOC operator who acknowledged this alarm event.']
  actual_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes from alarm raised to alarm cleared. Used for MTTR KPI calculation and SLA compliance reporting.']
  additional_text varchar [note: 'Free-form additional information or context provided by the network element or OSS system about the alarm event.']
  affected_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of customer service potentially impacted by this alarm event (e.g., mobile_voice, mobile_data, broadband, iptv, enterprise_vpn).']
  alarm_acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alarm event was acknowledged by NOC or SOC personnel, indicating human review has occurred.']
  alarm_cleared_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alarm event was cleared, indicating the fault condition has been resolved. Null if alarm is still active.']
  alarm_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the alarm event, typically assigned by the source OSS system (Nokia NetAct or Ericsson Network Manager).']
  alarm_raised_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alarm event was first raised by the network element. Represents the real-world fault occurrence time.']
  alarm_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the alarm event indicating business impact. Critical indicates service-affecting; major indicates significant degradation; minor indicates non-service-affecting; warning indicates potential issue; indeterminate indicates unknown impact.']
  alarm_state varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alarm event. Raised indicates active fault; cleared indicates fault resolved; acknowledged indicates NOC has reviewed; suppressed indicates alarm is temporarily hidden from active view.']
  alarm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alarm event by fault category. Communications indicates connectivity failure; quality_of_service indicates performance degradation; processing_error indicates software fault; equipment indicates hardware failure; environmental indicates physical conditions (temperature, power).']
  cleared_by_system varchar [note: 'System or process that cleared the alarm event (e.g., network element auto-recovery, manual intervention, OSS automation).']
  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier used to correlate related alarm events across multiple network elements or systems. Enables root cause analysis and alarm storm management.']
  customer_impact_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customers or subscribers potentially affected by this alarm event. Used for prioritization and SLA breach assessment.']
  first_occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the very first occurrence of this alarm condition. Used for tracking recurring or flapping alarms.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where the affected network element is located. Used for regional NOC routing and reporting.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this alarm event record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer from the source OSS system.']
  last_occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent occurrence of this alarm condition. Updated when alarm repeats or flaps.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this alarm event record was last modified in the lakehouse, reflecting state changes or attribute updates.']
  managed_object_class varchar [note: 'Object-oriented class name of the network resource that raised the alarm (e.g., eNodeB, BNG, OLT, Router, Optical_Amplifier).']
  managed_object_instance varchar [note: 'Unique instance identifier of the managed object within the network element that raised the alarm. Typically a distinguished name or hierarchical path.']
  mttr_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target resolution time in minutes for this alarm event based on severity and service impact, derived from SLA commitments.']
  network_domain varchar [note: 'High-level network domain classification of the affected network element. RAN indicates Radio Access Network; transport indicates backhaul/fronthaul; core indicates packet core; access indicates GPON/FTTH/DSLAM; edge indicates edge computing nodes.']
  occurrence_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this alarm condition has occurred since first detection. Used to identify flapping or intermittent faults.']
  oss_source_system varchar [note: 'Source OSS system that generated and published this alarm event (Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, or other network management system).']
  probable_cause varchar [note: 'Standardized description of the likely root cause of the alarm event as determined by the network element or OSS system.']
  proposed_repair_action varchar [note: 'Recommended corrective action or troubleshooting steps for resolving the alarm condition, typically provided by the OSS system or knowledge base.']
  service_affecting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this alarm event is currently affecting customer service delivery. True indicates active service impact; false indicates no customer impact.']
  site_identifier varchar [note: 'Physical site or facility identifier where the affected network element is deployed (e.g., cell site ID, central office code, data center ID).']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this alarm event has resulted in an SLA breach based on resolution time or service availability thresholds.']
  specific_problem varchar [note: 'Vendor-specific or system-specific problem code providing detailed fault identification beyond the standardized probable cause.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Specific technology or protocol of the affected network element (e.g., LTE, 5G NR, GPON, MPLS, IP, Optical).']

  Note: 'SSOT for network alarm events ingested from Nokia NetAct and Ericsson Network Manager. Captures real-time fault signals raised by network elements including RAN, transport, core, and GPON/FTTH nodes. Stores alarm severity, alarm type, affected network element, alarm state (raised/cleared/acknowledged), first occurrence timestamp, last occurrence timestamp, probable cause, specific problem code, managed object class, managed object instance, OSS source system, and correlation identifier. Feeds trouble ticket creation workflows in ServiceNow ITSM.'
}

Table assurance.trouble_ticket {
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trouble ticket record. Primary key.']
  affected_cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Tracks which customer premise equipment (modem, router, STB) is subject of trouble ticket for warranty claim processing, replacement decisions, and device failure pattern analysis across manufacturers']
  affected_network_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific network element (RAN node, router, switch, OLT, BNG) where the fault originated or was detected.']
  affected_ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Links fiber access trouble tickets to specific ONT assets for technician dispatch with correct replacement inventory, optical power troubleshooting, and ONT failure rate tracking by vendor/model.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service case in CRM system if the ticket was initiated from a customer complaint or inquiry.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Trouble tickets track service issues by product type; enables root cause analysis, warranty validation, product quality reporting, and defect trend analysis by catalog item. Essential for product life']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Trouble tickets incur resolution costs (labor, vendor charges) allocated to specific operational cost centers for IT chargeback and NOC budget tracking. Standard telecom operations practice for depart']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account affected by the service fault. Nullable for network-level faults not tied to a specific customer.']
  data_breach_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_breach_incident. Business justification: Tickets track breach remediation tasks: containment, system patching, customer notification, forensic analysis. Incident response teams manage breach response via ticketing systems. Critical for breac']
  device_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.device_registration. Business justification: Device-specific faults generate trouble tickets. Links ticket to registered device for warranty claims, device quality analysis, and manufacturer defect tracking. Real business process: device fault m']
  dq_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.dq_issue. Business justification: Data quality issues in billing/provisioning systems generate customer-impacting trouble tickets. Real-world operations track which DQ issues spawned tickets for root cause remediation and systemic dat']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual engineer or technician currently assigned to work on the ticket.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Trouble tickets for billing disputes or service issues reference specific invoices. Essential for customer care dispute resolution, linking service quality complaints to billing records for credit pro']
  iptv_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.iptv_channel. Business justification: Channel-specific service issues (no signal, pixelation, audio sync problems) require direct channel reference for NOC teams to isolate multicast stream, encoder, or CDN distribution problems affecting']
  lawful_intercept_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.lawful_intercept_order. Business justification: Tickets created to provision/terminate lawful intercepts per court orders. NOC/provisioning teams track which tickets fulfill which intercept orders for legal compliance, chain of custody, and audit r']
  mvno_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: MVNO-specific trouble tickets (HLR provisioning failures, APN issues, IMSI range problems) require MVNO profile linkage for proper escalation, SLA breach attribution, and wholesale billing adjustments']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.noc_incident. Business justification: Trouble tickets can be linked to NOC incidents when they are part of a larger operational incident. This is N:1 (many tickets can be associated with one NOC incident). The FK column does not currently']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.ott_platform. Business justification: OTT platform integration issues (SSO failures, catalog sync errors, API authentication problems) require platform-specific troubleshooting by integration teams to resolve partner platform connectivity']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: Billing disputes and service quality issues require price plan context for resolution; enables correlation of service issues with pricing tier, validates SLA entitlements, and supports tier-based esca']
  privacy_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_request. Business justification: Tickets fulfill GDPR/CCPA data subject requests (access, deletion, portability). Privacy teams create tickets for IT/operations to execute requests within regulatory SLA deadlines. Essential for priva']
  problem_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.problem_record. Business justification: Trouble tickets can be linked to problem records for root cause investigation tracking. This is N:1 (many tickets can be associated with one problem record). The FK column does not currently exist and']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Service-affecting tickets impact specific profit centers (enterprise, consumer, wholesale). Required for P&L impact analysis, service quality cost tracking, and profit center performance reporting in ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Tickets track compliance-driven work: lawful intercept provisioning, privacy request fulfillment, regulatory reporting tasks. Operations teams need to link tickets to specific regulatory obligations f']
  roaming_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_roaming_agreement. Business justification: Roaming trouble tickets (TAP file discrepancies, fraud threshold breaches, steering policy violations) must reference the roaming agreement for dispute resolution, rate verification, and NRTRDE thresh']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_profile. Business justification: Trouble tickets must reference applicable SLA commitments to determine response time targets, escalation procedures, and breach risk. Required for SLA compliance reporting and penalty avoidance.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific subscriber experiencing the service issue. Nullable for infrastructure faults.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service instance experiencing the fault or degradation.']
  vod_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.vod_asset. Business justification: Streaming quality complaints (buffering, playback failures, encoding defects) require linking tickets to specific VOD assets for content engineering teams to diagnose and resolve asset-specific delive']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field service work order dispatched to resolve the ticket if on-site intervention was required.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assigned team or engineer acknowledged receipt of the ticket and began investigation.']
  affected_customer_count varchar [note: 'Estimated or actual number of customer accounts impacted by the fault. Used for prioritization and impact assessment.']
  affected_service_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the service affected by the fault (e.g., Broadband Internet, VoLTE, IPTV, Enterprise MPLS).']
  affected_subscriber_count varchar [note: 'Estimated or actual number of individual subscribers impacted by the fault. Used for SLA penalty calculation and customer communication.']
  alarm_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary alarm event that triggered the creation of this trouble ticket. Links ticket to network monitoring system alarm.']
  assigned_group varchar [note: 'Name of the NOC, SOC, or field operations team assigned to investigate and resolve the ticket (e.g., NOC Tier 2, RAN Operations, Field Dispatch).']
  assignee_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the engineer or technician assigned to the ticket.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ticket was formally closed after verification and customer confirmation.']
  correlated_alarm_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related alarm IDs that were correlated and consolidated into this single trouble ticket. Supports alarm correlation and root cause analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this trouble ticket record was first inserted into the data warehouse. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fault was first reported by a customer (reactive) or proactively detected by network monitoring systems.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ticket was escalated to a higher support tier or management due to complexity, SLA risk, or customer impact.']
  escalation_tier varchar [note: 'The support tier or level to which the ticket was escalated (Tier 1 = frontline NOC, Tier 2 = specialist engineers, Tier 3 = vendor support, Management = executive escalation).']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ticket was escalated to a higher tier or management.']
  impact_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or customer scope of the fault impact (single customer, multiple customers, entire site, regional, network-wide outage).']
  mttr_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated duration in minutes from ticket opened to ticket resolved. Key performance indicator for fault management efficiency and SLA compliance.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trouble ticket was first created in the fault management system. Represents the start of the fault lifecycle.']
  outage_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the service was unavailable or degraded. Used for SLA credit calculation and availability reporting.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the ticket based on impact and urgency. Determines response and resolution time targets per SLA.']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the resolution action taken. Used for trend analysis and knowledge base categorization.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the actions taken to resolve the fault (e.g., equipment replacement, configuration change, software patch, service restart).']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fault was resolved and service restored. Used to calculate MTTR.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the underlying cause of the fault identified during investigation.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the root cause identified during fault analysis. Supports knowledge management and problem prevention.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Technical severity level indicating the scope and impact of the fault. Sev1 = service down, Sev2 = major degradation, Sev3 = minor impact, Sev4 = cosmetic/informational.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ticket resolution exceeded the SLA target time for the assigned priority level. Critical for revenue assurance and customer satisfaction tracking.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'The contractual or policy-defined target resolution time in minutes for this ticket priority level.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this trouble ticket record (ServiceNow ITSM, Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, Netcracker OSS, or Manual entry).']
  ticket_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the trouble ticket indicating the domain of the fault (network infrastructure, service delivery, customer equipment, security incident, performance degradation).']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique ticket number assigned by the fault management system. Used for customer and NOC communication.']
  ticket_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of trouble ticket aligned with ITIL/ITSM classification: incident (unplanned interruption), problem (root cause investigation), change request, service request, or planned maintenance.']
  trouble_ticket_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trouble ticket in the fault management workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|assigned|in_progress|pending|resolved|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this trouble ticket record was last modified in the data warehouse. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.']
  vendor_ticket_number varchar [note: 'External ticket number assigned by equipment vendor or third-party support provider if the issue was escalated to vendor technical support.']

  Note: 'SSOT for fault management trouble tickets sourced from ServiceNow ITSM. Represents a formal record of a network or service fault requiring investigation and resolution. Captures ticket number, category (network/service/customer), priority, severity, affected service, affected network element, assigned NOC/SOC group, assignee, open timestamp, acknowledged timestamp, resolved timestamp, closed timestamp, root cause category, resolution description, MTTR (minutes), escalation flag, escalation tier, SLA breach flag, and source alarm correlation IDs. Aligns with eTOM process 1.1.1 Fault Management.'
}

Table assurance.noc_incident {
  noc_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NOC/SOC incident record. Primary key.']
  affected_pop_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.pop_facility. Business justification: Tracks which network facility is incident command center or primary affected site for major incident management, war room coordination, and facility-level incident pattern analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Major incidents incur significant response costs (war room, overtime, vendor escalation) allocated to NOC or network operations cost centers. Essential for incident cost tracking and operational budge']
  data_breach_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_breach_incident. Business justification: Major security breaches managed as NOC incidents with war rooms, executive involvement, cross-functional response. Links incident command structure to breach record for regulatory reporting, post-inci']
  iptv_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.iptv_channel. Business justification: Major broadcast incidents (nationwide channel blackouts, sports event transmission failures, breaking news coverage disruptions) require channel identification for war room coordination, regulatory no']
  primary_network_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Major incidents must identify the primary affected network element for vendor escalation, spare parts logistics, and post-incident review. Core NOC operational requirement for incident command and res']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Major outages trigger regulatory reporting obligations (FCC Part 4 outage reporting, EU NIS Directive incident notification). NOC teams must identify which incidents require regulatory filings and tra']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident record was formally closed after verification, documentation, and post-incident review completion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this incident record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proactive customer communication was sent regarding the incident and its impact on services.']
  declared_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was officially declared by NOC/SOC operations, marking the start of the incident lifecycle.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was first detected by monitoring systems or reported by field personnel, which may precede the declared timestamp.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation tier of the incident within the support hierarchy (L1=frontline NOC, L2=engineering, L3=vendor/specialist, executive=C-level notification).']
  estimated_revenue_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the incident in terms of lost revenue, service credits, or penalties, used for revenue assurance and executive reporting.']
  impacted_customer_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customers affected by the incident, used for impact assessment and prioritization.']
  impacted_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic region, market, or service area affected by the incident (e.g., Northeast Region, Metro Atlanta, California Central Coast).']
  impacted_network_elements varchar [note: 'List of network infrastructure components affected by the incident (e.g., RAN sites, core routers, OLT devices, BNG gateways).']
  impacted_services_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of service types or service identifiers affected by the incident (e.g., LTE Data, VoLTE, IPTV, Broadband, 5G NR).']
  incident_category varchar [note: 'Operational categorization of the incident based on service impact pattern (complete outage, performance degradation, security compromise, capacity constraint, or maintenance-related event).']
  incident_commander varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the NOC/SOC personnel assigned as the incident commander responsible for coordinating response and resolution activities.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique incident identifier used for tracking and communication across NOC/SOC teams and stakeholders.']
  incident_priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the incident based on severity and business impact, determining resource allocation and response time targets (P1=highest, P4=lowest).']
  incident_source varchar [note: 'Origin or detection method through which the incident was first identified (automated monitoring, customer complaint, field observation, proactive analysis, or vendor alert).']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the incident within the NOC/SOC operational workflow.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incident based on the nature of the operational event (e.g., network outage, DDoS attack, power failure, fiber cut, environmental hazard, hardware failure).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system name that last modified this incident record, used for audit and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this incident record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  mttr_minutes varchar [note: 'Calculated time in minutes from incident declaration to resolution, used as a key performance indicator for operational efficiency.']
  noc_shift varchar [note: 'NOC operational shift during which the incident was declared, used for staffing analysis and shift performance metrics.']
  post_incident_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal post-incident review (PIR) is required or has been completed for this incident, typically mandated for high-severity or customer-impacting events.']
  preventive_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of preventive measures or corrective actions implemented to prevent recurrence of similar incidents in the future.']
  rca_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the formal RCA document or post-incident review report stored in the document management system.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident meets thresholds requiring formal reporting to regulatory bodies such as FCC, BEREC, or national telecommunications authorities.']
  related_alarm_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of network alarm identifiers from Nokia NetAct, Ericsson NM, or other NMS/EMS systems that triggered or are associated with this incident.']
  related_trouble_ticket_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of trouble ticket identifiers associated with this incident, representing individual customer complaints or network alarms that contributed to incident declaration.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was resolved and normal service operations were restored.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the underlying root cause identified during post-incident analysis.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause identified through Root Cause Analysis (RCA), including technical details and contributing factors.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the incident, determining priority and escalation path. Critical incidents typically affect large customer populations or core network infrastructure.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident resulted in a breach of service level agreement commitments to customers or business units.']
  vendor_involved varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or equipment manufacturer involved in incident resolution (e.g., Nokia, Ericsson, Cisco, Juniper), if applicable.']
  vendor_ticket_reference varchar [note: 'External ticket or case number opened with the vendor for support or escalation related to this incident.']
  war_room_bridge_number varchar [note: 'Conference bridge phone number or virtual meeting link used for real-time incident coordination and communication among response teams.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system name that created this incident record, used for audit and accountability.']

  Note: 'NOC/SOC operational incident record representing a declared network or security incident managed by the Network Operations Center or Security Operations Center. Distinct from a trouble ticket in that it represents a broader operational event (e.g., major outage, DDoS attack, fiber cut) that may span multiple trouble tickets and alarms. Captures incident ID, incident type (network/security/power/environmental), severity level, declared timestamp, resolved timestamp, impacted geography, impacted customer count estimate, impacted services list, incident commander, war-room bridge details, post-incident review flag, and RCA document reference.'
}

Table assurance.performance_measurement {
  performance_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the performance measurement record. Primary key.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Network performance KPIs must be validated against product specifications (guaranteed bandwidth, latency SLAs); enables automated SLA compliance monitoring and product performance benchmarking against']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Performance measurements implement specific KPI definitions. This is the foundational link between network telemetry collection and KPI reporting. Every measurement is collected per a defined KPI spec']
  location_site_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical site or location where the network element resides. Links to the site inventory for geographic and facility context.']
  measurement_object_network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: When measurement_object_type=\'EQUIPMENT\', links KPI data (port utilization, CPU, temperature) to specific routers/switches for capacity planning, fault correlation, and predictive maintenance in netwo']
  measurement_object_olt_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.olt_asset. Business justification: When measurement_object_type=\'OLT\', monitors PON port utilization, ONT count per port, and uplink bandwidth for fiber access network capacity management and OLT expansion planning.']
  measurement_object_ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: When measurement_object_type=\'ONT\', tracks optical power levels, distance, and bandwidth utilization per ONT for GPON network quality assurance and proactive maintenance of fiber access infrastructure']
  monitoring_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Network performance monitoring infrastructure costs (probes, analytics platforms, OSS licenses) are allocated to network operations or assurance cost centers. Required for monitoring infrastructure co']
  spectrum_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.spectrum_license. Business justification: Performance measurements validate spectrum license compliance: utilization thresholds (build-out requirements), interference limits, coverage obligations. Regulators audit spectrum usage; measurements']
  aggregation_method varchar [note: 'Statistical method used to aggregate raw samples into the measurement value (e.g., average, sum, min, max, percentile, count). Essential for correct interpretation.']
  availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Computed availability percentage for the measurement object during the measurement interval. Derived from uptime and downtime measurements. Used for SLA compliance reporting.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Historical baseline or expected value for this KPI under normal operating conditions. Used for anomaly detection and deviation analysis.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement record was collected and ingested into the data platform from the source OSS/EMS/NMS system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric quality score (0-100) representing the reliability and completeness of the measurement data. Used for filtering and weighting in analytics.']
