  deviation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage deviation of the measurement value from the baseline value. Positive indicates above baseline; negative indicates below baseline. Used for trend analysis.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where the measured network element is deployed (e.g., North, South, East, West, or specific market names). Supports regional performance analysis.']
  measurement_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the measurement interval window. Defines the conclusion of the period over which the KPI was computed.']
  measurement_interval_type varchar [note: 'Time granularity at which the measurement was aggregated: 15-minute, 1-hour, 24-hour, or real-time. Aligns with standard OSS collection intervals.']
  measurement_object_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the network element being measured (cell, link, node, shelf, port, or other infrastructure component). Maps to the physical or logical resource in the network inventory.']
  measurement_object_type varchar [note: 'Type of network element being measured. Categorizes the infrastructure layer at which the KPI was collected.']
  measurement_reliability_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measurement is considered reliable for reporting and analytics. False indicates the measurement should be excluded from SLA calculations.']
  measurement_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which the measurement was collected (e.g., Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, generic NMS/EMS/OSS).']
  measurement_source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or instance ID of the source system. Enables traceability back to the originating OSS/EMS/NMS instance.']
  measurement_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the measurement interval window. Defines the beginning of the period over which the KPI was computed.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Quality status of the measurement record. Indicates whether the data is valid, suspect (questionable quality), invalid (failed validation), or incomplete (missing samples).']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measurement value (e.g., dBm, Mbps, ms, percent, count, ratio). Essential for correct interpretation and threshold comparison.']
  measurement_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Raw numeric value of the performance measurement as collected from the network element. Unit of measure is specified in measurement_unit.']
  network_domain varchar [note: 'High-level network domain where the measurement was collected: RAN (Radio Access Network), transport, core, edge, or access network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the measurement record. May include context about anomalies, maintenance windows, or data quality issues.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of raw samples or data points aggregated to produce the measurement value. Indicates statistical confidence and granularity of the measurement.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Technology standard or protocol of the measured network element (e.g., 5G NR, LTE, FTTH, GPON, MPLS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 5G NR|LTE|UMTS|GSM|FTTH|GPON|MPLS|Ethernet — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  threshold_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measurement value breached the configured threshold. True indicates a breach; False indicates compliance. Used for SLA tracking and alarm correlation.']
  threshold_breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the threshold breach if threshold_breach_flag is True. Aligns with alarm severity classifications in fault management systems.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Configured threshold value for this KPI. Used to determine whether the measurement breaches acceptable performance limits. Null if no threshold is defined.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the equipment vendor for the measured network element (e.g., Nokia, Ericsson, Huawei, Cisco). Used for vendor-specific performance analysis.']

  Note: 'SSOT for time-series performance measurement records collected at the network-element layer from Nokia NetAct and Ericsson Network Manager across RAN, transport, and core domains. Captures infrastructure-level KPIs: RSRP, SINR, cell throughput, packet loss, latency, link utilization, node availability, and capacity metrics. Each record captures measurement object (cell/link/node/shelf/port), KPI name, measurement interval (15-min/1-hour/24-hour), raw value, threshold value, threshold breach flag, measurement source (OSS/EMS/NMS), collection timestamp, and source system. Serves as the unified network-element measurement store for availability computation, capacity planning, and threshold breach detection. Service-layer and bearer-level QoS measurements are owned by qos_measurement. Aligns with eTOM process 1.1.2 Performance Management.'
}

Table assurance.sla_contract {
  sla_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA contract record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_agreement. Business justification: SLA contracts are defined within partner agreements and must reference the governing agreement for compliance verification, penalty cap enforcement, and dispute resolution per contract terms.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: SLA contracts are defined per product offering; this is the master relationship between service level commitments and product catalog, enabling contract template management and product-specific SLA de']
  corporate_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.corporate_hierarchy. Business justification: Enterprise hierarchy nodes have specific SLA contracts for multi-site deployments. Essential for tiered service agreements, consolidated billing, and hierarchy-level SLA reporting. Real business proce']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Enterprise SLA contracts are negotiated with specific customer accounts. Essential for SLA breach billing, compliance reporting, and service level enforcement. Real business process: SLA management an']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to people.employee. Business justification: SLA contracts in telecommunications require designated employee ownership for contract governance, escalation handling, customer negotiations, renewal management, and breach resolution. The existing o']
  parent_sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent or master SLA contract when this SLA is a specialized or subsidiary agreement (e.g., service-specific SLA under an enterprise master agreement). Nullable for top-level SLAs.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: SLA contracts for MVNOs, roaming partners, and wholesale customers require partner attribution for SLA monitoring, breach tracking, and penalty calculation in partner settlement processes.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'The role or individual who has authority to approve, modify, or terminate this SLA contract (e.g., \'VP Network Operations\', \'Chief Technology Officer\', \'Regulatory Affairs Director\').']
  availability_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The committed service availability target expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.95% uptime). Used for SLA breach detection and penalty calculation.']
  breach_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic notification to customers or stakeholders is required when an SLA breach occurs (True) or not (False).']
  breach_threshold_count varchar [note: 'The number of allowable SLA breaches within the measurement window before penalties are triggered or escalation occurs. Provides tolerance for minor incidents.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA contract: draft (being defined), active (in force), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (past expiry date), terminated (cancelled before expiry), or under review (being renegotiated).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SLA contract record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The target customer segment for this SLA (e.g., \'Consumer\', \'Small Business\', \'Enterprise\', \'Government\', \'Wholesale Carrier\'). Used for segmented SLA reporting and analytics.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this SLA contract becomes active and enforceable. SLA breach tracking begins from this date.']
  escalation_procedure varchar [note: 'Description of the escalation process and contact hierarchy when SLA targets are breached or at risk. May reference external documentation or runbooks.']
  exclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions or events that are excluded from SLA calculations (e.g., \'Planned maintenance windows\', \'Force majeure events\', \'Customer-caused outages\', \'Third-party network failures\').']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this SLA contract expires or terminates. Nullable for evergreen or indefinite agreements.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic region or coverage area to which this SLA applies (e.g., \'National\', \'Regional\', \'Metro Area\', \'International\'). Critical for multi-region operators.']
  governing_regulatory_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority or standards body that mandates or oversees this SLA (e.g., \'FCC\', \'Ofcom\', \'BEREC\', \'ITU\'). Applicable for regulatory QoS compliance SLAs.']
  jitter_target_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable jitter (variation in packet delay) in milliseconds. Essential for voice and video quality assurance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SLA contract record was last updated. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  latency_target_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable network latency (round-trip time) in milliseconds. Applicable to real-time services such as VoLTE, VoIP, and gaming.']
  measurement_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or platform that provides the performance data for SLA measurement (e.g., \'Nokia NetAct\', \'Ericsson Network Manager\', \'ServiceNow ITSM\', \'Custom NMS\').']
  measurement_window varchar [note: 'The time period over which SLA performance is measured and evaluated (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual, rolling 30-day window).']
  mttr_target_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable mean time to repair (MTTR) for service faults, measured in hours. Critical for NOC/SOC incident management and penalty assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or operational notes related to this SLA contract. Used for non-structured information that does not fit other fields.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'The internal business unit or department responsible for managing and enforcing this SLA (e.g., \'Network Operations Center\', \'Customer Care\', \'Enterprise Services\', \'Wholesale Operations\').']
  packet_loss_target_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable packet loss rate expressed as a percentage. Critical for QoS monitoring in IP-based services.']
  penalty_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to calculate financial penalties or service credits when SLA targets are breached: fixed amount per incident, percentage of monthly recurring revenue (MRR), tiered based on severity, prorated by downtime duration, or service credit.']
  penalty_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum total penalty or credit amount that can be assessed within a single measurement window. Protects both provider and customer from unlimited liability.']
  penalty_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA contract includes financial penalty clauses for breach events (True) or is informational only (False).']
  regulatory_reference_code varchar [note: 'The specific regulation, directive, or standard code that this SLA implements (e.g., \'FCC 17-287\', \'Ofcom GC22\', \'ITU-T E.800\'). Used for compliance reporting and audit trails.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which SLA performance reports are generated and delivered to stakeholders (e.g., real-time dashboards, daily summaries, monthly compliance reports).']
  service_class varchar [note: 'The product or service category to which this SLA applies (e.g., \'Fiber Broadband\', \'5G Mobile\', \'IPTV\', \'Enterprise MPLS\', \'VoLTE\').']
  sla_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier or code for the SLA contract used in operational systems and customer communications.']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the SLA contract (e.g., \'Gold Business SLA\', \'Enterprise Uptime Guarantee\').']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA contract by its application scope: customer-facing (B2C/B2B service commitments), internal (inter-department operational agreements), wholesale (MVNO/carrier interconnect), partner (third-party service provider), or regulatory (mandated QoS compliance).']
  throughput_target_mbps decimal(12,2) [note: 'The minimum guaranteed throughput or bandwidth in megabits per second (Mbps). Applicable to broadband, fiber, and enterprise connectivity services.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The version identifier for this SLA contract definition. Incremented when terms are renegotiated or updated. Supports audit trail and change management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining SLA (Service Level Agreement) contracts applicable to customer services or internal network operations. Captures SLA ID, SLA name, SLA type (customer-facing/internal/wholesale), associated product or service class, availability target (%), MTTR target (hours), latency target (ms), packet loss target (%), jitter target (ms), measurement window, penalty clause flag, penalty calculation method, effective date, expiry date, and governing regulatory body reference. Owned by the assurance domain as the authoritative SLA definition used for breach tracking, penalty calculation, and regulatory QoS compliance reporting. Aligns with eTOM process 1.1.3 SLA Management.'
}

Table assurance.sla_breach_event {
  sla_breach_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each SLA breach event instance. Primary key for the SLA breach event record.']
  affected_cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: When breach is customer-specific (MTTR, availability), identifies which CPE asset contributed to SLA violation for proactive replacement, device upgrade programs, and vendor performance penalty calcul']
  affected_network_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific network element (RAN, BNG, OLT, router, etc.) that experienced the fault leading to the SLA breach.']
  breached_kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: SLA breaches are measured against specific KPI definitions (availability, MTTR, latency). Regulatory and customer breach reporting requires linking to the violated KPI definition for compliance docume']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: SLA breaches must be tracked per product offering for contract compliance validation, penalty calculation, and product-specific performance reporting. Critical for regulatory reporting and customer co']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account affected by the SLA breach. Used for penalty calculation and customer notification.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the NOC engineer assigned to investigate and resolve the SLA breach. Used for accountability and workload tracking.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: SLA penalty amounts post to specific GL accounts (revenue deductions, customer credits, liability accruals). Required for accurate revenue recognition, penalty accrual accounting, and financial statem']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: SLA breaches triggering service credits must link to invoices where credits are applied. Direct requirement for financial reconciliation, credit note generation, and contractual penalty tracking in en']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_package. Business justification: Content availability SLAs are contracted at package level (premium sports, movie bundles); breaches must reference the specific package for penalty calculation, customer credits, and license complianc']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: SLA commitments and penalties vary by price plan tier; linking enables validation of contracted performance levels, accurate penalty calculation, and tier-specific breach threshold enforcement.']
  regulatory_penalty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_penalty. Business justification: SLA breaches (especially consumer protection SLAs like broadband speed guarantees) can result in regulatory fines from consumer protection agencies. Links breach events to resulting penalties for fina']
  sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLA contract that defines the service level commitments and thresholds that were breached.']
  sla_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.sla_definition. Business justification: SLA breach events must reference the specific SLA definition violated for accurate penalty calculation using the defined penalty formula, credit calculation, and compliance reporting per partner agree']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_profile. Business justification: Breach events must link to contracted SLA terms for penalty calculation, customer credit eligibility, and regulatory reporting. Essential for financial impact assessment and dispute resolution.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service instance that experienced the SLA breach. Links to the service provisioned to the customer.']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated trouble ticket or incident record that triggered or documented the SLA breach event.']
  actual_measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual performance metric value measured during the breach period. Used to calculate the severity of the breach deviation.']
  affected_customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers impacted by the SLA breach event. Used for impact assessment and regulatory reporting thresholds.']
  affected_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of telecommunications service that experienced the SLA breach. Used for service-specific breach analysis and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadband|mobile|voice|iptv|enterprise_wan|cloud_service|iot — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  breach_deviation_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage deviation of the actual measured value from the contracted threshold. Quantifies the magnitude of the SLA violation.']
  breach_duration_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total duration of the SLA breach measured in minutes. Calculated from breach start to breach end timestamp. Used for penalty calculation.']
  breach_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the service metric returned to acceptable levels, marking the end of the breach period. Null if breach is ongoing.']
  breach_event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the SLA breach event. Used for external communication and reporting.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the SLA breach based on impact to service quality and business operations. Determines escalation and penalty tier.']
  breach_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the service metric first fell below the SLA threshold, marking the beginning of the breach period.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA breach event. Tracks the breach from detection through resolution and penalty application.']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Category of SLA metric that was breached. Defines which Key Performance Indicator (KPI) failed to meet the contracted threshold.']
  contracted_threshold_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The SLA threshold value defined in the contract that was breached. Represents the minimum acceptable performance level agreed upon.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SLA breach event record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  customer_impact_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the business impact on the customer due to the SLA breach. Considers service criticality and customer segment.']
  customer_notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify the customer about the SLA breach. Tracks notification method for audit and compliance purposes.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been notified about the SLA breach event. Tracks compliance with contractual notification obligations.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer notification was sent regarding the SLA breach. Used to verify compliance with notification SLAs.']
  detection_delay_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time difference between breach start and detection timestamps. Measures monitoring system responsiveness and Mean Time to Detect (MTTD).']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the SLA breach was detected. Used to evaluate monitoring effectiveness and improve detection capabilities.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SLA breach was first detected by monitoring systems or reported. May differ from breach start timestamp.']
  dispute_raised_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer or internal team has disputed the SLA breach determination or penalty calculation. Triggers dispute resolution workflow.']
  dispute_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the dispute resolution process for the SLA breach. Tracks dispute lifecycle from initiation to final decision.']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach requires escalation to senior management or specialized teams based on severity and duration thresholds.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the breach was escalated to higher management or specialized resolution teams. Tracks escalation timeliness.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SLA breach event record was most recently modified. Tracks data currency and change history.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated financial penalty or service credit amount owed to the customer due to the SLA breach. Used for revenue assurance and billing adjustments.']
  penalty_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial penalty or service credit is applicable for this breach according to the SLA contract terms.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount. Ensures accurate financial processing across multi-currency operations.']
  preventive_action_recommended varchar [note: 'Recommended preventive measures to avoid similar SLA breaches in the future. Used for continuous improvement and network optimization.']
  regulatory_report_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory report was submitted to the governing authority. Used for compliance audit trail.']
  regulatory_report_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required regulatory report has been submitted for this breach event. Tracks compliance with regulatory obligations.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA breach must be reported to regulatory authorities (FCC, Ofcom, etc.) based on severity and impact thresholds.']
  resolution_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective actions taken to resolve the SLA breach and restore service to acceptable levels. Documents remediation steps.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the underlying cause of the SLA breach. Used for trend analysis and preventive action planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: network_outage|equipment_failure|capacity_overload|configuration_error|external_factor|planned_maintenance|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the root cause analysis findings for the SLA breach. Documents technical and operational factors.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that originated or recorded the SLA breach event. Identifies the system of record for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for both the contracted threshold and actual measured values. Ensures consistent interpretation of performance metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percent|milliseconds|seconds|minutes|mbps|gbps|packets_per_second — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each instance where a service or network KPI breaches a defined SLA threshold. Captures breach event ID, referenced SLA contract, affected service instance, affected customer account, breach type (availability/MTTR/latency/packet-loss), breach start timestamp, breach end timestamp, breach duration (minutes), contracted threshold, actual measured value, breach severity, penalty applicable flag, penalty amount, notification sent flag, and notification timestamp. Critical for revenue assurance penalty calculations and regulatory reporting.'
}

  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique case number assigned to the revenue leakage investigation. Used for tracking and reporting purposes across Revenue Assurance teams.']
  case_opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue leakage case was formally opened in the Revenue Assurance system and assigned for investigation. Represents the start of the case lifecycle.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revenue leakage case: open (newly detected), under investigation (analysis in progress), pending validation (awaiting confirmation), resolved (root cause fixed), closed (case finalized), or escalated (requires senior management intervention).']
  confirmed_leakage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final validated monetary value of the revenue leakage after investigation completion and root cause confirmation. Represents the actual revenue loss or at-risk amount. Expressed in the billing currency.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the remediation actions implemented to stop the revenue leakage and prevent recurrence. Includes system changes, process updates, policy revisions, and control enhancements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue leakage case record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the leakage amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the billing system currency for the affected customer segment or service.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the revenue leakage was identified: automated reconciliation (system-driven variance detection), CDR audit (Call Detail Record analysis), billing system check (BSS integrity scan), manual review (analyst investigation), customer complaint (dispute-triggered), or external audit (third-party finding).']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue leakage event was first detected by the Revenue Assurance system or analyst. Represents the business event timestamp for case initiation.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case was escalated to senior management, executive leadership, or cross-functional steering committee due to high financial impact, regulatory risk, or resolution complexity. True if escalated.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was escalated to higher authority. Null if no escalation occurred.']
  estimated_leakage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Initial estimated monetary value of the revenue leakage based on preliminary analysis and detection algorithms. Expressed in the billing currency. May be revised as investigation progresses.']
  financial_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the financial materiality of the revenue leakage based on confirmed amount and percentage of total revenue: material (executive reporting required), significant (senior management attention), moderate (standard process), or minor (routine handling).']
  fraud_indicator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the revenue leakage shows characteristics of intentional fraud or abuse (e.g., SIM box fraud, subscription fraud, interconnect bypass). True if fraud is suspected or confirmed.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue leakage case record was last modified in the data platform. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  leakage_end_date date [note: 'Date when the revenue leakage was stopped or mitigated. Null if the leakage is ongoing. Used to calculate the total duration of revenue loss.']
  leakage_start_date date [note: 'Estimated or confirmed date when the revenue leakage first began occurring. Used to calculate the total exposure period and historical impact.']
  leakage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue leakage root cause category: unbilled usage (services consumed but not billed), rating error (incorrect tariff application), provisioning mismatch (service active but not in billing system), fraud (deliberate revenue theft), interconnect discrepancy (wholesale settlement errors), or discount abuse (unauthorized promotional application).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, investigation findings, analyst observations, or special handling instructions related to the revenue leakage case.']
  preventive_control_implemented varchar [note: 'Description of new preventive controls, automated checks, or monitoring mechanisms implemented to detect similar leakage patterns in the future. Part of continuous improvement process.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the revenue leakage case based on estimated financial impact, customer impact, and regulatory risk. Critical cases require immediate action and executive visibility.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual monetary amount recovered through billing adjustments, customer rebilling, or collections activities. May be less than confirmed leakage amount due to write-offs or uncollectible amounts.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recover the leaked revenue: customer rebill (invoice correction), billing adjustment (account credit/debit), write-off (uncollectible), collections (debt recovery), legal action (litigation), or not recoverable (loss accepted).']
  recovery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recovery was completed and funds were successfully collected or credited. Null if no recovery has occurred.']
  recurrence_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the likelihood that this type of revenue leakage could recur based on the effectiveness of corrective actions and preventive controls. High risk requires ongoing monitoring.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the revenue leakage case was reported to the relevant regulatory authority. Null if no regulatory reporting was required or has not yet occurred.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revenue leakage case must be reported to regulatory authorities (FCC, ITU, national telecom regulators) due to materiality thresholds, fraud indicators, or compliance obligations. True if reporting is required.']
  resolution_duration_hours varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in hours from case opening to case closure. Used to measure Revenue Assurance team efficiency and identify process bottlenecks. Calculated as the difference between case_closed_timestamp and case_opened_timestamp.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the underlying cause of the revenue leakage: system configuration (BSS/OSS setup error), process failure (workflow breakdown), data quality (incomplete or inaccurate data), human error (manual mistake), vendor issue (third-party system problem), fraud (intentional abuse), or policy gap (missing business rule). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: system_configuration|process_failure|data_quality|human_error|vendor_issue|fraud|policy_gap — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the specific root cause identified during the investigation, including technical details, affected systems, and contributing factors.']
  source_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the BSS, OSS, or Revenue Assurance system where the leakage was detected or originated. Examples: Amdocs Revenue Management, Comverse ONE, Oracle Communications OSM.']
  source_system_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier or case number from the originating system (e.g., Amdocs case ID, ServiceNow incident number). Used for cross-system traceability.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a detected or suspected revenue leakage event identified through reconciliation, CDR auditing, or billing system discrepancy analysis. Captures case ID, leakage type (unbilled usage/rating error/provisioning mismatch/fraud/interconnect discrepancy), detection method (automated/manual), detection timestamp, affected service type, estimated leakage amount (currency), confirmed leakage amount, case status (open/under-investigation/resolved/closed), assigned analyst, root cause category, corrective action taken, recovery amount, and recovery timestamp. Core to the Revenue Assurance function.'
}

Table assurance.reconciliation_run {
  reconciliation_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue assurance reconciliation execution run. Primary key.']
  affected_ip_address_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ip_address_pool.   affected_msisdn_range_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.msisdn_range. Business justification: When control_type=\'NUMBER_INVENTORY\', validates MSISDN allocation records against billing/HLR systems for revenue assurance, identifies ghost numbers, and ensures regulatory number utilization reporti']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Billing reconciliation operations (mediation, CDR processing, discrepancy resolution) incur costs allocated to revenue assurance or billing operations cost centers. Required for operational cost track']
  primary_reconciliation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue assurance analyst assigned to review and resolve discrepancies from this reconciliation run.']
  source_pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.pipeline_run. Business justification: Reconciliation runs ARE data pipeline executions. Real-world revenue assurance systems track which ETL pipeline run produced reconciliation results for data lineage, troubleshooting, and SLA complianc']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the reconciliation run began processing, capturing the real execution start time.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this reconciliation run requires management approval before resolution actions can be executed.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved the reconciliation results and resolution actions.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation run results were formally approved by authorized personnel.']
  cdr_file_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or reference identifiers of CDR files processed during this reconciliation run.']
  control_category varchar [note: 'Category of the revenue assurance control defining the type of reconciliation being performed.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Name of the revenue assurance control being executed in this reconciliation run (e.g., CDR Completeness Check, Rating Accuracy Validation).']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Type of control execution indicating whether the reconciliation is fully automated, manual, or a hybrid approach requiring both system and human intervention.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reconciliation run record was first created in the system.']
  detection_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the detection rule or logic applied during this reconciliation run to identify discrepancies, mismatches, or anomalies.']
  discrepancy_amount_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total discrepancy amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discrepancy_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual discrepancies identified during this reconciliation run, representing line-level mismatches or anomalies.']
  duplicate_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of duplicate records identified during reconciliation, which may indicate data quality issues or system errors.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation run completed processing, whether successfully or with errors.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message or exception information if the reconciliation run failed or encountered processing errors.']
  execution_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which this revenue assurance control is executed (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly, on-demand).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reconciliation run record was last updated or modified.']
  matched_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that successfully matched between source and target systems with no discrepancies detected.']
  mediation_system_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or batch ID from the mediation system that processed the CDRs being reconciled.']
  missing_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records expected but not found in either source or target system, indicating potential data loss or completeness issues.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments added by revenue assurance analysts regarding this reconciliation run, including observations, issues, or follow-up actions.']
  overcharge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of discrepancies where customers were overcharged, requiring potential refunds or credits.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this reconciliation run based on financial impact, customer impact, or regulatory requirements.']
  reconciliation_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the business period being reconciled (e.g., billing cycle end, CDR collection period end).']
  reconciliation_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the business period being reconciled (e.g., billing cycle start, CDR collection period start).']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of reconciliation being performed, defining the source-to-target comparison scope (e.g., CDR-to-bill matching, inventory-to-provisioning alignment, TAP file reconciliation).']
  rejected_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records rejected during reconciliation processing due to validation failures, format errors, or business rule violations.']
  resolution_summary varchar [note: 'Summary description of resolution actions taken for discrepancies identified in this reconciliation run.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the reconciliation run, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reconciliation run indicating whether it is pending, in progress, completed successfully, failed, or cancelled.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the reconciliation run was scheduled to begin execution.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this reconciliation run breached defined SLA thresholds for completion time or discrepancy resolution.']
  source_record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of records extracted from the source system for reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system being monitored or reconciled (e.g., Comverse ONE, Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, mediation platform).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Technical identifier or instance ID of the source system from which reconciliation data is extracted.']
  target_record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of records extracted from the target system for comparison.']
  target_system varchar [note: 'Name of the target system being compared against the source (e.g., Amdocs Revenue Management, Oracle Communications OSM, billing system).']
  target_system_code varchar [note: 'Technical identifier or instance ID of the target system against which reconciliation data is compared.']
  total_discrepancy_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all discrepancies identified in this reconciliation run, representing potential revenue leakage or over-billing.']
  total_records_processed varchar [note: 'Total number of records processed during this reconciliation run across both source and target systems.']
  trigger_method varchar [note: 'Method by which this reconciliation run was initiated (e.g., scheduled job, manual trigger by analyst, event-driven, threshold breach alert).']
  unbilled_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of usage or services that were not billed to customers, representing direct revenue leakage.']
  undercharge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of discrepancies where customers were undercharged, representing revenue leakage to the business.']
  unmatched_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records that failed to match between source and target systems, indicating potential discrepancies or data quality issues.']

  Note: 'Master record of a revenue assurance reconciliation execution with embedded control definition and line-level discrepancy detail. Control layer captures: control name, control category (CDR completeness/rating accuracy/provisioning-billing alignment/interconnect settlement/prepaid balance integrity), control type (automated/manual/hybrid), execution frequency, source system monitored, target system compared, and detection rule description. Run header captures: run ID, reconciliation type (CDR-to-bill/inventory-to-provisioning/interconnect-TAP/prepaid-balance/CDR-completeness-audit), source system, target system, run timestamps, total records processed, matched/unmatched counts, expected vs received record counts, duplicate/missing/rejected record counts, discrepancy amount, run status, trigger method, CDR file references, mediation system reference, and assigned analyst. Detail layer captures: individual discrepancy records with discrepancy ID, discrepancy type (missing CDR/duplicate charge/rating mismatch/inventory gap/file gap), source and target record references, discrepancy amount, direction (over-charge/under-charge/unbilled), resolution status, resolution action, and resolved timestamp. Enables control framework management, summary-level reconciliation tracking, CDR completeness auditing, and drill-down to individual discrepancy items. Aligns with eTOM process 1.1.5 Revenue Assurance.'
}

  fraud_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score (0-100) indicating the likelihood that the detected activity is genuine fraud based on detection algorithms and investigation findings.']

  fraud_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fraud incident type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sim-swap|subscription-fraud|bypass-fraud|roaming-fraud|pbx-hacking|wangiri|irsf|dealer-fraud|identity-theft|device-fraud|refund-fraud|premium-rate-fraud — promote to reference product]']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where the fraud incident occurred or was detected.']
  investigation_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative notes documenting investigation activities, findings, interviews, and analysis performed during the fraud case lifecycle.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this fraud case record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this fraud case record was last updated or modified.']

  law_enforcement_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fraud case has been referred to law enforcement authorities for criminal investigation and prosecution.']
  law_enforcement_referral_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud case was formally referred to law enforcement agencies.']
  preventive_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of preventive measures or controls implemented as a result of this fraud case to prevent future similar incidents.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount successfully recovered from the fraudster or through insurance claims and chargebacks.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Method or mechanism used to recover financial losses from the fraud incident.']
  related_case_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related fraud case IDs that are linked to this case through common perpetrators, patterns, or coordinated attacks.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Summary notes documenting the final resolution, outcome, and closure rationale for the fraud case.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause that enabled the fraud incident (process gap, system vulnerability, insider threat, etc.).']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the identified root cause and contributing factors that allowed the fraud to occur.']
  trigger_type varchar [note: 'Type of detection mechanism or condition that triggered the fraud alert (velocity check, threshold breach, pattern match, etc.).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this fraud case record.']

  Note: 'Operational record covering the full fraud management lifecycle from initial detection alert through formal investigation, law enforcement referral, and resolution. Captures fraud case ID, fraud type (SIM-swap/subscription-fraud/bypass-fraud/roaming-fraud/PBX-hacking/wangiri/IRSF/dealer-fraud), detection source, detection rule reference, alert severity, trigger type (velocity/threshold/pattern/blacklist/ML-signal), alert timestamp, affected subscriber, affected service, estimated fraud loss amount, confirmed fraud loss amount, case status (open/under-investigation/suspended/closed-confirmed/closed-false-positive), assigned fraud analyst, investigation notes, evidence references, law enforcement referral flag, referral timestamp, recovery amount, recovery method, and case closure timestamp. Consolidates the detection-to-resolution workflow in a single entity with lifecycle stage tracking. Aligns with eTOM process 1.1.4 Fraud Management.'
}

Table assurance.fraud_alert {
  fraud_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fraud alert record. Primary key.']
  analytical_model_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.analytical_model_registry. Business justification: Fraud alerts are generated by real-time scoring models. Operations track which model version produced each alert for false positive analysis, model tuning, and regulatory audit trails.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Real-time fraud alerts trigger on account-level anomalies (unusual spend spikes, payment method changes, velocity attacks). Operational fraud prevention requires immediate account context for blocking']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Real-time fraud detection rules vary by product type; linking enables context-aware alerting, product-specific threshold configuration, and false positive reduction through product behavior profiling.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud analyst or Security Operations Center (SOC) team member assigned to review this alert. Null if unassigned.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the formal fraud investigation case if this alert was escalated. Null if alert was closed without escalation or is still under initial review.']
  kpi_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fraud detection rule in the fraud management system that generated this alert.']
  network_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the network element (cell tower, base station, gateway, switch) where the suspicious activity originated or was detected.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the subscriber account associated with the suspected fraudulent activity.']
  vod_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.vod_asset. Business justification: Real-time fraud detection for abnormal viewing patterns (credential sharing, concurrent stream violations, VPN/proxy abuse) on high-value assets (premium movies, live sports) requires asset-level aler']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when a fraud analyst acknowledged the alert and began review. Null if alert has not been acknowledged.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate remediation action taken in response to the alert, such as service suspension, account barring, SIM deactivation, transaction reversal, or customer notification.']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual observed value that triggered the alert, such as the number of calls made, total charge amount, or data volume consumed during the detection window.']
  affected_imei varchar [note: 'The IMEI of the device used during the suspected fraudulent activity, enabling device-level tracking and blacklisting. Personally identifiable device information.']
  affected_imsi varchar [note: 'The IMSI associated with the subscriber at the time of the alert, used to uniquely identify the subscriber on the network. Personally identifiable information.']
  affected_msisdn varchar [note: 'The mobile phone number (MSISDN) on which the suspicious activity was detected. Personally identifiable information.']
  alert_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert record was created in the fraud management system, may differ slightly from detection_timestamp due to processing latency.']
  alert_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the fraud alert, typically formatted as FA- followed by a 10-digit sequence number.']
  alert_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the alert for queue management and resource allocation, distinct from severity which reflects technical risk.']
  alert_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the fraud alert indicating the urgency and potential impact: critical (immediate action required), high (escalate quickly), medium (investigate within SLA), low (monitor).']
  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fraud alert: new (unreviewed), acknowledged (assigned to analyst), investigating (under review), escalated (promoted to fraud case), closed (resolved), false-positive (dismissed as non-fraud).']
  alert_trigger_type varchar [note: 'Category of fraud detection trigger that generated the alert: velocity (rapid usage), threshold (limit breach), pattern (behavioral anomaly), blacklist (known fraud entity), anomaly (statistical outlier), or geo-location (impossible travel).']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the fraud analyst during alert review, documenting findings, rationale for decisions, and any additional context.']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Name of the fraud operations team or SOC (Security Operations Center) shift responsible for this alert, such as Fraud-Tier1, SOC-EMEA, Revenue-Assurance.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert was closed, either by resolution, escalation to case, or dismissal as false positive. Null if still open.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Machine learning or rule-based confidence score (0-100) indicating the likelihood that the alert represents genuine fraud. Higher scores indicate higher confidence.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the suspicious activity was detected, such as USA, GBR, DEU.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fraud alert record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated revenue impact amount, such as USD, EUR, GBP.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fraud detection system identified the suspicious activity and generated the alert. Represents the real-world event time.']
  detection_window_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the time window during which the fraud detection rule evaluated activity. Used for velocity and pattern-based rules.']
  detection_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the time window during which the fraud detection rule evaluated activity. Used for velocity and pattern-based rules.']
  escalated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert was escalated to a formal fraud case for investigation. Null if not escalated.']
  estimated_revenue_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the suspected fraud in the billing currency, representing potential revenue loss or fraudulent charges. Business-sensitive financial data.']
  false_positive_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the alert was determined to be a false positive after analyst review. True if false positive, False if genuine fraud or still under investigation.']
  fraud_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping fraud types for reporting and analytics: revenue-assurance (billing/charging fraud), network-abuse (traffic pumping, toll fraud), identity-fraud (account takeover, SIM swap), device-fraud (cloning, IMEI manipulation), roaming-fraud, content-fraud (premium SMS, OTT abuse).']
  fraud_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the suspected fraud type, such as subscription fraud, identity theft, SIM swap fraud, international revenue share fraud (IRSF), wangiri fraud, PBX hacking, roaming fraud, or revenue leakage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: subscription-fraud|identity-theft|sim-swap|irsf|wangiri|pbx-hacking|roaming-fraud|revenue-leakage|account-takeover|device-cloning|premium-rate-abuse — promote to reference product]']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the suspicious activity originated, such as North America, EMEA, APAC, LATAM. Used for regional fraud pattern analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fraud alert record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit trail field.']
  network_element_type varchar [note: 'Type of network element involved in the alert, such as RAN (Radio Access Network), Core, BNG (Broadband Network Gateway), OLT (Optical Line Terminal), or CPE (Customer Premises Equipment).']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a fraud alert notification was sent to the affected subscriber. True if notification sent, False otherwise.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fraud alert notification was sent to the subscriber via SMS, email, or mobile app push notification. Null if no notification sent.']
  related_alert_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related fraud alert IDs that are part of the same fraud pattern or campaign, enabling correlation analysis.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score (0-100) combining fraud likelihood, potential financial impact, and subscriber risk profile. Used for prioritization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the fraud detection or revenue assurance system that generated the alert, such as Subex ROC, FICO Falcon Fraud Manager, WeDo RAID, or internal fraud engine.']
  source_system_alert_reference varchar [note: 'Native alert identifier from the originating fraud detection system, used for traceability and cross-system reconciliation.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold and actual values, such as calls, SMS, currency (USD, EUR), megabytes (MB), minutes, or transactions.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The threshold limit configured in the fraud rule that was breached, triggering the alert. For example, maximum call count, maximum charge amount, or maximum data volume.']

  Note: 'Real-time or near-real-time alert generated by fraud detection rules or threshold triggers indicating suspicious activity on a subscriber account or network element. Captures alert ID, alert rule name, alert trigger type (velocity/threshold/pattern/blacklist), alert severity, affected subscriber or network element, alert timestamp, alert status (new/acknowledged/escalated/closed/false-positive), linked fraud case ID (if escalated), and analyst notes. Distinct from fraud_case — an alert is the initial signal; a case is the formal investigation record.'
}

Table assurance.change_record {

Business justification: Network changes often implement capital projects (new cell sites, fiber deployment, core network upgrades). Required for capex project tracking, asset capitalization timing, and project completion ver']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Planned network changes incur implementation costs (labor, testing, rollback resources) allocated to change management or network operations cost centers. Standard practice for change management cost ']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.noc_incident. Business justification: Change records can be linked to NOC incidents when a planned change triggers or is related to an operational incident. This is N:1 (many changes can relate to one NOC incident). The FK column does not']
  regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_change. Business justification: Network changes implemented to comply with new regulations: spectrum rule changes, security mandates (STIR/SHAKEN), privacy requirements (CPNI protection). Links change record to regulatory change for']
  related_problem_problem_record_id varchar [note: 'Problem record ID in ServiceNow ITSM that this change addresses. Links corrective changes to root cause analysis and problem management process.']
  actual_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual duration of service downtime or degradation in minutes during change implementation. Used for variance analysis, SLA breach calculation, and change success metrics.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the change implementation completed. Used for maintenance window compliance tracking and post-implementation review.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the change implementation began. Used for variance analysis against planned schedule and accurate SLA exclusion tracking.']
  affected_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic region, market, or site affected by this change (e.g., \'Northeast Region\', \'Site-NYC-001\', \'National\'). Used for customer notification targeting and regional SLA tracking.']
  affected_network_element varchar [note: 'Specific network element, node, or equipment identifier impacted by this change (e.g., eNodeB ID, BNG hostname, OLT serial number). May reference multiple elements separated by delimiters.']
  affected_service varchar [note: 'Service or service group impacted by this change (e.g., \'Mobile Data\', \'VoLTE\', \'IPTV\', \'Enterprise MPLS\'). Used to identify customer-facing impact and SLA applicability.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change was formally approved for implementation. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']

  change_category varchar [note: 'Network domain or system category affected by this change. RAN (Radio Access Network), transport (MPLS/SD-WAN), core (IMS/EPC/5GC), BSS (Business Support Systems), OSS (Operations Support Systems), CPE (Customer Premises Equipment), fiber (FTTH/GPON), backhaul (microwave/fiber transport). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RAN|transport|core|BSS|OSS|CPE|fiber|backhaul — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the change including technical scope, implementation steps, and business justification. Captures what will be changed, why, and how.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique change identifier assigned by ServiceNow ITSM. Used for external communication and tracking (e.g., CHG0012345).']
  change_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority of the change based on urgency and impact. Critical priority changes receive expedited approval and implementation. Used for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change record. Tracks progression from initial draft through CAB approval, implementation, and closure. Failed status indicates unsuccessful implementation; rolled-back indicates reversal to previous state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|scheduled|in-progress|completed|failed|rolled-back|cancelled|closed — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the purpose and scope of the change (e.g., \'Upgrade RAN software to v21.3 in Northeast region\').']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change based on urgency, risk, and approval requirements. Standard changes are pre-approved low-risk changes; normal changes require CAB approval; emergency changes bypass normal approval for critical incidents. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|normal|emergency|maintenance-window|preventive|corrective|upgrade|capacity-expansion — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this change record was formally closed after successful implementation and post-implementation review. Used for change lifecycle duration metrics and compliance reporting.']
  configuration_item_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Configuration Item (CI) IDs from ServiceNow CMDB that are modified by this change. Links change records to specific network elements, software versions, and infrastructure components.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this change record was first created in ServiceNow ITSM. Used for audit trail and change request aging analysis.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advance notification was sent to affected customers about this planned change. Required for customer-impacting changes per regulatory and contractual obligations.']
  estimated_customer_impact_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customers (subscribers or accounts) potentially affected by this change. Used for risk assessment and customer notification scope determination.']
  estimated_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated duration of service downtime or degradation in minutes during change implementation. Used for customer notification, SLA impact assessment, and maintenance window planning.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Detailed assessment of potential impact including affected services, customer segments, geographic regions, and estimated downtime. Used for CAB (Change Advisory Board) review and approval decisions.']
  implementer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer, technician, or team responsible for executing the change. May be internal staff or vendor personnel.']
  implementer_team varchar [note: 'Organizational team or department responsible for change implementation (e.g., \'RAN Engineering\', \'NOC Operations\', \'Vendor Field Services\').']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this change record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  maintenance_window_code varchar [note: 'Reference code for the approved maintenance window during which this change is scheduled. Links to predefined maintenance calendar for recurring maintenance periods.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify customers about the planned change (e.g., email, SMS, IVR call, mobile app push notification, web portal banner).']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when customer notification was sent. Used to verify compliance with advance notice requirements (typically 24-72 hours before planned maintenance).']
  pir_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes from the post-implementation review including lessons learned, issues encountered, and recommendations for future similar changes.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the change implementation is planned to complete. Used for maintenance window duration calculation and SLA exclusion period definition.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the change implementation is planned to begin. Used for maintenance window scheduling and customer notification.']
  post_implementation_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the post-implementation review assessing whether the change achieved its objectives without adverse impact. Successful-with-issues indicates minor problems that were resolved. Used for continuous improvement and change success rate KPIs.']
  related_incident_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of NOC incident IDs that triggered or are related to this change. Used for correlating reactive changes with network incidents and alarm spikes.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of the change based on potential impact to service availability, customer experience, and revenue. Critical risk changes require executive approval and extensive rollback planning.']
  rollback_executed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rollback plan was executed due to change failure or adverse impact. Used for change success rate tracking and root cause analysis.']
  rollback_plan_documented_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a detailed rollback plan has been documented and approved as part of the change request. Required for high-risk and critical-risk changes.']
  sla_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether downtime or performance degradation during this change is excluded from SLA breach calculations. True for approved maintenance windows; false for emergency changes or changes that exceeded planned duration.']
  vendor_involved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external vendor or equipment manufacturer personnel are involved in implementing this change. Used for coordination and accountability tracking.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or equipment manufacturer involved in the change (e.g., \'Nokia\', \'Ericsson\', \'Cisco\', \'Huawei\'). Null if no vendor involvement.']
  vendor_ticket_reference varchar [note: 'External ticket or case number in the vendor\'s system associated with this change. Used for cross-system tracking and vendor SLA management.']

  Note: 'ServiceNow ITSM Change Management record tracking all planned changes and maintenance windows for network infrastructure, software, or configuration that may impact service quality or availability. Captures change ID, change type (standard/normal/emergency/maintenance-window/preventive/corrective/upgrade/capacity-expansion), change category (RAN/transport/core/BSS/OSS), change title, change description, risk level, impact assessment, affected network element or service, affected geography, planned start timestamp, planned end timestamp, actual start timestamp, actual end timestamp, change status (draft/approved/scheduled/in-progress/completed/failed/rolled-back/cancelled), approver, implementer, maintenance status tracking, post-implementation review outcome, SLA exclusion flag (whether downtime during this change is excluded from SLA calculations), customer notification sent flag, notification timestamp, and approving authority reference. Critical for correlating network changes with alarm spikes and SLA breaches, suppressing SLA breach calculations during approved maintenance periods, and tracking maintenance window compliance.'
}

Table assurance.problem_record {
  problem_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the problem record in ServiceNow ITSM Problem Management. Primary key for the problem_record product.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Recurring problems identified in compliance audits as control weaknesses (e.g., repeated outages due to inadequate change management). Links problem record to audit finding for remediation tracking an']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.noc_incident. Business justification: Problem records can be linked to NOC incidents when root cause investigation is needed for operational incidents. This is N:1 (many problem records can relate to one NOC incident). The FK column does ']
  primary_network_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Problem management tracks recurring issues to specific network elements for trend analysis, preventive maintenance planning, and vendor quality feedback. ITIL-standard process requires linking known e']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the problem was acknowledged by the assigned problem manager or team. Indicates investigation has commenced.']
  affected_customer_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customers impacted by incidents related to this problem. Quantifies business impact and supports prioritization.']
  affected_network_domain varchar [note: 'Network domain or technology layer impacted by the problem (e.g., RAN, Core, Transport, BSS, OSS). Enables domain-specific problem analysis and MTBF tracking.']
  affected_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of customer-facing service impacted by the problem (e.g., voice, data, IPTV, broadband). Links problem to service quality and SLA metrics.']
  affected_technology_type varchar [note: 'Specific technology or platform affected by the problem (e.g., 5G NR, LTE, FTTH, GPON, IMS, VoLTE). Supports technology-specific root cause analysis.']
  assigned_problem_manager varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the problem manager responsible for investigating and resolving this problem. Establishes accountability for problem resolution.']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Technical team or group assigned to investigate the problem (e.g., Network Engineering, Application Support, Database Administration). Supports workload distribution and expertise routing.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the problem record was formally closed after verification and documentation. Marks the end of the problem lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this problem record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  estimated_revenue_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact in currency units due to service disruption caused by this problem. Supports revenue assurance and business case for problem resolution.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where the problem was observed (e.g., Northeast, Southwest, EMEA, APAC). Supports regional problem trend analysis.']
  identified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the problem was first identified and the problem record was created. Marks the start of the problem investigation lifecycle.']
  impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the business impact scope of the problem. Measures the extent of service degradation or customer affect.']
  investigation_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time in hours spent investigating the problem from identification to root cause determination. Used for problem management efficiency KPIs.']
  kedb_article_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the KEDB article documenting this known error. Provides link to detailed knowledge base entry.']
  known_error_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this problem has been documented in the Known Error Database. True when root cause is identified and workaround is available.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this problem record was last updated. Tracks data currency and supports change data capture processes.']
  linked_alarm_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of alarm event IDs associated with this problem. Connects network alarms to problem investigation for correlation analysis.']
  linked_incident_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of incident IDs or trouble ticket IDs that are related to this problem. Establishes traceability between recurring incidents and their root cause.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or observations related to the problem investigation. Captures supplementary information not covered by structured fields.']
  permanent_fix_description varchar [note: 'Description of the permanent solution implemented to eliminate the root cause. Documents the change or remediation that resolves the problem.']
  post_incident_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal post-incident review was conducted for this problem. True when major incidents triggered formal review process.']
  preventive_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of proactive measures implemented to prevent recurrence of similar problems. Documents continuous improvement actions.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the problem based on impact and urgency. Determines resource allocation and investigation timeline.']
  problem_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the problem type. Used for classification and routing to appropriate problem management teams.']
  problem_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the problem, including symptoms, impact, and context. Provides comprehensive understanding of the issue under investigation.']
  problem_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the problem record, typically following ServiceNow PRB format (e.g., PRB0001234). Used for external communication and tracking.']
  problem_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the problem record. Tracks progression from identification through resolution and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|in_progress|known_error|resolved|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  problem_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory providing granular classification within the problem category. Enables precise problem taxonomy and trend analysis.']
  problem_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the problem being investigated. Provides quick identification of the problem nature.']
  rca_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the formal RCA document. Links to detailed analysis report with investigation findings and recommendations.']
  related_change_request_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of change request IDs associated with implementing the permanent fix. Links problem resolution to change management process.']
  resolution_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time in hours from problem identification to permanent fix implementation. Measures end-to-end problem resolution efficiency.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permanent fix was implemented and the problem was resolved. Indicates the problem no longer causes incidents.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the identified root cause (e.g., configuration error, hardware failure, software defect, capacity issue, human error). Enables root cause trend analysis.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the underlying root cause identified through problem investigation. Documents the fundamental reason for recurring incidents.']
  root_cause_identified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the root cause was successfully identified. Marks transition to known error status and enables MTTR calculation.']
  site_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of network site IDs affected by this problem. Links problem to specific physical infrastructure locations.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incidents related to this problem resulted in SLA breaches. True when customer SLA commitments were violated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that originated the problem record (e.g., ServiceNow ITSM, Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager). Tracks data lineage.']
  urgency varchar [note: 'Time sensitivity of the problem resolution. Indicates how quickly the problem must be addressed to prevent further incidents.']
  vendor_involved varchar [note: 'Name of external vendor or supplier involved in problem investigation or resolution (e.g., Nokia, Ericsson, Cisco). Tracks third-party engagement.']
  vendor_ticket_reference varchar [note: 'External ticket or case number from vendor support system. Enables tracking of vendor escalations and support interactions.']
  workaround_description varchar [note: 'Temporary solution or procedure to mitigate the problem impact while permanent fix is being developed. Enables incident teams to reduce service disruption.']

  Note: 'ServiceNow ITSM Problem Management record representing the root cause investigation of one or more recurring trouble tickets or incidents. Captures problem ID, problem title, problem category, linked incident/ticket IDs, affected network domain, root cause description, workaround description, permanent fix description, problem status (open/known-error/resolved/closed), priority, assigned problem manager, identified timestamp, resolved timestamp, and known error database (KEDB) flag. Enables proactive fault management and MTBF improvement tracking.'
}
