  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proactive customer notifications were sent regarding the outage event via SMS, email, or other channels.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the first customer notification was sent regarding the outage event.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the outage was first detected: network alarm, performance monitoring system, customer trouble report, or proactive health check.']
  estimated_revenue_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the outage in terms of lost revenue, service credits, and potential penalties.']
  impacted_business_customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of business or enterprise customers affected by the outage, tracked separately for priority restoration and regulatory reporting.']
  impacted_subscriber_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique subscribers affected by the outage event. Key metric for regulatory threshold determination.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the outage record was last updated or modified.']
  mttr_target_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target mean time to repair in minutes as defined by SLA or operational policy for this outage severity level.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, escalation details, or special circumstances related to the outage event.']
  notification_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Regulatory deadline timestamp by which the outage notification must be submitted to avoid compliance penalties.']
  notification_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory notification was officially submitted to the governing body.']
  outage_cause_category varchar [note: 'Root cause classification for the outage event: hardware failure, software defect, power loss, fiber cut, weather event, or human error.']
  outage_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause and contributing factors for the outage event.']
  outage_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the outage event in minutes, calculated from start to end timestamp. Critical for SLA compliance and regulatory reporting.']
  outage_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when service was fully restored and the outage event was closed.']
  outage_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the outage event, used for external communication and regulatory reporting.']
  outage_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the outage event based on service impact, subscriber count, and business criticality.']
  outage_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the outage event was first detected or when service disruption began.']
  outage_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outage record: detected, under investigation, restoration in progress, service restored, or record closed.']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage as planned maintenance or unplanned service disruption.']
  post_outage_review_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the post-outage review and root cause analysis have been completed and documented.']
  post_outage_review_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document link to the post-outage review report, root cause analysis, and preventive action plan.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing regulatory body to which the outage must be reported: FCC (USA), Ofcom (UK), BEREC (EU), NTIA (USA), CRTC (Canada), ACMA (Australia).']
  regulatory_notification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory notification workflow: not required, draft in preparation, submitted to regulator, acknowledged by regulator, or closed.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or case identifier assigned by the regulatory body upon submission of the outage notification.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage event meets regulatory reporting thresholds and must be reported to governing bodies.']
  related_alarm_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of alarm event IDs that triggered or correlated with this outage event.']
  related_trouble_ticket_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of trouble ticket IDs associated with customer-reported issues during the outage event.']
  reportable_threshold_met varchar [note: 'Description of which regulatory threshold criteria were met: subscriber count threshold, duration threshold, service type threshold, or geographic scope threshold.']
  restoration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to restore service: automatic recovery, manual intervention, network failover, traffic bypass, equipment replacement, or on-site repair.']
  restoration_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the restoration effort based on service impact, customer segment, and regulatory obligations.']
  restoration_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the NOC team, field operations team, or vendor team responsible for service restoration.']
  sla_breach_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SLA contracts breached as a result of this outage event.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage event resulted in a breach of contractual SLA commitments for availability, MTTR, or service quality.']
  vendor_involved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a third-party vendor or equipment manufacturer was involved in the outage cause or restoration effort.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor or equipment manufacturer involved in the outage event or restoration.']
  vendor_ticket_reference varchar [note: 'Vendor-assigned ticket or case number for tracking the outage issue with the equipment manufacturer or service provider.']

  Note: 'Master record of a confirmed network or service outage event with full lifecycle tracking from detection to restoration and regulatory notification. Distinct from noc_incident (which captures the operational response and war-room coordination) — outage_record owns the infrastructure event facts: what failed, when, where, how long, and how many subscribers were impacted. Captures outage ID, outage type (planned/unplanned), outage cause category (hardware/software/power/fiber-cut/weather/human-error/cyber), affected network element or service, affected geography (region/cell/exchange), outage start timestamp, outage end timestamp, outage duration (minutes), impacted subscriber count, impacted service types, restoration method, restoration team, regulatory reportable flag, regulatory body (FCC/Ofcom/BEREC/NTIA), reportable threshold met (subscriber count/duration/service type), regulatory notification status (not-required/draft/submitted/acknowledged/closed), regulatory reference number, notification submission timestamp, compliance officer sign-off, and post-outage review reference.'
}

Table assurance.qos_measurement {
  qos_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the QoS measurement record. Primary key.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: QoS measurements must be validated against product specifications (bandwidth, latency, uptime commitments) to verify service delivery compliance. Essential for SLA monitoring and product performance b']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the radio cell serving the subscriber at the time of measurement, applicable to mobile services.']
  iptv_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.iptv_channel. Business justification: Live streaming QoS metrics (latency, jitter, packet loss, video stall rate) are measured per-channel for broadcast quality assurance, enabling channel-specific performance trending and multicast optim']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: QoS measurements (throughput, latency, jitter, MOS) implement specific KPI definitions. Real-world QoS monitoring links measurements to KPI definitions for SLA compliance validation and customer exper']
  measured_cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Links QoS measurements (throughput, latency, packet loss, MOS) to specific customer equipment for device performance analysis, WiFi 6 upgrade targeting, and correlation of service quality with device ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: QoS measurements support compliance with consumer protection regulations: broadband speed disclosure (FCC), net neutrality monitoring, minimum service quality standards. Links measurements to specific']
  sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLA contract governing the expected QoS levels for this service instance.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_profile. Business justification: QoS measurements validate compliance against contracted SLA performance thresholds. Essential for proactive breach detection, customer reporting, and demonstrating service level achievement.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber experiencing this service quality level.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service instance being measured (e.g., broadband connection, VoLTE session, IPTV stream).']
  vod_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.vod_asset. Business justification: Video quality metrics (stall rate, resolution achieved, MOS score, buffering ratio) are measured per-asset to identify encoding quality issues, CDN performance problems, and content-specific delivery ']
  application_category varchar [note: 'The category of application or service being used during this measurement, as identified by DPI or traffic analysis (e.g., video_streaming, web_browsing, gaming, voip, social_media).']
  bearer_type varchar [note: 'The type of service bearer being measured: voice (circuit-switched), data (packet-switched), video (streaming), VoLTE (Voice over LTE), IMS (IP Multimedia Subsystem), or 5G network slice.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'The severity level of the SLA breach if one occurred: minor (slight deviation), major (significant impact), or critical (severe service degradation).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment to which the subscriber belongs: consumer (residential), SOHO (Small Office/Home Office), SME (Small/Medium Enterprise), enterprise (large business), wholesale (carrier/MVNO), or government.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A calculated score (0-100) representing the reliability and completeness of this QoS measurement record, based on validation rules and data completeness checks.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of customer premises equipment (CPE) or device used by the subscriber during this measurement (e.g., smartphone, tablet, ONT, set-top box, router).']
  downlink_throughput_mbps decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured downlink (network to subscriber) data throughput in megabits per second (Mbps).']
  dscp_marking varchar [note: 'The DSCP value applied to IP packets for QoS prioritization in the transport network (e.g., EF, AF41, BE).']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or market where this QoS measurement was captured (e.g., Northeast, California, London Metro).']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this QoS measurement record was ingested into the data lakehouse, used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  jitter_ms decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured packet delay variation (jitter) in milliseconds (ms), indicating consistency of packet arrival times.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this QoS measurement record was last modified or enriched, supporting audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  latency_ms decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured round-trip latency in milliseconds (ms) for packet transmission, representing delay experienced by the subscriber.']
  measurement_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'The duration in seconds over which this QoS measurement was aggregated or sampled (e.g., 60 for 1-minute interval, 300 for 5-minute interval).']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'The system or method that captured this QoS measurement: DPI (Deep Packet Inspection), network probe, PCRF (Policy and Charging Rules Function), active testing system, RAN (Radio Access Network) analytics, or OSS (Operations Support Systems).']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'The validation status of this QoS measurement: valid (passed quality checks), invalid (failed validation), incomplete (missing data), or suspect (anomalous values requiring review).']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when this QoS measurement was captured, representing the subscriber-experienced quality at that moment.']
  mos_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Mean Opinion Score for voice quality, ranging from 1.0 (poor) to 5.0 (excellent). Applicable to voice and VoLTE bearer types.']
  network_technology varchar [note: 'The underlying network technology delivering the service: 2G, 3G, 4G LTE, 5G NR (New Radio), FTTH (Fiber to the Home), GPON (Gigabit Passive Optical Network), DSL, cable, or satellite. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 2g|3g|4g_lte|5g_nr|ftth|gpon|dsl|cable|satellite — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, anomalies, or operational notes related to this QoS measurement.']
  packet_loss_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of packets lost during transmission, a key indicator of network congestion or quality degradation.']
  qci_5qi_value varchar [note: 'The QoS Class Identifier (QCI) for LTE or 5G QoS Identifier (5QI) for 5G networks, defining the bearer-level QoS treatment (e.g., 1=conversational voice, 5=IMS signaling, 9=default bearer).']
  qos_policy_applied varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the QoS policy rule applied to this service instance at the time of measurement (e.g., premium_data_policy, video_optimization_policy).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this QoS measurement must be included in regulatory QoS reporting to governing bodies (e.g., FCC, Ofcom).']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'The number of individual samples or packets analyzed to produce this aggregated QoS measurement.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The high-level service category being measured: mobile voice, mobile data, fixed broadband, IPTV (Internet Protocol Television), VoIP (Voice over IP), IMS (IP Multimedia Subsystem), or OTT (Over-The-Top). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_voice|mobile_data|fixed_broadband|iptv|voip|ims|ott — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  signal_quality_db decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured signal-to-noise ratio or signal quality in decibels (dB), indicating the clarity of the radio signal for wireless services.']
  signal_strength_dbm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured radio signal strength in decibels relative to one milliwatt (dBm) at the subscriber device, applicable to wireless services.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this QoS measurement violated the contracted SLA thresholds.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'The methodology used to capture this QoS measurement: passive (monitoring live traffic), active (injecting test traffic), or synthetic (simulated user experience).']
  throughput_mbps decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured data throughput in megabits per second (Mbps) experienced by the subscriber during this measurement interval.']
  uplink_throughput_mbps decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured uplink (subscriber to network) data throughput in megabits per second (Mbps).']
  video_resolution_achieved varchar [note: 'The actual video resolution delivered to the subscriber: SD (Standard Definition), HD (720p), Full HD (1080p), 4K (2160p), or 8K (4320p).']
  video_stall_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of video playback time affected by buffering or stalling events. Applicable to video and IPTV services.']

  Note: 'SSOT for end-to-end Quality of Service measurement records at the service and bearer layer, capturing subscriber-experienced quality for active services. Owns all service-layer QoS metrics distinct from network-element performance (owned by performance_measurement). Captures QoS measurement ID, service instance reference, subscriber reference, bearer type (voice/data/video/VoLTE/IMS/5G-slice), measurement timestamp, measurement source (DPI/probe/PCRF/active-test/RAN-analytics), throughput (Mbps), latency (ms), jitter (ms), packet loss (%), MOS score (for voice), video stall rate (%), video resolution achieved, QoS class identifier (QCI/5QI), DSCP marking, QoS policy applied, and SLA contract reference. Provides the service-experience perspective consumed by SLA breach detection, customer experience dashboards, and regulatory QoS reporting.'
}

Table assurance.kpi_threshold {
  kpi_threshold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the KPI threshold, alarm correlation rule, fraud detection rule, or suppression rule configuration record.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Threshold rules define breach conditions for specific KPIs. Real-world assurance systems configure alarm thresholds per KPI definition. This link enables threshold management, alarm tuning, and KPI go']
  parent_rule_kpi_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent rule if this rule is a derived or specialized version of another rule configuration. Null if this is a top-level rule.']
  applicable_service_type varchar [note: 'Service type or product category to which this rule applies, such as prepaid mobile, postpaid mobile, broadband, IPTV, VoIP, or enterprise services.']
  auto_ticket_creation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether breaches or detections triggered by this rule automatically create a trouble ticket in the incident management system.']
  correlated_alarm_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of alarm types or event codes that are correlated with the trigger alarm. Used by alarm correlation rules to identify related or cascading alarms.']
  correlation_window_minutes varchar [note: 'Time window in minutes within which correlated alarms must occur to be grouped together by this correlation rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rule configuration record was first created in the system.']
  detection_logic_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the detection logic, correlation algorithm, threshold condition, or fraud pattern that this rule implements.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method or technique used for fraud or anomaly detection: velocity check, threshold breach, pattern matching, blacklist lookup, whitelist validation, machine learning signal, or rule-based logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: velocity|threshold|pattern|blacklist|whitelist|ml_signal|rule_based — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this rule or threshold configuration becomes active and is applied by assurance monitoring systems.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this rule or threshold configuration expires and is no longer applied. Null if the rule has no expiration.']
  false_positive_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated or measured false positive rate for this detection rule, expressed as a percentage. Used to track and tune rule accuracy.']
  fraud_type varchar [note: 'Type of fraud or revenue leakage that this detection rule targets, such as subscription fraud, identity theft, SIM box fraud, international revenue share fraud, or usage anomaly. Applicable when rule_category is fraud_detection or revenue_leakage.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system that last modified this rule configuration record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rule configuration record was last modified or updated.']
  last_tuned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rule or threshold was last tuned, adjusted, or recalibrated based on performance analysis or operational feedback.']
  measurement_window_minutes varchar [note: 'Time window in minutes over which the KPI measurement or event count is evaluated before comparing to the threshold. Used for sliding window and aggregation-based detection.']
  network_domain varchar [note: 'Network domain or technology layer to which this rule applies: Radio Access Network (RAN), transport, core network, Fiber to the Home (FTTH), Gigabit Passive Optical Network (GPON), IP Multimedia Subsystem (IMS), or all domains. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RAN|transport|core|FTTH|GPON|IMS|all — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes, comments, or additional context about this rule configuration, including tuning history, known issues, or special handling instructions.']
  notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether breaches or detections triggered by this rule require immediate notification to operations teams or management.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the team, department, or organizational unit responsible for maintaining and tuning this rule, such as Network Operations Center (NOC), Security Operations Center (SOC), or Revenue Assurance.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority level assigned to this rule for processing order or escalation routing. Lower numbers typically indicate higher priority.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether breaches or detections triggered by this rule require reporting to regulatory authorities such as Federal Communications Commission (FCC) or International Telecommunication Union (ITU).']
  root_cause_determination_logic varchar [note: 'Description of the logic or algorithm used to determine the root cause alarm from a set of correlated alarms. Applicable for alarm correlation rules.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule type: KPI threshold breach detection, alarm correlation logic, alarm suppression rule, fraud detection rule, deduplication rule, or revenue leakage detection.']
  rule_identifier varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for this threshold or rule configuration, used for reference across assurance systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the threshold or rule configuration, describing its purpose and detection target.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the rule: active (in production use), inactive (disabled), testing (under evaluation), or deprecated (retired).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification assigned to breaches or alarms triggered by this rule: critical, major, minor, warning, or informational.']
  sla_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether breaches detected by this rule have potential impact on customer Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or platform from which this rule configuration originates, such as Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, ServiceNow ITSM, or custom fraud detection platform.']
  suppression_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration in minutes for which subsequent alarms of the same type are suppressed after the initial alarm is raised. Applicable for alarm suppression rules.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Specific technology or protocol to which this rule applies, such as 5G NR, LTE, VoLTE, MPLS, SD-WAN, or broadband access technology.']
  threshold_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of threshold breach that triggers an alert: above (greater than), below (less than), equal, or not equal to the threshold value.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold value, such as percent, milliseconds, Mbps, count, transactions per minute, or currency.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric threshold value that triggers a breach or alert when crossed. Used for KPI threshold rules and fraud detection velocity/threshold rules.']
  trigger_alarm_type varchar [note: 'Primary alarm type or event code that triggers this correlation or suppression rule. Applicable when rule_category is alarm_correlation or alarm_suppression.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this rule configuration, following semantic versioning format (major.minor.patch). Used to track rule evolution and changes over time.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system that created this rule configuration record.']

  Note: 'Unified reference master and single source of truth for ALL detection rules, correlation logic, suppression rules, and threshold configurations used across the assurance domain. Encompasses: (1) KPI breach thresholds for performance monitoring (threshold value, direction, measurement window, severity); (2) Alarm correlation and suppression rules from Nokia NetAct/Ericsson NM (trigger alarm type, correlated alarm types, correlation window, root cause determination logic, suppression duration); (3) Fraud detection rules (fraud type targeted, detection method — velocity/threshold/pattern/blacklist/ML-signal, trigger threshold, applicable service type, false positive rate). Captures rule/threshold ID, rule name, rule category (KPI-threshold/alarm-correlation/alarm-suppression/fraud-detection/deduplication), detection logic description, network domain applicability (RAN/transport/core/FTTH), severity classification, rule status (active/inactive/testing), effective date, expiry date, last tuned timestamp, and owning team. Provides the authoritative rules and threshold registry consumed by all assurance monitoring and detection systems.'
}

Table assurance.fraud_pattern {
  fraud_pattern_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fraud_pattern']
  evolved_from_fraud_pattern_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fraud_pattern (evolved_from_fraud_pattern_id)']
  alert_threshold decimal(10,4) [note: 'Threshold for escalating a detection to a high‑priority alert.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fraud pattern record was first created in the system.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to detect the pattern, such as rule‑based logic or machine‑learning model.']
  detection_threshold decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold that triggers the pattern (e.g., number of suspicious events within a time window).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the fraud pattern becomes applicable for detection.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the fraud pattern is no longer applied (null for indefinite).']
  fraud_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of fraud addressed by the pattern.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pattern is currently enabled for detection.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the pattern was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  mitigation_action varchar [note: 'Recommended operational response when the pattern is triggered (e.g., block account, flag for review).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the pattern.']
  pattern_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the fraud pattern (e.g., billing fraud, authentication fraud).']
  pattern_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the fraud scenario, including typical indicators and attack vectors.']
  pattern_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fraud pattern used by detection engines.']
  pattern_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the pattern within the fraud management catalog.']
  pattern_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of keywords for search and grouping (e.g., \'SIM swap,high value\').']
  pattern_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the pattern definition, supporting change management.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the pattern is required for compliance with telecom fraud regulations.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst or team that performed the last review.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score (0‑100) representing likelihood and impact of the pattern.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact rating of the fraud pattern if triggered.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the fraud pattern definition (e.g., NetAct, ServiceNow).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the fraud pattern record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fraud_pattern. Referenced by fraud_pattern_id.'
}

Table sales.lead {
  lead_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lead record. Primary key for the lead entity representing a prospective customer or sales inquiry in the early pipeline stage.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated this lead. Used for campaign attribution and ROI analysis.']
  converted_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the opportunity record created upon lead conversion. Null if not yet converted.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account record created upon lead conversion. Null if not yet converted.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative or agent assigned to follow up on this lead. Used for workload distribution and SLA tracking.']
  interest_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Leads capture prospect interest in specific products/services. Sales qualification and conversion tracking require knowing which catalog items prospects are interested in. Essential for lead-to-revenu']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner, dealer, or agent who referred this lead. Used for partner commission tracking and channel performance analysis.']
  privacy_consent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_consent. Business justification: Lead follow-up requires documented consent for contact under TCPA. Telecom sales must verify opt-in status before outreach (calls, texts, emails). Links lead contact activities to consent records, ena']
  segment_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.segment_definition. Business justification: Leads are segmented by propensity, value tier, and channel affinity for routing, prioritization, and conversion optimization. Telecom lead management systems assign leads to segments for targeted nurt']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.service_territory. Business justification: Leads are routed to sales teams by service territory for coverage qualification, pricing model application, and territory quota tracking. Essential for lead assignment workflow and sales capacity plan']
  age_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since the lead was created. Used for aging analysis and pipeline velocity metrics.']
  assigned_sales_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sales team responsible for this lead, such as consumer sales, enterprise sales, or partner channel team.']
  city varchar [note: 'City name of the lead contact address.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company or organization associated with the lead. Populated for B2B and MVNO segment leads.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date when the lead was converted to an opportunity, account, and contact in the CRM system. Null if not yet converted.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the lead contact address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lead record was first created in the system. Used for lead aging, SLA tracking, and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for estimated values. Defaults to business operating currency.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for why the lead was marked as unqualified or closed lost. Used for process improvement and lead source quality analysis.']
  do_not_call boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating the lead is on the Do Not Call list and must not be contacted via phone for marketing purposes.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the lead contact. Used for digital communication and campaign follow-up.']
  estimated_contract_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total contract value over the expected contract term. Relevant for B2B and enterprise leads with multi-year commitments.']
  estimated_monthly_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monthly recurring revenue (MRR) potential from this lead if converted to customer. Used for pipeline value forecasting.']
  first_contact_date date [note: 'Date when the lead was first contacted by sales representative. Used for SLA compliance tracking and lead aging analysis.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'First name or given name of the prospective customer contact.']
  follow_up_sla_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours within which the lead must be contacted per business SLA policy. Varies by lead priority and segment.']
  grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade representing the leads demographic and firmographic fit with ideal customer profile. A is highest fit, F is lowest.']
  interest_product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product or service category the lead expressed interest in, such as wireless, broadband, fiber internet, IPTV, enterprise connectivity, or IoT solutions.']
  interest_service_type varchar [note: 'Specific service type the lead is interested in, such as 5G mobile, FTTH, SD-WAN, VoIP, IPTV, or managed services.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contact attempt or interaction with the lead.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lead record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Last name or family name of the prospective customer contact.']
  lead_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the lead, typically auto-generated in format LED-XXXXXXXX. Used for external communication and tracking.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the lead was captured. Values include web form, marketing campaign, customer referral, retail walk-in, partner channel, event, or social media. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_form|campaign|referral|walk_in|partner|event|social_media — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lead_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lead in the sales pipeline. Values include new, contacted, qualified (SQL), unqualified, converted to opportunity, closed lost, or nurturing (MQL). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|contacted|qualified|unqualified|converted|closed_lost|nurturing — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number of the lead contact. Used for SMS campaigns and mobile-first engagement.']
  next_follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next follow-up activity with the lead. Used for task management and pipeline hygiene.']
  opt_in_email boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the lead has consented to receive email marketing communications. Required for CAN-SPAM and GDPR compliance.']
  opt_in_phone boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the lead has consented to receive phone marketing calls. Required for TCPA and Do Not Call registry compliance.']
  opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the lead has consented to receive SMS marketing communications. Required for TCPA compliance.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the lead. May include mobile or landline number.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the lead contact address. Critical for service availability validation and FTTH/GPON coverage checks.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification for follow-up urgency. Hot leads require immediate contact, warm leads require timely follow-up, cold leads are lower priority.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Marketing and sales qualification status. MQL (Marketing Qualified Lead) indicates marketing acceptance; SQL (Sales Qualified Lead) indicates sales acceptance and readiness for opportunity conversion.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Unique referral code or tracking identifier provided by the referring party or campaign. Used for attribution and incentive fulfillment.']
  sales_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational sales territory to which this lead is assigned, based on address or segment rules.']
  score varchar [note: 'Numeric score representing the leads propensity to convert, based on demographic fit, engagement behavior, and predictive analytics. Higher scores indicate higher conversion probability.']
  source_detail varchar [note: 'Additional detail about the lead source, such as specific campaign name, partner name, event name, or referral source identifier.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code of the lead contact address. Used for regulatory compliance and territory routing.']
  street_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the lead contact or business location. Used for service availability checks and territory assignment.']

  Note: 'Master record for a prospective customer or sales inquiry in the early pipeline stage. Captures lead source (web form, campaign, referral, walk-in, partner), contact details, segment classification (B2C/B2B/MVNO), qualification status (MQL/SQL), assigned sales rep, campaign attribution, lead score, and aging metrics. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead Management module. Represents the top-of-funnel entity for all sales pipelines with defined SLA for follow-up.'
}

Business justification: Telecom sales opportunities are segmented (enterprise, SMB, government, high-value) for pipeline analysis, forecasting, and resource allocation. Segment classification drives sales strategy and quota ']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.service_territory. Business justification: Opportunities require service territory for network availability assessment, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and sales forecasting by geography. Distinct from service_address_id; territory driv']
  slice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_slice. Business justification: 5G network slicing is sold as a distinct product offering to enterprise customers requiring dedicated virtual networks with specific SLA guarantees. Sales opportunities for slice-based services must r']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the existing subscriber if this opportunity represents an upsell, cross-sell, or service expansion for a current customer. Null for new customer acquisition opportunities.']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'Actual date when the opportunity reached a terminal closed-won or closed-lost state. Null for opportunities still in active sales stages.']
  competitive_threat_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of competitive pressure and risk of losing the opportunity to a competitor. Used for resource prioritization and executive escalation.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the opportunity record was first created in the system. Audit field for lifecycle tracking.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: b2c_consumer|b2c_soho|b2b_smb|b2b_mid_market|b2b_enterprise|mvno_wholesale|government — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deposit_required_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Security deposit amount required based on credit assessment. Zero if no deposit is required. Expressed in the opportunity currency.']

  estimated_mrr decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected monthly recurring revenue from subscription services if the opportunity closes successfully. Key metric for subscription-based business model forecasting.']

  expected_close_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the opportunity is expected to reach a terminal state (closed-won or closed-lost). Used for sales forecasting and pipeline management.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'Sales forecast classification indicating confidence level: pipeline (early stage), best case (possible), commit (high confidence), or closed (won). Used for revenue forecasting and quota tracking.']
  key_stakeholders varchar [note: 'Names and roles of key decision-makers and influencers within the prospect organization. Used for relationship mapping and sales strategy in complex B2B deals.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent sales activity (call, meeting, email, task) logged against this opportunity. Used for pipeline hygiene and stale opportunity identification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the opportunity record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  lead_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating lead record that was qualified and converted into this opportunity. Null for opportunities created directly without a lead stage.']
  next_step varchar [note: 'Description of the next action or milestone required to progress the opportunity. Used for sales activity tracking and pipeline hygiene.']
  number_of_devices varchar [note: 'Quantity of customer premises equipment, handsets, routers, or IoT devices included in the opportunity. Used for inventory allocation and logistics planning.']
  number_of_lines varchar [note: 'Quantity of mobile lines, broadband connections, or service instances included in this opportunity. Used for volume-based pricing and capacity planning.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the sales opportunity, typically describing the prospect and solution being pursued.']
  opportunity_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the opportunity, used in communications and reporting. Format: OPP-NNNNNNNN.']
  opportunity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the opportunity based on the nature of the sales pursuit: new customer acquisition, upsell to existing subscriber, cross-sell of additional services, contract renewal, customer winback, or technology migration.']
  primary_product_offering varchar [note: 'Name or description of the primary product or service offering being sold in this opportunity (e.g., 5G Unlimited Plan, Fiber 1Gbps, Enterprise SD-WAN, MVNO Wholesale Agreement).']
  probability_percent varchar [note: 'Estimated probability of winning this opportunity, expressed as an integer percentage (0-100). Used for weighted pipeline forecasting.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the product or service being offered: mobile postpaid, mobile prepaid, fixed broadband, fiber, enterprise connectivity, IoT, cloud services, managed services, or wholesale. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_postpaid|mobile_prepaid|fixed_broadband|fiber|enterprise_connectivity|iot|cloud_services|managed_services|wholesale — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  proposed_service_start_date date [note: 'Target date for service activation and contract commencement if the opportunity is won. Used for fulfillment planning and resource allocation.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal price quote or proposal document associated with this opportunity. Links to quote entity in sales domain.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the opportunity is being pursued: retail store, telesales center, digital web, mobile app, authorized dealer, independent agent, partner, or direct enterprise sales team. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail_store|telesales|digital_web|digital_mobile_app|dealer|agent|partner|direct_sales — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sales_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the opportunity in the sales pipeline lifecycle. Tracks progression from initial prospecting through qualification, proposal, negotiation, and terminal closed-won or closed-lost states. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|qualification|needs_analysis|proposal|negotiation|closed_won|closed_lost — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  strategic_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this opportunity is for a strategic or key account requiring executive sponsorship and special handling. True for enterprise strategic accounts.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Underlying network technology for the proposed service: 5G, LTE, 3G, fiber to the home, fiber to the building, cable, DSL, fixed wireless, satellite, SD-WAN, or MPLS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 5g|lte|3g|fiber_ftth|fiber_fttb|cable|dsl|fixed_wireless|satellite|sd_wan|mpls — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  whitespace_analysis varchar [note: 'For B2B enterprise opportunities, describes untapped revenue potential and expansion opportunities within the account (additional locations, services, or business units not yet penetrated).']
  win_loss_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for winning or losing the opportunity. Captured at close for sales effectiveness analysis and process improvement. Examples: price, coverage, features, competitor, timing, budget.']

  Note: 'Core sales opportunity record representing a qualified sales pursuit for a prospect or existing subscriber, serving as the central pipeline entity from qualification through closed-won handoff to order fulfillment. Tracks opportunity stage, estimated contract value, close date, sales channel (retail/telesales/digital/dealer), segment (B2C/B2B/MVNO wholesale), win/loss reason, associated product offerings, and fulfillment handoff details. For enterprise/B2B accounts, includes strategic account planning attributes: growth objectives, whitespace analysis, key stakeholder map, planned upsell/cross-sell initiatives, competitive threats, and target MRR growth. Terminal closed-won state triggers order creation in the order domain. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Opportunity Management module.'
}

Table sales.quote {
  quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for quote']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Quotes require billing account reference for credit limit checks, payment terms validation, billing address verification, account hierarchy in B2B scenarios, and consolidated billing eligibility asses']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise quotes require corporate account linkage for contract pricing validation, enterprise discount scheme application, approval workflow routing, and consolidated billing setup. Critical for B2B']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or user who created the quote.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Quotes must identify the authorized contact for presentation, approval, and signature. Required for contract formation, legal compliance, and tracking quote acceptance workflow in telecommunications s']
  dealer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer or agent who generated the quote, if applicable. Used for commission calculation and channel performance tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or approver who authorized the quote, if approval was required.']
  enterprise_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.site. Business justification: Enterprise service quotes require site-specific details for accurate pricing of installation, CPE deployment, circuit provisioning, and site-based SLA tiers. Essential for multi-site quote generation ']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the quote.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales opportunity that generated this quote. Links to the Salesforce CRM opportunity record.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.promotion. Business justification: Quotes currently have promotional_code (STRING) to capture promotion references. This should be normalized to a FK relationship to the promotion master table for referential integrity, promotion rule ']
  accepted_date date [note: 'Date when the customer formally accepted the quote, triggering contract creation or order fulfillment.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quote requires management approval before being presented to the customer, typically based on discount thresholds or contract value.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the quote received internal management approval.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the customer will be billed for recurring charges: monthly, quarterly, annually, or one-time.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competitor that the customer is evaluating or currently using, captured during the quoting process for competitive intelligence.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the proposed contract term in months (e.g., 12, 24, 36). Used to calculate Total Contract Value (TCV).']
  created_date date [note: 'Date when the quote was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the quote (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Target customer segment for this quote: Business-to-Consumer (B2C) retail, Business-to-Business (B2B) small-medium enterprise, B2B enterprise, Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) wholesale, or government.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from partner systems, dealer portals, or third-party quoting platforms.']
  is_primary_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this version is the primary/active version of the quote. Only one version per quote number should be primary.']
  modified_date date [note: 'Date when the quote was last modified.']
  monthly_recurring_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR): the predictable monthly revenue stream from subscription-based services in this quote.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments from the sales team regarding the quote, customer negotiations, special considerations, or follow-up actions.']
  one_time_charges decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total one-time charges in the quote, including activation fees, installation fees, Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) costs, and setup charges.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms specified in the quote: net 30/60/90 days, prepaid, postpaid, or due on receipt.']
  presented_date date [note: 'Date when the quote was formally presented or sent to the customer.']
  quote_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the quote, including service bundle details, special terms, and customer-specific notes.']
  quote_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title for the quote, typically reflecting the service bundle or customer segment.']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the quote. Format: QT-YYYYMMDD followed by sequence.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quote: draft (being prepared), pending approval (awaiting internal sign-off), approved (ready to present), presented (sent to customer), accepted (customer agreed), rejected (customer declined), expired (validity period lapsed), or withdrawn (cancelled by sales). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|presented|accepted|rejected|expired|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quote_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quote based on the nature of the commercial transaction: new service acquisition, service upgrade, contract renewal, service modification, or service disconnect.']
  rejected_date date [note: 'Date when the customer formally rejected or declined the quote.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or categorized reason provided by the customer or sales team for quote rejection.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the quote was generated: direct sales team, retail store, online self-service, telesales, dealer, agent, or partner. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_sales|retail_store|online|telesales|dealer|agent|partner — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_activation_date date [note: 'Proposed or committed date when services will be activated for the customer if the quote is accepted.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system or application where the quote was originally created (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Oracle CPQ, custom quoting tool).']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Subtotal amount before taxes and fees, representing the sum of all line item charges after discounts.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the quote, including sales tax, value-added tax (VAT), goods and services tax (GST), and regulatory fees.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Legal terms and conditions associated with the quote, including Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments, cancellation policies, and liability clauses.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Grand total amount of the quote including all charges, discounts, taxes, and fees. This is the final amount presented to the customer.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Contract Value (TCV): the total revenue expected over the full contract term, including all recurring and one-time charges.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied across all line items in the quote, including promotional discounts, volume discounts, and negotiated price reductions.']
  total_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall discount percentage applied to the quote, calculated as total discount divided by list price total.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the quote validity period. Quote pricing and terms are guaranteed from this date.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'End date of the quote validity period. Quote expires after this date and pricing/terms are no longer guaranteed.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the quote. Increments with each revision to track quote evolution during negotiation.']
  win_probability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability of winning the deal and converting the quote to a contract, expressed as a percentage (0-100).']

  Note: 'Commercial quote issued to a prospect or existing customer detailing proposed telecom services, pricing, discounts, promotional offers, and contract terms with full itemized line-level detail. Tracks quote version, validity period, approval status, total contract value (TCV), monthly recurring revenue (MRR), associated opportunity, and individual line items: product catalog reference, unit price, discount applied, quantity, recurring vs one-time charge classification, promotional code, and bundle composition. Supports B2C retail, B2B enterprise, and MVNO wholesale quoting workflows with complete pricing breakdown at both header and line level.'
}

Table sales.quote_line {
  quote_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quote line item. Primary key.']
  addon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.addon. Business justification: Many quote lines represent add-on services (international roaming, extra data, premium content). This link enables add-on attach rate analysis, cross-sell effectiveness tracking, and incremental reven']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a product bundle if this line is part of a multi-service package. Enables bundle pricing logic and cross-service dependencies.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Quote lines itemize specific catalog products being quoted. Essential for quote-to-order accuracy, product revenue attribution, and ensuring quoted items match orderable catalog. Removes denormalized ']
  cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Quote lines for equipment upgrades or replacements reference the specific asset being replaced to calculate trade-in credit, validate compatibility, and determine installation requirements. Standard q']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service delivery address for this line item. Critical for Fiber to the Home (FTTH), fixed broadband, and enterprise connectivity services.']
  device_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.device_offering. Business justification: When quoting device sales (phones, routers, modems), quote lines must reference the specific device offering with its subsidy/installment terms. Essential for device revenue tracking, inventory manage']
  fiber_cable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fiber_cable. Business justification: Wholesale fiber lease quote lines reference specific cable segments being leased to enterprise or carrier customers. Required for dark fiber and IRU sales, capacity validation, and route planning.']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: Enterprise and wholesale quotes for dedicated network services, colocation, or managed infrastructure reference specific network equipment to be provisioned, upgraded, or allocated to the customer. St']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Fiber service quote lines reference existing ONT assets when quoting upgrades, replacements, or bandwidth increases to determine if new equipment is required and calculate installation costs.']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.ott_platform. Business justification: Telecommunications providers bundle third-party OTT platforms (Netflix, Disney+, HBO Max) as standalone quote line items. Required for OTT partner billing reconciliation, provisioning workflows, and r']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_package. Business justification: Quote lines itemize content packages sold to customers (e.g., Premium Sports Package, Family Entertainment Bundle). Essential for quote generation, pricing calculations, order fulfillment, and revenue']
  parent_line_quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to another quote line if this line is a dependent or child item. Supports hierarchical quote structures for complex telecom solutions.']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: Quote lines must reference the specific price plan being offered to ensure billing system alignment, contract accuracy, and correct revenue recognition. Critical for quote-to-cash process integrity an']
  primary_quote_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative responsible for this quote line. Used for quota tracking and commission attribution.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commercial quote containing this line item. Links this line to the overall quote header.']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product catalog entry representing the specific telecom product, service plan, or bundle being quoted. Links to the master product catalog.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line requires management approval due to special pricing, high discount, or non-standard terms. Triggers approval workflow routing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this quote line was approved. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking and quote-to-order cycle time analysis.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which recurring charges will be billed. Defines the billing cycle for Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) calculation and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monthly|quarterly|annually|one_time|usage_based|daily|weekly — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the charge structure for this line item. Distinguishes between one-time fees, recurring subscription charges, and usage-based billing models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: one_time|recurring|usage_based|installation|activation|termination|overage — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commission_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated commission or dealer compensation amount for this line item. Used for sales incentive calculation and partner settlement.']
  commission_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item qualifies for sales commission or dealer compensation. Some promotional or subsidized offerings may be commission-exempt.']
  configuration_details varchar [note: 'Technical configuration parameters or service specifications for this line item. Captures bandwidth tiers, Quality of Service (QoS) levels, Service Level Agreement (SLA) parameters, or Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) specifications.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the service commitment in months for this line item. Used to calculate Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) and enforce early termination penalties.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this quote line record was first created in the system. Audit field for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Critical for multi-currency quote management.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer or agent partner if this line was sold through an indirect channel. Critical for Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) and dealer commission management.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary discount applied to this line item. Calculated as (list_price - unit_price) * quantity.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the list price. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 15.00 for 15% discount). Used for promotional pricing and volume discounts.']
  extended_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total line amount calculated as unit_price multiplied by quantity. Represents the subtotal for this line before taxes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the quote for ordering and display purposes. Determines the presentation order of line items on the quote document.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this quote line item. Tracks approval workflow and line-level acceptance or rejection.']
  list_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard catalog price per unit before any discounts or promotions are applied. Represents the published rate card price.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this quote line record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data lineage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions related to this quote line. Captures custom requirements, provisioning notes, or customer-specific terms.']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Marketing promotion or campaign code applied to this line item. Enables tracking of promotional effectiveness and discount attribution.']
  promotional_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the promotional offer applied to this line. Displayed on customer-facing quote documents.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Number of units or subscriptions being quoted for this line item. May represent device count, user licenses, circuit count, or service instances depending on product type.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which this quote line was originated. Enables channel performance analysis and commission calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|retail|online|telesales|partner|dealer|agent|enterprise|wholesale — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Proposed date when the service commitment ends for this line item. Used for contract renewal management and churn forecasting.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Proposed date when service delivery will commence for this line item. Critical for provisioning scheduling and revenue recognition start date.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this line item. Includes sales tax, VAT, GST, or other jurisdiction-specific telecommunications taxes.']
  total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final total for this line item including all charges and taxes. Calculated as extended_amount plus tax_amount.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit by which the quantity is measured. Critical for bandwidth products, capacity-based services, and subscription models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|subscription|license|circuit|port|line|user|device|gb|tb|mbps|gbps|month|year — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual price per unit after discounts and promotions have been applied. This is the effective rate the customer will be charged.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a commercial quote representing a specific product, service plan, or bundle being offered. Captures product catalog reference, unit price, discount applied, quantity, recurring vs one-time charge classification, and promotional code. Enables itemized pricing breakdown for complex multi-service telecom quotes.'
}

Critical']

Required for contract fulfillment, billin']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner, dealer, or agent organization if the contract was sold through an indirect channel. Null for direct sales.']
  primary_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Contracts formalize the sale of specific offerings. This link is essential for contract compliance monitoring, renewal management, product lifecycle tracking, and ensuring contracted services match pr']
  primary_sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or account executive responsible for this contract.']

  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Essential for telecommunications contract administration,']
