  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the business owner or executive who approved this segment definition for operational use. Null if segment has not been approved.']
  churn_risk_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Churn risk score threshold (0-100 scale) above which accounts in this segment trigger retention intervention workflows. Lower thresholds indicate more proactive retention strategies for high-value segments.']
  contract_term_minimum_months varchar [note: 'Minimum contract commitment period in months required for accounts in this segment. Longer terms may qualify for better pricing. Null if segment has no minimum term requirement.']
  contract_term_standard_months varchar [note: 'Standard contract duration in months typically offered to accounts in this segment. Represents the most common commitment period for this customer class.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this segment definition. Supports accountability and change management processes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credit verification is mandatory for new accounts in this segment during onboarding. True for segments requiring financial vetting, false for pre-approved segments such as government or established enterprise relationships.']
  deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit is required for new accounts in this segment. True for higher-risk segments or those with limited credit history, false for established enterprise segments with strong credit profiles.']
  discount_tier varchar [note: 'Standard discount tier available to accounts in this segment. Higher tiers receive greater discounts on products and services. Custom tier indicates individually negotiated discount structures for strategic accounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active segments. Used for historical reporting and migration tracking when segmentation strategies evolve.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition became active and available for account classification. Supports temporal segmentation strategy changes and historical analysis.']
  go_to_market_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary sales and distribution approach for acquiring and serving accounts in this segment. Direct sales uses internal sales force, channel partner leverages resellers and agents, hybrid combines both, digital-only uses web-based acquisition, strategic alliance involves co-selling with technology partners.']
  industry_vertical_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of industry vertical codes that qualify for this segment. Used for vertical-based segmentation. Examples: FINANCE, HEALTHCARE, MANUFACTURING, GOVERNMENT, EDUCATION. Null if segment is not restricted by industry vertical.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this segment definition. Tracks ownership of segmentation strategy changes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last updated. Tracks changes to segment definitions, criteria, or business rules over time.']
  maximum_annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annual revenue threshold in base currency for an enterprise account to qualify for this segment. Defines upper bound for revenue band. Null if segment has no upper revenue limit or does not use revenue criteria.']
  maximum_employee_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of employees for an enterprise account to qualify for this segment. Defines upper bound for size band. Null if segment has no upper employee limit or does not use employee count criteria.']
  minimum_annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum annual revenue threshold in base currency for an enterprise account to qualify for this segment. Used for revenue-based segmentation. Null if segment does not use revenue criteria.']
  minimum_employee_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of employees for an enterprise account to qualify for this segment. Used for size-based segmentation. Null if segment does not use employee count criteria.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, special considerations, migration notes, or strategic rationale not captured in structured fields. Used for knowledge transfer and decision documentation.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Business unit, department, or executive role responsible for strategy and performance of this segment. Examples: Enterprise Sales VP, Government Sales Director, Mid-Market Business Unit, Wholesale Operations.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days from invoice date for accounts in this segment. Common values include 15, 30, 45, or 60 days. Larger enterprise segments typically receive extended payment terms.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing strategy applied to accounts in this segment. Standard uses list pricing, volume discount applies graduated discounts based on spend, custom negotiated allows bespoke pricing for strategic accounts, tiered uses predefined price bands, flat rate offers fixed monthly fees.']
  reporting_category varchar [note: 'Financial and operational reporting category for revenue and performance metrics aggregation. Used for executive dashboards, regulatory filings, and investor reporting. Examples: Enterprise, Business, Government, Wholesale.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment for operational use and system integration. Examples: LRG_ENT, MID_MKT, GOV_FED, SME_TIER1.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the segment characteristics, target customer profile, and business rationale for segmentation. Includes criteria such as employee count, revenue thresholds, service complexity, and strategic importance.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the enterprise segment. Examples: Large Enterprise, Mid-Market, Government Federal, Small and Medium Enterprise Tier 1, Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) Host, Wholesale Carrier.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition. Active segments are in use for account classification and go-to-market strategies. Deprecated segments are being phased out. Proposed segments are under review for future implementation.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segmentation methodology used. Revenue-based segments by Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) or Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR), size-based by employee count, vertical-based by industry, service-based by solution complexity, strategic for key accounts, or hybrid combining multiple criteria.']
  service_eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining which services and solutions are available to accounts in this segment. May include eligibility for Software-Defined Wide Area Network (SD-WAN), Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS), dedicated fiber, unified communications, Internet of Things (IoT) platforms, managed security services, and premium Service Level Agreement (SLA) tiers.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Default Service Level Agreement tier for accounts in this segment. Defines response times, uptime guarantees, Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) commitments, and escalation procedures. Mission-critical tier provides highest priority support and fastest restoration times.']
  support_channel_access varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of support channels available to accounts in this segment. May include web portal, mobile app, phone support, email, chat, dedicated hotline, on-site support, Network Operations Center (NOC) direct access, and technical account manager.']
  target_customer_lifetime_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Expected Customer Lifetime Value in base currency for accounts in this segment over a standard planning horizon. Used for investment prioritization and customer acquisition cost justification. Null if not calculated for this segment.']
  upsell_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for proactive upsell and cross-sell campaigns targeting accounts in this segment. Critical priority segments receive dedicated sales resources and personalized offers. Low priority segments receive automated digital campaigns only.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining enterprise customer segmentation taxonomy — classifying corporate accounts by size, revenue band, industry vertical, and service eligibility. Segments include Large Enterprise, Mid-Market, Government, SME, MVNO Host, and Wholesale. Drives differentiated pricing, account management models, and go-to-market strategies across the B2B customer base.'
}

Table enterprise.ticket {
  ticket_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the enterprise support ticket. Primary key for the ticket entity.']
  circuit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the network circuit affected by the issue. Used for rapid technical troubleshooting and network operations coordination.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enterprise customer account that opened this ticket. Links to the B2B account master.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Enterprise support tickets generate operational costs (labor, escalations, vendor engagement) allocated to cost centers for support cost analysis and customer cost-to-serve calculations.']
  dq_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.dq_issue. Business justification: Enterprise support tickets often surface data quality issues (incorrect site addresses, billing account mismatches, service configuration errors). Links operational tickets to formal DQ issues for roo']
  enterprise_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enterprise site location experiencing the issue. Links to enterprise site master for geographic and service context.']
  location_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.location_address. Business justification: Enterprise trouble tickets reference specific service addresses for field technician dispatch, outage correlation with network events, customer communication, and geographic clustering of fault patter']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Enterprise support tickets can be specific to managed service instances (SD-WAN, MPLS, UC). While ticket already links to svc_instance (generic service), adding managed_service_id enables direct linki']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: Enterprise trouble tickets reference specific failed equipment (routers, switches, SD-WAN appliances) for RMA processing, dispatch optimization, and root cause analysis. Field technicians and NOC engi']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.noc_incident. Business justification: Enterprise customer tickets escalate to or reference NOC incidents when network-level events affect their services. TAMs correlate customer impact with NOC incident status for communication and SLA as']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: FTTH/FTTB trouble tickets for SMB and residential enterprise services reference specific ONT devices for remote diagnostics, firmware troubleshooting, and RMA processing. Field dispatch systems requir']
  outage_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.outage_record. Business justification: Enterprise tickets reference network outage records when customer-reported issues stem from documented outages. Required for SLA exclusion determination, root cause validation, and customer communicat']
  parent_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ticket (parent_ticket_id)']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to location.premise. Business justification: Tickets for premise-based services require premise linkage for network element identification, service history lookup, last-mile infrastructure fault isolation, and correlation with network outages af']
  related_change_change_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a change record if this ticket requires or resulted from a network or service configuration change.']
  related_problem_problem_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a known problem record if this ticket is linked to a systemic issue requiring root cause analysis and permanent fix.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific managed service instance experiencing the issue. Links to enterprise managed service inventory.']
  ticket_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the support engineer or technical account manager currently assigned to work on this ticket.']
  ticket_technical_account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dedicated Technical Account Manager assigned to this enterprise customer. TAM provides oversight and escalation management for high-priority tickets.']
  actual_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time in minutes from ticket creation to resolution. Used for SLA performance measurement and operational efficiency analysis.']
  actual_response_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time in minutes from ticket creation to first response by support team. Used for SLA performance measurement.']
  affected_user_count varchar [note: 'Number of end users at the enterprise customer site impacted by the issue. Used for severity assessment and resource allocation.']
  assigned_to_team varchar [note: 'Name of the support team or technical group currently responsible for this ticket. Used for workload distribution and escalation routing.']
  business_impact varchar [note: 'Description of the business impact and operational consequences of the issue as articulated by the customer. Used for priority assessment and executive reporting.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ticket was formally closed after customer confirmation or automatic closure period. Represents end of ticket lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ticket was first created in the system. Represents the official start of the SLA clock for response and resolution tracking.']
  customer_feedback varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback provided by the customer after ticket resolution. Used for service improvement and quality assurance.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction rating provided by the enterprise contact. Typically on a 1-5 scale used for service quality measurement.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation tier of the ticket. Level 0 is standard support, higher levels indicate management or executive escalation for critical issues.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for escalating the ticket to higher support tiers or management. Documents customer impact and urgency drivers.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the support team provided the first substantive response to the customer. Used for SLA response time compliance measurement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to any ticket field. Used for audit trail and activity tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes and comments added by support staff during ticket lifecycle. Not visible to customer but used for knowledge transfer and audit.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the ticket based on impact and urgency. Determines SLA response and resolution targets.']
  reported_by_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the enterprise customer contact who reported the issue. Used for ticket updates and resolution notifications.']
  reported_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the enterprise customer contact who reported the issue or opened the ticket.']
  reported_by_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the enterprise customer contact who reported the issue for urgent escalations and callbacks.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the actions taken to resolve the issue and restore service. Provided to customer and archived for knowledge management.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the issue was resolved and service restored. Stops the SLA resolution clock and triggers customer notification.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Technical root cause of the issue identified during troubleshooting and resolution. Used for knowledge base and preventive action planning.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Technical severity classification indicating the scope and impact of the issue on enterprise operations. Sev1 represents complete service outage, Sev4 represents minor inconvenience.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether this ticket breached contracted SLA response or resolution targets. Triggers credit entitlement and escalation processes.']
  sla_resolution_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Contracted maximum time in minutes for issue resolution based on priority and SLA tier. Used for SLA compliance tracking and breach detection.']
  sla_response_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Contracted maximum time in minutes for initial response to the customer based on priority and SLA tier. Used for SLA compliance tracking.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Contracted SLA tier for this customer account that determines response and resolution time commitments for this ticket.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the ticket was submitted by the customer. Used for channel effectiveness analysis and routing optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|email|portal|chat|api|tam|noc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed classification within the primary category for granular issue tracking and knowledge base routing.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the issue or request provided by the customer or support agent. Used for quick identification and search.']
  tam_involved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether the Technical Account Manager has been engaged in this ticket for escalation or customer relationship management.']
  ticket_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the issue type for routing and reporting purposes. Aligns with enterprise service portfolio categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: network|voice|data|security|billing|provisioning|performance|configuration — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ticket_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the issue, symptoms, and business impact as reported by the customer or documented by the support agent.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible ticket reference number provided to the customer for tracking and communication purposes.']
  ticket_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the support ticket in the enterprise workflow. Tracks progression from initial submission through resolution and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|assigned|in_progress|pending_customer|pending_vendor|resolved|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vendor_ticket_reference varchar [note: 'External ticket or case number from third-party vendor or carrier if the issue requires vendor escalation or coordination.']
  workaround_applied varchar [note: 'Description of any temporary workaround implemented to restore service while permanent resolution is in progress.']

  Note: 'Enterprise customer support ticket record for B2B accounts — distinct from consumer cases, tracking enterprise-grade SLA response times, escalation paths, and TAM involvement'
}

Table enterprise.bandwidth_profile {
  bandwidth_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bandwidth profile. Primary key for this entity.']
  enterprise_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.segment. Business justification: Bandwidth profiles are designed for specific customer segments (e.g., SMB profiles with lower CIR, enterprise profiles with higher SLA targets). Currently bandwidth_profile has applicable_customer_seg']
  parent_profile_bandwidth_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent bandwidth profile for hierarchical bandwidth management. Enables envelope-based policing where multiple child profiles share a parent bandwidth constraint. Null for top-level profiles.']
  upgraded_from_bandwidth_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bandwidth_profile (upgraded_from_bandwidth_profile_id)']
  application_priority varchar [note: 'Primary application type this bandwidth profile is optimized for: voice (VoIP, UC), video (conferencing, streaming), data (general business applications), management (network control), scavenger (low-priority bulk).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for this bandwidth profile. New or modified profiles require approval before becoming active for provisioning.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bandwidth profile was approved for production use. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who approved this bandwidth profile for production use. Null if not yet approved.']
  availability_target_percentage decimal(5,3) [note: 'Target service availability percentage for circuits using this bandwidth profile, typically expressed as uptime percentage (e.g., 99.99% = four nines).']
  bidirectional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bandwidth profile applies symmetrically to both ingress and egress traffic (true) or is unidirectional (false). Most enterprise services use symmetric profiles.']
  burst_allowance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage above CIR that burst traffic is allowed, calculated as ((PIR - CIR) / CIR) * 100. Represents the burst capacity as a percentage of committed rate.']
  cbs_bytes varchar [note: 'Committed Burst Size - the maximum amount of data in bytes that can be transmitted at line rate while remaining conformant to the CIR. Defines the token bucket depth for committed traffic.']
  cir_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Committed Information Rate - the guaranteed minimum bandwidth in megabits per second that the service provider commits to deliver under normal conditions. This is the baseline bandwidth the customer can always expect.']
  color_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the bandwidth profile policing is color-blind (ignores existing packet color markings) or color-aware (respects pre-marked packet colors from upstream devices).']
  cos_marking varchar [note: 'IEEE 802.1p Class of Service priority value (0-7) for Layer 2 traffic marking. Used for VLAN-tagged Ethernet frames to indicate priority at the data link layer.']
  coupling_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused committed tokens can be used for excess traffic (true) or if committed and excess buckets are independent (false). Affects burst behavior.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this bandwidth profile record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bandwidth profile record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.']
  dscp_marking varchar [note: 'DSCP value used to mark packets conforming to this bandwidth profile. Can be specified as a standard class (EF, AF11-AF43, CS0-CS7, BE) or numeric value (0-63).']
  ebs_bytes varchar [note: 'Excess Burst Size - the maximum amount of excess data in bytes that can be transmitted above the CBS up to the PIR. Defines the token bucket depth for excess traffic.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this bandwidth profile is retired and no longer available for new provisioning. Existing services may continue using the profile until migration. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this bandwidth profile becomes active and available for provisioning new services. Part of the profile lifecycle management.']
  envelope_coupling_flag boolean [note: 'For hierarchical bandwidth profiles, indicates whether unused tokens from one envelope can be shared with another envelope within the same profile hierarchy.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage scope for services using this bandwidth profile: local (metro), regional (multi-city), national (country-wide), international (multi-country), global (worldwide).']
  jitter_target_ms decimal(8,3) [note: 'Target packet delay variation (jitter) in milliseconds for traffic using this bandwidth profile. Critical for real-time applications like VoIP and video.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who most recently modified this bandwidth profile record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bandwidth profile record was most recently modified. Updated on every change to track the latest modification time.']
  latency_target_ms decimal(8,3) [note: 'Target one-way network latency in milliseconds for traffic using this bandwidth profile. Part of the QoS performance objectives.']
  metering_type varchar [note: 'Token bucket metering algorithm used for bandwidth policing: single rate two color (srTCM), single rate three color, or two rate three color (trTCM). Determines how traffic is classified and policed.']
  mtu_bytes varchar [note: 'Maximum Transmission Unit - the largest packet size in bytes that can be transmitted without fragmentation for this bandwidth profile. Standard Ethernet is 1500 bytes; jumbo frames can be up to 9000 bytes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments about this bandwidth profile, including configuration rationale, special considerations, or operational guidance.']
  oversubscription_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of total subscribed bandwidth to available physical bandwidth on the network segment. A value of 1.0 means no oversubscription; higher values indicate statistical multiplexing.']
  packet_loss_target_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'Target maximum packet loss ratio as a percentage for traffic using this bandwidth profile. Typically expressed as a very small percentage (e.g., 0.01% = 0.0001).']
  pir_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Peak Information Rate - the maximum bandwidth in megabits per second that the service can burst to when network capacity is available. Traffic above CIR up to PIR may be delivered on a best-effort basis.']
  policing_action varchar [note: 'Action taken on traffic exceeding the bandwidth profile limits: drop (discard excess packets), remark (change DSCP/CoS to lower priority), shape (buffer and delay), pass (allow through without action).']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Billing model for services using this bandwidth profile: flat rate (fixed monthly), usage based (per GB), tiered (volume discounts), burstable billing (95th percentile).']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the bandwidth profile, used for external reference and catalog management. Unique across all profiles.']
  profile_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the bandwidth profile, including use cases, target customer segments, and technical characteristics.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the bandwidth profile, describing its purpose or tier (e.g., Gold Enterprise 100M, Silver SD-WAN 50M).']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bandwidth profile. Active profiles are available for provisioning; deprecated profiles are phased out; retired profiles are no longer used.']
  qos_tier varchar [note: 'Quality of Service tier indicating the priority and treatment level for traffic using this bandwidth profile. Higher tiers receive better latency, jitter, and packet loss guarantees.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of enterprise service this bandwidth profile applies to: MPLS (Multiprotocol Label Switching), SD-WAN (Software-Defined Wide Area Network), DIA (Dedicated Internet Access), VPLS (Virtual Private LAN Service), EVPL (Ethernet Virtual Private Line), EPL (Ethernet Private Line).']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA tier associated with this bandwidth profile, determining performance guarantees, support response times, and credit entitlements for breaches.']
  traffic_class varchar [note: 'Primary traffic class mapping for this bandwidth profile using DiffServ Code Point (DSCP) classes: EF (Expedited Forwarding - real-time), AF1-AF4 (Assured Forwarding classes 1-4), BE (Best Effort).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this bandwidth profile definition. Incremented when profile parameters are modified, enabling change tracking and audit trails.']

  Note: 'Defines committed and burst bandwidth profiles for enterprise MPLS/SD-WAN/DIA services — CIR, PIR, burst allowance, traffic class mappings, and QoS parameters'
}

Table enterprise.site_access {
  site_access_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this site access authorization record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the workforce manager or security administrator who granted this site access authorization. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  enterprise_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the enterprise site for which access authorization has been granted.']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the technician who has been granted access authorization to the enterprise site.']
  access_badge_number varchar [note: 'Physical or electronic badge number issued for site access. Corresponds to access_badge_number from detection data. Used for physical security and entry logging.']
  access_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the site access authorization indicating whether the technician can currently access the site.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site access authorization record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when site access authorization expires and must be renewed. Corresponds to site_access_expiry_date from detection data. Used for compliance and security management.']
  granted_date date [note: 'Date when site access authorization was granted to the technician. Corresponds to site_access_granted_date from detection data.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site access authorization record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  last_service_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent field technician visit to this site for installation, maintenance, or troubleshooting. [Moved from site: This attribute currently exists on the site product but actually represents the most recent visit by ANY technician. In the M:N model, last_site_visit_date in the site_access association tracks visits per technician-site combination, providing more granular operational data. The site-level attribute may be retained as a denormalized summary or removed in favor of deriving it from the association.]']
  last_site_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent visit by this technician to this site. Used for access review and credential renewal decisions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes capturing site-specific access instructions, restrictions, or special requirements for this technician at this site.']
  primary_site_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this technician is designated as the primary contact for this site. Corresponds to primary_site_contact_flag from detection data. Used for escalations and site relationship management.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date when site access was revoked, if applicable. Used for security audit and compliance tracking.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for access revocation (e.g., employment termination, security violation, contract end, customer request). Used for compliance and security incident tracking.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Level of security clearance granted for this site access. Corresponds to security_clearance_level from detection data. Determines which areas of the site the technician can access.']
  site_training_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technician has completed site-specific safety and access training requirements. Corresponds to site_training_completed_flag from detection data. Required before first site visit.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the authorization and access control relationship between field technicians and enterprise customer sites. It captures security clearances, site-specific credentials, training requirements, and access validity periods that exist only in the context of a technician being authorized to access a specific enterprise site. Each record links one technician to one site with security and access management attributes. Critical for secure site access management in telecommunications field operations where technicians must access sensitive customer facilities (data centers, government buildings, corporate campuses).. Existence Justification: In telecommunications field operations, technicians require authorized access to multiple enterprise customer sites (a regional technician may have credentials for 50+ sites across their territory), and each enterprise site has multiple authorized technicians (primary technician, backup technicians, and specialists for different service types like fiber, CPE, or network equipment). The business actively manages site access authorizations as a security and compliance function, tracking clearance levels, badge numbers, training completion, and access validity periods for each technician-site combination.'
}

Table enterprise.enterprise_account_coverage {
  enterprise_account_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for enterprise_account_coverage']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the corporate account being covered by this assignment']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee assigned to cover this corporate account']
  account_coverage_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this account coverage assignment record. Primary key.']
  account_role varchar [note: 'The specific role this employee plays in covering this corporate account. Defines responsibilities and engagement model. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when this employee was assigned to cover this corporate account in this role. Used for tenure tracking and transition management. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when this coverage assignment ended or is scheduled to end. Null for active assignments. Used for historical tracking and transition planning.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this coverage assignment. Used for active coverage reporting and transition management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this coverage assignment record was created.']
  primary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employee is the primary owner/contact for this corporate account in their role. Used for routing and escalation. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  quota_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of this employee\'s sales quota allocated to this corporate account. Used for compensation calculations and performance tracking. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  territory_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational territory scope for this coverage assignment (e.g., EMEA, North America East, Federal). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this coverage assignment record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment relationship between corporate_account and employee in the context of enterprise account management. It captures the multi-role coverage model where enterprise accounts require dedicated teams with specific roles (account executive, solutions engineer, customer success manager, technical account manager). Each record links one corporate_account to one employee with attributes that define the role, territory, quota allocation, and ownership status of that specific coverage assignment.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications enterprise B2B operations, corporate accounts require multi-role team coverage where multiple employees serve each account in specialized roles (account executive, solutions engineer, customer success manager, technical account manager). Each employee typically covers multiple corporate accounts with varying quota allocations and territory responsibilities. The business actively manages these coverage assignments with role definitions, quota splits, primary ownership flags, and territory scopes.'
}

Table people.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee record. Primary key for the employee entity. System-generated surrogate key used across all internal systems.']
  location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary physical work location or office where the employee is based. Used for space planning, emergency response, and regional compliance.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record of this employee\'s direct manager. Used for reporting hierarchy, approval workflows, and organizational structure visualization.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational department to which the employee is assigned. Used for cost allocation, reporting hierarchies, and organizational analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Employee\'s date of birth. Used for age verification, benefits eligibility, retirement planning, and compliance with labor laws.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person to contact in case of employee emergency. Used for workplace incidents, medical emergencies, and critical notifications.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the designated emergency contact. Used for urgent notifications and workplace incident response.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the employee (e.g., spouse, parent, sibling, friend). Used to validate contact authority and prioritize notifications.']
  employee_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique employee identifier assigned by Human Resources. Used on badges, timesheets, and payroll systems. Externally-known identifier for the employee.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee within the organization. Determines access rights, payroll processing, and system permissions.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee\'s employment arrangement. Determines benefits eligibility, work schedule expectations, and contract terms.']
  fte_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of full-time hours the employee is contracted to work. 100.00 represents full-time; part-time employees have values less than 100. Used for headcount reporting and capacity planning.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Employee\'s self-identified gender. Used for diversity reporting, benefits administration, and compliance with equal employment opportunity regulations.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee most recently joined the organization. For rehires, this is the date of the most recent hire event. Used for seniority calculations, benefits vesting, and anniversary recognition.']
  hr_system_source_code varchar [note: 'Original identifier from the source HR system of record. Used for data lineage, system integration, and reconciliation with upstream HR platforms.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Employee\'s current job title or position name. Used for organizational charts, business cards, email signatures, and role-based access control.']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal first name as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Used for payroll, tax forms, and official HR records.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal last name (surname/family name) as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Used for payroll, tax forms, and official HR records.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal middle name or initial as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Optional field; may be null if not applicable.']
  marital_status varchar [note: 'Employee\'s current marital status. Used for tax withholding calculations, benefits enrollment, and emergency contact validation.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number (e.g., Social Security Number in USA, National Insurance Number in UK). Used for tax reporting, benefits administration, and legal compliance.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Employee\'s country of citizenship using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Used for work authorization verification, international assignment eligibility, and compliance reporting.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of advance notice required for voluntary resignation or termination, as specified in the employment contract. Used for workforce planning and transition management.']
  original_hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee first joined the organization, even if they left and were rehired. Used for calculating total service time and long-term benefits eligibility.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Employee\'s compensation grade or band within the organization\'s salary structure. Used for compensation planning, equity analysis, and career progression tracking.']
  personal_email varchar [note: 'Employee\'s personal email address. Used for emergency communications, benefits notifications, and post-employment correspondence.']
  personal_mobile varchar [note: 'Employee\'s personal mobile phone number. Used for emergency contact, two-factor authentication, and urgent business communications.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Employee\'s preferred language for business communications and system interfaces, using ISO 639-1 two-letter language code. Used for localization, training materials, and HR communications.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Name the employee prefers to be called in day-to-day business interactions. May differ from legal name. Used in email signatures, directory listings, and internal communications.']
  probation_end_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s probationary period ends. Used to trigger performance reviews, benefits eligibility changes, and employment status updates.']
  rehire_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for rehire based on their employment history and termination circumstances. Used for recruitment screening and applicant tracking.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Employee\'s security clearance or access authorization level. Used for physical access control, data access permissions, and project assignment eligibility. Null if no clearance required.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended. Null for active employees. Used for final payroll processing, benefits termination, and access revocation.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for employment termination. Used for turnover analysis, exit interview tracking, and rehire eligibility determination. Null for active employees.']
  union_membership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of a labor union. Used for collective bargaining agreement compliance, dues deduction, and labor relations reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.']
  work_email varchar [note: 'Employee\'s primary corporate email address. Used for business communications, system notifications, and authentication. Typically follows company email naming convention.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'Employee\'s primary work phone number (desk phone or assigned mobile). Used for business contact and directory listings.']
  work_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee\'s work schedule pattern. Used for time tracking, shift planning, and workforce management.']

  Note: 'Master record for every internal workforce identity across the telecommunications enterprise — full-time, part-time, and fixed-term employees. Captures legal name, employee number, national ID, date of birth, gender, marital status, nationality, employment type, employment status (active, on-leave, terminated, retired), hire date, original hire date (for rehires), termination date, termination reason, probation end date, notice period, work location, primary work email, work phone, personal email, personal mobile, emergency contact name and relationship, emergency contact phone, preferred language, and HR system source identifier. This is the SSOT for all internal person identities and is the anchor entity for the entire people domain. Distinct from workforce.technician (field operations personnel) and customer.subscriber (external subscribers).'
}

Table people.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the organizational unit within the telecommunications enterprise hierarchy.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Organizational units map to cost centers for hierarchy-based budget rollups and management reporting. Telecommunications requires alignment between HR org structure and financial reporting structure. ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity to which this organizational unit belongs, used for financial consolidation, regulatory reporting, and compliance purposes.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent organizational unit in the hierarchy, enabling multi-level organizational structure traversal and roll-up reporting. Null for top-level units.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Organizational units in telecommunications align to profit centers for P&L reporting by business segment (consumer mobile, enterprise, wholesale). Enables revenue/cost center alignment and segment pro']
  actual_headcount varchar [note: 'Current actual headcount or number of employees assigned to this organizational unit, used for variance analysis against budget.']
  annual_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual operating budget allocated to this organizational unit for OPEX (Operational Expenditure) planning and financial control.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the annual budget amount, supporting multi-currency enterprise operations.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Higher-level business unit code to which this organizational unit belongs, used for strategic reporting and business segment analysis.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the organizational unit is primarily located or headquartered.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting this organizational unit, used for internal communication and directory services.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for contacting this organizational unit, used for internal communication and directory services.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the primary country of operation for this organizational unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit ceased operations or was dissolved. Null for currently active units. Supports historical organizational structure analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit became active and operational within the enterprise structure, supporting temporal hierarchy modeling.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Primary functional area or business domain classification of the organizational unit (e.g., network operations, commercial, finance, IT, HR, legal, regulatory, customer service). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: network_operations|commercial|finance|information_technology|human_resources|legal|regulatory|customer_service|engineering|marketing|sales|procurement|supply_chain — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or territory served or managed by this organizational unit, used for regional reporting and market segmentation.']
  head_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who serves as the head, manager, or leader of this organizational unit, responsible for its operations and performance.']
  headcount_budget varchar [note: 'Approved headcount budget or authorized number of full-time equivalent (FTE) positions allocated to this organizational unit for workforce planning.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this organizational unit within the enterprise hierarchy, with 1 being the top level (e.g., CEO office) and increasing numbers representing deeper levels.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full hierarchical path from root to this organizational unit, typically represented as a delimited string of org unit codes or IDs for efficient hierarchy queries.']
  is_customer_facing boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this organizational unit has direct customer interaction responsibilities (e.g., customer service, sales, field operations).']
  is_revenue_generating boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this organizational unit directly generates revenue (e.g., sales, commercial units) or is a support/cost center.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was last updated, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.']
  manager_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the organizational unit head or manager (e.g., Vice President, Director, Manager, Team Lead).']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Specific office location, building name, or site identifier where the organizational unit is physically located.']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the organizational unit, used in financial systems, reporting hierarchies, and access control. Typically alphanumeric and unique across the enterprise.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the organizational unit\'s purpose, responsibilities, scope of work, and role within the broader enterprise structure.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the organizational unit as recognized in corporate documentation, organizational charts, and HR systems.']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organizational unit indicating whether it is active, inactive, pending activation, suspended, dissolved, or merged into another unit.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational unit indicating its hierarchical level and structural role within the enterprise (e.g., division, department, team, cost center group, business unit). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: division|department|team|cost_center_group|business_unit|function|region|subsidiary — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or short form name of the organizational unit used in reports, dashboards, and user interfaces where space is limited.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA tier classification for this organizational unit indicating priority level for internal service delivery and support escalation.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the primary operating location of this organizational unit, used for scheduling and workforce management.']
  workforce_type varchar [note: 'Primary workforce composition type for this organizational unit (e.g., permanent employees, contractors, hybrid, outsourced, offshore).']

  Note: 'Master record for every organizational unit (department, division, business unit, cost center group, team) within the telecommunications enterprise hierarchy. Captures org unit code, name, description, org unit type (division, department, team, cost center group), parent org unit reference for hierarchy traversal, effective start and end dates, org unit head employee reference, legal entity association, geographic region, functional area classification (network operations, commercial, finance, IT, HR, legal, regulatory), headcount budget, and active status. Supports multi-level organizational hierarchy modeling for reporting, access control, and workforce planning.'
}

Table people.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the position record. Primary key for the position entity.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to people.job_profile. Business justification: Link each Position to its standardized Job_Profile, consolidating duplicate descriptive fields and enabling consistent reporting.']
  location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work location or site where the position is based. Links to facility or office location master data.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit (department, division, cost center) to which this position is assigned. Links position to the organizational hierarchy.']
  reporting_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager position to which this position reports. Establishes the reporting hierarchy independent of individual employees. Null for top-level executive positions.']
  reports_to_position_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on position (reports_to_position_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the position was officially approved by management or HR governance. Marks formal authorization to create and fund the position.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or manager who approved the position creation. Used for governance and audit trail purposes.']
  budgeted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is included in the approved headcount budget. True if budgeted and approved for funding, False if not budgeted or pending budget approval.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the position\'s compensation and benefits expenses are charged. Used for financial planning and expense allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  creation_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason for creating the position. Captures whether the position was created for new business needs, as a replacement, due to restructuring, expansion, regulatory requirements, or project needs.']
  critical_position_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is designated as business-critical or key to operations. True for positions where vacancy would significantly impact business continuity or service delivery.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position is scheduled to end or was eliminated from the organizational structure. Null for ongoing permanent positions. Used for fixed-term positions and position elimination tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position becomes active and available in the organizational structure. Marks the beginning of the position\'s lifecycle.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Standard employment classification indicating whether the position is full-time, part-time, job-share, or on-call. Complements FTE allocation with categorical classification.']
  exempt_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the position under Fair Labor Standards Act for overtime eligibility. Exempt positions are not eligible for overtime pay, non-exempt positions are eligible.']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Full-time equivalent allocation for the position. 1.00 represents a full-time position, 0.50 represents half-time, etc. Used for headcount planning and budgeting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the position was last reviewed for relevance, classification accuracy, and organizational fit. Used for periodic position management and organizational design reviews.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next position review. Used to ensure regular evaluation of position relevance and classification accuracy.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the position, typically used in HR systems and organizational charts. Unique alphanumeric code assigned to each approved headcount slot.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the position\'s responsibilities, duties, and scope. Provides comprehensive overview of what the role entails independent of the incumbent.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position. Indicates whether the position is active and available, currently filled, vacant and open for recruitment, frozen (temporarily unavailable), eliminated, or pending approval.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the position based on employment duration and nature. Distinguishes between permanent headcount, fixed-term roles, contractor slots, and temporary positions.']
  required_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Minimum security clearance or background check level required for the position. Relevant for positions with access to sensitive network infrastructure, customer data, or classified information.']
  required_qualifications_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of minimum required qualifications, certifications, education, and experience for the position. Used for recruitment and succession planning.']
  span_of_control varchar [note: 'Number of direct report positions that report to this position. Used for organizational design and management capacity planning. Zero for non-supervisory positions.']
  succession_plan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal succession plan exists for this position. True if succession planning is active, False otherwise. Typically True for critical and leadership positions.']
  supervisory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position has supervisory or management responsibilities. True if the position manages direct reports, False otherwise.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'Expected percentage of time the position requires business travel. Expressed as integer percentage (0-100). Used for role expectations and expense planning.']
  union_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for union membership or covered by collective bargaining agreements. Relevant for labor relations and compliance.']
  work_arrangement varchar [note: 'Standard work arrangement or location flexibility for the position. Indicates whether the role is on-site, fully remote, hybrid, field-based, or mobile.']

  Note: 'Master record for every approved headcount position within the organizational structure — the slot that an employee fills, independent of the person occupying it. Captures position code, position title, position grade/band, job family, job function, org unit assignment, reporting position (manager position), FTE allocation, position type (permanent, fixed-term, contractor slot), required qualifications summary, required clearance level, budgeted flag, filled/vacant status, effective start and end dates, and position creation reason. Enables workforce planning, vacancy tracking, and succession management independent of individual employee records.'
}

Table people.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job profile. Primary key for the job profile reference master.']
  parent_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on job_profile (parent_job_profile_id)']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the job profile is eligible for performance-based bonus or incentive compensation. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Primary business unit or division to which the job profile is aligned. May be null for enterprise-wide job profiles applicable across multiple business units.']
  commission_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the job profile is eligible for sales commission or variable compensation tied to revenue generation. True if eligible, False otherwise. Typically applies to sales and business development roles.']
  core_competencies varchar [note: 'List of essential skills, knowledge areas, and behavioral competencies required for successful performance in the role. May include technical skills (e.g., Long-Term Evolution (LTE), 5G New Radio (5G NR), Software-Defined Networking (SDN)), leadership competencies, and domain expertise.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Default cost center code for budgeting and financial planning purposes associated with the job profile. Used in headcount planning and Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) / Operational Expenditure (OPEX) allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the job profile record was first created in the system. Record audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the job profile was or will be retired or deprecated. Null for currently active profiles with no planned end date. Used for temporal validity and historical tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the job profile became or will become active and available for use in the organization. Used for temporal validity and historical tracking.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Classification under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) indicating whether the role is exempt or non-exempt from overtime pay requirements. Critical for payroll compliance and labor law adherence.']
  grade_band varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or pay band assigned to the job profile. Determines salary range, bonus eligibility, and benefits tier. Confidential business-sensitive data used in compensation planning.']
  is_customer_facing boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the job profile involves direct interaction with external customers. True if the role regularly engages with customers, False otherwise. Used for Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and training planning.']
  is_people_manager boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the job profile includes direct people management responsibilities (supervising, coaching, performance evaluation of subordinates). True if the role manages people, False otherwise.']
  is_safety_sensitive boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the job profile is classified as safety-sensitive, requiring adherence to occupational health and safety regulations, drug testing, and specialized training. True for roles such as field technicians, network engineers working on infrastructure, False otherwise.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive narrative describing the purpose, responsibilities, duties, and scope of the job profile. Used in recruitment, performance management, and role clarification.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the job profile into a major functional area or discipline. Primary classification for workforce segmentation and career pathing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Network Engineering|Sales|Finance|Information Technology|Human Resources|Legal|Customer Service|Marketing|Operations|Product Management|Supply Chain|Security|Compliance|Data Analytics|Corporate Strategy — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level or seniority tier of the job profile within the organizational structure. Used for compensation banding, reporting hierarchy definition, and career progression planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Individual Contributor|Team Lead|Manager|Senior Manager|Director|Senior Director|Vice President|Senior Vice President|Executive Vice President|C-Level — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_profile_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the job profile across the enterprise. Used in Human Resources (HR) systems, payroll, and workforce planning. Business identifier for external reference.']
  job_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile. Active profiles are available for use in hiring and workforce planning. Inactive or deprecated profiles are retained for historical reference but not used for new hires.']
  job_subfamily varchar [note: 'Secondary classification within the job family providing finer granularity. For example, within Network Engineering: Radio Access Network (RAN), Core Network, Transport, Operations Support Systems (OSS).']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title as it appears in organizational charts, offer letters, and employee records. Human-readable identity label for the role.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the job profile record was most recently updated. Record audit field for change tracking and data governance.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the job profile was last reviewed and validated by Human Resources (HR) or the business owner. Used to ensure job profiles remain current and aligned with organizational needs.']
  minimum_education_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum level of formal education required for the job profile. Used in candidate screening and workforce planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: High School Diploma|Associate Degree|Bachelor Degree|Master Degree|Doctoral Degree|Professional Certification|No Formal Requirement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  minimum_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for the job profile. Used in recruitment and succession planning.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the job profile. Used for governance and ensuring job architecture remains aligned with business strategy.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the job profile is eligible for overtime compensation. Typically aligns with Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) non-exempt classification. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Human Resources (HR) business partner or functional leader responsible for maintaining and governing the job profile. Accountability field for data stewardship.']
  predecessor_job_profile_code varchar [note: 'Job profile code of the typical prior role in the career path leading to this job profile. Used for career progression planning and internal mobility analysis. May be null for entry-level roles.']
  remote_work_eligibility varchar [note: 'Classification of the job profiles eligibility for remote or hybrid work arrangements. Reflects organizational policy and role requirements.']
  security_clearance_required varchar [note: 'Level of government or organizational security clearance required for the job profile. Applicable for roles handling sensitive national security, lawful intercept, or critical infrastructure data.']
  stock_option_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the job profile is eligible for equity compensation such as stock options, restricted stock units, or employee stock purchase plans. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  successor_job_profile_code varchar [note: 'Job profile code of the typical next role in the career path following this job profile. Used for career progression planning and succession management. May be null for terminal roles.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'Expected percentage of work time requiring business travel. Expressed as an integer percentage (0-100). Used in recruitment, expense planning, and work-life balance considerations.']
  union_eligibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the job profile is eligible for union membership or collective bargaining representation. Used for labor relations and compliance with collective bargaining agreements.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining standardized job profiles and role definitions used across the telecommunications enterprise. Captures job profile code, job title, job family (e.g., Network Engineering, Sales, Finance, IT, HR, Legal), job subfamily, job level/grade band, FLSA classification (exempt/non-exempt), standard job description, core competencies required, minimum education requirement, minimum years of experience, typical career path predecessor and successor roles, and active status. Serves as the canonical reference for job architecture and compensation banding. Distinct from position (which is an org-specific headcount slot) and from workforce.skill (which tracks individual proficiency).'
}

Table people.assignment {
  assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee assignment record. Primary key for the assignment entity.']
  assignment_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the manager or HR administrator who approved this assignment. Null if assignment_approval_status is not approved.']
  assignment_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the HR administrator or system user who created this assignment record. Used for audit trails and accountability.']
  assignment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who holds this assignment. Links to the employee master record in the people domain.']
  assignment_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the direct manager or supervisor for this assignment. Enables reporting hierarchy reconstruction.']
  assignment_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the HR administrator or system user who last modified this assignment record. Used for audit trails and change management.']
  location_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or virtual work location where the employee performs their duties under this assignment. Links to location master record.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit where the employee is assigned. Links to the org_unit master record representing department, division, or business unit.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position that the employee is assigned to. Links to the position master record defining the role, grade, and job responsibilities.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Assignments in telecommunications require P&L attribution of labor costs to business segments (network operations, customer service, sales). Enables revenue-generating vs support cost segregation and ']
  superseded_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on assignment (superseded_assignment_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for this assignment. Indicates whether the assignment has been approved by appropriate authorities such as HR, finance, or management.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assignment was approved. Null if assignment_approval_status is not approved. Used for compliance and audit purposes.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the assignment record. Follows format ASG-########. Used for operational reference and audit trails.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment. Indicates whether the assignment is currently active, suspended, terminated, pending activation, or on leave.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignment indicating whether it is the employee\'s primary role, a secondary/concurrent assignment, or a temporary acting/interim position.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center to which this assignment\'s labor costs are charged. Used for financial accounting, budgeting, and OPEX (Operational Expenditure) tracking. Follows format CC-######.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'The data classification level for this assignment record. Indicates the sensitivity and access control requirements for the assignment information.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this assignment became or will become effective. Used for time-based queries and historical reporting hierarchy reconstruction.']
  employment_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment relationship for this assignment. Distinguishes between regular employees, temporary workers, interns, contractors, and consultants.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the assignment ended or is scheduled to end. Null for open-ended assignments. Used for historical tracking and workforce planning.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this assignment expired or will expire. Null for open-ended assignments. Used for temporal queries and workforce planning.']
  fte_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of full-time work that this assignment represents. 100.00 indicates full-time, values below 100 indicate part-time. Used for workforce capacity planning and OPEX (Operational Expenditure) calculations.']
  is_critical_role boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this assignment is designated as a critical role for business continuity and succession planning purposes. Used for workforce risk management.']
  is_primary_assignment boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the employee\'s primary assignment. An employee may have multiple concurrent assignments, but only one can be primary for reporting and benefits purposes.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'The broad occupational category or job family that this assignment belongs to, such as Engineering, Network Operations, Customer Service, Finance, or Sales. Used for workforce analytics and career pathing.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchical level or grade of the position within the organization\'s job architecture. Examples include Individual Contributor, Team Lead, Manager, Senior Manager, Director, Vice President.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this assignment record was last modified. Used for audit trails, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional notes, comments, or special circumstances related to this assignment. Used by HR administrators for context and documentation.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days of advance notice required for termination of this assignment, as defined by employment contract or policy. Used for workforce planning and compliance.']
  overtime_eligibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employee in this assignment is eligible for overtime compensation. Exempt employees are not eligible for overtime pay under labor regulations.']
  probation_end_date date [note: 'The date when the probationary period for this assignment ends. Applicable for new hires or employees in new roles. Null if no probation period applies.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for creating or modifying this assignment. Supports HR analytics on workforce movements and organizational changes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_hire|transfer|promotion|demotion|reorganization|secondment|return_from_leave|contract_renewal|role_change — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description providing additional context for the assignment reason. Captures business justification and circumstances of the assignment action.']
  remote_work_eligibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employee in this assignment is eligible for remote work arrangements based on role requirements, policy, and business needs.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order of this assignment in the employee\'s assignment history. Starts at 1 for first assignment and increments with each new assignment.']
  source_system_assignment_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this assignment in the source system of record. Used for data reconciliation and integration traceability.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this assignment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in multi-system HR landscapes.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the employee formally began this assignment. Represents the effective start of the employment relationship in this position and org unit.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific labor union or collective bargaining unit that represents the employee in this assignment. Null if union_membership_flag is false.']
  union_membership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of a labor union under this assignment. Used for compliance with collective bargaining agreements and labor relations management.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of where the employee performs work under this assignment. Distinguishes between on-site office work, remote work, hybrid arrangements, field-based roles, and mobile workforce.']
  work_schedule_pattern varchar [note: 'The standard work schedule pattern for this assignment. Defines the expected working hours arrangement such as standard 40-hour week, compressed workweek, shift rotation, or flexible schedule.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each formal employment assignment of an employee to a position within an org unit — the core HR action record that tracks WHERE an employee works, in WHAT role, and under WHOM. Captures assignment sequence number, employee reference, position reference, org unit reference, manager employee reference, assignment type (primary, secondary/concurrent), employment category (regular, temporary, intern, contractor), FTE percentage, work schedule pattern, work location type (on-site, remote, hybrid), assignment start date, assignment end date, assignment reason (new hire, transfer, promotion, demotion, reorganization, secondment), and assignment status. Supports full organizational history and reporting hierarchy reconstruction at any point in time.'
}

Table people.compensation {
  compensation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compensation record. Primary key for the compensation entity.']
  compensation_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'The employee ID of the manager or HR representative who approved this compensation plan or change.']
